2026-02-26T02:03:35.5572378Z ## Specific Content Guidelines
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5572882Z 
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5573071Z ### DFT/Quantum Chemistry tools accurate info:
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5574770Z - **Quantum ESPRESSO**: plane-wave DFT, pseudopotentials, PWscf module, ultrasoft/PAW, developed by SISSA/CINECA, GPL license, https://www.quantum-espresso.org, GitHub: https://github.com/QEF/q-e
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5576915Z - **VASP**: Vienna Ab initio Simulation Package, plane-wave DFT, PAW method, commercial license, developed at University of Vienna, https://www.vasp.at
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5579297Z - **ORCA**: multireference methods, DFT, coupled cluster, developed by Frank Neese at MPI Mülheim, free for academic use, https://orcaforum.kofo.mpg.de
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5580805Z - **Gaussian**: pioneer commercial QC software, G16 current version, Gaussian Inc., https://gaussian.com
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5582145Z - **NWChem**: open-source, EMSL/PNNL, MPI parallel, https://www.nwchem-sw.org, GitHub: https://github.com/nwchemgit/nwchem
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5583723Z - **CP2K**: mixed Gaussian/plane-wave GPW method, QUICKSTEP module, open-source GPL, https://www.cp2k.org, GitHub: https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5585300Z - **Psi4**: Python-driven, open-source, many-body methods, https://psicode.org, GitHub: https://github.com/psi4/psi4
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5586477Z - **MOPAC**: semi-empirical QM, PM7/PM6 methods, James Stewart, http://openmopac.net
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5587773Z - **Octopus**: TDDFT, real-space grids, strong-field physics, https://octopus-code.org, GitHub: https://github.com/octopus-code/octopus
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5600684Z - **GAMESS**: General Atomic and Molecular Electronic Structure System, Gordon group Iowa State, http://www.msg.ameslab.gov/gamess/
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5601618Z 
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5601776Z ### MD Simulation tools:
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5602933Z - **GROMACS**: fast MD, LINCS constraints, Verlet integrator, AMBER/CHARMM/OPLS force fields, open-source LGPL, https://www.gromacs.org, GitHub: https://github.com/gromacs/gromacs
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5604224Z - **LAMMPS**: many-body potentials, reactive force fields (ReaxFF), extended systems, Sandia National Labs, open-source GPL, https://www.lammps.org, GitHub: https://github.com/lammps/lammps
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5605360Z - **AMBER**: biomolecular simulation, AMBER force fields, GPU acceleration, University of California, commercial+academic, https://ambermd.org
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5606311Z - **NAMD**: VMD-integrated, biomolecular MD, CHARMM force fields, Schulten group UIUC, free academic, https://www.ks.uiuc.edu/Research/namd
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5607529Z - **OpenMM**: GPU-accelerated, Python API, extensible, Pande group Stanford → OpenMM community, MIT license, https://openmm.org, GitHub: https://github.com/openmm/openmm
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5608159Z 
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5608253Z ### Cheminformatics:
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5608934Z - **RDKit**: open-source cheminformatics, Morgan fingerprints, substructure search, 2D/3D generation, BSD license, https://www.rdkit.org, GitHub: https://github.com/rdkit/rdkit
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5610510Z - **Open Babel**: chemical file format interconversion, 100+ formats, SMILES/InChI, GPL, https://openbabel.org, GitHub: https://github.com/openbabel/openbabel
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5611594Z - **DeepChem**: deep learning for chemistry/drug discovery, TensorFlow/PyTorch backends, MIT license, https://deepchem.io, GitHub: https://github.com/deepchem/deepchem
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5612229Z 
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5612317Z ### Visualization:
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5612853Z - **VMD**: Visual Molecular Dynamics, trajectory analysis, UIUC Schulten group, free for non-commercial, https://www.ks.uiuc.edu/Research/vmd
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5614040Z - **PyMOL**: protein/molecular visualization, Schrödinger commercial + open-source version, https://pymol.org, GitHub: https://github.com/schrodinger/pymol-open-source
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5615087Z - **Avogadro**: molecular editor/visualizer, open-source, Qt-based, https://avogadro.cc, GitHub: https://github.com/avogadro/avogadro2
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5616275Z - **VESTA**: crystal structure visualization, electron density, Koichi Momma, free for non-commercial, https://jp-minerals.org/vesta/en/
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5616779Z 
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5616867Z ### Toxicity/ADMET:
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5617537Z - **SwissADME**: web tool for ADME/drug-likeness prediction, Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics, free, http://www.swissadme.ch
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5618317Z - **pkCSM**: ADMET prediction using graph-based signatures, free web tool, https://biosig.lab.uq.edu.au/pkcsm/
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5618740Z 
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5618836Z ## Additional Instructions:
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5619413Z 1. Create all directories first with `mkdir -p`
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5619755Z 2. Use bash heredoc to write files
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5620285Z 3. Make content substantive - each file should have at least 100 lines of useful content
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5620839Z 4. Include real method names, equations descriptions, and accurate feature lists
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5621249Z 5. Cross-link between related tools
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5621749Z 6. After creating all files, do `git add -A && git commit -m "Add comprehensive chemistry tools knowledge repository
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5622162Z 
2026-02-26T02:03:35.5622365Z Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>"`

2026-02-26T02:04:02.8440781Z       ## How to Use This Repository
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8441018Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8441438Z       This repository is organized as a knowledge base for computational chemistry tools. Each tool entry contains:
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8441916Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8442233Z       1. **index.md** – Overview, key features, license, quick start
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8442756Z       2. **technology.md** – Theoretical foundations, algorithms, software architecture
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8443308Z       3. **methods.md** – Computational methods, input/output formats, workflows
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8443866Z       4. **use-cases.md** – Real-world applications, example use cases, research areas
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8444460Z       5. **references.md** – Primary citations, documentation, tutorials, community links
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8444855Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8445046Z       ### Finding the Right Tool
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8445273Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8445548Z       - Browse by [Category](categories/) to explore tools for your domain
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8445991Z       - Use the table above to quickly compare licenses and capabilities
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8446462Z       - Each tool page cross-references related tools to help you find alternatives
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8446835Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8447012Z       ### Running the Docs Locally
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8447240Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8447409Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8447615Z       pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8447868Z       mkdocs serve
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8448052Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8448217Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8448489Z       Then open [http://127.0.0.1:8000](http://127.0.0.1:8000).
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8448798Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8449420Z       ## Contributing
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8449625Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8450294Z       Contributions are welcome! See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on adding tools, improving accuracy, and updating references.
2026-02-26T02:04:02.8450913Z

2026-02-26T02:04:37.5714312Z       # Contributing to Tools for Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5714782Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5716096Z       Thank you for helping improve this knowledge repository! Contributions of all sizes are welcome — from fixing a typo to adding a fully documented new tool.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5717243Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5717542Z       ## Ways to Contribute
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5717889Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5718453Z       - **Add a new tool** – Follow the template structure below
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5719671Z       - **Improve existing content** – Correct inaccuracies, expand sections, update URLs
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5720711Z       - **Add references** – Include DOIs, tutorial links, or community resources
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5721879Z       - **Fix formatting** – Improve readability, tables, code blocks
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5722450Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5722750Z       ## Getting Started
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5723092Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5723398Z       1. Fork the repository on GitHub
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5723837Z       2. Clone your fork:
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5724177Z          ```bash
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5724681Z          git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/Tools-for-Chemistry.git
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5725347Z          cd Tools-for-Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5725745Z          ```
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5726064Z       3. Create a branch:
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5726425Z          ```bash
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5726772Z          git checkout -b add-tool-name
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5727188Z          ```
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5727580Z       4. Make your changes following the templates below
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5728093Z       5. Preview locally:
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5728447Z          ```bash
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5729310Z          pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5729777Z          mkdocs serve
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5730319Z          ```
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5730757Z       6. Commit and push your branch, then open a Pull Request
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5731284Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5731597Z       ## File Structure for a New Tool
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5732006Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5732521Z       Each tool lives in `tools/<tool-slug>/` and must contain all five files:
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5733137Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5733417Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5733724Z       tools/my-tool/
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5734353Z       ├── index.md        # Overview, features, license, quick start
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5735210Z       ├── technology.md   # Theory, algorithms, architecture, performance
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5736069Z       ├── methods.md      # Methods, file formats, workflows, code examples
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5736922Z       ├── use-cases.md    # Applications, example use cases, industries
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5737761Z       └── references.md   # Citations, docs, tutorials, community
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5738303Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5738602Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5738908Z       ### Naming Conventions
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5739487Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5740015Z       - Use lowercase, hyphen-separated slugs: `open-babel`, `quantum-espresso`
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5740827Z       - Keep filenames consistent: exactly the five files listed above
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5741408Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5741691Z       ## Templates
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5741968Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5742234Z       ### index.md
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5742524Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5742789Z       ```markdown
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5743083Z       # Tool Name
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5743402Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5743843Z       **Category:** DFT / Molecular Dynamics / Cheminformatics / etc.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5744518Z       **Developer/Organization:** Person or institution name
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5745187Z       **License:** GPL / MIT / Commercial / Free for academic use
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5745775Z       **Website:** https://example.com
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5746377Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/org/repo  *(if applicable)*
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5746911Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5747198Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5747502Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5748194Z       Two or three paragraphs describing what the tool does, its history, and its
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5748884Z       position in the field.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5749452Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5749736Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5750070Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5750418Z       - Feature 1 with enough detail to be useful
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5750974Z       - Feature 2 with enough detail to be useful
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5751474Z       - Feature 3 ...
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5751831Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5752127Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5752446Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5752856Z       Brief installation command and a minimal usage example.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5753391Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5753679Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5754012Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5754624Z       - [Other Tool](../../tools/other-tool/index.md) – one-line comparison
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5755218Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5755491Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5755787Z       ### technology.md
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5756135Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5756425Z       ```markdown
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5756876Z       # Tool Name — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5757306Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5757620Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5758003Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5758383Z       Describe the underlying physics/chemistry theory.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5758881Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5759553Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5759986Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5760385Z       - Method A – short description
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5760904Z       - Method B – short description
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5761597Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5761910Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5762504Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5763018Z       Language, parallelization strategy (MPI, OpenMP, CUDA), major modules.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5763660Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5763984Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5764386Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5764787Z       Benchmark numbers, HPC capabilities, GPU support.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5765290Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5765595Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5765989Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5766487Z       Key libraries (BLAS, LAPACK, libxc, etc.) and integration with other tools.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5767116Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5767406Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5767693Z       ### methods.md
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5768015Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5768294Z       ```markdown
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5768725Z       # Tool Name — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5769356Z

2026-02-26T02:04:37.5770034Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5770370Z       Detailed list with descriptions.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5770795Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5771100Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5771478Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5771826Z       Supported file formats and converters.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5772267Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5772569Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5772926Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5773383Z       Step-by-step example, including code blocks where appropriate.

2026-02-26T02:04:37.5795783Z       ## Content Quality Standards
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5796187Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5796749Z       - **Accuracy**: Verify all URLs, version numbers, and method names before submitting.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5797857Z       - **Depth**: Each file should contain substantive content — at least 80–120 lines.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5799195Z       - **Real examples**: Include real command-line examples, real method names (e.g., `pw.x`, `B3LYP`, `CCSD(T)`), and real DOIs.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5800465Z       - **Cross-linking**: Link to related tools using relative paths: `../../tools/other-tool/index.md`.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5801487Z       - **No placeholders**: Remove any `[TODO]` or placeholder text before opening a PR.
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5802389Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5802697Z       ## Style Guide
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5803025Z       
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5803563Z       - Use sentence case for section headings: `## Key features` not `## Key Features`
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5804289Z         (exception: proper nouns and acronyms)
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5804976Z       - Use fenced code blocks with language identifiers: ` ```bash `, ` ```python `
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5805731Z       - Tables should use standard Markdown table syntax
2026-02-26T02:04:37.5806408Z       - DOIs should be formatted as: `https://doi.org/10.XXXX/XXXXXX`

2026-02-26T02:05:05.2766614Z       # Density Functional Theory (DFT) Tools
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2768204Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2781042Z       Density Functional Theory is the workhorse of modern computational materials science and chemistry. DFT maps the intractable many-body Schrödinger equation onto a set of single-particle equations (the Kohn-Sham equations) by expressing the ground-state energy as a functional of the electron density. This approach reduces the complexity from O(N^3N) to O(N^3) (or better with linear-scaling methods), making calculations on systems of hundreds to thousands of atoms feasible.
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2782855Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2783051Z       ## Key Concepts
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2783254Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2783738Z       - **Exchange-correlation functionals**: LDA, GGA (PBE, PBEsol), meta-GGA (SCAN, r2SCAN), hybrid (B3LYP, HSE06), double-hybrid
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2784566Z       - **Basis sets**: Plane waves, Gaussian-type orbitals (GTOs), numeric atom-centered orbitals (NAOs), real-space grids
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2785465Z       - **Pseudopotentials / PAW**: Norm-conserving, ultrasoft (USPP), projector-augmented wave (PAW)
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2786540Z       - **Periodic boundary conditions**: Essential for crystals, surfaces, and bulk materials
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2787149Z       - **Dispersion corrections**: DFT-D3, DFT-D4, many-body dispersion (MBD), vdW-DF
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2787686Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2787982Z       ## Tools in This Category
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2788299Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2788548Z       | Tool | Basis Set | Parallelization | License | Best For |
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2788917Z       |------|-----------|-----------------|---------|----------|
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2789752Z       | [Quantum ESPRESSO](../tools/quantum-espresso/index.md) | Plane waves | MPI+OpenMP+GPU | GPL | Periodic solids, phonons, surfaces |
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2790601Z       | [VASP](../tools/vasp/index.md) | Plane waves | MPI+OpenMP+GPU | Commercial | Materials science, high-throughput DFT |
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2791322Z       | [CP2K](../tools/cp2k/index.md) | Gaussian + PW (GPW) | MPI+OpenMP+GPU | GPL | AIMD, large periodic systems |
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2792011Z       | [ORCA](../tools/orca/index.md) | Gaussian (GTO) | MPI | Free (academic) | Molecular DFT, spectroscopy |
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2793138Z       | [Gaussian](../tools/gaussian/index.md) | Gaussian (GTO) | Shared memory | Commercial | Molecular: thermochemistry, reactions |
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2793894Z       | [NWChem](../tools/nwchem/index.md) | GTO + PW | MPI | Apache-2.0 | Large-scale periodic and molecular DFT |
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2794593Z       | [Psi4](../tools/psi4/index.md) | Gaussian (GTO) | MPI+OpenMP | LGPL | Python-driven DFT and wavefunction methods |
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2795345Z       | [Octopus](../tools/octopus/index.md) | Real-space grids | MPI+OpenMP+GPU | GPL | TDDFT, strong-field, non-equilibrium |
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2795855Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2796036Z       ## Choosing a DFT Code
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2796251Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2796533Z       - **Periodic solids/surfaces/phonons**: Quantum ESPRESSO, VASP, CP2K
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2797006Z       - **Molecular spectroscopy and excited states**: ORCA, Gaussian, NWChem
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2797519Z       - **Ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD)**: CP2K, Quantum ESPRESSO (CP module), VASP
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2798107Z       - **High-throughput screening**: VASP (with AFlow/pymatgen), Quantum ESPRESSO (with AiiDA)
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2798635Z       - **Time-dependent phenomena, laser-matter interaction**: Octopus
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2799199Z       - **Python scripting and automation**: Psi4, CP2K (via ASE/AiiDA)
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2799527Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2799704Z       ## Common Workflows
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2799910Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2800097Z       ### Single-Point Energy Calculation
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2800411Z       1. Prepare crystal structure (CIF or POSCAR format)
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2800844Z       2. Select exchange-correlation functional and pseudopotentials/PAW datasets
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2801269Z       3. Set plane-wave cutoff and k-point mesh
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2801546Z       4. Run SCF calculation
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2801829Z       5. Post-process for band structure, DOS, charge density
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2802142Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2802319Z       ### Geometry Optimization
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2802567Z       1. Start from initial structure
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2802919Z       2. Relax ionic positions (and optionally cell) until forces < threshold
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2803354Z       3. Verify convergence and check for imaginary phonon modes
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2803668Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2803842Z       ### Phonon Calculations
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2804245Z       - Density functional perturbation theory (DFPT): Quantum ESPRESSO (PHonon), VASP (DFPT)
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2804759Z       - Finite differences: PHONOPY (post-processing any DFT code)
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2805080Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2805256Z       ## Further Reading
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2805455Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2805802Z       - Kohn, W. & Sham, L. J. (1965). *Phys. Rev.* 140, A1133. https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRev.140.A1133
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2806527Z       - Perdew, J. P., Burke, K. & Ernzerhof, M. (1996). *Phys. Rev. Lett.* 77, 3865. https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevLett.77.3865 (PBE functional)
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2807448Z       - Blöchl, P. E. (1994). *Phys. Rev. B* 50, 17953. https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevB.50.17953 (PAW method)
2026-02-26T02:05:05.2807815Z

2026-02-26T02:05:27.4028460Z       # Molecular Simulation & Molecular Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4036460Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4039179Z       Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations numerically integrate Newton's equations of motion for a system of atoms, propagating positions and velocities through time using interatomic force fields. MD provides time-resolved insight into conformational dynamics, thermodynamic properties, transport coefficients, and reaction mechanisms at atomic resolution.
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4041862Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4042191Z       ## Foundational Concepts
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4042569Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4043246Z       - **Force fields**: Parameterized potential energy functions (AMBER, CHARMM, OPLS-AA, GROMOS, UFF)
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4044277Z       - **Integrators**: Velocity-Verlet, leapfrog, RESPA (multiple time-stepping)
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4045376Z       - **Thermostats**: Nosé-Hoover, Berendsen, V-rescale, Andersen
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4046167Z       - **Barostats**: Parrinello-Rahman, Berendsen, Monte Carlo barostat
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4047104Z       - **Periodic boundary conditions (PBC)**: Minimum image convention, Ewald summation, PME
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4047935Z       - **Constraints**: LINCS, SHAKE/RATTLE (bond/angle constraints)
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4048834Z       - **Enhanced sampling**: Metadynamics, umbrella sampling, replica exchange (REMD), steered MD
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4049876Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4050179Z       ## Tools in This Category
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4050561Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4051000Z       | Tool | Primary Use | Force Fields | GPU Support | License |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4051641Z       |------|-------------|-------------|-------------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4052569Z       | [GROMACS](../tools/gromacs/index.md) | Biomolecular MD | AMBER, CHARMM, OPLS-AA, GROMOS | CUDA/OpenCL | LGPL |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4053811Z       | [LAMMPS](../tools/lammps/index.md) | Materials/reactive MD | ReaxFF, EAM, Tersoff, AIREBO | CUDA/OpenCL | GPL |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4055085Z       | [AMBER](../tools/amber/index.md) | Biomolecular MD | AMBER ff14SB, ff19SB, GAFF | CUDA | Commercial + AmberTools (GPL) |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4056313Z       | [NAMD](../tools/namd/index.md) | Biomolecular MD | CHARMM, AMBER, OPLS | CUDA | Free (non-commercial) |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4057490Z       | [OpenMM](../tools/openmm/index.md) | GPU-accelerated MD | AMBER, CHARMM, OPLS | CUDA/OpenCL/Metal | MIT |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4058299Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4058610Z       ## Force Field Families
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4059200Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4059535Z       ### Biomolecular Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4060301Z       - **AMBER**: ff14SB (proteins), ff19SB (proteins), OL3/OL15 (RNA/DNA), GAFF/GAFF2 (small molecules)
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4061256Z       - **CHARMM**: CGenFF (small molecules), CHARMM36m (proteins), CHARMM36 (lipids)
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4062181Z       - **OPLS-AA / OPLS3e**: Optimized for liquid simulations, good small-molecule coverage
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4063150Z       - **GROMOS**: 54A7, 2016H66 — often used with GROMACS
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4063646Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4063957Z       ### Materials Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4064630Z       - **ReaxFF**: Reactive force field for bond breaking/forming (metals, oxides, hydrocarbons)
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4065571Z       - **EAM / FS**: Embedded atom method — metals, alloys
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4066186Z       - **Tersoff / AIREBO**: Covalent materials (Si, C, SiC)
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4066848Z       - **Buckingham / Lennard-Jones**: Ionic materials, noble gases
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4067405Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4067734Z       ## Typical MD Simulation Workflow
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4068157Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4068690Z       1. **System preparation**: Build initial coordinates (PDB, CIF), add solvent/ions
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4069747Z       2. **Parameterization**: Assign force field parameters to all atoms
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4070477Z       3. **Energy minimization**: Remove clashes and bad contacts
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4071199Z       4. **Equilibration**: NVT then NPT ensemble until properties stabilize
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4071984Z       5. **Production run**: Collect trajectory at production conditions
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4072740Z       6. **Analysis**: RMSD, RMSF, RDF, diffusion coefficients, free energies
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4073620Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4074116Z       ## Enhanced Sampling Methods
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4074532Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4074839Z       | Method | Tools | Use Case |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4075256Z       |--------|-------|----------|
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4075974Z       | Umbrella sampling + WHAM | GROMACS, AMBER, NAMD | PMF along reaction coordinate |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4076888Z       | Metadynamics | GROMACS + PLUMED, AMBER + PLUMED | Free energy landscapes |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4077757Z       | Replica exchange MD (REMD) | GROMACS, AMBER, NAMD | Conformational sampling |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4078548Z       | Steered MD (SMD) | NAMD, GROMACS | Protein unfolding, ligand unbinding |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4079626Z       | Free energy perturbation (FEP) | AMBER, GROMACS, NAMD | Binding affinity, solvation |
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4080347Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4080667Z       ## Integration with Other Tools
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4081082Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4081580Z       - **Structure preparation**: VMD, PyMOL, Avogadro, AmberTools/tleap
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4082354Z       - **Force field parameterization**: ACPYPE, CGenFF, Antechamber
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4083133Z       - **Analysis**: MDAnalysis (Python), MDTraj (Python), CPPTRAJ (AMBER)
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4083858Z       - **Visualization**: VMD, PyMOL, OVITO (for materials)
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4084559Z       - **Workflow orchestration**: OpenPathSampling, HTMD, BioSimSpace
2026-02-26T02:05:27.4085147Z

2026-02-26T02:05:50.5436549Z       # Quantum Chemistry Tools
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5436975Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5440202Z       Quantum chemistry (QC) methods solve the electronic Schrödinger equation using wavefunction-based approaches that systematically improve upon Hartree-Fock theory. These methods provide high-accuracy thermochemistry, spectroscopic constants, reaction barriers, and non-covalent interaction energies — often serving as benchmarks for DFT approximations.
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5442791Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5443147Z       ## Method Hierarchy (Jacob's Ladder of QC)
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5443591Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5443929Z       | Level | Methods | Cost | Accuracy |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5444378Z       |-------|---------|------|----------|
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5444976Z       | Hartree-Fock (HF) | RHF, UHF, ROHF | O(N^4) | Poor for correlation |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5448047Z       | DFT | LDA, GGA, hybrid, double-hybrid | O(N^3)–O(N^4) | Good–excellent |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5449248Z       | MP2 | MP2, RI-MP2, SCS-MP2 | O(N^5) | Good for correlation |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5450256Z       | Coupled Cluster | CCSD, CCSD(T), CCSDT | O(N^6)–O(N^8) | Excellent ("gold standard") |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5451244Z       | Multi-reference | CASSCF, CASPT2, NEVPT2, MRCI | Varies | Essential for degenerate states |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5452122Z       | Full CI | FCI, FCIQMC | Exponential | Exact within basis |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5452643Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5452945Z       ## Tools in This Category
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5453606Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5453913Z       | Tool | Highlights | License |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5454564Z       |------|-----------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5455237Z       | [ORCA](../tools/orca/index.md) | DFT, CC, multi-reference, spectroscopy | Free (academic) |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5456185Z       | [Gaussian](../tools/gaussian/index.md) | Broad QC suite, thermochemistry, NMR | Commercial |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5457233Z       | [NWChem](../tools/nwchem/index.md) | Scalable parallel QC + planewave DFT | Apache-2.0 |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5458241Z       | [Psi4](../tools/psi4/index.md) | Python API, CCSD(T), SAPT, density fitting | LGPL |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5459482Z       | [GAMESS](../tools/gamess/index.md) | MCSCF, EFP, FMO, TDDFT | Free (academic) |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5460440Z       | [MOPAC](../tools/mopac/index.md) | Semi-empirical PM6/PM7, large molecules | LGPL |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5461540Z       | [Quantum ESPRESSO](../tools/quantum-espresso/index.md) | Periodic DFT, plane waves, phonons | GPL |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5462566Z       | [CP2K](../tools/cp2k/index.md) | GPW, AIMD, periodic HF/hybrid | GPL |
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5463155Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5463472Z       ## Key Methodological Areas
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5463871Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5464171Z       ### Single-Reference Methods
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5464747Z       - **Hartree-Fock (HF)**: Mean-field theory; no electron correlation
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5465812Z       - **MP2 / RI-MP2**: Second-order Møller-Plesset perturbation; good for weak interactions
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5466774Z       - **CCSD(T)**: "Gold standard" of quantum chemistry; highly accurate thermochemistry
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5467770Z       - **EOM-CCSD**: Equation-of-motion coupled cluster for excited states, ionization potentials
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5468522Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5468834Z       ### Multi-Reference Methods
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5469898Z       - **CASSCF**: Complete Active Space SCF — treats static correlation in degenerate systems
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5471043Z       - **CASPT2 / NEVPT2**: Perturbation theory on top of CASSCF — adds dynamic correlation
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5472153Z       - **MRCI**: Multi-reference configuration interaction — near-exact for small systems
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5473275Z       - **DMRG**: Density matrix renormalization group — extends active space to ~50 orbitals
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5473964Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5474254Z       ### Excited States
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5475021Z       - **TDDFT**: Linear-response time-dependent DFT — efficient for organic chromophores
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5475871Z       - **EOM-CCSD**: Accurate excited states for molecules < ~100 atoms
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5476628Z       - **CASPT2 / NEVPT2**: Multi-reference excited states, photochemistry
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5477628Z       - **ADC(2) / ADC(3)**: Algebraic diagrammatic construction — good balance of cost/accuracy
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5478303Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5478593Z       ### Basis Sets
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5479359Z       - **Pople**: 6-31G*, 6-311+G** — legacy but widely used
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5480342Z       - **Dunning (cc-pVXZ)**: cc-pVDZ, cc-pVTZ, cc-pVQZ — designed for CBS extrapolation
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5481383Z       - **Ahlrichs (def2)**: def2-SVP, def2-TZVP, def2-QZVPP — efficient for ORCA/Gaussian
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5482261Z       - **ANO**: Atomic natural orbital basis sets — high accuracy
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5482773Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5483094Z       ## Computational Considerations
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5483546Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5484411Z       - **Resolution of identity (RI / density fitting)**: Reduces formal scaling of MP2 and DFT exchange; use RI-MP2, RI-J, RI-K approximations
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5485667Z       - **Frozen core approximation**: Correlate only valence electrons to save cost
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5486814Z       - **Complete basis set (CBS) extrapolation**: Estimate the infinite-basis-set limit from cc-pVTZ + cc-pVQZ calculations
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5488464Z       - **Relativistic effects**: Scalar relativistic corrections (ZORA, DKH2) required for heavier elements (Z > 36)
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5489757Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5490067Z       ## Key References
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5490418Z       
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5491448Z       - Helgaker, T., Jørgensen, P., Olsen, J. (2000). *Molecular Electronic-Structure Theory*. Wiley. ISBN: 978-0-471-96755-2
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5493037Z       - Bartlett, R. J. & Musiał, M. (2007). Coupled-cluster theory. *Rev. Mod. Phys.* 79, 291. https://doi.org/10.1103/RevModPhys.79.291
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5494280Z       - Cramer, C. J. (2004). *Essentials of Computational Chemistry*. Wiley. ISBN: 978-0-470-09182-1
2026-02-26T02:05:50.5494888Z

2026-02-26T02:06:13.9281554Z       # Computational Spectroscopy Tools
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9283654Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9285940Z       Computational spectroscopy connects quantum chemical calculations to experimentally measurable spectra. By computing molecular response properties, excitation energies, transition dipole moments, and vibrational modes, these tools allow direct comparison with infrared (IR), Raman, UV/Vis, NMR, EPR, X-ray absorption (XAS), and other spectroscopic measurements.
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9288367Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9288826Z       ## Spectroscopic Techniques Supported by Computational Tools
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9289784Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9290435Z       | Technique | Computed Quantity | Common Methods |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9291194Z       |-----------|-----------------|----------------|
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9292049Z       | IR / RAMAN | Harmonic frequencies, intensities | DFT, MP2 |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9293117Z       | UV/Vis (absorption) | Excitation energies, oscillator strengths | TDDFT, EOM-CCSD, ADC |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9294425Z       | Circular Dichroism (CD) | Rotatory strengths | TDDFT |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9295281Z       | NMR | Chemical shifts, coupling constants | DFT (GIAO) |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9296134Z       | EPR / ESR | g-tensor, hyperfine coupling | DFT, CASSCF |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9297110Z       | Mössbauer | Isomer shifts, quadrupole splittings | DFT |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9297900Z       | X-ray Absorption (XAS/XANES) | Core-level excitations | TDDFT, ROCIS, RASPT2 |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9298755Z       | X-ray Emission (XES) | Core-to-valence transitions | DFT, TDDFT |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9299839Z       | Photoelectron (PES/UPS/XPS) | Ionization potentials, binding energies | TDDFT, EOM-IP-CCSD |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9300745Z       | Resonance Raman | Excited-state gradients | TDDFT |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9301289Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9301680Z       ## Tools with Strong Spectroscopy Capabilities
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9302155Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9302491Z       | Tool | Spectroscopy Features | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9302966Z       |------|-----------------------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9303858Z       | [ORCA](../tools/orca/index.md) | IR, Raman, UV/Vis, NMR, EPR, MCD, XAS, XES, NRVS | Excellent for transition-metal spectroscopy |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9305284Z       | [Gaussian](../tools/gaussian/index.md) | IR, Raman, UV/Vis, NMR (GIAO), VCD, Anharmonic | NMR and vibrational analysis very well established |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9306647Z       | [NWChem](../tools/nwchem/index.md) | IR, Raman, UV/Vis (TDDFT), NMR | Good parallel performance |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9307763Z       | [Psi4](../tools/psi4/index.md) | IR, Raman, UV/Vis, EOM-CC properties | Strong for benchmarking |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9309494Z       | [Quantum ESPRESSO](../tools/quantum-espresso/index.md) | Phonons, IR intensities, Raman (PHonon), NMR (Chemshift) | Solids, surfaces, periodic systems |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9311364Z       | [CP2K](../tools/cp2k/index.md) | IR, Raman (periodic), NMR | Good for periodic molecules |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9312518Z       | [Octopus](../tools/octopus/index.md) | UV/Vis via RT-TDDFT, XANES, strong-field | Real-time electron dynamics |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9313688Z       | [GAMESS](../tools/gamess/index.md) | IR, Raman, UV/Vis (TDDFT), EFP-NMR | Molecular gas-phase |
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9314405Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9314758Z       ## Vibrational Spectroscopy (IR and Raman)
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9315217Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9316177Z       Harmonic frequencies are computed from the mass-weighted Hessian matrix of second derivatives of the energy with respect to nuclear coordinates:
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9317261Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9317840Z       - **Analytical second derivatives**: Efficient, available in Gaussian, ORCA (DFT), Psi4
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9318910Z       - **Numerical second derivatives**: Finite-difference approach, more generally applicable
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9320495Z       - **Anharmonic corrections**: VPT2 (vibrational perturbation theory 2nd order) — available in Gaussian and ORCA
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9321681Z       - **IR intensities**: Squared derivatives of dipole moment with respect to normal coordinates
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9322785Z       - **Raman intensities**: Derivatives of polarizability tensor with respect to normal coordinates
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9323575Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9323896Z       ## Electronic Spectroscopy (UV/Vis)
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9324321Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9324722Z       Linear-response TDDFT is the most widely used method:
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9325514Z       - **Tamm-Dancoff approximation (TDA-TDDFT)**: More stable for charge-transfer states
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9326824Z       - **Range-separated hybrids**: CAM-B3LYP, ωB97X-D — improve long-range charge-transfer excitations
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9327833Z       - **EOM-CCSD**: Higher accuracy for small molecules; expensive (O(N^6))
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9328731Z       - **CASPT2 / NEVPT2**: Multi-reference excited states, photochemistry, metal complexes
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9329598Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9329931Z       ## NMR Chemical Shift Calculations
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9330362Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9331039Z       - **GIAO (Gauge-Including Atomic Orbitals)**: Standard approach; implemented in Gaussian, ORCA, NWChem
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9331907Z       - **IGLO / CSGT**: Alternative gauge methods
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9332784Z       - **Reference compounds**: TMS for ¹H/¹³C; computed shifts referenced to known standards
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9333636Z       - **Solvent effects**: PCM, CPCM, or explicit solvent models
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9334177Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9334495Z       ## X-ray Spectroscopy
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9334872Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9335559Z       - **XAS/XANES**: Core-level excitations using TDDFT with special core-hole treatment, ROCIS (ORCA), RASPT2
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9336565Z       - **XES**: Ground-state DFT calculations of transition energies
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9337652Z       - **Δ-SCF approach**: Direct computation of core-ionized states
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9338224Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9338527Z       ## Key References
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9338872Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9339816Z       - Runge, E. & Gross, E. K. U. (1984). TDDFT. *Phys. Rev. Lett.* 52, 997. https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevLett.52.997
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9341111Z       - Neese, F. (2012). ORCA spectroscopy. *Wiley Interdiscip. Rev. Comput. Mol. Sci.* 2, 73. https://doi.org/10.1002/wcms.81
2026-02-26T02:06:13.9342167Z       - Frisch, M. J. et al. Gaussian 16. Gaussian Inc., Wallingford CT, 2016.

2026-02-26T02:06:40.4040662Z       # Toxicity & ADMET Prediction Tools
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4041424Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4044258Z       ADMET — Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion, and Toxicity — properties are critical determinants of drug success. Computational ADMET prediction tools allow early-stage screening of thousands of compounds before costly and time-consuming experimental assays, significantly accelerating drug discovery pipelines.
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4046821Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4047256Z       ## Key ADMET Properties
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4047748Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4048155Z       ### Absorption
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4048702Z       - **Caco-2 permeability**: Intestinal absorption prediction
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4050984Z       - **HIA (Human Intestinal Absorption)**: % absorbed after oral dosing
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4051870Z       - **P-glycoprotein (P-gp) substrate/inhibitor**: Efflux pump recognition
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4052865Z       - **Bioavailability**: F% — fraction reaching systemic circulation
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4053472Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4053783Z       ### Distribution
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4054432Z       - **Plasma protein binding (PPB)**: % bound to albumin/α1-AGP
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4055191Z       - **Volume of distribution (Vd)**: Apparent distribution volume (L/kg)
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4055948Z       - **Blood-brain barrier (BBB) permeability**: CNS penetration
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4056586Z       - **CNS activity**: Predicted CNS activity score
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4057076Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4057362Z       ### Metabolism
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4057889Z       - **CYP450 substrate/inhibitor**: CYP1A2, CYP2C9, CYP2C19, CYP2D6, CYP3A4
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4058729Z       - **Half-life (t½)**: Predicted metabolic stability
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4059615Z       - **Clearance**: Hepatic or total body clearance (mL/min/kg)
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4060171Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4060460Z       ### Excretion
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4060892Z       - **Renal clearance**: Predicted renal elimination rate
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4061578Z       - **OCT2 substrate**: Renal organic cation transporter recognition
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4062142Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4062411Z       ### Toxicity
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4062820Z       - **hERG inhibition**: Cardiac QT prolongation risk
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4063478Z       - **AMES mutagenicity**: Bacterial reverse mutation assay
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4064167Z       - **Hepatotoxicity (DILI)**: Drug-induced liver injury risk
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4064833Z       - **Skin sensitization**: LLNA sensitization potential
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4065405Z       - **LD50**: Acute oral toxicity prediction
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4065984Z       - **Carcinogenicity**: Predicted carcinogenic potential
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4066650Z       - **Aquatic toxicity**: Fathead minnow LC50, Tetrahymena EC50
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4067186Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4067500Z       ## Tools in This Category

2026-02-26T02:06:40.4078400Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4078702Z       ## Drug-likeness Rules
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4079256Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4079560Z       ### Lipinski's Rule of Five (Ro5)
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4080097Z       A drug candidate is likely orally bioavailable if:
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4080751Z       - Molecular weight (MW) ≤ 500 Da
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4081263Z       - logP ≤ 5
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4081683Z       - H-bond donors ≤ 5
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4082147Z       - H-bond acceptors ≤ 10
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4082540Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4082891Z       ### Veber Rules (Oral Bioavailability)
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4083438Z       - Rotatable bonds ≤ 10
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4083909Z       - TPSA ≤ 140 Å²
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4084247Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4084549Z       ### Ghose Filter
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4084974Z       - -0.4 ≤ logP ≤ 5.6
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4085396Z       - 160 ≤ MW ≤ 480
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4085794Z       - 40 ≤ MR ≤ 130
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4086206Z       - 20 ≤ atom count ≤ 70
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4086572Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4086926Z       ### PAINS (Pan-Assay Interference Compounds)
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4088005Z       Structural alerts for false positives in HTS: reactive groups, metal chelators, fluorescent scaffolds. RDKit implements PAINS filters.
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4089194Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4089583Z       ## Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4089966Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4090665Z       - **Fragment-based (SMARTS matching)**: Rule-based alerts — fast but limited scope
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4091869Z       - **Quantitative Structure-Activity/Property Relationships (QSAR/QSPR)**: Statistical models on molecular descriptors
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4093116Z       - **Graph-based signatures**: pkCSM uses pharmacophoric distance graph signatures
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4094233Z       - **Machine learning (ML/DL)**: Random forests, gradient boosting, graph neural networks (GNNs), transformers
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4095433Z       - **Physiologically-based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) modeling**: Mechanistic compartmental models
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4096223Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4096542Z       ## Molecular Descriptors
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4096925Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4097302Z       Common descriptor families used in ADMET models:
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4097989Z       - **Physicochemical**: MW, logP, logD, TPSA, HBD/HBA, rotatable bonds
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4098773Z       - **Topological**: Wiener index, Zagreb indices, Balaban J index
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4099773Z       - **Fingerprints**: Morgan/ECFP, MACCS keys, atom pairs, topological torsions
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4100746Z       - **3D descriptors**: Surface area, volume, shape descriptors (PMI), pharmacophore features
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4101491Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4101795Z       ## Key References
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4102129Z       
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4103184Z       - Lipinski, C. A. et al. (2001). Experimental and computational approaches to drug-likeness. *Adv. Drug Deliv. Rev.* 46, 3. https://doi.org/10.1016/S0169-409X(00)00129-0
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4104737Z       - Daina, A., Michielin, O., Zoete, V. (2017). SwissADME. *Sci. Rep.* 7, 42717. https://doi.org/10.1038/srep42717
2026-02-26T02:06:40.4106045Z       - Pires, D. E. V., Blundell, T. L., Ascher, D. B. (2015). pkCSM. *J. Med. Chem.* 58, 4066. https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jmedchem.5b00104

2026-02-26T02:07:04.1193683Z       # Cheminformatics Tools
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1194367Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1197424Z       Cheminformatics (or chemoinformatics) applies informatics methods to chemical data — representing, storing, searching, and mining molecular structures and their associated properties. It is central to drug discovery, materials design, and chemical database management.
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1199485Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1199853Z       ## Core Cheminformatics Tasks
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1200265Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1200804Z       - **Molecular representation**: SMILES, InChI, InChIKey, SMARTS, molfile (SDF), CML
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1201628Z       - **Substructure search**: Pattern matching using SMARTS queries
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1202345Z       - **Similarity search**: Tanimoto/Jaccard coefficient on fingerprints
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1203178Z       - **Molecular fingerprints**: Binary/count vectors encoding structural features
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1204097Z       - **Property calculation**: Physicochemical descriptors (MW, logP, TPSA, HBD, HBA)
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1204999Z       - **Conformer generation**: 3D coordinate generation (ETKDG, DG, systematic search)
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1205676Z       - **Scaffold analysis**: Bemis-Murcko scaffolds, R-group decomposition
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1206213Z       - **Chemical file format conversion**: Interconversion between SDF, MOL2, PDB, CIF, etc.
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1206743Z       - **Reaction handling**: Reaction SMARTS, atom mapping, retrosynthesis
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1207089Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1207274Z       ## Tools in This Category
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1207498Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1207700Z       | Tool | Language | License | Highlights |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1207993Z       |------|----------|---------|-----------|
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1208449Z       | [RDKit](../tools/rdkit/index.md) | C++/Python | BSD | Comprehensive; fingerprints, 3D, reactions |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1209425Z       | [Open Babel](../tools/open-babel/index.md) | C++ | GPL | Format conversion (100+ formats), CLI and library |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1210129Z       | [DeepChem](../tools/deepchem/index.md) | Python | MIT | Deep learning models for chemistry |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1210840Z       | [CDK (Chemistry Development Kit)](https://cdk.github.io/) | Java | LGPL | Java-based; database handling, fingerprints |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1211666Z       | [Indigo](https://lifescience.opensource.epam.com/indigo/) | C++/Python | Apache-2.0 | Rendering, fingerprints, reactions |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1212565Z       | [ChemPy](https://github.com/bjodah/chempy) | Python | BSD | Chemical kinetics and equilibrium |

2026-02-26T02:07:04.1215509Z       ## Molecular Representations
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1215743Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1215979Z       ### SMILES (Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System)
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1216287Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1216455Z       # Aspirin
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1216646Z       CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1216856Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1217016Z       # Caffeine
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1217209Z       Cn1cnc2c1c(=O)n(C)c(=O)n2C
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1217428Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1217590Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1217761Z       ### InChI and InChIKey
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1218266Z       - **InChI**: International Chemical Identifier — layered textual representation
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1218831Z       - **InChIKey**: 27-character hash of InChI — ideal for database indexing
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1219487Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1219719Z       ### SMARTS (SMILES Arbitrary Target Specification)
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1220293Z       Pattern language for substructure queries:
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1220679Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1220870Z       # Match any aromatic ring with OH
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1221117Z       c1ccccc1O
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1221292Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1221460Z       # Match amide bond
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1221673Z       [NX3][CX3](=[OX1])
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1221864Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1222025Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1222206Z       ## Molecular Fingerprints
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1222425Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1222609Z       | Fingerprint | Type | Bits | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1222877Z       |------------|------|------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1223370Z       | Morgan / ECFP | Circular | 512–4096 | Most popular; encodes atomic environments |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1223875Z       | MACCS Keys | Structural keys | 166 | Interpretable; well-validated |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1224356Z       | Topological Torsion | Path-based | Variable | Good for 3D-like 2D encoding |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1224808Z       | Atom Pair | Path-based | Variable | Distance-aware |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1225271Z       | FCFP | Feature-based circular | 512–4096 | Pharmacophore-centric |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1225883Z       | RDKit Fingerprint | Path-based | 2048 | RDKit default; fast |
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1226354Z

2026-02-26T02:07:04.1230718Z       ## Python Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1230922Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1231083Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1231364Z       # RDKit example: compute Morgan fingerprint and Tanimoto similarity
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1231715Z       from rdkit import Chem
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1231979Z       from rdkit.Chem import AllChem, DataStructs
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1232244Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1232491Z       mol1 = Chem.MolFromSmiles('CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O')  # aspirin
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1233191Z       mol2 = Chem.MolFromSmiles('CC(=O)Nc1ccc(O)cc1')      # paracetamol
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1233741Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1234250Z       fp1 = AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(mol1, radius=2, nBits=2048)
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1235027Z       fp2 = AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(mol2, radius=2, nBits=2048)
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1235662Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1236027Z       sim = DataStructs.TanimotoSimilarity(fp1, fp2)
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1236382Z       print(f"Tanimoto similarity: {sim:.3f}")
2026-02-26T02:07:04.1236639Z       ```

2026-02-26T02:07:28.1748671Z       # Molecular Visualization Tools
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1750829Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1754194Z       Molecular visualization software translates numerical coordinates and simulation data into interactive 3D representations that allow scientists to inspect structures, trajectories, electron densities, and surfaces. High-quality visualization is essential for both scientific analysis and publication-quality figures.
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1756253Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1756456Z       ## Visualization Categories
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1756697Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1757016Z       - **Molecular editors**: Build and edit molecular structures interactively
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1757524Z       - **Trajectory viewers**: Animate MD simulation trajectories
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1758033Z       - **Crystal structure viewers**: Display unit cells, symmetry, diffraction data
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1758542Z       - **Electron density viewers**: Isosurfaces of charge density, ELF, NCI
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1759355Z       - **Surface renderers**: Solvent-accessible surface, electrostatic potential maps
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1759891Z       - **Publication renderers**: Ray-traced, photorealistic molecular images
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1760253Z

2026-02-26T02:07:28.1773447Z       ## Trajectory Analysis with VMD
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1773845Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1774086Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1774402Z       # Load trajectory
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1774754Z       mol new protein.psf
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1775126Z       mol addfile trajectory.dcd waitfor all
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1775382Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1775566Z       # Calculate RMSD over trajectory
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1775832Z       set nf [molinfo top get numframes]
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1776086Z       set rmsd_vals {}
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1776301Z       for {set i 0} {$i < $nf} {incr i} {
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1776595Z           set sel [atomselect top "backbone" frame $i]
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1776899Z           set rmsd [measure rmsd $sel $ref_sel]
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1777176Z           lappend rmsd_vals $rmsd
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1777404Z       }
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1777566Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1777728Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1777912Z       ## PyMOL Scripting
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1778117Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1778282Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1778513Z       # Create publication-quality figure in PyMOL
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1778799Z       from pymol import cmd
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1779138Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1779322Z       cmd.load("protein.pdb")
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1779562Z       cmd.hide("all")
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1779778Z       cmd.show("cartoon", "chain A")
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1780027Z       cmd.show("sticks", "organic")
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1780278Z       cmd.color("skyblue", "chain A")
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1780529Z       cmd.color("yellow", "organic")
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1780775Z       cmd.set("ray_shadows", 0)
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1781003Z       cmd.ray(2400, 1800)
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1781219Z       cmd.save("figure.png")
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1781432Z       ```

2026-02-26T02:07:28.1781781Z       ## Electron Density Visualization
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1782022Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1782306Z       - **VESTA**: Load VASP CHGCAR, Quantum ESPRESSO charge.dat, or CUBe files
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1782778Z       - **VMD**: Load Gaussian CUBE files, display isosurfaces of MOs or density
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1783267Z       - **PyMOL**: Load CCP4/MRC electron density maps for protein-ligand visualization
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1783723Z       - **Chimera**: Load cryo-EM density maps, fit atomic models
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1784043Z

2026-02-26T02:07:28.1785088Z       | PDB | Atomic coordinates, bonds | Universal |
2026-02-26T02:07:28.1785432Z       | SDF/MOL | Small molecule structure | RDKit, Avogadro |

2026-02-26T02:07:51.4053778Z function:
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4056807Z   name: create
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4057543Z   args:
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4061776Z     file_text: |
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4063415Z       # Quantum ESPRESSO
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4064995Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4066708Z       **Category:** Density Functional Theory (DFT), Ab Initio Molecular Dynamics  
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4067679Z       **Developer/Organization:** SISSA, CINECA, and the QE Foundation  
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4068359Z       **License:** GNU General Public License (GPL v2+)  
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4069677Z       **Website:** https://www.quantum-espresso.org  
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4070505Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/QEF/q-e  
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4071277Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4071565Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4072096Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4074291Z       Quantum ESPRESSO (QE) is an open-source, integrated suite of codes for electronic-structure calculations and materials modeling at the nanoscale based on density functional theory, plane waves, and pseudopotentials. The acronym stands for **opEn-Source Package for Research in Electronic Structure, Simulation, and Optimization**.
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4076676Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4078834Z       Originally developed at SISSA (Scuola Internazionale Superiore di Studi Avanzati, Trieste) and CINECA (Italy), QE has grown into a community-driven project maintained by the QE Foundation and a large international developer base. It is one of the most widely used DFT packages in the world, particularly for condensed matter physics, surface science, and materials science.
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4081389Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4084516Z       Quantum ESPRESSO's core strength is its treatment of periodic systems using plane-wave basis sets combined with pseudopotentials (norm-conserving, ultrasoft, or PAW). The PWscf (Plane-Wave Self-Consistent Field) module handles ground-state DFT, geometry optimization, and ab initio molecular dynamics. Additional modules handle phonon calculations via density functional perturbation theory (DFPT), electron-phonon coupling, response properties, maximally-localized Wannier functions, and more.
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4087708Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4088006Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4088338Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4089259Z       - **Plane-wave DFT** with norm-conserving (NC), ultrasoft (USPP), and PAW pseudopotentials
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4090425Z       - **PWscf module** for self-consistent field (SCF) calculations, band structures, and geometry optimization
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4091593Z       - **PHonon module** for phonon dispersion, IR/Raman intensities, dielectric constants via DFPT
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4092541Z       - **CP module** for Car-Parrinello ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD)
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4093665Z       - **PostProc utilities**: `pp.x` for charge density, ELF, STM images; `bands.x` for band structures; `dos.x` for density of states
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4094994Z       - **Wannier90 interface**: Maximally-localized Wannier functions for transport and topological properties
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4096159Z       - **EPW code**: Electron-phonon coupling and superconductivity via Wannier interpolation
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4097060Z       - **TDDFPT**: Linear-response time-dependent DFT for optical spectra
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4097777Z       - **HP module**: Hubbard U parameters from DFPT (DFT+U+J)
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4098513Z       - **turboTDDFPT**: Turbo Lanczos algorithm for large-scale optical spectra
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4099501Z       - **ENVIRON**: Implicit solvent and electrostatic embedding
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4100215Z       - **GPU acceleration** via CUDA/HIP (experimental and production)
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4101326Z       - **Hybrid exchange-correlation functionals**: PBE0, HSE06 via exact exchange
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4102382Z       - **Extensive XC library**: libxc integration with hundreds of functionals
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4103212Z       - **MPI + OpenMP parallelization** over k-points, plane waves, and bands
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4104018Z       - **AiiDA and ASE integration** for automated high-throughput calculations
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4104744Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4105046Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4105366Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4105648Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4105967Z       # Install via conda
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4106374Z       conda install -c conda-forge qe
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4106812Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4107118Z       # Or build from source
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4107565Z       git clone https://github.com/QEF/q-e.git
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4108054Z       cd q-e && ./configure && make all
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4108475Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4108809Z       # Run a self-consistent field calculation
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4109557Z       pw.x < silicon.scf.in > silicon.scf.out
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4110020Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4110364Z       # Basic silicon SCF input (silicon.scf.in)
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4110825Z       # &CONTROL
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4111160Z       #   calculation = 'scf'
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4111561Z       #   outdir = './tmp/'
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4111934Z       #   prefix = 'silicon'
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4112301Z       # /
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4112589Z       # &SYSTEM
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4112973Z       #   ibrav = 2, celldm(1) = 10.21, nat = 2, ntyp = 1
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4113466Z       #   ecutwfc = 30.0
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4113815Z       # /
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4114124Z       # ATOMIC_SPECIES
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4114538Z       #  Si  28.086  Si.pbe-n-rrkjus_psl.1.0.0.UPF
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4115026Z       # ATOMIC_POSITIONS {alat}
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4115424Z       #  Si  0.00  0.00  0.00
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4115796Z       #  Si  0.25  0.25  0.25
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4116185Z       # K_POINTS {automatic}
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4116556Z       #  8 8 8 0 0 0
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4116884Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4117169Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4117468Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4117803Z       
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4118824Z       - [VASP](../../tools/vasp/index.md) – Commercial plane-wave DFT; similar capabilities, different license
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4120204Z       - [CP2K](../../tools/cp2k/index.md) – Mixed Gaussian/plane-wave DFT, strong for AIMD
2026-02-26T02:07:51.4121316Z       - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – Molecular DFT/CC; complementary for molecular systems

2026-02-26T02:08:19.3125803Z function:
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3126832Z   name: create
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3127380Z   args:
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3132428Z     file_text: |
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3133271Z       # Quantum ESPRESSO — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3133788Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3134124Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3135184Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3136410Z       Quantum ESPRESSO is built on Kohn-Sham Density Functional Theory (KS-DFT). The key approximation maps the interacting many-electron problem onto a set of single-particle equations:
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3137749Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3139587Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3140217Z       [-½∇² + V_ext(r) + V_Hartree[n](r) + V_xc[n](r)] ψ_i(r) = ε_i ψ_i(r)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3141205Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3141510Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3142984Z       where the electron density is `n(r) = Σ_i |ψ_i(r)|²` and V_xc is the exchange-correlation potential derived from a chosen functional approximation.
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3144068Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3144575Z       The electronic wavefunctions are expanded in a plane-wave basis set:
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3145201Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3145482Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3145911Z       ψ_k(r) = Σ_G c_k+G · e^{i(k+G)·r}
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3146366Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3146672Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3148633Z       The number of plane waves is controlled by the kinetic energy cutoff `ecutwfc` (Ry): only plane waves with `|k+G|² < ecutwfc` are included. This basis is systematically improvable and does not introduce basis set superposition error (BSSE).
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3150588Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3150909Z       ## Pseudopotentials and PAW
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3151319Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3151841Z       To avoid treating core electrons explicitly, QE uses pseudopotentials:
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3152493Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3153610Z       - **Norm-conserving pseudopotentials (NCPP)**: Satisfy norm conservation within the core region; computationally heavier (require high ecutwfc for transition metals)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3156165Z       - **Ultrasoft pseudopotentials (USPP)**: Introduced by Vanderbilt (1990); allow lower energy cutoffs; require a dual charge density treatment with an additional `ecutrho` parameter (typically 4–12× ecutwfc)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3157586Z       - **Projector Augmented Wave (PAW)**: All-electron accuracy in a computationally efficient scheme; recommended for accurate total energies, forces, and stress
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3158259Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3158927Z       Pseudopotentials are available from the PSLibrary (https://pseudopotentials.quantum-espresso.org) and from the SSSP (Standard Solid State Pseudopotentials) library.
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3159980Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3160179Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3160433Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3160978Z       - **SCF diagonalization**: Davidson iterative diagonalization (default), Conjugate Gradient (CG), PPCG (parallel projected Conjugate Gradient)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3161778Z       - **Mixing**: Broyden mixing (default), linear mixing, Kerker preconditioned mixing for metals
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3162610Z       - **Smearing**: Methfessel-Paxton, Marzari-Vanderbilt cold smearing, Fermi-Dirac, simple Gaussian
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3163668Z       - **Geometry optimization**: BFGS (quasi-Newton), Damped MD, FIRE algorithm
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3164229Z       - **Variable-cell optimization**: Full stress tensor relaxation (vc-relax)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3165556Z       - **DFPT (PHonon module)**: Linear-response perturbation theory for phonons, Born effective charges, dielectric constants, Raman tensors, interatomic force constants
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3166471Z       - **Car-Parrinello MD (CP module)**: Extended Lagrangian AIMD with fictitious electron kinetic energy
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3167119Z       - **Hybrid functionals**: Exact exchange via ACE (Adaptively Compressed Exchange) operator
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3167702Z       - **DFT+U (Dudarev / Liechtenstein)**: Hubbard correction for correlated d/f electrons
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3168280Z       - **Spin-orbit coupling**: Fully relativistic pseudopotentials and noncollinear magnetism
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3168704Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3168894Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3169374Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3169751Z       Quantum ESPRESSO is written primarily in **Fortran 90/95** with some C routines. The codebase uses:
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3170193Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3170500Z       - **MPI** (Message Passing Interface) for distributed-memory parallelization
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3179482Z       - **OpenMP** for shared-memory threading within MPI tasks
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3180227Z       - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Dense linear algebra (Intel MKL, OpenBLAS, AOCL)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3181088Z       - **ScaLAPACK**: Distributed dense linear algebra for large diagonalizations
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3181750Z       - **FFT**: 3D Fast Fourier Transforms for converting between real and reciprocal space (FFTW3, Intel MKL FFT, cuFFT)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3182747Z       - **HDF5**: Optional binary checkpoint files for large-scale runs
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3183277Z       - **CUDA/HIP**: GPU offloading for FFTs and matrix operations (production quality from QE 7.0+)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3183703Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3183896Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3184136Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3184337Z       QE parallelizes over multiple levels:
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3184592Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3184926Z       1. **k-points**: Embarrassingly parallel; each MPI group handles a subset of k-points
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3185483Z       2. **Plane waves (G-vectors)**: Distributed among MPI tasks within each k-point group
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3186055Z       3. **Bands**: Additional parallelization over bands (useful for metals with many bands)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3186576Z       4. **Task groups**: 3D-FFT domain decomposition for large plane-wave sets
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3186923Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3187843Z       Typical scaling: QE scales efficiently to thousands of CPU cores for large systems. GPU acceleration (CUDA) via the `pw-gpu` variant provides 5–10× speedups on NVIDIA A100 GPUs for large systems.
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3188619Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3189286Z       Benchmark example: A 512-atom silicon supercell SCF calculation runs in ~2 minutes on 128 CPU cores.
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3189768Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3189952Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3190183Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3190554Z       - **libxc**: External XC functional library (optional but recommended for non-standard functionals)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3191138Z       - **Wannier90**: Maximally-localized Wannier functions (tightly integrated)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3191623Z       - **PLUMED**: Metadynamics and advanced sampling (via CP module)
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3192133Z       - **BerkeleyGW**: Many-body perturbation theory (GW + BSE) starting from QE wavefunctions
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3192668Z       - **PHONOPY**: Force constant calculations and phonon post-processing
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3193084Z       - **AiiDA**: Workflow management and provenance tracking
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3193524Z       - **ASE (Atomic Simulation Environment)**: Python interface for QE calculations
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3194037Z       - **pymatgen**: High-level Python materials science library with QE interface
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3194341Z 
2026-02-26T02:08:19.3194660Z     path: /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/quantum-espresso/technology.md
