2026-02-26T02:08:46.5476622Z function:
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5477122Z   name: create
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5478608Z   args:
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5487123Z     file_text: |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5488101Z       # Quantum ESPRESSO — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5488754Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5489309Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5489698Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5490100Z       ### Ground State DFT (PWscf)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5490696Z       - LDA, GGA (PBE, PBEsol, revPBE, RPBE, WC), meta-GGA (via libxc)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5491472Z       - Hybrid functionals: PBE0, HSE06, B3LYP (via exact exchange / ACE)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5492008Z       - DFT+U (Dudarev simplifed and Liechtenstein formulations)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5492524Z       - DFT+U+V (inter-site Hubbard interactions)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5493315Z       - van der Waals corrections: Grimme DFT-D3, rVV10, vdW-DF, vdW-DF2
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5494189Z       - Noncollinear spin and spin-orbit coupling (fully relativistic pseudopotentials)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5495211Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5495746Z       ### Response Properties (PHonon / DFPT)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5496319Z       - Phonon dispersion curves and density of states
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5496912Z       - Interatomic force constants (IFC) in real space
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5497432Z       - Born effective charge tensors Z*
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5498079Z       - High-frequency dielectric tensor ε∞
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5498576Z       - LO-TO splitting in polar materials
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5499264Z       - Infrared absorption intensities
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5499746Z       - Raman scattering cross-sections
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5500233Z       - Electron-phonon coupling matrix elements
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5500679Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5500999Z       ### Time-Dependent DFT
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5501566Z       - Turbo Lanczos (turboTDDFPT): optical spectra for large systems
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5502250Z       - EELS (electron energy loss spectra)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5502773Z       - XAS from core levels (via XSPECTRA module)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5503236Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5503560Z       ### Many-Body Methods
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5504144Z       - Interface with BerkeleyGW for GW self-energy and BSE (Bethe-Salpeter)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5504799Z       - Interface with YAMBO for GW and BSE
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5505401Z       - Interface with SternheimerGW for GW with Sternheimer equation
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5505964Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5506268Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5506654Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5506943Z       ### Input Files
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5507704Z       - **QE input format**: Namelist-based Fortran-style input (`&CONTROL`, `&SYSTEM`, `&ELECTRONS`, `&IONS`, `&CELL`)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5509180Z       - **Pseudopotential files**: UPF format (v1 and v2); download from https://pseudopotentials.quantum-espresso.org
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5510428Z       - **Structure import**: Can read CIF, XYZ via `pw2wannier90.x` or ASE/pymatgen conversion tools
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5511180Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5511473Z       ### Output Files
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5512170Z       - **XML data file** (`.xml`): Portable binary/XML checkpoint with wavefunctions, density, Hamiltonian
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5513179Z       - **Charge density** (`charge-density.dat`): For post-processing with `pp.x`
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5514050Z       - **Wavefunctions** (`.wfc*`): Band wavefunctions for Wannier90 or BerkeleyGW
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5514959Z       - **Phonon files** (`*.dyn*`): Dynamical matrices at each q-point for PHONOPY or `matdyn.x`
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5515878Z       - **Stdout output**: SCF convergence, forces, stress tensor, final energy
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5516482Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5516809Z       ### Post-Processing Executables
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5517250Z       | Executable | Purpose |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5517642Z       |-----------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5518259Z       | `pp.x` | Charge density, ELF, STM, NCI, LDOS, Bader analysis input |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5518908Z       | `bands.x` | Band structure along k-path |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5519660Z       | `dos.x` | Total and projected density of states |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5520349Z       | `projwfc.x` | Projections of wavefunctions on atomic orbitals |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5520998Z       | `pw2wannier90.x` | Interface to Wannier90 |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5521528Z       | `ph.x` | Phonon calculation via DFPT |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5522056Z       | `matdyn.x` | Phonon dispersion from IFC |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5522647Z       | `q2r.x` | Transform dynamical matrices to real-space IFC |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5523246Z       | `plotband.x` | Plot phonon dispersion |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5523811Z       | `epw.x` | Electron-phonon coupling (EPW package) |
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5524312Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5524604Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5524951Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5525260Z       ### Band Structure Calculation
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5525666Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5526194Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5526537Z       # Step 1: SCF calculation
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5527122Z       pw.x < si.scf.in > si.scf.out
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5527530Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5527969Z       # Step 2: Non-self-consistent (NSCF) calculation along k-path
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5528567Z       pw.x < si.nscf.in > si.nscf.out
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5529258Z       # (nscf input has calculation='bands' and a k-path)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5529759Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5530083Z       # Step 3: Extract and plot bands
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5530549Z       bands.x < si.bands.in > si.bands.out
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5531145Z       # Produces si.bands.dat for plotting with gnuplot or Python
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5531714Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5532009Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5532310Z       ### Phonon Calculation
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5532678Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5532960Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5533271Z       # Step 1: SCF
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5533627Z       pw.x < si.scf.in > si.scf.out
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5534042Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5534407Z       # Step 2: Phonons at all q-points in a grid
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5534907Z       ph.x < si.ph.in > si.ph.out
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5535300Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5535678Z       # Step 3: Collect dynamical matrices and apply ASR
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5536214Z       q2r.x < si.q2r.in > si.q2r.out
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5536635Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5536975Z       # Step 4: Interpolate phonon dispersion
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5537493Z       matdyn.x < si.matdyn.in > si.matdyn.out
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5537951Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5538236Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5538538Z       ### Geometry Optimization
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5538923Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5539370Z       ```fortran
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5539686Z       &CONTROL
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5540097Z         calculation = 'vc-relax'   ! variable-cell relaxation
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5540736Z         forc_conv_thr = 1.0d-4     ! force threshold (Ry/bohr)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5541264Z       /
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5541544Z       &CELL
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5541949Z         press_conv_thr = 0.1       ! pressure threshold (kbar)
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5542587Z         cell_dofree = 'all'        ! all lattice parameters free
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5543120Z       /
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5543956Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5544541Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5544859Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5545233Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5545571Z       ### Hubbard DFT+U from First Principles
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5546804Z       The HP module computes Hubbard U parameters self-consistently using DFPT, eliminating the need for empirical U values. Run `hp.x` after a DFT+U SCF calculation.
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5547981Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5548355Z       ### EPW: Electron-Phonon from Wannier Functions
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5549452Z       EPW computes electron-phonon coupling matrix elements on dense k/q grids via Wannier interpolation, enabling:
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5550679Z       - Superconducting Tc via Migdal-Éliashberg equations
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5551266Z       - Phonon-limited carrier mobility
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5551749Z       - Temperature-dependent band gaps
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5552191Z       
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5552509Z       ### ENVIRON: Implicit Solvation
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5553528Z       The ENVIRON plugin adds continuum solvation models (PCM, SCCS) and electrostatic embedding for electrochemistry and surface science.
2026-02-26T02:08:46.5554418Z

2026-02-26T02:09:08.5452639Z       # Quantum ESPRESSO — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5453079Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5453396Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5453964Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5456563Z       Quantum ESPRESSO is primarily used for electronic structure calculations of periodic systems — crystalline solids, surfaces, interfaces, and nanostructures. Its strength lies in producing accurate ground-state properties, phonon spectra, and response properties for inorganic materials, semiconductors, metals, and 2D materials.
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5479369Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5479738Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5480084Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5480460Z       ### Semiconductor Band Structure and Gap Engineering
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5480927Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5481736Z       QE is widely used to compute electronic band structures of semiconductors (Si, Ge, GaAs, GaN, perovskites). A typical workflow involves:
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5482652Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5483032Z       1. Geometry optimization with PBE GGA functional
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5483951Z       2. Band structure calculation along high-symmetry k-paths (Γ-X-M-Γ for FCC, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5485107Z       3. Correction of the DFT band gap underestimation using hybrid functionals (HSE06), GW correction (via BerkeleyGW/YAMBO), or DFT+U
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5485993Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5486880Z       This approach is routinely applied to photovoltaic materials (halide perovskites), transparent conducting oxides, and topological insulators.
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5487882Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5488215Z       ### Phonon Dispersion and Thermal Properties
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5499408Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5499911Z       The PHonon module computes phonon dispersion via DFPT. Results are used to:
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5500898Z       - Identify dynamical instabilities (imaginary phonon modes signal structural phase transitions)
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5501859Z       - Compute heat capacity Cv(T) and entropy within the harmonic approximation
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5502821Z       - Compute thermal conductivity via the BTE (interfaced with BTE codes like ShengBTE or ALAMODE)
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5503748Z       - Study superconducting properties via the Allen-Dynes Tc formula and EPW
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5504355Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5505034Z       Example: Computing phonon dispersion of MgB2 to understand its record conventional superconducting Tc of 39 K.
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5505836Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5506193Z       ### Catalysis: Adsorption Energies on Metal Surfaces
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5506660Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5507274Z       Slab models of transition-metal surfaces (Pt(111), Cu(100), Ni(110)) with adsorbates (CO, H2, OH, N2):
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5508159Z       - Compute adsorption energies relative to gas-phase references
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5508899Z       - Map reaction pathways via NEB (nudged elastic band, via QE+ASE or CINEB)
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5529925Z       - Include DFT-D3 dispersion correction for accurate physisorption energies
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5530537Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5531235Z       This is extensively used in computational heterogeneous catalysis (Fischer-Tropsch, CO2 reduction, HER, OER).
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5532051Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5532395Z       ### 2D Materials: Graphene, MoS2, h-BN
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5532798Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5533216Z       QE handles 2D materials via slab models with sufficient vacuum:
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5533854Z       - Band structure and effective mass of monolayer MoS2
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5534395Z       - Valley polarization via SOC calculations
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5535141Z       - Interlayer binding energies in van der Waals heterostructures (with vdW-DF corrections)
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5536272Z       - Moiré patterns in twisted bilayer graphene (large supercells, > 1000 atoms with GPU)
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5536938Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5537232Z       ### Li-ion Battery Materials
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5537606Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5538267Z       Screening cathode materials for Li-ion batteries:
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5539235Z       - Compute Li intercalation voltages from total energy differences
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5539967Z       - Study Li migration barriers via NEB on DFT-optimized structures
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5540711Z       - Compute electronic conductivity and polaron formation in LiFePO4, NMC
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5541311Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5541674Z       ### Electron-Phonon Coupling and Superconductivity
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5542141Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5542464Z       Using EPW (part of the QE distribution):
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5543056Z       - Compute electron-phonon coupling matrix elements λ
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5543783Z       - Solve Migdal-Éliashberg equations for superconducting Tc
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5544592Z       - Compute phonon-limited electrical conductivity in metals and doped semiconductors
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5545479Z       - Study phonon-assisted optical transitions in indirect semiconductors
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5546084Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5546427Z       ### X-ray Absorption Spectroscopy (XAS/XANES)
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5546898Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5569675Z       The XSPECTRA module computes core-level X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) spectra:
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5570560Z       - Model K-edges and L-edges of transition metals and ligands
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5571317Z       - Compare with synchrotron XANES data for structural characterization
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5572034Z       - Study oxidation state changes in electrochemical systems
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5572552Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5572852Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5573239Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5573775Z       - **Semiconductors & electronics**: Band gaps, effective masses, defect levels, doping
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5574608Z       - **Energy storage**: Battery cathodes, electrolytes, interfaces
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5575385Z       - **Catalysis**: Heterogeneous and electrocatalysis, active site identification
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5576340Z       - **Superconductivity**: Conventional phonon-mediated superconductors, high-Tc screening
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5577303Z       - **2D materials & nanotechnology**: Properties of monolayers and heterostructures
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5578215Z       - **Magnetic materials**: Magnetic anisotropy, exchange couplings, spin-orbit effects
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5589613Z       - **Thermoelectrics**: Electronic and thermal transport coefficients
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5590394Z       - **Photovoltaics**: Perovskite solar cells, defect-tolerant absorbers
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5591174Z       - **Surface science**: Adsorbate geometries, work functions, surface states
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5592025Z       - **Topological materials**: Berry phases, Z2 invariants (via Wannier90 interface)
2026-02-26T02:09:08.5592555Z

2026-02-26T02:09:57.0560465Z       # VASP (Vienna Ab initio Simulation Package)
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0560951Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0561425Z       **Category:** Density Functional Theory (DFT), Ab Initio Molecular Dynamics  
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0562466Z       **Developer/Organization:** University of Vienna (Georg Kresse, Jürgen Furthmüller, and team)  
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0563084Z       **License:** Commercial (license required per site/group)  
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0563461Z       **Website:** https://www.vasp.at  
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0563839Z       **GitHub:** Not public (source distributed to licensees only)  
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0564180Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0564358Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0564547Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0565973Z       VASP is the Vienna Ab initio Simulation Package, a plane-wave DFT code that has become the de facto standard in computational materials science. Developed since the early 1990s by Georg Kresse and colleagues at the University of Vienna, VASP is used by thousands of research groups worldwide and underlies a large fraction of the published computational materials science literature.
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0567493Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0570494Z       VASP implements DFT and Hartree-Fock theory using plane-wave basis sets and the projector augmented-wave (PAW) method, which is its primary approach to treating core-valence interactions. The PAW method — developed by Peter Blöchl and implemented in VASP by Kresse and Joubert — provides all-electron accuracy at pseudopotential computational cost and is now considered the gold standard for plane-wave DFT. VASP excels at electronic structure, geometry optimization, ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD), and response properties.
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0573805Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0575894Z       A major strength of VASP is its extensive set of post-processing and analysis tools, highly optimized performance (particularly with GPU acceleration), and tight integration with the broader materials informatics ecosystem through AFlow, pymatgen, ASE, and the Materials Project database (which uses VASP for all its ~150,000 computed entries).
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0578098Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0578403Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0578745Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0579575Z       - **PAW method**: All-electron accuracy with plane-wave basis; extensive PAW dataset library
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0581250Z       - **Hybrid DFT**: HSE06, PBE0, B3LYP hybrid functionals with exact exchange (ACE algorithm)
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0582638Z       - **AIMD**: Born-Oppenheimer and Nosé-Hoover thermostat molecular dynamics
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0583582Z       - **vdW corrections**: DFT-D2, DFT-D3, DFT-D3(BJ), vdW-DF, optPBE, SCAN+rVV10, MBD
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0584493Z       - **DFT+U**: Liechtenstein and Dudarev DFT+U for correlated d/f electrons
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0585161Z       - **Spin-orbit coupling**: Noncollinear magnetism and fully relativistic calculations
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0585745Z       - **Response functions**: DFPT for dielectric tensors, Born charges, piezoelectric tensors
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0586404Z       - **GW approximation**: G₀W₀, self-consistent GW for quasi-particle energies
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0586942Z       - **RPA correlation energy**: Random Phase Approximation for accurate total energies
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0587426Z       - **TDDFT**: Optical spectra via linear-response TDDFT
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0587850Z       - **NEB**: Nudged elastic band for minimum energy paths and transition states
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0588364Z       - **Phonons**: Finite-difference force constants or DFPT (via PHONOPY interface)
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0588890Z       - **Band unfolding**: Effective band structures for supercells and alloys
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0589673Z       - **GPU acceleration**: CUDA-based GPU offloading for FFTs and exact exchange
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0590175Z       - **LOBSTER interface**: Chemical bonding analysis from VASP wavefunctions
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0590632Z       - **WANNIER90 interface**: Maximally-localized Wannier functions
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0591083Z       - **Bader charge analysis**: Integration with Bader code by Henkelman group
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0591448Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0591629Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0591822Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0591985Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0592258Z       # VASP requires a commercial license; installation is manual
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0592648Z       # Standard VASP calculation requires four input files:
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0592940Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0593124Z       # POSCAR: Crystal structure
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0593381Z       # INCAR:  Calculation parameters  
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0593648Z       # KPOINTS: k-point mesh
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0593991Z       # POTCAR: PAW pseudopotential (from VASP license distribution)
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0594330Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0594506Z       # Run calculation
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0594738Z       mpirun -np 16 vasp_std > vasp.out
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0594982Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0595184Z       # Example INCAR for geometry optimization:
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0595479Z       # ISTART = 0      # Start from scratch
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0595799Z       # ICHARG = 2      # Initial charge from atom superposition
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0596130Z       # ENCUT = 520     # Plane-wave cutoff (eV)
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0596437Z       # EDIFF = 1E-6    # Electronic convergence (eV)
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0596846Z       # EDIFFG = -0.02  # Ionic convergence (force, eV/Å)
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0597150Z       # NSW = 100       # Max ionic steps
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0597445Z       # IBRION = 2      # Conjugate gradient relaxation
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0597761Z       # ISIF = 3        # Relax ions + cell shape + volume
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0598053Z       # GGA = PE        # PBE functional
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0598342Z       # ISMEAR = 1      # Methfessel-Paxton smearing
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0598635Z       # SIGMA = 0.2     # Smearing width (eV)
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0598882Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0599273Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0599446Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0599643Z       
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0600156Z       - [Quantum ESPRESSO](../../tools/quantum-espresso/index.md) – Open-source plane-wave DFT; similar capabilities
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0600834Z       - [CP2K](../../tools/cp2k/index.md) – Open-source GPW DFT; strong for AIMD
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0601877Z       - [VESTA](../../tools/vesta/index.md) – Primary visualization tool for VASP CHGCAR and POSCAR files
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0602569Z       - [PyMOL](../../tools/pymol/index.md) – Alternative visualization for molecular VASP outputs
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0603219Z       - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – Molecular DFT/CC; complementary for molecular benchmarks
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0603630Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:09:57.0603835Z       echo "VASP index.md created"

2026-02-26T02:11:22.9267601Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/vasp/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9268829Z       # VASP — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9269442Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9269755Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9270096Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9273202Z       VASP solves the Kohn-Sham equations self-consistently within the projector augmented-wave (PAW) formalism. The PAW method, introduced by Blöchl (1994) and implemented in VASP by Kresse & Joubert (1999), partitions space into augmentation spheres around each nucleus and an interstitial region. The smooth pseudo-wavefunction is defined everywhere, but within the augmentation spheres, an all-electron representation is reconstructed via PAW projectors:
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9275141Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9275321Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9275631Z       |ψ> = |ψ̃> + Σ_i (|φ_i> - |φ̃_i>) <p̃_i|ψ̃>
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9275908Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9276079Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9276914Z       where `|ψ̃>` is the smooth plane-wave part, `|φ_i>` are all-electron partial waves, `|φ̃_i>` are pseudo partial waves, and `<p̃_i|` are projectors localized in the augmentation spheres. This allows:
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9277803Z       - All-electron quality electronic structure at pseudopotential cost
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9278251Z       - Accurate forces, stresses, and electric field gradients
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9278794Z       - Core-level binding energy calculations (via ΔKS or initial-state approximation)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9279413Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9279635Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9279891Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9280475Z       - **RMM-DIIS**: Residual minimization method – direct inversion in the iterative subspace (default diagonalizer for large systems)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9281189Z       - **Davidson block iterative diagonalization**: Robust, used for initial SCF steps
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9281699Z       - **Pulay mixing**: Charge density mixing via DIIS for SCF convergence
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9282534Z       - **VASP-hybrid (ACE)**: Exact exchange via Adaptively Compressed Exchange operator — drastically reduces cost of hybrid functionals
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9283331Z       - **Constrained random-phase approximation (cRPA)**: Effective Hubbard U from first principles
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9283990Z       - **G₀W₀ / GW₀ / scGW**: Many-body perturbation theory for quasi-particle energies
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9284955Z       - **BSE (Bethe-Salpeter equation)**: Two-particle excitations, optical absorption including excitonic effects
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9286091Z       - **Finite-field methods**: Polarizability, Born effective charges, piezoelectric tensors
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9287146Z       - **DFPT**: Phonons, IR intensities, Born charges, dielectric constants
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9288027Z       - **NEB/CI-NEB**: Minimum energy path and saddle-point (transition state) search
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9289163Z       - **Dimer method**: Saddle-point search without knowledge of the final state
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9290911Z       - **AIMD**: Born-Oppenheimer MD with Nosé-Hoover chain thermostat and Parrinello-Rahman barostat
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9291753Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9292070Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9292472Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9293163Z       VASP is written in **Fortran 90** with heavily optimized kernels. Key implementation details:
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9293949Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9294333Z       - **MPI + OpenMP** hybrid parallelization
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9294847Z       - **ScaLAPACK**: Parallel dense linear algebra for diagonalization
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9295471Z       - **FFTW / Intel MKL FFT / cuFFT**: 3D FFTs for plane-wave operations
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9295899Z       - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Dense linear algebra (Intel MKL recommended)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9296336Z       - **CUDA**: GPU-accelerated variant (`vasp_gpu`) for FFTs and exact exchange
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9297051Z       - Four executables: `vasp_std` (standard), `vasp_gam` (Γ-only), `vasp_ncl` (noncollinear), `vasp_gpu` (CUDA)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9297533Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9297731Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9297969Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9298282Z       VASP is heavily optimized for shared-memory multi-core systems. Typical scaling:
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9298763Z       - Efficient to ~1000 MPI tasks for large systems (> 500 atoms)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9299537Z       - GPU version provides 5–20× speedup for exact exchange (hybrid DFT) calculations
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9300178Z       - `vasp_gam` (Γ-point only) is ~2× faster than `vasp_std` for molecular/large-cell calculations
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9300591Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9300776Z       Key performance controls:
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9301112Z       - `NCORE`: Number of cores per orbital (parallelization over bands)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9301520Z       - `KPAR`: Number of k-point groups (k-parallelization)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9301849Z       - `NPAR`: Deprecated; use NCORE instead
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9302111Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9302291Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9302518Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9303019Z       - **PAW datasets**: Distributed with VASP license; regular updates at https://www.vasp.at/wiki/index.php/Available_PAW_potentials
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9303777Z       - **pymatgen**: High-level Python interface; reads/writes all VASP files; used by Materials Project
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9304486Z       - **ASE**: Atomic Simulation Environment VASP calculator
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9305027Z       - **VESTA**: Standard visualization tool for VASP outputs (CHGCAR, LOCPOT, POSCAR)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9305500Z       - **PHONOPY**: Phonon calculations using VASP force constants
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9305876Z       - **PHONON**: Kristin Persson group phonon analysis
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9306241Z       - **WANNIER90**: Tight-binding models from VASP wavefunctions
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9306688Z       - **LOBSTER**: Chemical bonding analysis (COHP, COOP) from VASP wavefunctions
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9307137Z       - **Bader**: Bader charge analysis from VASP charge density
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9307547Z       - **vaspkit**: Command-line post-processing toolkit for VASP outputs
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9307942Z       - **p4vasp**: GUI for VASP output visualization
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9308226Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9308405Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9308796Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/vasp/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9309582Z       # VASP — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9309827Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9310005Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9310218Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9310416Z       ### DFT Exchange-Correlation Functionals
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9310700Z       - **LDA**: CA-PZ, PW91
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9310959Z       - **GGA**: PBE, PBEsol, revPBE, AM05, PW91
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9311291Z       - **meta-GGA**: SCAN, r2SCAN, MS2, TPSS, M06-L (via libxc)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9311835Z       - **Hybrid**: HSE06 (screened), PBE0, B3LYP, SCAN0
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9312346Z       - **Double-hybrid**: MP2 correlation + HF exchange (expensive but accurate)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9312961Z       - **van der Waals**: DFT-D3, DFT-D3(BJ), DFT-D4, vdW-DF, optPBE-vdW, rev-vdW-DF2, SCAN+rVV10, many-body dispersion (MBD)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9313562Z       - **DFT+U**: Dudarev (LDATYPE=2) and Liechtenstein (LDATYPE=1) formulations
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9313925Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9314094Z       ### Beyond DFT
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9314416Z       - **G₀W₀**: One-shot GW quasi-particle correction
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9315102Z       - **scGW**: Self-consistent GW (eigenvalue or full self-consistency)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9315576Z       - **BSE**: Bethe-Salpeter equation for optical spectra with excitonic effects
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9316021Z       - **RPA**: Random Phase Approximation for correlation energy
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9316329Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9316504Z       ### Molecular Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9316799Z       - Born-Oppenheimer MD with velocity Verlet integrator
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9317247Z       - NVE, NVT (Nosé-Hoover, Andersen, Langevin), NPT ensembles
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9317593Z       - Metadynamics via PLUMED interface
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9317840Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9318016Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9318232Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9318403Z       ### VASP Input Files
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9318610Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9318781Z       | File | Description |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9319202Z       |------|-------------|
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9319614Z       | `POSCAR` | Crystal structure: lattice vectors + atomic positions (Cartesian or fractional) |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9320170Z       | `INCAR` | Calculation parameters (200+ tags controlling all aspects) |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9320754Z       | `KPOINTS` | k-point mesh (Monkhorst-Pack, Γ-centered, explicit list, band path) |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9321394Z       | `POTCAR` | PAW pseudopotential — concatenated from per-element files in `$VASP_PP_PATH` |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9321819Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9322003Z       ### Key VASP Output Files
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9322220Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9322392Z       | File | Description |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9322609Z       |------|-------------|
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9323178Z       | `OUTCAR` | Full text output: energies, forces, stress, dielectric properties |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9323703Z       | `vasprun.xml` | Machine-readable XML with complete calculation data |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9324162Z       | `CONTCAR` | Final or current ionic positions (POSCAR-compatible) |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9324630Z       | `CHGCAR` | Charge density + magnetization density (used in Bader, VESTA) |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9325104Z       | `LOCPOT` | Local electrostatic potential (work function calculations) |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9325511Z       | `DOSCAR` | Total and projected density of states |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9325844Z       | `EIGENVAL` | Band eigenvalues at each k-point |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9326229Z       | `WAVECAR` | Binary wavefunction file (for restart or Wannier90) |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9326633Z       | `PROCAR` | Projected wavefunctions on atomic orbitals |
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9326931Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9327105Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9327309Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9327542Z       ### High-Throughput Materials Screening with pymatgen
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9327834Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9328000Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9328230Z       from pymatgen.io.vasp.sets import MPRelaxSet
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9328536Z       from pymatgen.core import Structure
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9328779Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9329121Z       # Load structure from CIF
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9329414Z       structure = Structure.from_file("material.cif")
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9329691Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9329926Z       # Generate VASP inputs using Materials Project settings
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9330247Z       relax = MPRelaxSet(structure)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9330654Z       relax.write_input("vasp_input/")
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9330894Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9331183Z       # After VASP run, parse results
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9331476Z       from pymatgen.io.vasp.outputs import Vasprun
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9331772Z       vr = Vasprun("vasprun.xml")
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9332128Z       print(f"Band gap: {vr.get_band_structure().get_band_gap()['energy']:.3f} eV")
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9332537Z       print(f"Total energy: {vr.final_energy:.6f} eV")
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9332813Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9332977Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9333154Z       ### NEB Transition State Search
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9333392Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9333551Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9333961Z       # 1. Optimize initial and final states
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9334556Z       # 2. Create intermediate images with nebmake.pl (VTST tools)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9335088Z       nebmake.pl POSCAR_initial POSCAR_final 8  # 8 intermediate images
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9335426Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9335600Z       # 3. INCAR settings for NEB
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9335836Z       # ICHAIN = 0   # NEB method
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9336104Z       # IMAGES = 8   # Number of intermediate images
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9336473Z       # SPRING = -5  # Spring constant (eV/Å²)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9336763Z       # LCLIMB = .TRUE.  # Climbing image NEB
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9337007Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9337168Z       # 4. Run NEB
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9337468Z       mpirun -np 80 vasp_std  # 10 CPUs per image × 8 images
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9337757Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9337925Z       # 5. Analyze barrier
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9338152Z       nebef.pl  # Print energy profile
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9338389Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9338553Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9338728Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9338929Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9339353Z       ### Band Unfolding for Alloys
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9340047Z       VASP supports spectral function unfolding for supercell calculations, mapping supercell bands back onto the primitive cell Brillouin zone (LORBIT = 14, KPOINTS_OPT).
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9340733Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9340921Z       ### Electric Field Gradients and NMR
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9341730Z       VASP computes electric field gradients (EFGs) at nuclear sites for Mössbauer and NQR spectroscopy, and chemical shift tensors for solid-state NMR (using LNMR = .TRUE.).
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9342392Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9342574Z       ### Constrained Magnetic Moments
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9343198Z       Non-collinear calculations with orbital moment constraints (I_CONSTRAINED_M = 1 or 2) allow study of magnetic anisotropy energy and exchange parameters.
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9344032Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9344330Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9344837Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/vasp/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9345353Z       # VASP — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9345554Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9345740Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9345954Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9346821Z       VASP is the leading tool for ab initio computational materials science. Its robust PAW datasets, well-validated workflows, and deep integration with the Materials Project make it the standard choice for systematic study of crystalline materials.
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9347750Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9347934Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9348148Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9348336Z       ### The Materials Project Database
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9348585Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9349332Z       The Materials Project (https://materialsproject.org) uses VASP exclusively to compute properties of ~150,000 inorganic compounds. Standard workflows include:
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9350030Z       - Structure relaxation with PBE GGA
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9350315Z       - Band gap and electronic structure
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9350635Z       - Formation energies and phase stability (convex hull)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9351137Z       - Phonon stability (PHONOPY + VASP)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9351509Z       - Elastic constants tensor
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9360424Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9360884Z       This represents the largest systematic application of VASP and enables data-driven materials discovery.
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9361377Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9361579Z       ### Perovskite Solar Cells
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9361823Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9362154Z       VASP is heavily used to study hybrid halide perovskites (MAPbI3, CsPbBr3, FAPbI3):
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9362655Z       - Band gaps with HSE06+SOC (essential for heavy Pb compounds)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9363066Z       - Defect formation energies and charge transition levels
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9363395Z       - Ion migration barriers via NEB
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9363778Z       - Carrier effective masses from parabolic band fitting
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9364289Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9364629Z       ### Transition Metal Dichalcogenides (TMDs)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9365010Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9365221Z       2D materials like MoS2, WS2, WSe2, MoTe2:
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9365583Z       - Optical spectra with BSE (excitonic binding energies)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9365940Z       - Valley splitting with spin-orbit coupling
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9366299Z       - Charged defect calculations with finite-size corrections
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9366798Z       - Moiré heterostructures (large supercells with GPU)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9367111Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9367302Z       ### Battery Cathode Materials
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9367540Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9367775Z       Li-ion, Na-ion, and solid-state electrolyte screening:
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9368166Z       - Li/Na intercalation voltages from total energy differences
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9368515Z       - Migration barriers via NEB (CINEB)
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9368842Z       - Ionic conductivity from AIMD at elevated temperatures
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9369371Z       - Interface stability between electrolyte and electrode
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9369675Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9369863Z       ### High-Entropy Alloys
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9370083Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9370288Z       Multi-principal-element alloys (HEAs):
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9370628Z       - SQS (special quasirandom structures) with ATAT or mcsqs
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9370971Z       - Elastic moduli, stacking fault energies
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9371287Z       - Phase stability from DFT formation enthalpies
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9371608Z       - Magnetic properties of FeMnCoCrNi systems
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9371876Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9372076Z       ### Surface Catalysis and Electrochemistry
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9372336Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9372577Z       Reaction pathway analysis for heterogeneous catalysts:
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9372947Z       - Adsorption energies on Pt, Cu, Ni, Fe, Ru surfaces
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9373293Z       - NEB barriers for CO oxidation, N2 reduction, CO2RR
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9373678Z       - Computational hydrogen electrode model for electrocatalysis
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9374307Z       - Pourbaix diagrams from surface Gibbs free energies
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9374836Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9375059Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9375298Z       
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9375611Z       - **Energy storage**: Li/Na-ion batteries, solid-state electrolytes, supercapacitors
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9376142Z       - **Photovoltaics**: Perovskite, chalcogenide, and multijunction solar cells
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9376628Z       - **Heterogeneous catalysis**: Transition-metal surface chemistry
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9377106Z       - **Spintronics**: Magnetic anisotropy, exchange coupling, topological magnets
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9377590Z       - **Semiconductors**: Defect engineering, doping, carrier transport
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9378080Z       - **Structural materials**: High-entropy alloys, refractory ceramics, superalloys
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9378584Z       - **Thermoelectrics**: ZT optimization, phonon scattering engineering
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9379546Z       - **Quantum materials**: Topological insulators, Weyl semimetals, strongly correlated systems
2026-02-26T02:11:22.9379986Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:13:06.6155401Z       # ORCA
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6155736Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6156169Z       **Category:** Quantum Chemistry, DFT, Spectroscopy  
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6157933Z       **Developer/Organization:** Frank Neese and team at MPI für Kohlenforschung (formerly Max Planck Institut für Bioanorganische Chemie), Mülheim an der Ruhr, Germany  
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6159470Z       **License:** Free for academic use; commercial use requires a license  
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6160151Z       **Website:** https://orcaforum.kofo.mpg.de  
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6160688Z       **Download:** https://www.faccts.de/orca/  
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6161165Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6161451Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6161748Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6164265Z       ORCA is a comprehensive quantum chemistry package developed by Frank Neese and collaborators, renowned for its exceptional breadth of methods and particularly strong capabilities in spectroscopy calculations and transition metal chemistry. It handles everything from semi-empirical methods and DFT to multireference methods and coupled cluster theory, making it one of the most versatile quantum chemistry codes available.
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6167223Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6171080Z       ORCA is free for academic use and has amassed a large user community since its initial public release. It is particularly valued for its ability to calculate a wide range of spectroscopic properties — including EPR/ESR parameters, Mössbauer spectra, UV/Vis absorption, MCD, XAS, and more — with accuracy that often rivals or surpasses competing commercial codes. The development team has a particular expertise in transition metal complexes and bioinorganic chemistry.
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6173974Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6176000Z       The code uses Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis sets and is optimized for molecular calculations. Parallelization is handled via MPI, and many key steps benefit from the resolution-of-identity (RI) approximation and the COSX (chain-of-spheres) algorithm for exact exchange, enabling efficient hybrid DFT calculations on large molecules.
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6179325Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6179630Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6179954Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6180900Z       - **DFT**: Comprehensive library via libxc; LDA, GGA, meta-GGA, hybrid, double-hybrid, range-separated
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6181920Z       - **Coupled cluster**: CCSD, CCSD(T), CCSD(T)-F12, DLPNO-CCSD(T), DLPNO-MP2
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6182949Z       - **DLPNO methods**: Domain-based local pair natural orbital (DLPNO) approximation for near-linear scaling CC
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6183949Z       - **Multi-reference**: CASSCF, CASPT2, NEVPT2, MRCI, MRCI+Q, MRACPF
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6184676Z       - **TDDFT**: Excited states, UV/Vis spectra, CD, MCD, ECD spectra
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6185386Z       - **EOM-CCSD**: Equation-of-motion CC for excited states, IP, EA
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6186246Z       - **EPR/NMR**: g-tensor, hyperfine coupling, ZFS, NMR chemical shifts and coupling constants
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6187273Z       - **Mössbauer**: Isomer shifts and quadrupole splittings
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6188072Z       - **X-ray spectroscopy**: XAS (XANES), XES, resonance Raman via ROCIS and TDDFT
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6188918Z       - **IR and Raman**: Harmonic and anharmonic (VPT2) vibrational analysis
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6189880Z       - **NCI/AIM**: Non-covalent interaction analysis, atoms-in-molecules
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6190587Z       - **Dispersion**: Grimme DFT-D3, D4; Becke-Johnson damping
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6191240Z       - **Solvation**: SMD, CPCM, COSMO implicit solvation models
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6192050Z       - **Geometry optimization**: L-BFGS, RFO; internal coordinates; NEB and growing string
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6192936Z       - **COSX**: Chain-of-spheres approximation for efficient exact exchange
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6193536Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6193826Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6194162Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6194442Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6194850Z       # Download ORCA from https://www.faccts.de/orca/
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6195409Z       # Run a DFT geometry optimization:
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6195836Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6196169Z       # Create input file (h2o_opt.inp):
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6196626Z       # ! B3LYP def2-TZVP Opt
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6196993Z       # * xyz 0 1
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6197312Z       # O   0.000  0.000  0.119
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6197680Z       # H   0.000  0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6198053Z       # H   0.000 -0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6198414Z       # *
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6198703Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6199139Z       # Run ORCA
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6199534Z       /path/to/orca h2o_opt.inp > h2o_opt.out
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6199991Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6200308Z       # For DLPNO-CCSD(T) single point:
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6200820Z       # ! DLPNO-CCSD(T) def2-TZVPP def2-TZVPP/C TightSCF
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6201304Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6201589Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6201873Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6202202Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6203099Z       - [Gaussian](../../tools/gaussian/index.md) – Commercial QC; overlapping capabilities, strong thermochemistry
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6204385Z       - [Psi4](../../tools/psi4/index.md) – Open-source; Python-driven; strong CCSD(T) and SAPT
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6205492Z       - [NWChem](../../tools/nwchem/index.md) – Open-source; MPI-parallel QC + periodic DFT
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6206452Z       - [GAMESS](../../tools/gamess/index.md) – Free academic; MCSCF, FMO
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6207344Z       - [VMD](../../tools/vmd/index.md) – Visualization of ORCA orbitals and spectra
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6207966Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6208263Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6209117Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/orca/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6210085Z       # ORCA — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6210480Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6210757Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6211069Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6214545Z       ORCA is built on post-Hartree-Fock quantum chemistry theory using Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis sets. All methods start from the Hartree-Fock reference and systematically add electron correlation via perturbation theory, coupled cluster, or multi-reference methods.
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6216225Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6217556Z       The one-electron integral evaluation, SCF procedure, and post-HF methods share common infrastructure. ORCA uses an efficient Resolution of Identity (RI) approximation throughout to reduce the formal computational scaling of Coulomb and exchange integrals.
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6218553Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6218759Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6219282Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6219844Z       - **RI-J (density fitting)**: Approximate Coulomb integrals using auxiliary basis sets (def2/J, def2/JK); reduces cost of DFT Coulomb from O(N^4) to O(N^3)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6221085Z       - **RI-K / COSX**: Approximate exchange integrals — RI-K uses auxiliary basis, COSX (Chain-of-Spheres Exchange) uses numerical integration on a grid; enables efficient hybrid DFT
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6221998Z       - **RIJCOSX**: Combined RI-J + COSX for hybrid functionals (default in most ORCA hybrid DFT runs)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6223043Z       - **DLPNO (Domain-based Local Pair Natural Orbital)**: The flagship feature of ORCA; expresses electron correlation in localized orbital basis, enabling near-linear scaling CCSD(T) for large molecules (up to ~1000 atoms)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6224076Z       - **PNO (Pair Natural Orbitals)**: Compact representation of pair correlation; basis for DLPNO
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6224635Z       - **ICE-CI**: Iterative Configuration Expansion CI for large active spaces
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6225207Z       - **CASSCF**: State-averaged CASSCF with analytical gradient; active spaces up to CAS(20,20) practical
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6225819Z       - **NEVPT2**: Strongly-contracted and partially-contracted variants on top of CASSCF
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6226361Z       - **MRCI**: Size-consistent Davidson-corrected MRCI (MRCI+Q, MRACPF, MRAQCC)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6226728Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6226912Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6227184Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6227420Z       - **Language**: C++ (main codebase) with Fortran libraries
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6227894Z       - **Parallelization**: MPI-based; embarrassingly parallel for DFT and linear scaling CC
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6228511Z       - **Integral engine**: ORCA implements its own efficient integral routines using Obara-Saika scheme
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6229359Z       - **SHARK integral package** (ORCA 5+): New, faster integral engine replacing older code; uses SIMD and other modern optimizations
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6230179Z       - **Grid**: Numerical integration on Lebedev angular grids + radial grids for DFT XC; Grid1 through Grid7 (accuracy levels)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6230867Z       - **Geometry**: Uses both internal coordinates (GDIIS/L-BFGS) and Cartesian for optimization
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6231288Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6231472Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6231711Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6231932Z       ORCA scales well for molecular DFT and DLPNO-CC:
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6232461Z       - **DLPNO-CCSD(T)**: Near-linear scaling with molecule size; practical for proteins (~500 aa) in a large basis
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6233070Z       - **TDDFT**: Efficient for excited state calculations with Davidson diagonalizer
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6233755Z       - MPI scaling: Good to ~16–32 cores for most calculations; limited MPI scaling for some advanced methods
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6234408Z       - ORCA 5.x introduced OpenMP threading in addition to MPI, improving single-node performance
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6234835Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6235017Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6235247Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6235526Z       - **libxc**: External XC functional library (200+ functionals available)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6236099Z       - **SHARK**: Internal integral library (ORCA 5+)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6236540Z       - **CPCM/SMD**: Solvation via internal implementation
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6236890Z       - **ORCA_2AIM**: Interface for Bader AIM analysis
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6237283Z       - **Multiwfn**: Post-processing of ORCA wavefunction files (.gbw format)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6237740Z       - **VMD/Avogadro**: Visualization of MOs and structures from ORCA output
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6238203Z       - **orca_plot**: Internal tool for generating cube files of MOs and density
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6238559Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6238739Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6239254Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/orca/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6239787Z       # ORCA — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6240027Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6240206Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6240417Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6240579Z       ### DFT
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6240781Z       - LDA: PWLDA, VWN
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6241030Z       - GGA: BP86, BLYP, PBE, OLYP, OPBE, revPBE
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6241333Z       - meta-GGA: TPSS, M06-L, SCAN, r2SCAN
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6241662Z       - Hybrid: B3LYP (most popular), PBE0, TPSSh, M06, M06-2X
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6242097Z       - Range-separated hybrid: CAM-B3LYP, ωB97X-D, ωB97X-D3
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6242486Z       - Double-hybrid: B2PLYP, B2GP-PLYP, PWPB95, DLPNO-B2GP-PLYP
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6242848Z       - DFT-D3, DFT-D4 dispersion corrections
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6243102Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6243279Z       ### Wavefunction Methods
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6243538Z       - HF (RHF, UHF, ROHF), MP2, RI-MP2, SCS-MP2
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6243830Z       - CCSD, CCSD(T), CCSD-F12, CCSD(T)-F12
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6244226Z       - **DLPNO-MP2, DLPNO-CCSD, DLPNO-CCSD(T)** — near-linear scaling
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6244576Z       - EOM-CCSD (EE, IP, EA variants)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6244926Z       - ADC(2), ADC(3) — algebraic diagrammatic construction
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6245284Z       - CASSCF, NEVPT2 (SC-NEVPT2, FIC-NEVPT2), CASPT2
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6245593Z       - MRCI, MRCI+Q, MRACPF, MRAQCC
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6245886Z       - ICE-CI: iterative CI for large active spaces
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6246157Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6246333Z       ### Excited State Methods
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6246576Z       - TDDFT (full and TDA-TDDFT)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6246878Z       - ROCIS: restricted open-shell CIS (excellent for XAS)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6247176Z       - EOM-CCSD
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6247408Z       - CASPT2/NEVPT2 multi-reference excited states
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6247696Z       - Spin-flip TDDFT
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6247898Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6248084Z       ### Spectroscopy Properties
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6248353Z       - UV/Vis absorption and emission spectra
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6248716Z       - Circular dichroism (CD) and magnetic circular dichroism (MCD)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6249264Z       - EPR: g-tensor, A-tensor (hyperfine), zero-field splitting (D-tensor)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6249669Z       - NMR: chemical shifts (GIAO), coupling constants
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6250077Z       - Mössbauer: isomer shifts, quadrupole splittings
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6250415Z       - IR and Raman (harmonic and anharmonic VPT2)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6250705Z       - Resonance Raman
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6250921Z       - XAS/XANES via TDDFT or ROCIS
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6251189Z       - X-ray emission spectroscopy (XES)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6251504Z       - NRVS: nuclear resonance vibrational spectroscopy
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6251786Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6251964Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6252183Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6252351Z       ### Input Format
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6252570Z       ORCA uses a keyword-based input:
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6252813Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6253029Z       ! [method] [basis] [auxiliary basis] [options]
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6253300Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6253626Z       %maxcore 4000      # Memory per core in MB
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6253920Z       %pal nprocs 8 end  # Parallel execution
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6254276Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6254461Z       * xyz [charge] [multiplicity]
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6254710Z       [atomic coordinates]
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6254918Z       *
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6255082Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6255244Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6255501Z       ### Output Files
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6255848Z       | File | Description |
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6256088Z       |------|-------------|
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6256312Z       | `.out` | Main text output |
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6256597Z       | `.gbw` | Binary wavefunction file (MOs, density) |
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6256906Z       | `.engrad` | Energy and gradient |
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6257182Z       | `_trj.xyz` | Optimization trajectory |
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6257452Z       | `.hess` | Hessian matrix |
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6257699Z       | `.spectrum` | Spectral data |
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6257942Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6258115Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6258320Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6258535Z       ### Geometry Optimization + Frequency Analysis
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6258807Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6259192Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6259437Z       ! B3LYP-D3BJ def2-TZVP RIJCOSX def2/J Opt Freq
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6259725Z       %pal nprocs 8 end
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6259931Z       %maxcore 4000
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6260127Z       * xyz 0 1
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6260315Z       C   0.000  0.000  0.000
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6260533Z       O   1.200  0.000  0.000
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6260740Z       ...
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6260904Z       *
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6261070Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6261231Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6261418Z       ### DLPNO-CCSD(T) Single Point
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6261656Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6261814Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6262060Z       ! DLPNO-CCSD(T) def2-TZVPP def2-TZVPP/C TightSCF TightPNO
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6262385Z       %pal nprocs 16 end
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6262603Z       %maxcore 6000
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6262808Z       * xyzfile 0 1 optimized.xyz
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6263043Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6263206Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6263639Z       ### TDDFT UV/Vis Spectrum
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6263864Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6264023Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6264235Z       ! CAM-B3LYP def2-TZVP RIJCOSX def2/J TightSCF
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6264508Z       %tddft
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6264711Z         nroots 20     # Number of excited states
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6265009Z         maxdim 5      # Davidson subspace expansion
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6265437Z       end
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6265720Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6266005Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6266329Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6266714Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6267068Z       ### DLPNO for Protein-Ligand Interactions
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6268885Z       DLPNO-CCSD(T) can be applied to protein active sites (~200–500 atoms) using fragmentation or direct calculation, providing high-accuracy interaction energies beyond what standard DFT can offer.
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6270152Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6270388Z       ### ROCIS for XAS of Transition Metal Complexes
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6271322Z       The ROCIS (Restricted Open-shell CIS) method is specially designed for K-edge and L-edge X-ray absorption spectroscopy of transition metal complexes, where multi-reference character is important but full MRCI is too expensive.
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6272208Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6272389Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6272791Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/orca/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6273326Z       # ORCA — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6273533Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6273725Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6273947Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6275066Z       ORCA is the leading tool for molecular spectroscopy calculations, particularly for transition metal complexes, bioinorganic chemistry, and organic photochemistry. Its DLPNO-CCSD(T) implementation enables high-accuracy thermochemistry for drug-sized molecules.
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6276184Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6276363Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6276573Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6276794Z       ### EPR Spectroscopy of Transition Metal Complexes
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6277076Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6277355Z       ORCA is widely regarded as the best code for computing EPR parameters:
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6277965Z       - g-tensors and hyperfine coupling constants (A-tensors) for Cu²⁺, Fe³⁺, Mn²⁺ complexes
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6278666Z       - Zero-field splitting (D, E parameters) for S > ½ systems
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6279551Z       - Spin density analysis and comparison with experimental EPR spectra
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6280119Z       - Study of geometric distortions' effects on EPR parameters in copper enzymes
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6280497Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6280893Z       ### Mössbauer Spectroscopy of Iron Compounds
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6281495Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6281895Z       For ⁵⁷Fe Mössbauer spectroscopy:
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6282463Z       - Isomer shifts computed from electron density at the Fe nucleus
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6283190Z       - Quadrupole splittings from electric field gradient tensor
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6284206Z       - Enables characterization of iron oxidation states and spin states in iron proteins (e.g., ferredoxins, cytochrome P450)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6284732Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6284951Z       ### Reaction Mechanisms in Bioinorganic Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6285233Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6285479Z       ORCA is used to study reaction mechanisms of metalloenzymes:
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6285990Z       - Cytochrome P450 C-H activation via compound I (Fe⁴⁺=O porphyrin radical)
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6286422Z       - Nitrogenase N₂ reduction mechanism
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6286727Z       - Ribonucleotide reductase radical generation
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6287167Z       - DFT (B3LYP or TPSSh) geometry optimization + DLPNO-CCSD(T) for accurate barriers
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6287553Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6287741Z       ### Drug Molecule Thermochemistry
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6287987Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6288382Z       DLPNO-CCSD(T) enables high-accuracy thermochemistry for drug-sized molecules (up to ~100 heavy atoms):
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6289130Z       - Tautomer stability ratios relevant to drug bioavailability
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6289583Z       - Conformer energy differences for force field parameterization
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6290186Z       - Binding enthalpy calculations for host-guest systems
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6290720Z       - Benchmarking DFT functionals against CCSD(T) reference data
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6291041Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6291257Z       ### Photochemistry and Excited-State Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6291526Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6291767Z       UV/Vis spectra of organic dyes and photoactive proteins:
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6292182Z       - Vertical excitation energies and oscillator strengths via TDDFT
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6292581Z       - Franck-Condon factors and vibronic progressions
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6292990Z       - S₁ geometry optimization and emission spectra
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6293369Z       - Conical intersection search for photodegradation pathways
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6293687Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6293879Z       ### X-ray Absorption Spectroscopy
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6294120Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6294307Z       ROCIS calculations for XAS/XANES:
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6294587Z       - Fe K-edge XANES of iron-sulfur clusters
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6294883Z       - Cu L-edge spectra of copper proteins
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6295279Z       - Pre-edge analysis for determining oxidation state and coordination geometry
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6295658Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6295839Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6296075Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6296415Z       - **Bioinorganic chemistry**: Metalloenzyme mechanisms, EPR, Mössbauer
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6297309Z       - **Drug discovery**: Thermochemistry, tautomer/conformer analysis, reaction mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6297840Z       - **Photochemistry**: Dye sensitizers, photoredox catalysts, UV filters
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6298298Z       - **Inorganic synthesis**: Transition metal complex characterization
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6298739Z       - **Computational spectroscopy**: UV/Vis, CD, NMR, EPR benchmarking
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6299579Z       - **Homogeneous catalysis**: Organometallic reaction mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6300006Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6300188Z       
2026-02-26T02:13:06.6300594Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/orca/references.md << 'ENDOFFILE'

2026-02-26T02:14:38.1022759Z       # Gaussian
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1023080Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1023491Z       **Category:** Quantum Chemistry, DFT, Spectroscopy  
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1024251Z       **Developer/Organization:** Gaussian, Inc. (founded by John Pople)  
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1024947Z       **License:** Commercial  
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1025440Z       **Website:** https://gaussian.com  
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1025948Z       **Current Version:** Gaussian 16 (G16)  
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1026350Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1026623Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1026906Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1029919Z       Gaussian is one of the most widely used quantum chemistry programs in the world and has been a cornerstone of computational chemistry since its initial release in 1970 by Nobel laureate John Pople and collaborators. The name refers to the Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis functions used to represent molecular wavefunctions. The current version, Gaussian 16, continues a lineage from Gaussian 70 through Gaussian 09 and supports an enormous range of quantum chemical methods.
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1032786Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1035024Z       Gaussian 16 introduced support for CUDA GPU acceleration for DFT and HF calculations, improving performance on modern workstations and HPC nodes. The software runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows, and has an extensive track record in academic and industrial research for thermochemistry, reaction mechanism studies, NMR prediction, spectroscopy, and catalysis.
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1038184Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1041177Z       A key strength of Gaussian is its comprehensive and well-validated implementation of composite thermochemistry methods (G4, CBS-QB3, W1), NMR chemical shift prediction (GIAO), and anharmonic vibrational analysis (VPT2). It also features a wide variety of optimization algorithms and transition state search methods.
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1043991Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1044540Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1044872Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1046446Z       - **DFT**: Comprehensive functional library via libxc plus internal implementations; B3LYP, M06-2X, ωB97X-D, CAM-B3LYP
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1047669Z       - **HF and post-HF**: MP2, MP3, MP4, CCSD, CCSD(T), QCISD(T)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1048427Z       - **Composite thermochemistry**: G1, G2, G3, G4; CBS-4M, CBS-QB3, CBS-APNO; W1BD
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1049759Z       - **TDDFT**: Excited states, absorption, emission, CD spectra
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1050731Z       - **EOM-CCSD**: Ionization potentials, electron affinities, excited states
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1051483Z       - **CASSCF**: Multi-reference, conical intersections
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1052267Z       - **Geometry optimization**: Berny algorithm; GDIIS; transition state (QST2, QST3); IRC
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1053227Z       - **NMR**: GIAO chemical shifts and coupling constants (J); NICS aromaticity indices
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1054188Z       - **Vibrational analysis**: Harmonic frequencies, IR/Raman intensities, VPT2 anharmonic
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1055038Z       - **Solvation**: PCM, IPCM, CPCM, SMD implicit solvent models
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1055690Z       - **ONIOM**: QM/MM and multi-level QM/QM calculations
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1056329Z       - **Fragment-based**: FMO-like fragmentation for large systems
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1057152Z       - **Periodic boundary**: Periodic Hartree-Fock and DFT for 1D, 2D, 3D periodic systems
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1057999Z       - **Counterpoise**: BSSE correction with counterpoise method
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1058737Z       - **GPU acceleration**: CUDA for DFT and HF (Linux, NVIDIA)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1059425Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1059724Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1060059Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1060344Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1060700Z       # Create input file (water_opt.gjf):
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1061158Z       # %chk=water.chk
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1061509Z       # %nprocshared=4
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1061840Z       # %mem=4GB
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1062178Z       # #p B3LYP/6-311+G** Opt Freq
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1062569Z       # 
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1062960Z       # Water geometry optimization and frequency analysis
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1063678Z       # 
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1063949Z       # 0 1
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1064493Z       # O   0.000  0.000  0.119
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1064870Z       # H   0.000  0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1065464Z       # H   0.000 -0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1065979Z       # 
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1066258Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1066565Z       # Run Gaussian 16
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1066920Z       g16 water_opt.gjf
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1067246Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1067536Z       # View results
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1068064Z       formchk water.chk water.fchk  # convert checkpoint to formatted
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1068660Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1069100Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1069399Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1069743Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1070579Z       - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – Free for academic use; strong spectroscopy and multi-reference
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1071669Z       - [Psi4](../../tools/psi4/index.md) – Open-source Python-driven alternative
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1072579Z       - [NWChem](../../tools/nwchem/index.md) – Open-source, MPI-parallel
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1073600Z       - [GaussView](https://gaussview.net) – Gaussian's graphical interface for input/output
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1074819Z       - [Multiwfn](http://sobereva.com/multiwfn/) – Wavefunction analysis from Gaussian .fchk files
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1075604Z       ENDOFFILE
