2026-02-26T02:14:38.1077612Z       # Gaussian — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1078045Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1078350Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1078733Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1081296Z       Gaussian solves the electronic Schrödinger equation using Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis functions. All wavefunctions are expanded in atom-centered Gaussian primitives contracted into basis functions. The SCF procedure minimizes the Hartree-Fock energy (or KS-DFT energy) with respect to orbital coefficients.
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1083395Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1083707Z       Key theoretical features:
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1085118Z       - **GIAO (Gauge-Including Atomic Orbitals)**: Magnetic field-dependent basis functions for NMR shielding, eliminating gauge dependence
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1086851Z       - **Berny geometry optimization**: Internal coordinate optimization using quadratic approximation; handles transition states via eigenvector following
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1088709Z       - **ONIOM (Our own N-layered Integrated Molecular Orbital and Molecular mechanics)**: Multi-layer QM/MM; developed by Morokuma group and implemented in Gaussian
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1090014Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1090350Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1090795Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1091400Z       - **SCF solvers**: Pulay DIIS, ADIIS, RCA (relaxed constraint algorithm) for difficult convergence
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1092253Z       - **Density fitting (RI/DF)**: Optional density fitting for Coulomb integrals
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1093142Z       - **IRC (Intrinsic Reaction Coordinate)**: Follows steepest descent path from saddle point
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1094079Z       - **QST2/QST3**: Synchronous Transit-Guided Quasi-Newton for TS searches
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1095017Z       - **VPT2**: Vibrational perturbation theory for anharmonic frequencies and intensities
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1096039Z       - **Composite methods**: Carefully validated multilevel energy extrapolation schemes (G4, CBS-QB3)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1096778Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1097087Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1097460Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1097721Z       - **Language**: Fortran and C
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1098250Z       - **Shared-memory parallelism**: Linda parallel version for distributed computing; shared-memory OpenMP for multicore
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1098931Z       - **GPU**: CUDA acceleration for DFT exchange-correlation and Coulomb integrals (NVIDIA)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1099796Z       - **Linda**: Network-shared memory model for multi-node execution (requires Linda license)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1100621Z       - **Formatted Checkpoint (.fchk)**: Standard format for wavefunction data; readable by many third-party tools
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1101379Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1101730Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1111566Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1111954Z       - Excellent single-node shared-memory performance (up to 64 cores typical)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1112683Z       - Linda network parallel for multi-node (less common than MPI)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1113710Z       - GPU acceleration (CUDA) available for DFT and HF; up to 4× speedup for exchange integrals
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1114537Z       - Composite methods (G4, CBS-QB3) are highly optimized and fast for drug-sized molecules
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1115207Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1115506Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1115881Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1116406Z       - **GaussView 6**: Official graphical interface (separate commercial product)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1117220Z       - **formchk/cubegen**: Utilities for checkpoint and cube file generation
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1118002Z       - **Multiwfn**: Comprehensive wavefunction analysis from .fchk files
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1118807Z       - **NBO (Natural Bond Orbital)**: Widely used via Gaussian interface (NBO6/7)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1119856Z       - **GaussSum**: Open-source spectrum analysis tool for Gaussian output
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1120633Z       - **cclib**: Python library for parsing Gaussian output files
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1121218Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1121536Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1122267Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/gaussian/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1123312Z       # Gaussian — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1123748Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1124059Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1124434Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1124758Z       ### Hartree-Fock and DFT
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1125184Z       - RHF, UHF, ROHF, Complex HF
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1125713Z       - LDA, GGA (BLYP, PBE, BP86), meta-GGA (TPSS, M06-L)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1126864Z       - Hybrid: B3LYP, B3PW91, PBE0, TPSSh, M06, M06-2X, M06-HF
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1127675Z       - Range-separated: CAM-B3LYP, ωB97X-D, LC-ωPBE
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1128249Z       - Double-hybrid: B2PLYP, mPW2PLYP
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1128738Z       - DFT-D3 dispersion corrections
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1129398Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1129700Z       ### Post-HF
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1130077Z       - MP2, MP3, MP4(SDQ), MP4(SDTQ)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1130543Z       - CCSD, CCSD(T)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1130796Z       - QCISD, QCISD(T)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1131047Z       - CID, CISD, CIS, BD (Brueckner doubles)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1131312Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1131514Z       ### Composite Thermochemistry Methods
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1131794Z       | Method | Target Accuracy | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1132059Z       |--------|----------------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1132460Z       | G4 | ±1 kcal/mol | Current flagship; recommended |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1132914Z       | G3 | ±1.5 kcal/mol | Well-validated for organic molecules |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1133366Z       | CBS-QB3 | ±1 kcal/mol | Efficient, uses B3LYP geometry |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1133819Z       | CBS-APNO | ±0.5 kcal/mol | High accuracy, limited to first-row |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1134210Z       | W1BD | <1 kcal/mol | Expensive, benchmarking |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1134487Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1134666Z       ### Excited States
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1134948Z       - CIS, CIS(D): Single-reference excited states (cheap)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1135338Z       - TDDFT: Linear-response, full or Tamm-Dancoff approximation
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1135743Z       - EOM-CCSD: Equation-of-motion for accurate excited states
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1136129Z       - SAC-CI: Symmetry-adapted cluster CI (unique to Gaussian)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1136489Z       - CASSCF: Active space for multi-reference states
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1136770Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1136950Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1137183Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1137370Z       ### Input (Route Line Keywords)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1137619Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1137826Z       # [method]/[basis] [job-type] [options]
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1138081Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1138252Z       # Examples:
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1138455Z       # B3LYP/6-311+G** Opt Freq
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1138694Z       # CCSD(T)/aug-cc-pVTZ SP
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1138934Z       # TD-B3LYP/6-311+G** NStates=10
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1139436Z       # CASSCF(6,6)/6-31G* Opt
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1139671Z       # ONIOM(B3LYP/6-311G*:PM6)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1139891Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1140063Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1140235Z       ### Key Output Files
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1140464Z       | Extension | Description |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1140707Z       |-----------|-------------|
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1140954Z       | `.log` / `.out` | Main text output |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1141269Z       | `.chk` | Binary checkpoint (restart, NBO analysis) |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1141651Z       | `.fchk` | Formatted checkpoint (human-readable, portable) |
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1141973Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1142147Z       ### Useful Utilities
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1142357Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1142636Z       formchk input.chk output.fchk     # Convert binary .chk to .fchk
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1143077Z       cubegen 0 MO=homo input.fchk homo.cube 80  # Generate HOMO cube file
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1143414Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1143584Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1143764Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1143975Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1144247Z       ### Reaction Profile (Reactant → TS → Product)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1144523Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1144685Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1144871Z       # Step 1: Optimize reactant
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1145116Z       #p B3LYP/6-31G* Opt
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1145323Z       ...
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1145492Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1145689Z       # Step 2: Guess TS geometry and optimize
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1146197Z       #p B3LYP/6-31G* Opt=(QST2,CalcFC,MaxCycles=100) Freq
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1146595Z       ...
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1146767Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1147009Z       # Step 3: Verify TS (one imaginary frequency) and follow IRC
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1147377Z       #p B3LYP/6-31G* IRC=(CalcFC,Forward) Freq=SaveNM
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1147661Z       ...
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1147834Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1148026Z       # Step 4: High-level single point
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1148300Z       #p CCSD(T)/aug-cc-pVTZ SP
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1148531Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1148697Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1148884Z       ### NMR Chemical Shift Prediction
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1149367Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1149531Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1149796Z       #p B3LYP/6-311+G(2d,p) NMR=GIAO SCRF=(PCM,Solvent=Chloroform)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1150121Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1150289Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1150465Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1150683Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1150871Z       ### ONIOM Multi-Layer Calculations
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1151123Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1151320Z       #p ONIOM(B3LYP/6-311G*:UFF) Opt
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1151656Z       # Layer assignment: atoms with H for high layer, L for low layer
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1152017Z       # Allows QM/MM for enzymatic reactions
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1152275Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1152438Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1152622Z       ### VPT2 Anharmonic Frequencies
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1152858Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1153089Z       #p B3LYP/6-311+G** Freq=(Anharmonic,Cubic,Quartic)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1153475Z       # Computes fundamental, overtone, combination band frequencies
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1153869Z       # More accurate comparison with experimental IR/Raman
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1154168Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1154338Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:14:38.1155494Z       # Gaussian — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1155718Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1155901Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1156135Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1156995Z       Gaussian is widely used for organic and medicinal chemistry, thermochemistry, spectroscopy prediction, and reaction mechanism studies. Its composite methods and NMR capabilities are particularly valued in synthesis-oriented research.
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1157921Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1158101Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1158344Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1158525Z       ### NMR Structure Elucidation
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1158760Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1159481Z       Gaussian's GIAO-NMR is routinely used by synthetic chemists to confirm or assign the structures of natural products and synthetic molecules:
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1160219Z       - Compute ¹H and ¹³C chemical shifts for candidate structures
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1160680Z       - Compare with experimental NMR spectra using DP4+ probability analysis
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1161146Z       - Determine relative and absolute configuration of stereocenters
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1161603Z       - Level: B3LYP/6-311+G(2d,p) or mPW1PW91/6-311+G(2d,p) with implicit solvent
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1161963Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1162194Z       ### Reaction Mechanism Elucidation in Organic Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1162491Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1162715Z       Gaussian is widely used to map reaction pathways:
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1163166Z       - DFT geometry optimization of reactants, intermediates, transition states, products
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1163702Z       - IRC (Intrinsic Reaction Coordinate) calculations to verify TS connectivity
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1164219Z       - M06-2X/def2-TZVP for pericyclic reactions; B3LYP-D3/6-311+G** for organometallic
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1164777Z       - Published in major synthetic chemistry journals (JACS, Angew. Chem., Nature Chemistry)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1165334Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1165540Z       ### Thermochemical Database Construction
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1165903Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1166247Z       G4 and CBS-QB3 composite methods are used to build high-quality thermochemical databases:
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1166780Z       - Heats of formation (ΔHf°) for organic molecules
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1167095Z       - Bond dissociation energies (BDE)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1167394Z       - Ionization energies and electron affinities
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1167860Z       - The Active Thermochemical Tables (ATcT) database uses high-level Gaussian calculations
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1168286Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1168486Z       ### Drug Design: Docking Score Correction
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1168743Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1169121Z       QM-based corrections to molecular docking scores:
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1169462Z       - Compute ligand strain energy (CCSD(T) or DFT)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1169819Z       - Protein-ligand interaction energy in QM cluster model
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1170176Z       - ONIOM QM/MM for active site calculations
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1170551Z       - Torsional barrier corrections for force field parameterization
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1170890Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1171078Z       ### Excited-State Photochemistry
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1171319Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1171521Z       TDDFT and CIS methods for photochemistry:
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1171856Z       - Vertical excitation energies for chromophore design
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1172318Z       - S₁ geometry optimization for emission wavelength prediction
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1172765Z       - Conical intersection search for photodegradation or photoswitching
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1173191Z       - Stokes shift calculation for fluorescent probe design
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1173492Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1173692Z       ### Peptide and Protein Fragment Studies
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1173950Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1174147Z       ONIOM calculations for enzyme catalysis:
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1174476Z       - High layer: Active site residues + substrate in DFT
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1174833Z       - Low layer: Surrounding protein in MM (AMBER or UFF)
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1175229Z       - Reaction barrier comparison between wild-type and mutant enzymes
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1175574Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1175755Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1175991Z       
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1176343Z       - **Pharmaceutical / medicinal chemistry**: Reaction mechanism, ADMET property computation
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1176909Z       - **Synthetic chemistry**: NMR prediction, stereochemistry, reaction prediction
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1177405Z       - **Physical organic chemistry**: Hammett correlations, BDE, aromaticity
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1177858Z       - **Atmospheric chemistry**: Radical reactions, reaction rates
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1178262Z       - **Polymer chemistry**: Chain growth mechanisms, tacticity
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1178701Z       - **Environmental chemistry**: Pesticide degradation, pollutant reactivity
2026-02-26T02:14:38.1179212Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:15:48.1341212Z       # NWChem
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1341531Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1342134Z       **Category:** Quantum Chemistry, DFT, Ab Initio Molecular Dynamics  
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1342970Z       **Developer/Organization:** Environmental Molecular Sciences Laboratory (EMSL) / Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL)  
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1343553Z       **License:** Apache License 2.0  
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1343865Z       **Website:** https://www.nwchem-sw.org  
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1344202Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/nwchemgit/nwchem  
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1344489Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1344657Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1344841Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1346652Z       NWChem (Northwest Chemistry) is a comprehensive open-source quantum chemistry package developed at Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL) under the EMSL (Environmental Molecular Sciences Laboratory). First released in the mid-1990s, NWChem was designed from the ground up for massively parallel computing, using a Global Arrays (GA) programming model that enables efficient communication on distributed-memory supercomputers.
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1349268Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1352121Z       NWChem supports a broad range of quantum chemical methods — from Hartree-Fock and DFT through coupled cluster, CASSCF, TDDFT, and plane-wave DFT — making it one of the most versatile codes available. It has been deployed on some of the world's largest supercomputers and is used for large-scale electronic structure problems that would be impractical with single-node codes.
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1354503Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1356327Z       The code is licensed under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, making it freely available for both academic and commercial use. Regular releases are made to GitHub, and active development continues through the NWChemEx project, which extends NWChem's capabilities for exascale computing.
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1358243Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1358589Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1359125Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1359786Z       - **Parallel scalability**: Designed for massively parallel execution on thousands of CPU cores
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1360863Z       - **Gaussian basis DFT**: LDA, GGA, meta-GGA, hybrid functionals; DFT-D3 dispersion
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1361871Z       - **Plane-wave DFT (NWPW)**: Periodic DFT with Car-Parrinello MD for solids and surfaces
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1363021Z       - **MP2/RI-MP2**: Second-order Møller-Plesset perturbation theory
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1363641Z       - **Coupled cluster**: CCSD, CCSD(T), EOM-CCSD, CR-EOMCCSD(T)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1364361Z       - **TDDFT**: Linear-response time-dependent DFT for excited states and spectra
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1365295Z       - **CASSCF/MCSCF**: Multi-reference methods for strongly correlated systems
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1366091Z       - **Response theory**: NMR shielding, polarizabilities, hyperpolarizabilities
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1366532Z       - **Solvation**: COSMO implicit solvent model
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1366859Z       - **QM/MM**: Interface with AMBER and other MM codes
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1367281Z       - **Geometry optimization**: Quasi-Newton BFGS, internal coordinates, NEB
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1367831Z       - **Property calculations**: Dipole moments, multipole moments, polarizabilities
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1368433Z       - **TCE (Tensor Contraction Engine)**: Automated generation of CC and MBPT codes up to very high order
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1369329Z       - **DFT+U**: Hubbard U correction for correlated d/f systems in plane-wave mode
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1369822Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1369996Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1370190Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1370352Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1370574Z       # Install from source or via conda:
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1370852Z       conda install -c conda-forge nwchem
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1371093Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1371273Z       # Basic DFT input (h2o.nw):
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1371528Z       # echo "geometry units angstroms
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1371775Z       # O 0.000 0.000 0.000
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1372213Z       # H 0.000 0.757 0.587
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1372427Z       # H 0.000 -0.757 0.587
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1372753Z       # end
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1372929Z       # basis
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1373114Z       # * library 6-31g*
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1373321Z       # end
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1373488Z       # dft
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1373658Z       #   xc b3lyp
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1373843Z       # end
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1374027Z       # task dft energy"  > h2o.nw
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1374253Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1374419Z       # Run NWChem
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1374632Z       mpirun -np 4 nwchem h2o.nw > h2o.out
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1374892Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1375057Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1375390Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1375729Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1376285Z       - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – Free academic, strong spectroscopy and DLPNO-CC
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1376908Z       - [Psi4](../../tools/psi4/index.md) – Open-source Python-driven, strong QC methods
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1377630Z       - [Quantum ESPRESSO](../../tools/quantum-espresso/index.md) – Open-source plane-wave DFT for periodic systems
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1378338Z       - [GAMESS](../../tools/gamess/index.md) – Free academic, complementary methods
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1378713Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1378893Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1379568Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/nwchem/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1380137Z       # NWChem — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1380383Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1380563Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1380783Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1381974Z       NWChem implements quantum chemistry methods using both Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis functions for molecular calculations and plane-wave basis sets for periodic systems. The molecular code supports the full range from HF to CCSD(T) and multi-reference methods. The plane-wave code (NWPW) implements DFT and Car-Parrinello MD for solids.
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1383235Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1383433Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1383686Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1384156Z       - **Global Arrays (GA)**: NWChem's unique parallel programming model; one-sided communication for distributed arrays across nodes
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1385114Z       - **TCE (Tensor Contraction Engine)**: Automated source code generation for high-order many-body methods; generates efficient, parallel coupled cluster codes
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1385920Z       - **CR-EOMCCSD(T)**: Completely renormalized EOM-CC for quasidegenerate excited states
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1386809Z       - **Analytical gradients**: Available for HF, DFT, MP2, CCSD, TDDFT for geometry optimization
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1387419Z       - **RI/DF approximation**: Resolution of identity for efficient MP2 and DFT calculations
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1387819Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1387994Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1388224Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1388403Z       - **Language**: Fortran 77/90 and C
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1388767Z       - **Parallelism**: MPI + Global Arrays (one-sided communication), ARMCI
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1389448Z       - **Memory model**: Distributed-memory; Global Arrays abstraction
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1389819Z       - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Intel MKL or OpenBLAS
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1390210Z       - **Plane-wave code (NWPW)**: Car-Parrinello MD with CPMD equations of motion
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1390577Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1390759Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1390991Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1391170Z       NWChem was designed for HPC:
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1391445Z       - Production runs on thousands of MPI tasks
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1391756Z       - Used on Cray and IBM BG/Q supercomputers
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1392148Z       - Global Arrays enable efficient communication patterns for large molecules
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1392515Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1392965Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1393195Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1393567Z       - **Global Arrays (GA) toolkit**: Core parallel communication library
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1393954Z       - **ARMCI**: One-sided communication via GA
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1394257Z       - **libxc**: External XC functional library
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1394554Z       - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Intel MKL, OpenBLAS, AOCL
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1394889Z       - **ASE**: Python interface for NWChem calculations
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1395228Z       - **AiiDA**: Workflow management with NWChem plugin
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1395558Z       - **cclib**: Python parser for NWChem output files
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1395991Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1396278Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1396680Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/nwchem/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1397235Z       # NWChem — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1397475Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1397660Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1397867Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1398036Z       ### Molecular DFT
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1398304Z       - LDA (SVWN), GGA (BLYP, PBE, BP86), meta-GGA (TPSS)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1398631Z       - Hybrid: B3LYP, PBE0, M06, M06-2X, CAM-B3LYP
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1399151Z       - Double-hybrid: B2PLYP via DFT + MP2 combination
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1399475Z       - DFT-D3 dispersion corrections
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1399714Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1399887Z       ### Wavefunction Theory
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1400121Z       - HF (RHF, UHF, ROHF)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1400339Z       - MP2, RI-MP2
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1400531Z       - CCSD, CCSD(T)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1400738Z       - QCISD, QCISD(T)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1400960Z       - Brueckner doubles BD, BD(T)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1401194Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1401371Z       ### TCE High-Order Methods
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1401604Z       - MP2, MP3, MP4
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1401806Z       - CCD, LCCD, LCCSD
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1402031Z       - CCSD, CCSD(T), CCSDT, CCSDTQ
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1402342Z       - EOM-CCSD for excited states, IP-EOM-CCSD, EA-EOM-CCSD
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1402765Z       - CR-EOMCCSD(T): Completely renormalized EOM for quasi-degenerate states
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1403117Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1403290Z       ### Multi-Reference
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1403521Z       - CASSCF / MCSCF with state averaging
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1403793Z       - MP2-CASSCF gradient
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1404001Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1404171Z       ### Excited States
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1404428Z       - TDDFT (linear-response, full and Tamm-Dancoff)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1404728Z       - EOM-CCSD, CR-EOMCCSD(T)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1404948Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1405125Z       ### Plane-Wave DFT (NWPW module)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1405381Z       - Car-Parrinello AIMD
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1405681Z       - Γ-point and k-point sampling
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1406066Z       - Norm-conserving Hamann-Schlüter-Chiang pseudopotentials
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1406439Z       - Ultrasoft Vanderbilt pseudopotentials
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1406704Z       - DFT+U
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1407028Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1407212Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1407425Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1407583Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1407784Z       # NWChem input uses task-based structure:
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1408099Z       # geometry / basis / dft (or scf, mp2, ccsd) / task
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1408376Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1408549Z       geometry units angstrom
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1408770Z         O  0.000  0.000  0.119
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1409186Z         H  0.000  0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1409462Z         H  0.000 -0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1409670Z       end
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1409834Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1410000Z       basis "ao basis"
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1410209Z         * library cc-pVTZ
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1410409Z       end
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1410572Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1410893Z       dft
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1411060Z         xc pbe0
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1411235Z         mult 1
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1411537Z         convergence energy 1e-8
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1411762Z       end
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1411928Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1412097Z       task dft optimize
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1412309Z       task dft frequencies
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1412515Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1412673Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1412842Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1413040Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1413218Z       ### MP2 Geometry Optimization
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1413451Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1413614Z       mp2
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1413783Z         freeze atomic
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1413978Z         tight
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1414154Z       end
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1414333Z       task mp2 optimize
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1414528Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1414687Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1414860Z       ### TDDFT Excited States
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1415073Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1415239Z       tddft
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1415406Z         nroots 10
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1415594Z         notriplet
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1415780Z         target 2
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1415957Z         civecs
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1416128Z       end
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1416298Z       task tddft energy
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1416499Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1416656Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1416828Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1417137Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1417482Z       ### TCE (Tensor Contraction Engine)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1418112Z       The TCE module provides high-order CC methods not available in most codes:
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1418558Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1418723Z       tce
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1419108Z         ccsdtq    # CCSD with full triples and quadruples
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1419452Z         2eorb     # Two-electron orbital
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1419759Z         io ga     # Use Global Arrays for distributed storage
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1420050Z       end
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1420227Z       task tce energy
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1420416Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1420579Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1420756Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1421159Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/nwchem/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1421699Z       # NWChem — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1421905Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1422081Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1422296Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1423008Z       NWChem is primarily used for large-scale molecular and periodic QC calculations on HPC systems, particularly when other codes lack the parallelization required for the system size or method needed.
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1423791Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1423963Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1424165Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1424349Z       ### Large-Scale DFT on Metal Clusters
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1424597Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1425094Z       NWChem's parallel scalability makes it suitable for large transition metal clusters (> 100 atoms) where serial codes would be too slow:
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1425729Z       - Catalytic nanoparticles (Pd, Pt, Au clusters)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1426160Z       - Metalloenzyme active sites with QM region of 100–300 atoms
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1426525Z       - Reaction pathways on nanoparticle surfaces
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1426800Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1427209Z       ### Environmental Chemistry: Uranium Speciation
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1427490Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1427717Z       PNNL's original use case includes actinide chemistry:
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1428056Z       - Uranium(VI) aquo complexes in groundwater
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1428394Z       - Speciation of Pu and Np in different oxidation states
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1428802Z       - Actinide spin-orbit effects via scalar relativistic DFT (ZORA, DK)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1429434Z       - F-element chemistry requiring large-core pseudopotentials
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1429755Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1430107Z       ### High-Order Coupled Cluster Benchmarks
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1430368Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1430692Z       The TCE module enables CCSDT, CCSDTQ, and beyond:
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1431029Z       - Benchmarking datasets for heats of formation
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1431410Z       - Testing approximate CC methods against full triples/quadruples
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1431868Z       - HEAT (High-accuracy Extrapolated Ab Initio Thermochemistry) protocol
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1432226Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1432425Z       ### Periodic DFT with Car-Parrinello MD
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1432679Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1432861Z       NWPW module for condensed phase:
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1433214Z       - Water structure and dynamics under ambient and extreme conditions
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1433625Z       - Mineral surface reactions (actinide adsorption)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1433970Z       - Liquid-solid interfaces relevant to geochemistry
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1434253Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1434439Z       ### Biomolecular QM/MM
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1434656Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1434864Z       NWChem's QM/MM interface connects to AMBER:
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1435165Z       - Enzyme catalysis with large QM region
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1435490Z       - Protein-ligand binding with electronic polarization
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1435851Z       - Photoreceptor proteins (rhodopsin, photolyase)
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1436133Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1436317Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1436549Z       
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1436801Z       - **Environmental science**: Actinide and heavy metal speciation
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1437223Z       - **Catalysis**: Homogeneous and heterogeneous reaction mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1437919Z       - **Atmospheric chemistry**: Radical chemistry, aerosol formation
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1438455Z       - **Biochemistry**: Metalloenzyme mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1438801Z       - **Materials science**: Periodic DFT for inorganic solids
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1439405Z       - **Benchmark thermochemistry**: Reference data for method development
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1439759Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:15:48.1439935Z

2026-02-26T02:17:17.4831032Z       # CP2K
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4831354Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4831861Z       **Category:** DFT, Ab Initio Molecular Dynamics, Molecular Simulation  
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4833003Z       **Developer/Organization:** CP2K Developers Group (University of Zurich, IBM Research, ETHZ, and others)  
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4833947Z       **License:** GNU General Public License v2+  
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4834433Z       **Website:** https://www.cp2k.org  
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4834899Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k  
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4835305Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4835573Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4835880Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4838785Z       CP2K is an open-source quantum chemistry and solid-state physics software package for atomistic simulations. It is particularly renowned for its efficient implementation of the Gaussian and Plane Waves (GPW) method in the QUICKSTEP module, which enables density functional theory calculations of large periodic systems at a fraction of the cost of traditional plane-wave codes. This makes CP2K the tool of choice for ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) of systems with hundreds to thousands of atoms.
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4842228Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4844471Z       The GPW approach combines the efficiency of Gaussian basis functions for the wavefunction representation with the convenience of a plane-wave auxiliary basis for the electron density. This dual representation enables linear-scaling DFT (using sparse matrix algebra) for large systems and efficient calculation of the Hartree energy via 3D FFTs.
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4846742Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4848711Z       CP2K supports a wide range of methods: DFT (including hybrids and meta-GGAs), Hartree-Fock, MP2, RPA, periodic coupled cluster (pCCSD), semi-empirical methods (PM6, XTB), classical force fields, and QM/MM. This versatility makes it a central tool for computational materials science, condensed phase chemistry, and biophysics.
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4851051Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4851353Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4851706Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4852348Z       - **GPW method (QUICKSTEP)**: Gaussian + plane-wave DFT; efficient for large periodic systems
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4853879Z       - **AIMD (Born-Oppenheimer MD)**: DFT-based molecular dynamics with various thermostats
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4854795Z       - **CP MD (Car-Parrinello)**: Extended Lagrangian AIMD via CP2K's CP module
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4855708Z       - **Linear scaling DFT**: ALMO (absolutely localized MOs), density matrix-based O(N) methods
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4856691Z       - **Hybrid DFT**: PBE0, HSE06, screened exchange; efficient via truncated Coulomb operator
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4860637Z       - **HF-X (exact exchange)**: RI-HFX approach for efficient exact exchange in periodic systems
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4861641Z       - **MP2 and RPA**: Periodic post-HF for accurate correlation energies
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4862577Z       - **Semi-empirical**: PM6, XTB (GFN1-xTB, GFN2-xTB) via integration with xTB
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4884588Z       - **DFTB**: Density functional tight-binding (DFTB, DFTB+D3)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4885328Z       - **Force fields**: AMBER, CHARMM, classical MD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4886054Z       - **QM/MM**: Gaussian expansion of electrostatic potential QM/MM scheme
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4886930Z       - **Free energy methods**: Metadynamics, thermodynamic integration, via PLUMED
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4887717Z       - **NEB**: Nudged elastic band for minimum energy paths
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4888180Z       - **Phonons**: Via finite-differences or interface with PHONOPY
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4888669Z       - **ML potentials**: Interface with DeePMD, NequIP for machine learning MD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4889326Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4889528Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4889733Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4889900Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4890093Z       # Install via conda
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4890334Z       conda install -c conda-forge cp2k
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4890591Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4890903Z       # Or compile from source (requires MKL/OpenBLAS, FFTW, libxc, LIBINT2, LIBXSMM)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4891362Z       git clone https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k.git
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4891703Z       cd cp2k && make -j 8 ARCH=local VERSION="ssmp"
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4891989Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4892186Z       # Run a DFT single point (water.inp):
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4892517Z       mpirun -np 4 cp2k.popt -i water.inp -o water.out
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4892803Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4892972Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4893145Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4893348Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4894041Z       - [Quantum ESPRESSO](../../tools/quantum-espresso/index.md) – Plane-wave DFT, complementary for phonon calculations
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4894700Z       - [VASP](../../tools/vasp/index.md) – Commercial plane-wave DFT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4895318Z       - [GROMACS](../../tools/gromacs/index.md) – Classical MD; CP2K handles QM/MM with GROMACS (CP2K-GROMACS)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4895931Z       - [LAMMPS](../../tools/lammps/index.md) – Classical and ML potential MD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4896459Z       - [VMD](../../tools/vmd/index.md) – Visualization of CP2K AIMD trajectories
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4896822Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4897006Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4897406Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/cp2k/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4897935Z       # CP2K — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4898178Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4898357Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4898578Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4899189Z       The core of CP2K is the **Gaussian and Plane Waves (GPW)** method, implemented in the QUICKSTEP module. In GPW:
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4899697Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4900096Z       1. **Molecular orbitals** are expanded in atom-centered Gaussian basis functions (like molecular QC codes)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4900751Z       2. **Electron density** is represented on a regular real-space grid (plane-wave auxiliary basis)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4901310Z       3. The **Hartree energy** is computed efficiently via 3D FFT on the density grid
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4902140Z       4. The **exchange-correlation** energy is evaluated on the real-space grid
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4902710Z       5. The **kinetic energy** and **nuclear attraction** are computed analytically from Gaussian integrals
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4903149Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4903330Z       This separation allows:
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4903639Z       - Efficient Hartree energy computation: O(N log N) via FFT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4904045Z       - Accurate kinetic and overlap integrals from Gaussian basis
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4904542Z       - Linear scaling via sparse matrix operations (DBCSR library) when using localized Gaussians
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4904968Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4905517Z       The **GAPW (Gaussian Augmented Plane Waves)** extension adds an all-electron correction in the core region for accurate description of core properties.
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4906125Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4906321Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4906576Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4906844Z       - **QUICKSTEP**: GPW DFT with Gaussian + plane-wave representation
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4907252Z       - **GAPW**: All-electron GPW augmented with core correction
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4907912Z       - **OT (Orbital Transformation)**: Direct minimization of KS energy functional; avoids explicit diagonalization; very efficient for insulators
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4909218Z       - **ALMO (Absolutely Localized MOs)**: Linear scaling via spatial localization
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4910269Z       - **DBCSR (Distributed Block Compressed Sparse Row)**: Parallel sparse matrix library for O(N) methods
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4911286Z       - **RI-HFX**: Resolution-of-identity exact exchange for hybrid DFT in periodic boundary conditions
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4912113Z       - **ADMM (Auxiliary Density Matrix Method)**: Approximate RI-like scheme for exchange in periodic hybrid DFT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4912696Z       - **MP2 / RPA**: Periodic post-HF methods for correlation (RI-MP2, dRPA)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4913142Z       - **CP-MD**: Car-Parrinello extended Lagrangian AIMD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4913442Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4913628Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4913848Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4914035Z       - **Language**: Fortran 2008 + C/C++
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4914425Z       - **MPI + OpenMP**: Hybrid parallelization; efficient on multi-node HPC clusters
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4914924Z       - **CUDA/HIP**: GPU acceleration for some modules (DBCSR, grid operations)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4915312Z       - **LIBINT2**: Gaussian integral library
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4915681Z       - **LIBXC**: Exchange-correlation functional library (200+ functionals)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4916130Z       - **LIBXSMM**: SIMD-optimized small matrix multiplication (Intel)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4916548Z       - **FFTW3 / Intel MKL FFT**: 3D FFTs for plane-wave density grid
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4917010Z       - **DBCSR library**: Standalone sparse matrix multiplication for large-scale DFT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4917394Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4917581Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4917812Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4918009Z       CP2K scales to thousands of MPI tasks:
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4918341Z       - Tested on 100,000+ cores on BlueGene/Q and Cray systems
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4918730Z       - GPW DFT scales near-linearly for large systems (N > 200 atoms)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4919338Z       - Efficient AIMD: ~1 fs/step for 1000-atom water box on 512 cores
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4919735Z       - GPU acceleration via CUDA for grid operations and DBCSR
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4920042Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4920222Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4920450Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4920669Z       - **LIBINT2**: Gaussian electron repulsion integrals
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4921020Z       - **LIBXSMM**: Fast small-matrix multiplication kernels
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4921326Z       - **LIBXC**: XC functionals
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4921722Z       - **FFTW3**: FFT library
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4922092Z       - **ELPA**: Eigenvalue solver for large matrices
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4922416Z       - **PLUMED**: Enhanced sampling and metadynamics
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4922740Z       - **i-PI**: Path integral MD via socket interface
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4923113Z       - **DeePMD-kit / NequIP**: Machine learning potential interface
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4923489Z       - **ASE**: Python interface for CP2K calculations
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4923805Z       - **AiiDA-CP2K**: Workflow automation plugin
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4924077Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4924256Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4924648Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/cp2k/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4925210Z       # CP2K — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4925450Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4925625Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4925834Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4926016Z       ### DFT (QUICKSTEP)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4926253Z       - LDA: LDA_X + LDA_C_PW (PW92), VWN
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4926575Z       - GGA: PBE, PBEsol, BLYP, BP86, OLYP, revPBE, RPBE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4926908Z       - meta-GGA: TPSS, M06-L, SCAN, r2SCAN (via libxc)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4927230Z       - Hybrid: PBE0, HSE06, B3LYP (via RI-HFX or ADMM)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4927542Z       - Double-hybrid: B2PLYP (DFT + RI-MP2)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4927835Z       - DFT-D3, DFT-D4, DFT-D3BJ dispersion
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4928167Z       - DFT+U: Dudarev formulation for correlated d/f electrons
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4928530Z       - vdW-DF, rVV10 non-local correlation functionals
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4928809Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4929199Z       ### Post-HF (Periodic)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4929559Z       - RI-MP2: Resolution-of-identity MP2 for periodic systems
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4929965Z       - dRPA: Direct random-phase approximation correlation energy
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4930311Z       - Laplace-transform SOS-MP2
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4930608Z       - pCC: Periodic coupled cluster (experimental)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4930887Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4931087Z       ### Semi-Empirical and Tight-Binding
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4931358Z       - PM6 (via MOPAC interface)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4931600Z       - DFTB, DFTB+D3, DFTB+U
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4931855Z       - GFN1-xTB, GFN2-xTB (via xTB interface)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4932117Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4932295Z       ### Force Field MD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4932523Z       - AMBER, CHARMM, OPLS force fields
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4932827Z       - Classical MD with all CP2K thermostats/barostats
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4933108Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4933272Z       ### QM/MM
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4933539Z       - GEEP (Gaussian expansion of electrostatic potential) QM/MM
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4934046Z       - Coupling to MM via link atoms or capping
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4934454Z       - Periodic boundary condition QM/MM
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4934869Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4935160Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4935512Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4935877Z       CP2K uses a hierarchical keyword/section input format:
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4936178Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4936339Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4936507Z       &GLOBAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4936692Z         PROJECT water_md
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4936898Z         RUN_TYPE MD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4937091Z         PRINT_LEVEL LOW
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4937295Z       &END GLOBAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4937475Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4937639Z       &FORCE_EVAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4937829Z         METHOD Quickstep
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4938030Z         &DFT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4938232Z           BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME BASIS_MOLOPT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4938511Z           POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME GTH_POTENTIALS
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4938764Z           &QS
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4939162Z             EPS_DEFAULT 1.0E-12
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4939398Z           &END QS
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4939578Z           &MGRID
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4939926Z             CUTOFF 400
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4940134Z             REL_CUTOFF 60
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4940475Z           &END MGRID
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4940667Z           &XC
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4940857Z             &XC_FUNCTIONAL PBE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4941091Z             &END XC_FUNCTIONAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4941311Z             &VDW_POTENTIAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4941556Z               DISPERSION_FUNCTIONAL PAIR_POTENTIAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4941837Z               &PAIR_POTENTIAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4942060Z                 TYPE DFTD3
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4942297Z                 PARAMETER_FILE_NAME dftd3.dat
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4942579Z                 REFERENCE_FUNCTIONAL PBE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4942835Z               &END PAIR_POTENTIAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4943077Z             &END VDW_POTENTIAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4943301Z           &END XC
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4943495Z         &END DFT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4943685Z         &SUBSYS
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4943862Z           &CELL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4944049Z             ABC 12.0 12.0 12.0
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4944269Z           &END CELL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4944454Z           &COORD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4944648Z             O  0.000  0.000  0.000
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4944879Z             H  0.000  0.757  0.587
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4945103Z             H  0.000 -0.757  0.587
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4945325Z           &END COORD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4945520Z           &KIND O
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4945732Z             BASIS_SET DZVP-MOLOPT-SR-GTH
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4946006Z             POTENTIAL GTH-PBE-q6
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4946241Z           &END KIND
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4946425Z           &KIND H
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4946631Z             BASIS_SET DZVP-MOLOPT-SR-GTH
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4946888Z             POTENTIAL GTH-PBE-q1
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4947113Z           &END KIND
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4947296Z         &END SUBSYS
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4947490Z       &END FORCE_EVAL
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4947685Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4947850Z       &MOTION
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4948030Z         &MD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4948209Z           ENSEMBLE NVT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4948419Z           STEPS 10000
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4948621Z           TIMESTEP 0.5
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4948828Z           TEMPERATURE 300
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4949226Z           &THERMOSTAT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4949423Z             TYPE NOSE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4949610Z             &NOSE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4949797Z               TIMECON 100
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4950000Z             &END NOSE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4950193Z           &END THERMOSTAT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4950396Z         &END MD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4950575Z       &END MOTION
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4950755Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4950927Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4951103Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4951303Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4951480Z       ### AIMD of Liquid Water
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4951861Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4952422Z       1. Equilibrate with classical force field (TIP4P) to get reasonable starting structure
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4952970Z       2. Pre-equilibrate with DFTB for 5–10 ps
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4953367Z       3. Production AIMD with PBE-D3 or BLYP-D3 for 20–50 ps
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4953798Z       4. Analysis: RDF, diffusion coefficient, hydrogen-bond dynamics
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4954129Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4954315Z       ### Free Energy with Metadynamics
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4954564Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4954724Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4954889Z       &MOTION
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4955067Z         &FREE_ENERGY
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4955263Z           &METADYN
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4955458Z             DO_HILLS .TRUE.
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4955678Z             NT_HILLS 100
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4955922Z             WW 0.001           # Hill height (Hartree)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4956192Z             &METAVAR
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4956386Z               SCALE 0.1
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4956587Z               COLVAR 1
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4956785Z             &END METAVAR
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4957151Z           &END METADYN
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4957348Z         &END FREE_ENERGY
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4957547Z       &END MOTION
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4957832Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4958003Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4958272Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4958486Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4958672Z       ### Machine Learning Potential AIMD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4959613Z       CP2K integrates with DeePMD-kit for ML potential-driven MD at DFT cost after training. Generate training data with short AIMD runs, train DeePMD model, then run long MD at classical speed.
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4960357Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4960542Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4960941Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/cp2k/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4961465Z       # CP2K — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4961665Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4961845Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4962064Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4963204Z       CP2K's strength is AIMD of large condensed-phase systems — liquid water, ionic liquids, zeolites, solid-liquid interfaces, and biomolecular systems. Its linear-scaling GPW approach enables DFT-quality simulations that are impractical with traditional plane-wave codes.
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4964257Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4964432Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4964645Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4964837Z       ### Liquid Water Structure and Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4965095Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4965381Z       CP2K is the community standard for ab initio simulations of liquid water:
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4965845Z       - Radial distribution functions of water at ambient conditions
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4966302Z       - Effect of XC functional on water structure (PBE, BLYP, PBE0, revPBE-D3)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4966744Z       - Proton transfer and hydronium/hydroxide solvation dynamics
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4967221Z       - Isotope effects (H₂O vs D₂O) on hydrogen-bond structure
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4967548Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4967727Z       ### Zeolite Catalysis
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4967945Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4968205Z       Methanol-to-Hydrocarbon (MTH) and other zeolite reactions:
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4968647Z       - Brønsted acid site proton transfer mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4969194Z       - Carbenium ion intermediates in zeolite cavities
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4969622Z       - Van der Waals host-guest interactions (DFT-D3 essential)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4970121Z       - Large unit cell zeolites (Si₁₉₂O₃₈₄) tractable with linear-scaling DFT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4970474Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4970692Z       ### Electrochemistry and Solid-Liquid Interfaces
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4971119Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4971585Z       Electrode-electrolyte interfaces for batteries and electrocatalysis:
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4972042Z       - Li/Na ion solvation and desolvation at electrode surfaces
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4972447Z       - H₂O dissociation on metal oxide surfaces
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4972829Z       - CO₂ reduction intermediates on Cu(100) in water
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4973158Z       - Electric double layer structure from AIMD
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4973428Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4973628Z       ### Protein-Ligand Interactions (QM/MM)
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4973884Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4974065Z       Large-scale QM/MM with CP2K:
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4974350Z       - Enzyme active site in full protein environment
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4974708Z       - QM region: 50–200 atoms treated with DFT
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4975029Z       - MM region: rest of protein in AMBER force field
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4975359Z       - Free energy perturbation for binding affinity
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4975636Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4975828Z       ### Battery Electrolyte Decomposition
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4976081Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4976355Z       Solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) formation in Li-ion batteries:
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4976770Z       - Reductive decomposition of EC, DMC, LiPF6 on graphite
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4977390Z       - Li₂CO₃ and LiF SEI layer formation mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4977813Z       - AIMD of electrolyte at electrode surface
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4978082Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4978272Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4978512Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4978775Z       - **Energy storage**: Li/Na-ion batteries, solid-state electrolytes
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4979532Z       - **Heterogeneous catalysis**: Zeolites, metal-organic frameworks, oxide surfaces
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4980095Z       - **Electrochemistry**: CO₂ reduction, water splitting, corrosion
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4980487Z       - **Biochemistry**: Enzyme QM/MM, protein dynamics
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4980845Z       - **Materials science**: Amorphous oxides, glass, ceramics
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4981239Z       - **Atmospheric chemistry**: Aerosol nucleation, ice surfaces
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4981562Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4981750Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4982153Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/cp2k/references.md << 'ENDOFFILE'

2026-02-26T02:17:17.4982902Z       
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4983079Z       ## Primary Citations
2026-02-26T02:17:17.4983286Z

2026-02-26T02:17:17.4984375Z          Kühne, T. D. et al. (2020). CP2K: An electronic structure and molecular dynamics software package — Quickstep: Efficient and accurate electronic structure calculations. *J. Chem. Phys.* 152, 194103.

2026-02-26T02:18:39.1178762Z       # Psi4
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1179316Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1179659Z       **Category:** Quantum Chemistry  
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1180839Z       **Developer/Organization:** Psi4 Developers (David Sherrill group, Georgia Tech; Daniel Crawford group, Virginia Tech; and community)  
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1182083Z       **License:** GNU Lesser General Public License v3 (LGPL)  
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1182641Z       **Website:** https://psicode.org  
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1183111Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/psi4/psi4  
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1183522Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1183792Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1184104Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1186233Z       Psi4 is a modern, open-source quantum chemistry package with an exceptionally clean Python API that makes it ideal for scripting, automation, and integration with other software. Originally derived from PSI3, Psi4 was rewritten with a modular architecture that supports both interactive Python scripting (via `psi4` Python module) and traditional input files.
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1188490Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1192224Z       Psi4's strength lies in its comprehensive implementation of wavefunction-based methods: MP2, CCSD(T), EOM-CC, SAPT (symmetry-adapted perturbation theory), and multireference methods. The SAPT module is particularly notable — Psi4 is considered the reference implementation for SAPT and its variants, making it the go-to code for non-covalent interaction analysis and intermolecular force field development.
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1194875Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1196859Z       The PSI4NumPy project bridges Psi4's infrastructure with NumPy, enabling rapid prototyping of new quantum chemistry methods entirely in Python. Psi4 also integrates with many external codes: CFOUR, MRCC, Grimme's xtb, and v2rdm-casscf.
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1198686Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1199235Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1199563Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1200054Z       - **Python API**: Full Psi4 functionality accessible as a Python library
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1200799Z       - **CCSD(T) and higher**: Canonical and density-fitted CC methods
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1201597Z       - **SAPT**: Symmetry-adapted perturbation theory for non-covalent interactions
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1202408Z       - **DF-MP2 / RI-MP2**: Density-fitted MP2; fast and memory-efficient
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1203124Z       - **DFT**: LDA, GGA, meta-GGA, hybrid, range-separated, double-hybrid
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1203726Z       - **TDDFT**: Excited states, UV/Vis spectra
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1204352Z       - **EOM-CC**: Excited states, ionization potentials, electron affinities
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1205091Z       - **ADC**: Algebraic diagrammatic construction for excited states
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1205703Z       - **MCSCF/CASSCF**: Multi-reference methods
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1206166Z       - **CI**: FCI, DETCI, CIS, CISD
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1206805Z       - **Geometry optimization**: Optking (internal coordinates), NEB via geomeTRIC
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1207646Z       - **Properties**: Dipole, multipoles, NMR shielding, polarizabilities
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1208335Z       - **Dispersion**: DFT-D3, DFT-D4, -XDM, NL via VV10
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1209590Z       - **PSI4NumPy**: Python reference implementations for education and development
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1210612Z       - **External code drivers**: CFOUR, MRCC, CheMPS2 interfaces
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1211179Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1211469Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1211784Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1212055Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1212366Z       # Install via conda
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1212766Z       conda install psi4 -c conda-forge
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1213197Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1213507Z       # Python API usage:
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1213874Z       import psi4
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1214174Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1214470Z       psi4.set_memory('4 GB')
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1214864Z       psi4.set_num_threads(4)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1215233Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1215526Z       mol = psi4.geometry("""
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1215894Z       0 1
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1216192Z       O  0.000  0.000  0.119
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1216545Z       H  0.000  0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1216912Z       H  0.000 -0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1217266Z       """)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1217555Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1217876Z       # DFT optimization
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1218375Z       psi4.set_options({'basis': 'def2-TZVP', 'scf_type': 'df'})
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1219258Z       energy, wfn = psi4.optimize('b3lyp-d3bj', return_wfn=True)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1219782Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1220086Z       # CCSD(T) single point
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1220521Z       psi4.set_options({'basis': 'aug-cc-pVTZ'})
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1221026Z       e_ccsd_t = psi4.energy('ccsd(t)')
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1221435Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1221708Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1221987Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1222326Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1223064Z       - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – Free academic; strong DLPNO-CC and spectroscopy
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1224087Z       - [NWChem](../../tools/nwchem/index.md) – Open-source; MPI-parallel HPC focus
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1225148Z       - [Gaussian](../../tools/gaussian/index.md) – Commercial; comprehensive, good NMR/VPT2
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1226134Z       - [GAMESS](../../tools/gamess/index.md) – Free academic; EFP, FMO
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1227159Z       - [RDKit](../../tools/rdkit/index.md) – Cheminformatics; complement Psi4 for virtual screening
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1227912Z       ENDOFFILE
