2026-02-26T02:18:39.1228899Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/psi4/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1230038Z       # Psi4 — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1230461Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1230772Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1231123Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1232748Z       Psi4 uses Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis functions and implements the full hierarchy of wavefunction-based methods. A central design philosophy is clean, modular code that separates integral computation, wavefunction drivers, and property evaluation.
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1234539Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1235059Z       The density fitting (DF/RI) approximation is pervasive throughout Psi4:
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1235895Z       - DF-HF: Approximate Coulomb and exchange integrals with auxiliary basis
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1236675Z       - DF-MP2: O(N^4) instead of O(N^5) with minimal accuracy loss
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1237457Z       - DF-CCSD: Faster coupled cluster with density-fitted two-electron integrals
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1238101Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1238428Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1238933Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1239771Z       - **Optking**: Geometry optimization in redundant internal coordinates; handles transition states
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1240812Z       - **geomeTRIC**: Modern optimizer (separate package) used as default in Psi4 1.5+
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1242222Z       - **SAPT**: Energy decomposition into electrostatics, exchange, induction, dispersion at multiple levels (SAPT0, SAPT2, SAPT2+, SAPT2+(3), SAPT2+3, SAPT2+(CCD))
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1243717Z       - **DFMP2**: Density-fitted MP2 with analytic gradients
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1244521Z       - **CCENERGY**: Canonical CCSD, CCSD(T), BCCD, BCCD(T)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1245110Z       - **EOM-CC**: EOM-CCSD for EE, IP, EA, DEA, DIP
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1245681Z       - **DETCI**: Determinantal CI; FCI, RAS-CI, CAS-CI
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1246266Z       - **ADCman**: ADC(2), ADC(2)-x, ADC(3) for excited states
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1246779Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1247091Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1247475Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1247854Z       - **Language**: C++ (core) + Python (driver and API)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1248769Z       - **Python module**: Import as `import psi4` — full Psi4 functionality in Python
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1249824Z       - **MPI**: Not standard (Psi4 uses shared-memory parallelism primarily)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1250594Z       - **OpenMP**: Multi-threaded integrals and tensor contractions
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1251269Z       - **Libint2**: Gaussian electron repulsion integrals
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1251901Z       - **libJK**: Coulomb/exchange matrix construction
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1252440Z       - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Dense linear algebra
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1253100Z       - **PSI4NumPy**: Python prototyping layer using NumPy for matrix operations
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1253733Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1254052Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1254455Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1254901Z       Psi4 is optimized for single-node shared-memory performance:
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1255742Z       - Excellent multi-core performance with OpenMP (16–64 cores)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1256471Z       - DF-MP2 and DF-CCSD scale well to large systems (> 50 heavy atoms)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1257273Z       - No MPI: not designed for multi-node; use NWChem or ORCA for HPC cluster jobs
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1257912Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1258217Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1258624Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1258933Z       - **Libint2**: Two-electron integrals
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1259618Z       - **libxc**: External XC functional library
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1260144Z       - **geomeTRIC**: Modern geometry optimizer
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1260750Z       - **CFOUR**: High-accuracy CC; Psi4 can call CFOUR as backend
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1261363Z       - **MRCC**: Arbitrary-order CC via MRCC interface
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1261890Z       - **CheMPS2**: DMRG for large active spaces
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1262454Z       - **v2rdm-casscf**: Variational 2-RDM CASSCF
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1263131Z       - **dftd3 / dftd4**: Grimme dispersion correction programs
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1263684Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1263983Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1264673Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/psi4/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1265640Z       # Psi4 — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1266059Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1266374Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1266731Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1267001Z       ### DFT
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1267421Z       - LDA, GGA (PBE, BLYP, BP86), meta-GGA (TPSS, SCAN)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1268243Z       - Hybrid (B3LYP, PBE0), range-separated (CAM-B3LYP, ωB97X-D, LC-ωPBE)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1269154Z       - Double-hybrid (B2PLYP, B2GP-PLYP, DSD-PBEP86)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1269755Z       - DFT-D3, DFT-D4, -XDM, VV10 non-local correlation
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1270247Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1270552Z       ### Wavefunction Methods
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1270967Z       - HF (RHF, UHF, ROHF), DF-HF
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1271424Z       - MP2, DF-MP2, SOS-MP2, SCS-MP2, OMP2
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1271899Z       - MP3, MP2.5 (interpolation)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1272332Z       - CCSD, CCSD(T), CCSD(T)-F12
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1272774Z       - BCCD (Brueckner CC), BCCD(T)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1273245Z       - OMP3, OOMP2 (orbital-optimized MP)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1273679Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1274286Z       ### SAPT (Symmetry-Adapted Perturbation Theory)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1274811Z       - SAPT0 (fastest, HF monomers)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1275558Z       - SAPT2, SAPT2+, SAPT2+(3), SAPT2+3
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1276110Z       - SAPT2+(CCD): uses CCD amplitudes for dispersion
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1276715Z       - δMP2 correction
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1277159Z       - F-SAPT: Functional group SAPT for decomposition
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1277737Z       - L-SAPT: Linking-SAPT for large complexes
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1278215Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1278533Z       ### Multi-Reference and CI
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1279242Z       - CASSCF, FCI, CISD, MRCISD (via DETCI)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1279718Z       - RASSCF, RASCI
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1280096Z       - DMRG-CASSCF (via CheMPS2)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1280517Z       - v2rdm-CASSCF
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1280849Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1281146Z       ### Excited States
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1281509Z       - TDDFT (full and TDA)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1281936Z       - EOM-CCSD (EE, IP, EA, DEA, DIP)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1282378Z       - ADC(2), ADC(2)-x, ADC(3)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1282776Z       - CIS, CIS(D)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1283094Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1283388Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1283772Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1284093Z       ### Python API (preferred)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1284496Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1284800Z       import psi4
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1285113Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1285423Z       psi4.set_memory('8 GB')
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1285826Z       psi4.set_num_threads(8)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1286205Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1286554Z       # SAPT0 interaction energy of water dimer
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1287054Z       dimer = psi4.geometry("""
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1287445Z       0 1
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1287744Z       O  -1.551  0.000  0.000
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1288122Z       H  -1.934  0.900  0.000
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1288496Z       H  -1.934 -0.900  0.000
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1288849Z       --
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1289307Z       0 1
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1289601Z       O   1.370  0.000  0.000
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1289977Z       H   1.752  0.900  0.000
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1290337Z       H   1.752 -0.900  0.000
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1290696Z       """)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1290984Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1291345Z       psi4.set_options({'basis': 'jun-cc-pVDZ'})
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1291841Z       e_sapt = psi4.energy('sapt0')
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1292386Z       print(f"SAPT0 interaction: {e_sapt * 627.51:.2f} kcal/mol")
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1292918Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1293194Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1293478Z       ### Traditional Input
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1293877Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1294200Z       memory 4 gb
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1294533Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1294831Z       molecule h2o {
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1295156Z         0 1
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1295462Z         O  0.000  0.000  0.119
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1295855Z         H  0.000  0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1296238Z         H  0.000 -0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1296614Z       }
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1296892Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1297178Z       set {
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1297485Z         basis cc-pVTZ
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1297833Z         scf_type df
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1298152Z       }
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1298428Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1298716Z       energy('ccsd(t)')
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1299243Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1299532Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1299826Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1300190Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1300492Z       ### CBS Extrapolation
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1300859Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1301171Z       import psi4
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1301480Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1301802Z       # Complete basis set extrapolation
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1302250Z       e_cbs = psi4.cbs('mp2',
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1302769Z                         corl_basis='aug-cc-pV[DT]Z',    # DZ+TZ extrapolation
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1303449Z                         corl_scheme=psi4.driver_cbs.corl_xtpl_helgaker_2)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1304220Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1304503Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1304994Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1305361Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1305685Z       ### PSI4NumPy for Method Development
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1306119Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1306428Z       import psi4
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1306764Z       import numpy as np
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1307165Z       from psi4.core import MintsHelper
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1307604Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1308052Z       # Access integrals as NumPy arrays for custom implementations
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1308660Z       mol = psi4.geometry("...")
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1309413Z       basis = psi4.core.BasisSet.build(mol, 'ORBITAL', 'sto-3g')
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1310001Z       mints = MintsHelper(basis)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1310487Z       S = np.array(mints.ao_overlap())  # Overlap matrix
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1311098Z       T = np.array(mints.ao_kinetic())  # Kinetic energy matrix
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1311640Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1311920Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1312216Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1312926Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/psi4/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1313869Z       # Psi4 — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1314219Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1314521Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1314907Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1316251Z       Psi4 is used for non-covalent interaction analysis (SAPT), high-accuracy thermochemistry benchmarks, method development and prototyping, and automated computational chemistry workflows via its Python API.
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1317730Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1318031Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1318392Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1318777Z       ### Non-Covalent Interaction Decomposition with SAPT
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1319459Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1319863Z       Psi4's SAPT implementation is the community reference:
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1320927Z       - Water dimer, benzene dimer, DNA base pairs: decompose interaction into electrostatics, exchange, induction, dispersion
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1322146Z       - Develop improved non-covalent force fields (AMOEBA, CHARMM Drude) from SAPT data
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1322924Z       - Study halogen bonds, pnicogen bonds, chalcogen bonds
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1323607Z       - Benchmark DFT-D methods against high-level SAPT reference
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1324192Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1324505Z       ### Benchmark Thermochemistry
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1324904Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1325298Z       High-accuracy CBS extrapolation for small molecules:
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1326097Z       - MP2/aug-cc-pV[DT]Z → ∞ extrapolation + CCSD(T) correction
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1326745Z       - Heats of formation for W4-11 and GMTKN55 datasets
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1327344Z       - BDE (bond dissociation energies) with UCCSD(T)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1327955Z       - Reaction barrier heights (DBH24, HTBH38 datasets)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1328460Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1328806Z       ### Automated Virtual Screening with Python
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1329494Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1329775Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1330091Z       import psi4
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1330435Z       from rdkit import Chem
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1330837Z       from rdkit.Chem import AllChem
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1331244Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1331603Z       # Batch conformer optimization + energy ranking
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1332158Z       smiles_list = ['CC(=O)O', 'CC#N', 'c1ccccc1O']
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1332656Z       for smi in smiles_list:
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1333065Z           mol = Chem.MolFromSmiles(smi)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1333589Z           AllChem.EmbedMolecule(mol, AllChem.ETKDGv3())
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1334214Z           # Convert to psi4 geometry and optimize at B3LYP/6-31G*
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1334750Z           ...
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1335041Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1347296Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1347917Z       ### Method Development with PSI4NumPy
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1348388Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1349406Z       Psi4's NumPy interface is used for teaching and developing new methods:
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1350222Z       - Implement a new correlation functional prototype
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1350805Z       - Test new integral approximations
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1351307Z       - Rapid development of SAPT extensions
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1351762Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1352096Z       ### Excited-State Benchmark Studies
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1352535Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1352946Z       ADC vs TDDFT vs EOM-CCSD for benchmark excited states:
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1353643Z       - Thiel's benchmark set (28 organic molecules, 104 excitations)
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1354354Z       - Compare oscillator strengths and excitation energies
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1355091Z       - Charge-transfer state failures of TDDFT identified and quantified
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1355704Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1356022Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1356452Z       
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1356960Z       - **Drug discovery**: Non-covalent interaction analysis, fragment binding
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1357737Z       - **Force field development**: SAPT-based parameterization
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1358516Z       - **Physical organic chemistry**: Reaction barriers, substituent effects
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1359489Z       - **Atmospheric chemistry**: Molecular cluster binding energies
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1360216Z       - **Method development**: Academic research in new QC methods
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1360982Z       - **Education**: Python API ideal for teaching computational chemistry
2026-02-26T02:18:39.1361608Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:19:53.4315577Z       # MOPAC
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4315918Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4316345Z       **Category:** Semi-Empirical Quantum Chemistry  
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4317177Z       **Developer/Organization:** James J. P. Stewart (Stewart Computational Chemistry)  
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4318044Z       **License:** LGPL (MOPAC2016 and later versions free)  
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4318645Z       **Website:** http://openmopac.net  
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4319447Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/openmopac/mopac  
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4319965Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4320271Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4320592Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4323198Z       MOPAC (Molecular Orbital PACkage) is a semi-empirical quantum chemistry program created by James Stewart and collaborators. It uses semi-empirical Hamiltonians that parameterize the computationally expensive electron repulsion integrals against experimental data, making it orders of magnitude faster than ab initio methods while retaining quantum-mechanical description of electronic structure.
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4325965Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4327868Z       The current flagship methods are PM7 (published 2013) and PM6, which incorporate improvements to hydrogen-bond descriptions, dispersion interactions, and solid-state properties. MOPAC2016, released with a free LGPL license, made MOPAC widely accessible for both academic and commercial use.
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4340550Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4342683Z       MOPAC is particularly valuable for: (1) rapid pre-screening of large numbers of molecules, (2) initial geometry optimization before higher-level calculations, (3) property calculations for very large molecules (proteins, polymers, crystals) where ab initio DFT would be prohibitively expensive, and (4) parameterization studies.
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4344883Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4345194Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4345519Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4350888Z       - **PM7**: Latest general-purpose semi-empirical method; good for organics, transition metals, and solid state
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4352315Z       - **PM6**: Predecessor to PM7; well-validated for organic molecules; PM6-DH2 and PM6-D3H4 include dispersion/H-bond corrections
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4353573Z       - **PM3, AM1, MNDO**: Legacy semi-empirical methods; still used in some specialized workflows
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4354554Z       - **RM1**: Re-parameterized AM1 for organic molecules with improved accuracy
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4355399Z       - **Geometry optimization**: BFGS and EF (eigenvector following) optimizers
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4356336Z       - **Vibrational analysis**: IR frequencies and intensities at semi-empirical level
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4357415Z       - **Solvent**: COSMO and SM8 implicit solvation
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4358271Z       - **Electronic spectra**: CI singles for excited states
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4359310Z       - **Solid-state**: PM7 solid-state (PM7-SS) for crystals and polymers; periodic boundary conditions
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4360348Z       - **Protein calculations**: Linear scaling via MOZYME for proteins (> 10,000 atoms)
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4361225Z       - **Thermochemistry**: Heats of formation via experimental parameterization
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4361812Z       - **NMR**: Chemical shift prediction at PM6/PM7 level
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4362118Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4362295Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4362578Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4362781Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4363037Z       # Install MOPAC from http://openmopac.net or GitHub
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4363489Z       # Create input file (benzene.mop):
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4363744Z       # PM7 OPT
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4363942Z       # Benzene optimization
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4364333Z       # 
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4364515Z       # C   0.000  1  0.000  1  0.000  1
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4364801Z       # ... (Cartesian coordinates or Z-matrix)
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4365203Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4365527Z       # Run MOPAC
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4365860Z       mopac benzene.mop
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4366197Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4366478Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4366787Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4367058Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4367892Z       - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – Higher accuracy DFT/CC; next step up from PM7
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4369198Z       - [Gaussian](../../tools/gaussian/index.md) – Full ab initio; MOPAC output can seed Gaussian jobs
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4369873Z       - [Open Babel](../../tools/open-babel/index.md) – Convert MOPAC input/output formats
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4370455Z       - [Avogadro](../../tools/avogadro/index.md) – GUI for building MOPAC input files
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4370845Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4371024Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4371443Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/mopac/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4371987Z       # MOPAC — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4372227Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4372403Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4372620Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4373875Z       MOPAC implements neglect of diatomic differential overlap (NDDO) semi-empirical Hamiltonians. In NDDO, three- and four-center two-electron integrals are neglected, and remaining two-center integrals are approximated using multipole expansions. The remaining parameters are fit to experimental heats of formation, geometries, and ionization potentials.
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4375182Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4375354Z       The total energy is:
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4375569Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4375731Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4375942Z       E_total = E_electronic + E_nuclear_repulsion
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4376219Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4376380Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4377132Z       where E_nuclear_repulsion includes the core-core repulsion that compensates for the neglected integrals. The electronic energy uses the self-consistent Roothaan equations with the approximate NDDO integrals.
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4377945Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4378127Z       ### PM7 Improvements Over PM6
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4378371Z       PM7 includes:
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4378625Z       - Improved dispersion (D/H correction from PM6-DH2)
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4379118Z       - Better treatment of hydrogen bonds
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4379510Z       - Re-parameterized for 70 elements including many transition metals
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4379938Z       - Improved solid-state and crystal property predictions
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4380237Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4380428Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4380672Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4380947Z       - **BFGS minimizer**: Quasi-Newton optimization for molecular geometry
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4381771Z       - **EF (Eigenvector Following)**: TS optimization following a single negative eigenvalue
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4382335Z       - **MOZYME**: Linear-scaling algorithm for large proteins; O(N) cost via localized MOs
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4382826Z       - **COSMO**: Conductor-like screening model for implicit solvation
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4383357Z       - **CI (Configuration Interaction)**: CIS and CISD for excited states and ground state of open-shell
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4383882Z       - **Periodic boundary conditions**: For crystalline materials
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4384199Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4384378Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4384604Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4384777Z       - **Language**: Fortran
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4385171Z       - **Single-core**: Primarily single-threaded (MOPAC is fast enough for most applications)
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4385658Z       - **OpenMP**: Limited parallel support in recent versions
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4386040Z       - **Simple binary**: Download and run; minimal dependencies
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4386363Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4386546Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4386776Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4386985Z       MOPAC is extremely fast compared to ab initio:
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4387386Z       - Typical molecule (< 50 atoms): milliseconds to seconds for optimization
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4387804Z       - Drug-sized molecule (100 atoms): seconds to minutes
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4388163Z       - Protein (1000+ atoms): MOZYME enables minutes to hours
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4388520Z       - Crystal: Periodic PM7-SS in minutes for unit cells
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4388803Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4389176Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4389420Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4389661Z       - **openbabel**: File format conversion to/from MOPAC format
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4390071Z       - **RDKit**: Generate initial 3D coordinates for MOPAC optimization
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4390458Z       - **ASE**: Python interface for MOPAC calculations
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4390889Z       - **AMS (Amsterdam Modeling Suite)**: Commercial suite includes MOPAC methods via DFTB
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4391290Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4391467Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4391858Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/mopac/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4392399Z       # MOPAC — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4392640Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4392816Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4393021Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4393202Z       ### Semi-Empirical Hamiltonians
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4393452Z       | Method | Year | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4393680Z       |--------|------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4393929Z       | MNDO | 1977 | Original NDDO; poor H-bonds |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4394267Z       | AM1 | 1985 | Improved H-bonds; widely used for organics |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4394616Z       | PM3 | 1989 | Better geometry than AM1 |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4394932Z       | RM1 | 2006 | Re-parameterized AM1; good for CHNO |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4395254Z       | PM6 | 2007 | Current workhorse; 70 elements |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4395591Z       | PM6-DH2 | 2010 | PM6 + dispersion + H-bond correction |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4395943Z       | PM6-D3H4 | 2012 | PM6 + DFT-D3 + H-bond correction v4 |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4396276Z       | PM7 | 2013 | Latest general method; 70 elements |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4396632Z       | PM7-TS | 2013 | PM7 optimized for transition state energies |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4396942Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4397123Z       ### Electronic Structure Options
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4397423Z       - Ground state: RHF (closed shell), UHF (open shell)
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4397748Z       - Excited states: CI singles (CIS), direct CI
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4398035Z       - Solvent: COSMO, SM5.4, SM8
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4398266Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4398445Z       ### Property Calculations
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4398870Z       - Heat of formation (ΔHf) in kcal/mol
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4399515Z       - Geometry (bond lengths, angles, dihedrals)
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4399838Z       - Vibrational frequencies and IR intensities
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4400119Z       - HOMO and LUMO energies
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4400365Z       - Dipole moment, polarizability
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4400631Z       - Partial charges: Mulliken, COSMO
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4400890Z       - NMR chemical shifts
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4401100Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4401276Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4401496Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4401669Z       ### MOPAC Input (.mop)
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4401910Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4402089Z       PM7 OPT BONDS DIPOLE
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4402321Z       Aspirin optimization with PM7
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4402563Z       Title line
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4402744Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4402913Z       C    0.000  1  0.000  1  0.000  1
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4403159Z       O    1.200  1  0.000  0  0.000  0
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4403383Z       ...
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4403542Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4403708Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4403880Z       ### Key Keywords
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4404088Z       | Keyword | Function |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4404306Z       |---------|----------|
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4404534Z       | `PM7` | Use PM7 Hamiltonian |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4404785Z       | `OPT` | Optimize geometry |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4405031Z       | `FREQ` | Vibrational analysis |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4405300Z       | `THERMO` | Thermodynamic properties |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4405583Z       | `COSMO` | Implicit COSMO solvation |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4405847Z       | `BONDS` | Print bond orders |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4406145Z       | `MOZYME` | Linear-scaling for large systems |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4406481Z       | `GRAPHF` | Generate formatted output for GUIs |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4406809Z       | `1SCF` | Single-point (no optimization) |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4407094Z       | `TS` | Transition state search |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4407486Z       | `IRC` | Intrinsic reaction coordinate |
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4407916Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4408219Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4408580Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4408800Z       ### Pre-optimization for DFT
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4409322Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4409493Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4409748Z       # 1. Generate 3D coordinates from SMILES (via Open Babel)
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4410454Z       obabel -:"CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O" --gen3D -omop > aspirin.mop
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4410965Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4411263Z       # 2. Add PM7 keyword to first line
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4411707Z       sed -i '1s/.*/PM7 OPT/' aspirin.mop
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4412125Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4412309Z       # 3. Run MOPAC
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4412514Z       mopac aspirin.mop
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4412715Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4412925Z       # 4. Convert optimized geometry to Gaussian input
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4413249Z       obabel aspirin.out -ogjf > aspirin.gjf
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4413501Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4413662Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4413843Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4414045Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4414215Z       ### MOZYME for Proteins
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4414428Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4414585Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4414763Z       PM7 MOZYME BONDS DIPOLE
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4415002Z       Lysozyme single point calculation
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4415253Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4415435Z       [protein coordinates from PDB...]
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4415672Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4415837Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4416474Z       MOZYME achieves near-linear scaling by expressing the density matrix in terms of localized molecular orbitals (LMOs), making PM7 calculations on 10,000-atom proteins routine.
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4417162Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4417348Z       ### Crystal Structure Optimization
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4417591Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4417923Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4418101Z       PM7-SS UNIT_CELL OPT
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4418520Z       Crystal structure of aspirin
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4418755Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4419211Z       CELL a=11.43 b=6.59 c=11.40 alpha=90.0 beta=95.7 gamma=90.0
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4419527Z       SYMMETRY=C2/c
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4419738Z       [fractional coordinates...]
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4419970Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4420147Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4420326Z

2026-02-26T02:19:53.4421285Z       # MOPAC — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4421495Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4421675Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4421889Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4422728Z       MOPAC is used for rapid property estimation, initial geometry generation, and large-molecule electronic structure calculations where DFT is too expensive. It is particularly valuable in virtual screening and cheminformatics pipelines.
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4423641Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4423817Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4424020Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4424221Z       ### Pre-screening Large Compound Libraries
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4424482Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4424829Z       In virtual screening, MOPAC provides fast QM-level properties for thousands of molecules:
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4425369Z       - PM7 geometry optimization as pre-processing step before docking
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4425781Z       - HOMO/LUMO energies as chemical reactivity descriptors
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4426141Z       - Heats of formation for stability screening
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4426539Z       - Typical throughput: 100,000 molecules per day on a single workstation
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4426899Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4427082Z       ### Protein Structure Refinement
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4427320Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4427562Z       MOZYME enables QM-level calculations on entire proteins:
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4427981Z       - Refine crystal structures from X-ray diffraction data using PM7
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4428452Z       - Compute partial charges for all atoms from population analysis
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4428880Z       - Study protonation states of histidine, aspartate, glutamate
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4429494Z       - Enzyme active site geometry without MM approximations
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4429797Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4429993Z       ### Initial Geometry for DFT Calculations
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4430251Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4430441Z       MOPAC serves as a fast pre-optimizer:
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4430780Z       - Generate 3D coordinates from SMILES and minimize with PM7
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4431196Z       - 100× faster than DFT geometry optimization
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4431582Z       - PM7-optimized geometry close enough for DFT to converge quickly
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4432017Z       - Standard practice in automated high-throughput QC workflows
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4432342Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4432522Z       ### Transition State Estimation
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4432758Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4432995Z       PM7 transition states provide initial guesses for DFT:
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4433397Z       - Scan potential energy surface along reaction coordinate with PM7
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4433759Z       - Identify approximate TS geometry
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4434056Z       - Use as starting point for DFT TS optimization
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4434450Z       - Useful for organic reactions where PM7 qualitatively captures the TS
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4434806Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4435007Z       ### Solid-State Pharmaceutical Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4435261Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4435487Z       PM7 periodic calculations for polymorphism studies:
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4435857Z       - Compare lattice energies of different crystal polymorphs
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4436190Z       - Compute sublimation enthalpies
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4436470Z       - Study hydrogen-bond networks in crystals
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4436940Z       - Relevant for pharmaceutical solid form selection
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4437320Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4437502Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4437734Z       
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4438037Z       - **Drug discovery**: Virtual screening pre-processing, ADMET property estimation
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4438496Z       - **Cheminformatics**: Feature generation for ML models
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4438912Z       - **Materials science**: Rapid polymer and solid-state property estimation
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4439470Z       - **Chemical education**: Fast, accessible QM for teaching
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4439891Z       - **Biochemistry**: Large-molecule property calculations (proteins, DNA)
2026-02-26T02:19:53.4440248Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:20:50.4026901Z       # Octopus
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4027250Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4027707Z       **Category:** TDDFT, Real-Space DFT, Strong-Field Physics  
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4028916Z       **Developer/Organization:** Octopus developers group (formerly ICN2 Barcelona, MPI Hamburg, MPI Halle, and others)  
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4030190Z       **License:** GNU General Public License v2+  
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4030779Z       **Website:** https://octopus-code.org  
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4031371Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/octopus-code/octopus  
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4031897Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4032118Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4032321Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4034109Z       Octopus is an open-source real-space DFT and time-dependent DFT (TDDFT) code that simulates the quantum mechanical behavior of electrons in atoms, molecules, and nanosystems. Unlike most DFT codes that use plane-wave or Gaussian basis sets, Octopus represents wavefunctions and densities on a real-space grid, which provides natural advantages for time-domain simulations, strong-field physics, and non-equilibrium electron dynamics.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4036485Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4039298Z       Octopus's real-space, real-time TDDFT implementation allows direct simulation of electron dynamics driven by external laser fields with femtosecond to attosecond time scales. This makes it uniquely suited for: ultrafast spectroscopy, high-harmonic generation (HHG), above-threshold ionization, and attosecond science. It also supports photoabsorption spectra via linear-response TDDFT in the frequency domain.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4041955Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4042571Z       The code supports GPU acceleration (CUDA, OpenCL) and runs efficiently on modern HPC architectures.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4043330Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4043616Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4044005Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4044658Z       - **Real-space, real-time TDDFT**: Direct time propagation of electron density under external fields
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4045734Z       - **Ground-state DFT**: LDA, GGA, meta-GGA, hybrid functionals on real-space grid
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4046582Z       - **Linear-response TDDFT**: Photoabsorption spectra, optical excitations
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4047627Z       - **Strong-field physics**: High-harmonic generation (HHG), above-threshold ionization (ATI)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4048672Z       - **Non-equilibrium dynamics**: Laser-induced electron and nuclear dynamics
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4049832Z       - **Berry phase**: Polarization, anomalous Hall conductivity
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4050498Z       - **Floquet theory**: Periodic driving fields
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4050943Z       - **Time-propagation algorithms**: Magnus, Lanczos, exponential Runge-Kutta
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4051605Z       - **Norm-conserving pseudopotentials**: Troullier-Martins, ONCV
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4052172Z       - **Periodic systems**: Bulk, surface, wire geometries
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4052571Z       - **Magnons**: Spin-wave spectra via time-dependent spin DFT
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4053227Z       - **GPU acceleration**: CUDA and OpenCL for grid operations
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4053843Z       - **Python API**: `oct-pythonize` for scripting
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4054302Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4054589Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4055220Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4055504Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4055851Z       # Install from conda or compile from source
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4056554Z       conda install -c conda-forge octopus
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4056969Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4057259Z       # Create input file (inp):
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4057647Z       # CalculationMode = gs
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4058032Z       # XYZCoordinates = "h2o.xyz"
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4058419Z       # FromScratch = yes
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4058786Z       # Spacing = 0.2*angstrom
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4059382Z       # Radius = 4.0*angstrom
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4059787Z       # XCFunctional = lda_x + lda_c_pz
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4060212Z       # Eigensolver = cg
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4060572Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4060769Z       # Run Octopus
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4060962Z       octopus
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4061133Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4061297Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4061464Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4061657Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4062393Z       - [Quantum ESPRESSO](../../tools/quantum-espresso/index.md) – Plane-wave DFT; complementary for solid-state ground state
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4063153Z       - [NWChem](../../tools/nwchem/index.md) – Gaussian basis TDDFT; complementary approach
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4063836Z       - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – Molecular TDDFT; different basis but more spectroscopy features
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4064636Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4064833Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4065245Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/octopus/technology.md << 'EOF'
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4065791Z       # Octopus — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4066037Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4066217Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4066435Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4066738Z       Octopus solves the time-dependent Kohn-Sham (TDKS) equations in real space:
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4067109Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4067268Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4067516Z       i∂ψ_j(r,t)/∂t = H_KS[n(t)] ψ_j(r,t)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4067765Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4067926Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4068614Z       where H_KS = -½∇² + v_ext(r,t) + v_Hartree[n](r,t) + v_xc[n](r,t). The electron density n(r,t) = Σ_j |ψ_j(r,t)|² drives the self-consistent Hamiltonian at each time step.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4069544Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4070414Z       Wavefunctions and densities are represented on a real-space grid. The Laplacian is approximated by finite differences (typically 7-point stencil) or by fast Fourier transform on a uniform grid. Pseudopotentials handle the core electrons.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4071328Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4071514Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4071762Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4072184Z       - **Time propagation**: Magnus exponential midpoint, Lanczos propagator, exponential midpoint, Runge-Kutta (RK4)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4072781Z       - **Diagonalization**: Conjugate gradient (CG), Davidson, RMMDIIS
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4073224Z       - **Poisson solver**: FFT-based; also supports multi-grid and ISF
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4073735Z       - **PAO (Parallel Adaptive Octree)**: Adaptive mesh refinement for atoms with steep features
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4074387Z       - **Absorbing boundaries**: Complex absorbing potential (CAP), PML (perfectly matched layer) for ionization
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4075182Z       - **Berry phase**: Geometric phases for polarization in periodic systems
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4075631Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4075814Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4076032Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4076212Z       - **Language**: Fortran 90 + C
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4076586Z       - **MPI + OpenMP**: Hybrid parallel; grid points distributed across MPI tasks
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4077048Z       - **CUDA/OpenCL**: GPU acceleration for FFT and grid operations
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4077467Z       - **PFFT**: Parallel FFT for distributed-memory grid computations
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4078023Z       - **libxc**: External XC functional library
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4078479Z       - **libvdwxc**: Non-local vdW correlation for real-space codes
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4078799Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4079219Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4079596Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4079851Z       - Scales to thousands of MPI tasks for large simulation boxes
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4080217Z       - GPU acceleration for FFT-intensive steps
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4080653Z       - Real-space grids: efficient for finite systems, requires large boxes for molecules
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4081048Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4081213Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4081587Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/octopus/methods.md << 'EOF'
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4082132Z       # Octopus — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4082376Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4082551Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4082760Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4083054Z       - Ground-state DFT: LDA, GGA (PBE), meta-GGA, hybrid (via OEP or perturbative)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4083550Z       - Adiabatic LDA/GGA time-dependent XC
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4084077Z       - Real-time TDDFT (full non-linear regime)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4084547Z       - Linear-response TDDFT (Casida equations or Sternheimer)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4084905Z       - Spin-DFT: collinear and non-collinear spin
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4085214Z       - DFT+U for correlated systems (periodic)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4085605Z       - Exact exchange (OEP — optimized effective potential)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4085900Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4086067Z       ## Input/Output
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4086259Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4086538Z       Octopus uses a free-format input file named `inp` with key=value pairs:
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4086893Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4087052Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4087331Z       CalculationMode = td           # Time-dependent (td) or ground state (gs)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4087700Z       ExperimentalFeatures = yes
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4087962Z       XYZCoordinates = "benzene.xyz"
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4088217Z       Spacing = 0.18*angstrom
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4088445Z       Radius = 6.0*angstrom
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4088678Z       XCFunctional = lda_x + lda_c_pz
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4089246Z       TDPropagator = aetrs            # 4th-order Taylor expansion
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4089583Z       TDTimeStep = 0.003
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4089800Z       TDMaxSteps = 2000
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4090075Z       TDExternalFields = laser_field  # External laser field
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4090373Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4090537Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4090710Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4090918Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4091100Z       ### Optical Absorption Spectrum
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4091335Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4091493Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4091669Z       # Step 1: Ground state
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4091895Z       CalculationMode = gs
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4092144Z       # Step 2: Apply delta kick and propagate
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4092426Z       CalculationMode = td
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4092672Z       TDDeltaKickStrength = 0.01*atomic_units
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4093008Z       # Step 3: Fourier transform dipole moment to get spectrum
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4093316Z       octopus_spectrum
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4093513Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4093676Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4093923Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4094256Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4094540Z       ### High-Harmonic Generation
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4094854Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4095499Z       Apply a long laser pulse and compute the emitted HHG spectrum by Fourier transforming the time-dependent dipole acceleration. Octopus is a standard tool for attosecond science.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4096204Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4096369Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4096755Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/octopus/use-cases.md << 'EOF'
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4097431Z       # Octopus — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4097641Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4097818Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4098149Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4098779Z       Octopus is used for time-domain electron dynamics, attosecond science, and non-equilibrium phenomena that cannot be captured by ground-state or frequency-domain methods.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4099880Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4100056Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4100262Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4100469Z       ### Optical Absorption Spectra via RT-TDDFT
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4100734Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4101480Z       Apply a broadband delta-kick perturbation and propagate TDKS equations for ~20 fs. Fourier transform the time-dependent dipole moment to obtain the full optical absorption spectrum in one calculation.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4102260Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4102446Z       ### High-Harmonic Generation (HHG)
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4102692Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4103506Z       Simulate an atom or molecule in an intense IR laser field (10¹⁴–10¹⁵ W/cm²). Track electron ionization and recombination to compute HHG spectrum — a key process in attosecond science.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4104229Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4104410Z       ### Above-Threshold Ionization
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4104799Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4105758Z       Compute photoelectron spectra of atoms in strong laser fields. Extract ionization rates and above-threshold ionization peaks for comparison with experimental COLTRIMS data.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4106461Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4106647Z       ### Laser-Induced Demagnetization
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4106892Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4107374Z       Study ultrafast demagnetization in transition metals (Fe, Ni, Co) after femtosecond laser excitation using non-collinear spin TDDFT.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4107923Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4108126Z       ### Non-Equilibrium Dynamics in 2D Materials
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4108389Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4109118Z       Study electron-phonon coupling and carrier thermalization in graphene, MoS2 after optical excitation using coupled electron-nuclear RT-TDDFT.
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4109919Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4110098Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4110330Z       
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4110662Z       - **Attosecond science**: HHG, above-threshold ionization, electron wavepacket dynamics
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4111199Z       - **Ultrafast spectroscopy**: Transient absorption, pump-probe simulations
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4111640Z       - **Photovoltaics**: Exciton dynamics, carrier generation
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4112054Z       - **Nanophotonics**: Optical properties of quantum dots, nanoclusters
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4112501Z       - **Spintronics**: Laser-induced demagnetization, spin transport
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4112834Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:20:50.4112998Z

2026-02-26T02:21:54.0356605Z       # GAMESS
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0357330Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0357737Z       **Category:** Quantum Chemistry  
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0358760Z       **Developer/Organization:** Mark Gordon research group, Iowa State University / Ames Laboratory  
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0360144Z       **License:** Free for academic and commercial use (requires registration)  
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0360717Z       **Website:** https://www.msg.ameslab.gov/gamess/  
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0361231Z       **Download:** https://www.msg.ameslab.gov/gamess/License_Agreement.html  
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0361686Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0361891Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0362120Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0364571Z       GAMESS (General Atomic and Molecular Electronic Structure System) is a comprehensive quantum chemistry package developed primarily by the Gordon research group at Iowa State University. Available since the 1980s, GAMESS implements an extensive range of quantum chemical methods and has particular strengths in EFP (Effective Fragment Potential) methods, Fragment Molecular Orbital (FMO) theory, solvation, and multireference methods.
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0367627Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0369902Z       GAMESS is freely available for academic and commercial use after registration and is widely used in academic research. It runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows, and supports MPI parallelization. The code has a strong tradition of implementing novel methods developed in the Gordon group, including EFP, EFP2, FMO, and various novel DFT approaches.
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0372061Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0374443Z       A unique strength of GAMESS is the Fragment Molecular Orbital (FMO) method, which enables accurate quantum chemical calculations on large molecular systems (proteins, DNA, molecular crystals) by dividing the system into overlapping fragments and solving each fragment's QC problem independently, then assembling the total energy and properties from fragment contributions.
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0376893Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0377200Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0377531Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0378111Z       - **DFT**: Broad functional library via libxc; LDA, GGA, meta-GGA, hybrid, range-separated
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0379417Z       - **HF and post-HF**: MP2, MP3, CCSD, CCSD(T), EOM-CCSD
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0380341Z       - **MCSCF/CASSCF**: Multi-reference with analytical gradients; FORS-MCSCF
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0381076Z       - **MRPT2**: Multi-reference perturbation theory (MRPT2, MRPT3)
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0381767Z       - **CI**: ALDET, ORMAS, GUGA-CI; general CI with various reference spaces
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0382678Z       - **EFP (Effective Fragment Potential)**: Semi-classical solvent model based on ab initio calculations
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0383692Z       - **EFP2**: Improved EFP with charge transfer, dispersion, exchange-repulsion
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0384478Z       - **FMO (Fragment Molecular Orbital)**: Linear-scaling for large systems; FMO2, FMO3
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0384980Z       - **TDDFT**: Linear-response for excited states and spectra
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0385378Z       - **RI-MP2 / RI-CC**: Density fitting for efficient MP2 and CC
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0385734Z       - **DFT-D3**: Grimme dispersion correction
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0386064Z       - **NMR**: Chemical shift calculations (GIAO)
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0386398Z       - **Solvation**: EFP, PCM (C-PCM, IEF-PCM), SMD
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0386777Z       - **Relativistic**: ZORA, Douglas-Kroll-Hess for heavy elements
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0387195Z       - **Periodic**: GAMPERI for periodic boundary conditions
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0387500Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0387673Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0387866Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0388028Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0388290Z        $CONTRL SCFTYP=RHF RUNTYP=ENERGY MAXIT=30 MULT=1 ICHARG=0 $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0388652Z        $SYSTEM TIMLIM=99999 MEMORY=125000000 $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0389213Z        $BASIS  GBASIS=N31 NGAUSS=6 NDFUNC=1 $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0389588Z        $GUESS  GUESS=HUCKEL $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0389820Z        $DATA
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0390009Z       Water single point
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0390218Z       C1
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0390397Z       O    8.0  0.000  0.000  0.119
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0390641Z       H    1.0  0.000  0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0390873Z       H    1.0  0.000 -0.758 -0.477
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0391089Z        $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0391258Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0391420Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0391594Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0391790Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0392344Z       - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – Free academic; strong spectroscopy, DLPNO-CC
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0392935Z       - [Psi4](../../tools/psi4/index.md) – Open-source Python-driven, SAPT
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0393428Z       - [NWChem](../../tools/nwchem/index.md) – Open-source, scalable HPC
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0393968Z       - [Gaussian](../../tools/gaussian/index.md) – Commercial; overlapping methods, ONIOM
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0394361Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0394544Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0394959Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/gamess/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0395520Z       # GAMESS — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0395766Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0395951Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0396174Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0396792Z       GAMESS uses Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis sets for all molecular calculations. Its distinctive features are the EFP and FMO methods, both developed by the Gordon group.
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0397485Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0398559Z       **EFP (Effective Fragment Potential)**: Replace solvent molecules with polarizable, non-covalent fragments that interact with the QM region through electrostatic, polarization, dispersion, and exchange-repulsion terms derived from ab initio calculations on isolated fragments. EFP2 adds charge transfer.
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0399933Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0401414Z       **FMO (Fragment Molecular Orbital)**: Divide large molecule into overlapping fragments, compute each fragment's wavefunction in the electrostatic field of all other fragments (using Mulliken charges initially), iterate to self-consistency, then compute pair, triple fragment interactions for total energy. FMO2 includes pair corrections; FMO3 includes trimer corrections.
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0402884Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0403074Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0403322Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0403647Z       - **GUGA-CI (Graphical Unitary Group Approach)**: Efficient CI via unitary group algebra
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0404145Z       - **ALDET/ORMAS**: Determinant-based CI for general references
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0404535Z       - **MCSCF with FORS**: Full Optimized Reaction Space MCSCF
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0405184Z       - **FMO parallelization**: Each fragment and fragment pair runs on separate MPI group
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0406055Z       - **GAMESS DFT**: Internal DFT implementation + libxc
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0406539Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0406723Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0406944Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0407133Z       - **Language**: Fortran 77/90
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0407834Z       - **MPI**: For multi-node parallel execution; DDI (Distributed Data Interface) communication layer
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0408862Z       - **DDI (Distributed Data Interface)**: GAMESS's own parallel communication library, similar to GA
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0409535Z       - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Dense linear algebra
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0409869Z       - **Limited OpenMP**: Some OpenMP in integral evaluation
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0410173Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0410354Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0410584Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0410839Z       - FMO can scale to thousands of cores for protein-sized systems
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0411279Z       - EFP solvent model allows large simulations without MM approximations
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0411815Z       - MP2 and CC scale to ~50–100 atoms efficiently on single node
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0412136Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0412316Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0412552Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0412733Z       - **libxc**: XC functional library
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0413072Z       - **FMO world**: Community of FMO users with dedicated tutorials
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0413497Z       - **Firefly (PC GAMESS)**: Separately maintained fork by Alex Granovsky
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0413912Z       - **MacroModel**: Some interfaces for hybrid calculations
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0414215Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0414395Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0414796Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/gamess/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0415338Z       # GAMESS — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0415577Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0415753Z       ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0415963Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0416133Z       ### SCF and DFT
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0416336Z       - RHF, ROHF, UHF
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0416683Z       - LDA, GGA (PBE, BLYP), meta-GGA, hybrid (B3LYP, PBE0), range-separated (CAM-B3LYP)
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0417087Z       - DFT-D3 dispersion
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0417295Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0417463Z       ### Post-HF
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0417654Z       - MP2, RI-MP2
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0417853Z       - CCSD, CCSD(T), EOM-CCSD
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0418092Z       - MCSCF (CASSCF, FORS-MCSCF)
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0418344Z       - MRPT2 (second-order MRPT)
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0418585Z       - GUGA-CI, ALDET, ORMAS
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0418808Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0419168Z       ### Fragment Methods
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0419502Z       - **EFP**: Effective Fragment Potential for solvation
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0419836Z       - **EFP2**: Enhanced EFP with charge transfer
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0420189Z       - **FMO2**: Fragment Molecular Orbital with 2-body corrections
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0420528Z       - **FMO3**: FMO with 3-body corrections
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0420834Z       - **ONIOM-like**: Multi-layer via FMO framework
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0421106Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0421444Z       ### Excited States
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0421670Z       - TDDFT (linear-response)
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0421999Z       - EOM-CCSD
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0422233Z       - MCSCF-CI for multi-reference excited states
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0422544Z       - CIS for single-excitation excited states
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0422804Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0422971Z       ## Input Format
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0423160Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0423409Z       GAMESS uses a card-based input with `$GROUP ... $END` blocks:
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0423727Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0423885Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0424121Z        $CONTRL SCFTYP=DFT RUNTYP=OPTIMIZE DFTTYP=B3LYP $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0424439Z        $BASIS  GBASIS=N31 NGAUSS=6 NDFUNC=1 $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0424755Z        $DFT    IDCVER=3 DC=.TRUE. $END   ! DFT-D3 correction
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0425055Z        $STATPT OPTTOL=0.0001 $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0425281Z        $DATA
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0425468Z       Aspirin optimization
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0425682Z       C1
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0425846Z       ...
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0426012Z        $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0426186Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0426348Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0426518Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0426727Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0426901Z       ### FMO Protein Calculation
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0427125Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0427285Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0427474Z        $CONTRL SCFTYP=RHF RUNTYP=ENERGY $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0427754Z        $FMO    NFRAG=200 NLAYER=2 NBODY=2 $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0428027Z        $FMOPRP NPRINT=9 MAXIT=100 $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0428265Z        $DATA
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0428457Z       Large protein FMO2 calculation
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0428693Z       ...
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0428855Z        $END
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0429275Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0429520Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0429699Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0430105Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/gamess/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0430652Z       # GAMESS — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0430860Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0431038Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0431260Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0431854Z       GAMESS is used for multi-reference calculations, large-system FMO calculations, EFP solvent modeling, and cases where its unique methods (EFP2, FMO) are required.
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0432514Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0432685Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0432890Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0433071Z       ### FMO Drug-Protein Interaction
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0433309Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0433569Z       Fragment Molecular Orbital calculations on drug-bound proteins:
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0434082Z       - Divide protein+ligand into amino acid fragments (~200–500 fragments)
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0434538Z       - FMO2/MP2 or FMO2/DFT to compute fragment pair interaction energies
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0434989Z       - Identify key binding residues and their contribution to binding energy
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0435487Z       - Used in drug optimization for EGFR, HIV protease, influenza neuraminidase
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0435848Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0436024Z       ### EFP Solvation Studies
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0436242Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0436424Z       Explicit quantum solvent via EFP:
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0436820Z       - Reaction in aqueous solution with 100–500 EFP water molecules
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0437197Z       - Better than implicit solvent; cheaper than full QM
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0437584Z       - Proton transfer reactions, nucleophilic substitutions in water
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0437915Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0438100Z       ### Multi-Reference Excited States
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0438348Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0438532Z       MCSCF + MRPT2 for photochemistry:
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0438855Z       - Conical intersection optimization for photodegradation
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0439495Z       - S₁/S₂ ordering in aromatic systems
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0439970Z       - Photoisomerization of retinal (Schiff base models)
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0440467Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0440654Z       ### ONIOM-like Zeolite Catalysis
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0440888Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0441069Z       Cluster model of zeolite with DFT:
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0441390Z       - Active site (T-site) treated with high-level DFT or MP2
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0441746Z       - Extended framework treated with lower method
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0442120Z       - Study Brønsted acid catalysis mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0442380Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0442557Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0442786Z       
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0443078Z       - **Drug discovery**: FMO for protein-ligand interaction energy decomposition
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0443503Z       - **Materials science**: Periodic DFT via GAMPERI
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0443893Z       - **Physical chemistry**: EFP solvation, reaction mechanisms in solution
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0444353Z       - **Atmospheric chemistry**: Cluster nucleation, aerosol formation
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0444770Z       - **Bioinorganic chemistry**: Multi-reference active sites
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0445083Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:21:54.0445261Z
