2026-02-26T02:23:21.7753589Z       # GROMACS
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7753909Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7754302Z       **Category:** Molecular Dynamics Simulation  
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7755417Z       **Developer/Organization:** GROMACS development team (KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Uppsala University, and community)  
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7756503Z       **License:** GNU Lesser General Public License v2.1 (LGPL)  
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7757068Z       **Website:** https://www.gromacs.org  
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7757587Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/gromacs/gromacs  
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7758054Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7758320Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7758608Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7761923Z       GROMACS (Groningen Machine for Chemical Simulations) is one of the most widely used molecular dynamics simulation packages in the world, originally developed at the University of Groningen. It is renowned for its exceptional computational performance — GROMACS contains extensive hand-optimized SIMD intrinsics, CUDA/OpenCL GPU kernels, and MPI parallelization that make it among the fastest MD codes available.
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7764722Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7766690Z       GROMACS excels at biomolecular simulations (proteins, lipids, nucleic acids) and small organic molecules using classical force fields. It implements AMBER, CHARMM, OPLS-AA, and GROMOS force field families, with the AMBER ff14SB and CHARMM36m force fields being the most popular for protein simulations today.
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7768741Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7770572Z       The GROMACS ecosystem is rich: it integrates with PLUMED for enhanced sampling, GROMACS-CP2K for QM/MM, and many analysis tools via the `gmx` command-line suite. The MDAnalysis and MDTraj Python libraries can read GROMACS trajectory formats for post-processing.
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7772274Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7772559Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7772877Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7773481Z       - **Performance**: Highly optimized SIMD, CUDA/OpenCL, MPI+OpenMP; among the fastest MD codes
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7774537Z       - **Force fields**: AMBER (ff14SB, ff99SB-ILDN, GAFF, GAFF2), CHARMM36, CHARMM36m, OPLS-AA, GROMOS
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7775427Z       - **Integrators**: Leapfrog, velocity Verlet, stochastic dynamics
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7776357Z       - **Thermostats**: Nosé-Hoover, v-rescale, Berendsen, Andersen
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7777213Z       - **Barostats**: Parrinello-Rahman, Berendsen, Monte Carlo barostat (MBAR)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7778024Z       - **Constraints**: LINCS (default, fast), SHAKE, SETTLE for water
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7778673Z       - **PME**: Smooth particle-mesh Ewald electrostatics
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7779577Z       - **Free energy**: Alchemical FEP, thermodynamic integration, BAR, MBAR
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7780454Z       - **Enhanced sampling**: Via PLUMED integration: metadynamics, REMD, umbrella sampling
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7781424Z       - **Analysis tools**: `gmx` suite: RMSD, RMSF, RDF, MSD, SASA, hydrogen bonds, and more
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7782472Z       - **Membrane simulations**: Virtual sites, elastic network models, coarse-grained via MARTINI
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7783305Z       - **QM/MM**: CP2K interface and ORCA interface
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7783960Z       - **Replica exchange MD (REMD)**: Temperature REMD, Hamiltonian REMD
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7784994Z       - **Periodic boundary conditions**: Cubic, rectangular, triclinic, truncated octahedron, rhombic dodecahedron
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7786242Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7786570Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7786919Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7787476Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7787809Z       # Install via conda
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7788211Z       conda install -c conda-forge gromacs
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7788642Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7789154Z       # Standard protein simulation workflow:
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7789657Z       # 1. Download PDB and clean structure
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7790121Z       # 2. Generate topology
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7790783Z       gmx pdb2gmx -f protein.pdb -o protein.gro -water spce -ff amber99sb-ildn
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7791408Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7791711Z       # 3. Create simulation box
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7792276Z       gmx editconf -f protein.gro -o box.gro -c -d 1.0 -bt cubic
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7792820Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7793106Z       # 4. Add solvent
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7793663Z       gmx solvate -cp box.gro -cs spc216.gro -o solvated.gro -p topol.top
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7794277Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7794577Z       # 5. Add ions
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7795078Z       gmx grompp -f ions.mdp -c solvated.gro -p topol.top -o ions.tpr
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7795914Z       gmx genion -s ions.tpr -o ionized.gro -p topol.top -pname NA -nname CL -neutral
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7796544Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7796829Z       # 6. Energy minimize
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7797351Z       gmx grompp -f em.mdp -c ionized.gro -p topol.top -o em.tpr
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7797967Z       gmx mdrun -v -deffnm em
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7798361Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7798659Z       # 7. NVT equilibration
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7799392Z       gmx grompp -f nvt.mdp -c em.gro -r em.gro -p topol.top -o nvt.tpr
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7800017Z       gmx mdrun -deffnm nvt
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7800371Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7800710Z       # 8. NPT equilibration, then production MD
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7801228Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7801512Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7801812Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7802147Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7802924Z       - [AMBER](../../tools/amber/index.md) – Competing biomolecular MD; strong GPU acceleration
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7804140Z       - [NAMD](../../tools/namd/index.md) – Biomolecular MD; Charm++ parallelism, VMD-integrated
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7805287Z       - [LAMMPS](../../tools/lammps/index.md) – Materials MD; reactive force fields, coarse-grained
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7806414Z       - [OpenMM](../../tools/openmm/index.md) – Python API MD; extensible, custom forces
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7807507Z       - [VMD](../../tools/vmd/index.md) – Visualization and analysis of GROMACS trajectories
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7808173Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7808506Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7809732Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/gromacs/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7810920Z       # GROMACS — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7811338Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7811702Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7812087Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7812877Z       GROMACS integrates Newton's equations of motion for a system of N particles interacting via a classical force field:
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7813762Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7814041Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7814430Z       F_i = -∇_i U(r_1,...,r_N)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7814792Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7815071Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7816692Z       The potential energy U consists of bonded (bonds, angles, dihedrals, impropers) and non-bonded (Lennard-Jones + Coulomb) terms. Non-bonded interactions are computed with a cutoff for short-range terms and smooth particle-mesh Ewald (PME) for long-range electrostatics.
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7818462Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7820070Z       **LINCS (Linear Constraint Solver)**: GROMACS's default constraint algorithm. LINear Constraint Solver is faster and more stable than SHAKE for protein systems; uses a matrix inversion-free approach, parallelizes well.
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7821675Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7823198Z       **PME (Particle-Mesh Ewald)**: GROMACS implements efficient PME for long-range electrostatics with O(N log N) scaling. The direct space sum is computed on CPU, while the reciprocal space FFT can be offloaded to GPU.
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7824648Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7824969Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7825404Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7826130Z       - **Leapfrog integrator**: Standard choice; velocity at t+½Δt, positions at t+Δt
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7826986Z       - **Velocity Verlet**: Positions and velocities at same time point
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7827837Z       - **Stochastic dynamics (SD/BD)**: Langevin dynamics with friction and random noise
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7828676Z       - **LINCS**: Linear constraint solver; implemented up to P=8 order
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7829676Z       - **SETTLE**: Analytical constraint solver for rigid 3-point water models
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7830615Z       - **v-rescale thermostat**: Velocity rescaling with stochastic term; correct NVT ensemble
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7831730Z       - **Nosé-Hoover thermostat**: Correct canonical ensemble; chain thermostat
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7832606Z       - **Parrinello-Rahman barostat**: Correct NPT ensemble; cell fluctuations
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7833497Z       - **PME for Lennard-Jones**: Optional smooth PME for vdW in addition to Coulomb
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7834368Z       - **Domain decomposition**: 3D spatial decomposition for MPI parallelism
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7834989Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7835302Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7835670Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7836230Z       - **Language**: C++ with extensive hand-coded SIMD (SSE2, AVX, AVX-512, ARM NEON, IBM VSX)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7837070Z       - **MPI**: Domain decomposition for multi-node simulations
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7837716Z       - **OpenMP**: Thread-level parallelism within MPI domains
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7838467Z       - **CUDA**: GPU acceleration (NVIDIA); offload non-bonded force kernels, PME
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7839414Z       - **OpenCL**: GPU support for AMD/Intel GPUs
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7839983Z       - **SYCL**: Intel oneAPI GPU support (GROMACS 2021+)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7840532Z       - **hipSYCL**: AMD ROCm GPU support
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7840957Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7841276Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7841679Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7842190Z       GROMACS is consistently among the top-performing MD codes in benchmarks:
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7843074Z       - ~100 ns/day for 100k-atom systems on 4× NVIDIA A100 GPUs
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7843747Z       - PME GPU offloading dramatically improves GPU utilization
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7844459Z       - Scales to 10,000+ MPI tasks for very large systems (>1M atoms)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7845273Z       - Standard benchmarks: DHFR (24k atoms), apoA1 (92k atoms), cellulose (400k atoms)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7845939Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7846250Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7846661Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7847061Z       - **FFTW3 / Intel MKL / cuFFT**: FFT for PME
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7847588Z       - **CUDA toolkit**: NVIDIA GPU support
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7848203Z       - **PLUMED**: Enhanced sampling plugin (widely used with GROMACS)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7848809Z       - **CP2K**: QM/MM interface
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7849446Z       - **ORCA**: QM/MM interface for high-level QM
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7850172Z       - **MDAnalysis / MDTraj**: Python trajectory analysis reading GROMACS .xtc/.trr
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7850958Z       - **parmed**: Parameter and topology editing for GROMACS
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7851675Z       - **CHARMM-GUI**: Web-based system builder outputting GROMACS format
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7852283Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7852595Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7853304Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/gromacs/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7854565Z       # GROMACS — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7854994Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7855304Z       ## Supported Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7856006Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7856370Z       | Force Field | Best For | Parameters |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7856855Z       |------------|---------|-----------|
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7857479Z       | AMBER ff14SB | Proteins, nucleic acids | Reliable backbone/side-chain |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7858290Z       | AMBER ff19SB | Proteins with improved CMAP | Newer, recommended |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7859311Z       | GAFF/GAFF2 | Small organic molecules | Drug-like compounds |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7860053Z       | CHARMM36 | Proteins, lipids | Excellent lipid parameters |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7860877Z       | CHARMM36m | Intrinsically disordered proteins | Modified backbone torsions |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7861724Z       | OPLS-AA | Small molecules, proteins | Good for organic liquids |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7862413Z       | GROMOS 54A7 | Proteins | Free energy calculations |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7863158Z       | MARTINI 3 | Coarse-grained biomolecules | Large-scale/long-time MD |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7863772Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7864086Z       ## Simulation Protocols
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7864471Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7864778Z       ### Free Energy Calculations
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7865171Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7865447Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7865804Z       ; FEP parameters in mdp file
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7866227Z       free-energy = yes
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7866602Z       init-lambda-state = 0
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7867028Z       ; Lambda vector for coupling
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7867471Z       vdw-lambdas   = 0.00 0.05 0.10 ... 1.00
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7867943Z       coul-lambdas  = 0.00 0.25 0.50 0.75 1.00
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7868404Z       ; Soft-core potential
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7868786Z       sc-alpha = 0.5
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7869345Z       sc-power = 1
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7869667Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7869954Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7870277Z       ### Umbrella Sampling + WHAM
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7870680Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7870965Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7871382Z       # Generate configurations along reaction coordinate
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7872147Z       gmx distance -s reference.tpr -f traj.xtc -select "..." -oall dist.xvg
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7872763Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7873081Z       # Run umbrella sampling windows
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7873525Z       for i in $(seq 0 0.1 3.0); do
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7874264Z           gmx grompp -f umbrella.mdp -c conf_${i}.gro -r conf_${i}.gro -p topol.top -o umbrella_${i}.tpr
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7875130Z           gmx mdrun -s umbrella_${i}.tpr -deffnm umbrella_${i}
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7875650Z       done
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7875932Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7876209Z       # WHAM analysis
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7876768Z       gmx wham -it tpr-files.dat -if pullf-files.dat -o pmf.xvg -hist hist.xvg
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7877411Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7877696Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7878010Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7878419Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7878702Z       | File | Description |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7879247Z       |------|-------------|
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7879671Z       | `.mdp` | Molecular dynamics parameter file |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7880232Z       | `.top` | Topology (force field parameters) |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7880833Z       | `.gro` | Structure (Gromos format) |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7881393Z       | `.tpr` | Portable binary run input (compiled) |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7882163Z       | `.xtc` | Compressed trajectory (lossy, 3 decimal places) |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7882874Z       | `.trr` | Full precision trajectory + velocities + forces |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7883448Z       | `.edr` | Energy file (binary) |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7883875Z       | `.log` | Log file |
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7884240Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7884552Z       ## Analysis with `gmx` Tools
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7884946Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7885230Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7885782Z       # RMSD
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7886144Z       gmx rms -s md.tpr -f md.xtc -o rmsd.xvg
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7886758Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7887081Z       # RMSF (residue fluctuations)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7887590Z       gmx rmsf -s md.tpr -f md.xtc -res -o rmsf.xvg
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7888066Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7888364Z       # Radius of gyration
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7888809Z       gmx gyrate -s md.tpr -f md.xtc -o gyration.xvg
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7889514Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7889829Z       # Radial distribution function
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7890387Z       gmx rdf -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -ref "resname SOL and name OW" \
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7891004Z               -sel "protein and name CA" -o rdf.xvg
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7891452Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7891732Z       # Hydrogen bonds
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7892157Z       gmx hbond -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -num hbonds.xvg
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7892629Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7892973Z       # SASA (solvent-accessible surface area)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7893499Z       gmx sasa -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -o sasa.xvg
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7893936Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7894228Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7894528Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7894882Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7895187Z       ### REMD (Replica Exchange MD)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7895601Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7895871Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7896280Z       # Run REMD with 8 replicas at temperatures 300-400 K
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7896950Z       mpirun -np 8 gmx_mpi mdrun -multidir rep0 rep1 rep2 ... rep7 \
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7897569Z              -deffnm remd -replex 1000 -nex 100
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7898015Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7898302Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7898618Z       ### Alchemical Free Energy with MBAR
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7899208Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7899498Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7899818Z       import alchemlyb
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7900262Z       from alchemlyb.parsing.gmx import extract_u_nk
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7900896Z       # Parse GROMACS dH/dl output and compute MBAR estimate
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7901414Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7901716Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:23:21.7903727Z       # GROMACS — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7904093Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7904405Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7904791Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7906121Z       GROMACS is the most widely used MD code for biomolecular simulations. Its excellent performance and comprehensive analysis tools make it the standard choice for protein, membrane, and nucleic acid simulations.
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7907564Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7907856Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7908214Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7908575Z       ### Protein Folding and Conformational Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7909295Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7909889Z       Study conformational changes in enzymes, receptors, and disordered proteins:
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7910944Z       - Microsecond-scale MD of protein folding (via specialized hardware like Anton or GPU clusters)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7911857Z       - Identify open/closed conformations of kinases, GPCRs
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7912486Z       - Study allostery and signal transduction pathways
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7913257Z       - Compare with NMR S² order parameters and relaxation data
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7913816Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7914148Z       ### Membrane Protein Simulations
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7914566Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7914935Z       GROMACS + CHARMM-GUI for membrane simulations:
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7915680Z       - Embed GPCR, ion channel, or transporter in lipid bilayer (POPC, DPPC, mixed)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7916497Z       - Study lipid-protein interactions, cholesterol effects
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7917127Z       - Simulate drug permeation through membranes
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7917929Z       - Compute water permeability of aquaporins
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7918403Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7918904Z       ### Drug-Protein Binding Free Energies
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7919554Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7919987Z       Alchemical free energy perturbation (FEP) with GROMACS:
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7920699Z       - Relative binding free energy (RBFE) for lead optimization
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7921425Z       - Absolute binding free energy (ABFE) for affinity prediction
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7922289Z       - FEP+ protocol: 12 λ-windows per ligand pair, 5 ns each
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7923108Z       - Used by Schrödinger, AstraZeneca, Pfizer in drug discovery
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7923672Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7923992Z       ### Lipid Bilayer Properties
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7924409Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7924733Z       Pure and mixed lipid bilayers:
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7925291Z       - Area per lipid, bilayer thickness, lateral diffusion
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7925951Z       - Order parameter S_CD (compare with NMR experiments)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7926737Z       - Coarse-grained MARTINI simulations for large membranes (ms timescales)
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7927509Z       - Cholesterol lateral organization and raft formation
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7928005Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7928311Z       ### Polymer and Material Simulations
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7928771Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7929292Z       GROMACS is not just for biomolecules:
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7929822Z       - Polyethylene, polystyrene chain dynamics
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7930367Z       - Ionic liquids and electrolyte solutions
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7930937Z       - Self-assembly of surfactants and lipopolymers
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7931512Z       - Hydrogel swelling and polymer networks
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7931971Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7932276Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7932691Z       
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7933135Z       - **Pharmaceutical**: Drug-protein binding, ADMET, formulation
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7933847Z       - **Biophysics**: Protein mechanics, membrane physics
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7934553Z       - **Biochemistry**: Enzyme mechanism, protein-protein interactions
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7935319Z       - **Materials**: Polymer dynamics, electrolytes, soft matter
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7936133Z       - **Structural biology**: Complement cryo-EM and X-ray structures with dynamics
2026-02-26T02:23:21.7936813Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:24:44.4370323Z       # LAMMPS
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4370651Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4371094Z       **Category:** Molecular Dynamics, Materials Simulation  
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4372211Z       **Developer/Organization:** Sandia National Laboratories (Steve Plimpton and team), with broad community contributions  
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4373188Z       **License:** GNU General Public License v2 (GPL)  
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4373714Z       **Website:** https://www.lammps.org  
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4374226Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/lammps/lammps  
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4374675Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4374943Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4375246Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4377036Z       LAMMPS (Large-scale Atomic/Molecular Massively Parallel Simulator) is a highly extensible open-source molecular dynamics code developed primarily at Sandia National Laboratories by Steve Plimpton and colleagues. Its original 1995 paper is one of the most cited papers in computational science.
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4379174Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4382753Z       LAMMPS is distinguished by its exceptional breadth of interatomic potentials and force field support, making it the primary tool for materials science simulations. It supports simple Lennard-Jones potentials for noble gases through reactive force fields (ReaxFF) for chemical reactions, to embedded atom methods (EAM) for metals, to machine learning potentials (NequIP, DeePMD). This versatility makes LAMMPS the go-to code for simulating metals, ceramics, polymers, explosives, tribology, fracture mechanics, and more.
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4386180Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4387411Z       The code uses a spatial decomposition approach for MPI parallelization and supports GPU acceleration via CUDA for pair potentials, enabling simulations of billions of atoms on petascale machines.
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4388732Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4389269Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4389601Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4390109Z       - **Reactive force fields**: ReaxFF for bond breaking/forming; C/Si/O/N/H/metals
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4390990Z       - **EAM/FS potentials**: Embedded atom method for metals (Ag, Al, Au, Cu, Fe, Ni, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4391834Z       - **Tersoff/AIREBO**: Covalent materials (Si, C, SiC, graphene, CNTs)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4392719Z       - **Lennard-Jones, Buckingham**: Simple pair potentials for molecular liquids, noble gases
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4393613Z       - **AMBER/CHARMM/OPLS**: Biomolecular force fields for hybrid materials simulations
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4394367Z       - **MEAM**: Modified embedded atom method for alloys
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4395027Z       - **Stillinger-Weber**: Si, Ge, III-V semiconductors
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4395757Z       - **Machine learning potentials**: DeePMD, NequIP, ACE, GAP/SOAP, SNAP via MLIP
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4396589Z       - **QM/MM**: Interface with QUANTUM ESPRESSO and other QC codes via i-PI
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4397311Z       - **COUL/LONG**: Ewald, PPPM for long-range electrostatics
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4398031Z       - **GPU acceleration**: CUDA/OpenCL for pair potentials (via GPU package)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4398768Z       - **KOKKOS**: Performance portable package for CPU+GPU
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4399638Z       - **PLUMED**: Enhanced sampling plugin
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4400337Z       - **Shock physics**: Multi-scale shock method (MSST), rNEMD for thermal conductivity
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4401120Z       - **Granular**: Granular mechanics, contact models
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4401808Z       - **SPIN**: Spin dynamics (atomistic spin models + spin-lattice coupling)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4402637Z       - **Input scripting**: Powerful, flexible scripting language for complex setups
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4403304Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4403593Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4403921Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4404202Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4404514Z       # Install via conda
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4404915Z       conda install -c conda-forge lammps
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4405332Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4405625Z       # Or download and compile
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4406102Z       git clone https://github.com/lammps/lammps.git
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4406597Z       cd lammps/src && make mpi
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4406954Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4407273Z       # Example LAMMPS input script (in.lj):
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4407739Z       units lj
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4408061Z       atom_style atomic
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4408438Z       lattice fcc 0.8442
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4408835Z       region box block 0 10 0 10 0 10
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4409459Z       create_box 1 box
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4409811Z       create_atoms 1 box
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4410152Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4410428Z       mass 1 1.0
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4410752Z       pair_style lj/cut 2.5
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4411136Z       pair_coeff 1 1 1.0 1.0 2.5
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4411507Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4411802Z       velocity all create 1.44 87287
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4412205Z       fix 1 all nve
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4412522Z       thermo 100
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4412833Z       run 10000
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4413128Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4413401Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4413685Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4414012Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4415007Z       - [GROMACS](../../tools/gromacs/index.md) – Biomolecular MD; faster for classical protein simulations
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4416641Z       - [AMBER](../../tools/amber/index.md) – Biomolecular MD; excellent GPU support
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4417675Z       - [OpenMM](../../tools/openmm/index.md) – Python API MD; extensible, custom forces
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4418754Z       - [CP2K](../../tools/cp2k/index.md) – QM/MM via i-PI interface; AIMD for reactive systems
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4419615Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4419926Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4420657Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/lammps/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4421639Z       # LAMMPS — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4422057Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4422355Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4422724Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4424195Z       LAMMPS integrates Newton's equations of motion using velocity Verlet (or other) integrators. Its distinguishing feature is its interatomic potential library, which spans from simple analytical forms to many-body reactive potentials.
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4425791Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4427560Z       **ReaxFF**: Reactive force field developed by van Duin and Goddard. Uses charge equilibration (QEq) at each step to assign partial charges. Bond order is dynamically computed from interatomic distances, enabling bond breaking/forming. Parameters available for C/H/O/N/S/Si/metals/oxides.
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4429620Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4431835Z       **EAM (Embedded Atom Method)**: Total energy = sum of pair repulsions + embedding energy. The embedding function F depends on the local electron density ρ contributed by neighboring atoms. EAM potentials accurately reproduce elastic constants, stacking fault energies, and surface energies of FCC metals.
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4433820Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4435110Z       **Machine Learning Potentials**: LAMMPS interfaces with external ML models (DeePMD, NequIP, ACE) that use neural networks or kernel methods trained on DFT data to provide DFT-accuracy at classical MD cost.
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4436533Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4436867Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4437305Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4437719Z       - **Velocity Verlet integrator**: Default time integrator
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4438347Z       - **Runge-Kutta (4th order)**: For stiff systems
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4439272Z       - **PPPM (Particle-Particle Particle-Mesh)**: O(N log N) long-range electrostatics
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4440030Z       - **Ewald summation**: Exact but O(N^3/2)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4440689Z       - **QEq charge equilibration**: For ReaxFF and charge-equilibration models
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4441463Z       - **SHAKE/RATTLE**: Bond/angle constraints for rigid groups
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4442270Z       - **Domain decomposition**: 3D spatial decomposition for MPI; dynamic load balancing
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4443485Z       - **Neighbor list**: Verlet neighbor list with skin distance; rebuilt only when atoms move Δ > skin/2
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4444564Z       - **KOKKOS**: Performance-portable abstraction layer; same kernel runs on CPU and GPU
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4445273Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4445582Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4445958Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4446320Z       - **Language**: C++ (main) with some C and Fortran
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4446903Z       - **MPI**: 3D domain decomposition; excellent scaling
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4447533Z       - **OpenMP**: Thread-level parallelism within MPI domains
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4448241Z       - **CUDA**: GPU package for pair potentials; KOKKOS for portable GPU
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4449201Z       - **Python interface**: `lammps` Python module for scripting and automation
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4450210Z       - **Modular packages**: LAMMPS compiled with optional packages (MOLECULE, KSPACE, GPU, KOKKOS, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4451000Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4451315Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4451720Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4452291Z       LAMMPS is among the most scalable MD codes:
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4453117Z       - Demonstrated scaling to 1M+ MPI tasks on petascale machines
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4453806Z       - GPU version: ~1 billion LJ atoms/second on 1024 GPUs
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4454519Z       - ReaxFF: ~1M atoms practical on 100 nodes; slower than non-reactive
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4455079Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4455410Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4455709Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4456099Z       - **DeePMD-kit**: ML potential training and inference
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4456761Z       - **NequIP / MACE**: Equivariant neural network potentials
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4457544Z       - **PLUMED**: Enhanced sampling (metadynamics, committor, path-metadynamics)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4458272Z       - **i-PI**: Path integral MD and QM/MM via socket
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4458921Z       - **OVITO**: Visualization and analysis of LAMMPS trajectories
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4459741Z       - **MDAnalysis**: Python trajectory analysis
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4460349Z       - **ASE**: Python interface for LAMMPS
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4460804Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4461120Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4461832Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/lammps/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4462836Z       # LAMMPS — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4463272Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4463616Z       ## Supported Potentials and Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4464063Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4464368Z       ### Pair Potentials
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4464749Z       | Style | Use Case |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4465123Z       |-------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4465593Z       | `lj/cut` | Simple LJ for noble gases, coarse-grained |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4466250Z       | `lj/cut/coul/long` | LJ + PME for molecular simulations |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4466892Z       | `buck/coul/long` | Buckingham for ionic materials |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4467483Z       | `morse` | Diatomic molecules, simple bonds |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4468041Z       | `yukawa` | Screened Coulomb for colloids |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4468520Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4468862Z       ### Many-Body Metallic Potentials
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4469497Z       | Style | Metals |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4469862Z       |-------|--------|
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4470357Z       | `eam/alloy` | Most metals and alloys (Al, Cu, Ni, Fe, etc.) |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4470965Z       | `eam/fs` | Finnis-Sinclair variant |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4471442Z       | `meam` | Modified EAM for alloys |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4471932Z       | `adp` | Angular-dependent potential |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4472378Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4472679Z       ### Covalent / Reactive
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4473073Z       | Style | Use Case |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4473452Z       |-------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4473842Z       | `tersoff` | Si, Ge, SiC, GaN |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4474410Z       | `airebo` | Carbon (graphene, diamond, CNT), hydrocarbons |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4475018Z       | `rebo` | Reactive empirical bond order |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4475541Z       | `reax/c` | ReaxFF reactive force field |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4476050Z       | `sw` | Stillinger-Weber (Si, Ge) |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4476533Z       | `bop` | Bond-order potentials |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4476959Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4477298Z       ### ML Potentials (via external packages)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4477776Z       | Style | Package |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4478229Z       |-------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4478620Z       | `deepmd` | DeePMD-kit neural network |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4479322Z       | `nequip` | NequIP equivariant NN |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4479795Z       | `ace` | Atomic cluster expansion |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4480329Z       | `snap` | Spectral neighbor analysis potential |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4480896Z       | `gap` | Gaussian approximation potential |
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4481364Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4481655Z       ### Biomolecular
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4482248Z       - AMBER ff14SB via `lj/cut/coul/long` + harmonic bonds/angles + CHARMM dihedrals
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4483192Z       - OPLS-AA via `lj/cut/coul/long`
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4483932Z       - MARTINI coarse-grained via special MARTINI pair styles
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4484488Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4484802Z       ## Input Script Structure
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4485185Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4485463Z       ```lammps
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4485796Z       # Initialization
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4486188Z       units metal          # eV, Angstrom, ps
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4486646Z       dimension 3
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4487059Z       boundary p p p       # periodic in all directions
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4487647Z       atom_style charge    # atoms have charge for ReaxFF
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4488133Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4488420Z       # Atom definition
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4488778Z       read_data iron.data
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4489319Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4489606Z       # Force field
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4489964Z       pair_style reax/c lammps.qeq
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4490420Z       pair_coeff * * FeOCH.reax Fe
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4490813Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4491099Z       # Settings
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4491465Z       fix 1 all qeq/reax 1 0.0 10.0 1.0e-6 reax/c
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4491944Z       timestep 0.001      # 1 fs
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4492328Z       thermo 100
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4492726Z       thermo_style custom step temp press etotal vol
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4493204Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4493506Z       # Equilibration at 300 K
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4493923Z       velocity all create 300.0 4928459
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4494371Z       fix 2 all nvt temp 300.0 300.0 0.1
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4494786Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4495081Z       # Production
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4495410Z       run 100000
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4495713Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4495999Z       # Save trajectory
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4496436Z       dump 1 all custom 1000 traj.lammpstrj id type x y z
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4496932Z       run 1000000
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4497248Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4497524Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4497819Z       ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4498173Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4498479Z       ### Fracture Simulation
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4498850Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4499283Z       ```lammps
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4499662Z       # Create notched sample, apply tensile strain
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4500228Z       fix deform all deform 1 x erate 0.001 units box
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4500744Z       compute stress all stress/atom NULL
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4501435Z       dump 1 all custom 100 dump.stress id type x y z c_stress[1] c_stress[2] c_stress[3]
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4502112Z       run 100000
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4502419Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4502706Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4503022Z       ### Thermal Conductivity (Green-Kubo)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4503460Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4503743Z       ```lammps
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4504077Z       compute myKE all ke/atom
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4504503Z       compute myPE all pe/atom
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4504962Z       compute myStress all stress/atom NULL virial
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4505532Z       compute heat all heat/flux myKE myPE myStress
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4506217Z       fix JJ all ave/correlate 10 100 1000 c_heat[1] c_heat[2] c_heat[3] type auto
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4506842Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4507123Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4507426Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4507787Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4508136Z       ### Machine Learning Potentials with DeePMD
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4508616Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4508902Z       ```lammps
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4509399Z       pair_style deepmd graph.pb
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4509820Z       pair_coeff * * C H O N
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4510267Z       # Run MD at DFT accuracy but classical speed
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4510737Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4511020Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:24:44.4513180Z       # LAMMPS — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4513701Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4514016Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4514375Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4516021Z       LAMMPS is the primary tool for materials simulation: metals, ceramics, polymers, carbon materials, explosives, geomaterials, and tribology. Its reactive force fields and ML potential support make it uniquely suited for chemical reactions in condensed phase.
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4517707Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4518017Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4518374Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4518691Z       ### Metal Fracture and Plasticity
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4519319Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4519840Z       Study crack propagation, dislocation dynamics, and grain boundary mechanics:
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4520585Z       - EAM potential for fcc metals (Al, Cu, Ni, Au)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4521214Z       - Apply tensile strain rate and track stress-strain curves
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4521955Z       - Visualize dislocation nucleation and propagation with OVITO
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4522633Z       - Study void growth and ductile fracture mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4523132Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4523474Z       ### Combustion and Explosives with ReaxFF
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4523923Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4524242Z       ReaxFF MD for reactive systems:
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4524806Z       - RDX (cyclotrimethylenetrinitramine) shock initiation
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4525403Z       - C/H/O combustion chemistry in flames
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4525971Z       - Detonation velocity and reaction product prediction
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4526637Z       - TNT decomposition pathways at high temperature/pressure
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4527188Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4527539Z       ### Carbon Nanotube and Graphene Mechanics
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4528004Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4528347Z       AIREBO or Tersoff potential for carbon:
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4529197Z       - Young's modulus and tensile strength of graphene (1 TPa, 130 GPa)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4529863Z       - CNT compression and buckling
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4530384Z       - Thermal conductivity of graphene via Green-Kubo
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4530996Z       - Friction and wear of carbon coatings (tribology)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4531501Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4531858Z       ### Machine Learning MD for Battery Materials
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4532335Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4532788Z       Train ML potential from DFT data; run long LAMMPS simulations:
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4533528Z       - Li-ion transport in solid-state electrolytes (LLZO, LiPON)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4534295Z       - Interface reaction kinetics at electrode-electrolyte interfaces
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4535201Z       - 10–100× larger systems and longer timescales than AIMD
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4535746Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4536085Z       ### Polymer Dynamics and Entanglement
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4536528Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4536878Z       Coarse-grained polymer simulations:
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4537472Z       - Reptation and chain entanglement (Kremer-Grest model)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4538092Z       - Glass transition temperature prediction
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4538626Z       - Diffusion coefficient vs. chain length
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4539435Z       - Blend morphology and phase separation
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4539897Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4540218Z       ### Tribology: Friction and Wear
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4540629Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4540949Z       Sliding contact simulations:
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4541474Z       - Friction between diamond-like carbon (DLC) coatings
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4542036Z       - Lubrication by molecular films
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4542583Z       - Adhesive contact mechanics (JKR theory validation)
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4543087Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4543401Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4543812Z       
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4544289Z       - **Aerospace/Defense**: Shock physics, explosives, armor materials
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4545511Z       - **Semiconductors**: Si/Ge/III-V device reliability, ion implantation
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4546301Z       - **Energy**: Battery materials, fuel cells, nuclear materials
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4546990Z       - **Nanomaterials**: Carbon nanotubes, graphene, MXenes
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4547621Z       - **Tribology**: Lubrication, wear, friction coatings
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4548211Z       - **Polymers**: Rubber, plastics, adhesives
2026-02-26T02:24:44.4548689Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:26:02.5408440Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5408873Z       **Category:** Molecular Dynamics Simulation (Biomolecular)  
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5410314Z       **Developer/Organization:** University of California (UCSF, UCSD, UCI, and collaborators); David Case, Carlos Simmerling, Tom Cheatham, and many others  
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5412066Z       **License:** Commercial (AMBER suite); AmberTools is free (GPL)  
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5412707Z       **Website:** https://ambermd.org  
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5413157Z       **Current Version:** AMBER 24  
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5413555Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5413844Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5414159Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5417366Z       AMBER (Assisted Model Building with Energy Refinement) is one of the most widely used MD simulation packages for biomolecular simulations. It has been developed continuously since the 1970s (Peter Kollman group at UCSF) and is renowned for its AMBER force fields — a family of carefully validated force field parameters for proteins, nucleic acids, lipids, carbohydrates, and small molecules.
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5421961Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5424194Z       AMBER consists of two parts: the commercial AMBER suite (which includes the GPU-accelerated `pmemd.cuda` engine) and AmberTools (freely available collection of utilities for system preparation, analysis, and some simulation capabilities). The GPU-accelerated `pmemd.cuda` is considered one of the fastest biomolecular MD engines, particularly for single-GPU simulations.
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5426534Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5427799Z       AMBER force fields (ff14SB, ff19SB, OL3, OL15, GAFF2) are among the most extensively validated force fields in the field and are widely used in GROMACS, NAMD, and OpenMM as well as in the native AMBER suite.
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5429329Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5429619Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5429950Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5430506Z       - **pmemd.cuda**: Highly optimized GPU-accelerated MD engine (single and multi-GPU)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5431634Z       - **AMBER force fields**: ff14SB, ff19SB (proteins); OL3, OL15 (RNA, DNA); lipid17 (lipids); GAFF/GAFF2 (small molecules)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5432650Z       - **sander**: General-purpose MD engine; free in AmberTools
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5433555Z       - **Antechamber**: Automatic parameterization of drug-like small molecules (GAFF/GAFF2)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5434407Z       - **tleap / xleap**: System preparation, topology generation
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5435136Z       - **cpptraj**: Comprehensive trajectory analysis (free in AmberTools)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5435823Z       - **Replica exchange**: Temperature REMD, H-REMD, REST2
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5436439Z       - **Free energy**: FEP, TI, MBAR with ti and mbar modules
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5437138Z       - **QM/MM**: Interface with Gaussian, ORCA, TeraChem, sqm (semi-empirical)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5437854Z       - **GB/SA**: Generalized Born implicit solvent models (OBC, GBn, GBn2)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5438483Z       - **REMD, GaMD**: Gaussian accelerated MD for enhanced sampling
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5439429Z       - **NMR restraints**: Distance, dihedral, RDC restraints for structure refinement
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5440364Z       - **Constant pH MD**: Continuous constant pH simulations for protonation state sampling
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5441101Z       - **LES**: Locally enhanced sampling
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5441760Z       - **PBSA**: Poisson-Boltzmann Surface Area for binding free energy estimation
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5442409Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5442710Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5443042Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5443327Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5443660Z       # AmberTools is free:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5444121Z       conda install -c conda-forge ambertools
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5444566Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5444896Z       # System preparation workflow:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5445373Z       # 1. Prepare PDB (remove HET atoms, add H)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5445868Z       # 2. Load and prepare in tleap
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5446285Z       cat > tleap.in << 'EOF'
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5446691Z       source leaprc.protein.ff14SB
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5447130Z       source leaprc.water.tip3p
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5447828Z       mol = loadpdb protein.pdb
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5448418Z       solvateBox mol TIP3PBOX 12.0
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5448841Z       addions mol Na+ 0
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5449396Z       addions mol Cl- 0
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5449835Z       saveamberparm mol protein.prmtop protein.inpcrd
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5450337Z       quit
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5450629Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5450946Z       tleap -f tleap.in
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5451293Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5451604Z       # 3. Minimize and equilibrate
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5452320Z       mpirun -np 4 sander -O -i min.in -o min.out -p protein.prmtop -c protein.inpcrd -r min.rst
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5453048Z       # 4. GPU production MD
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5453660Z       pmemd.cuda -O -i md.in -o md.out -p protein.prmtop -c nvt.rst -r md.rst -x md.nc
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5454317Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5454602Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5454892Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5455225Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5456024Z       - [GROMACS](../../tools/gromacs/index.md) – Excellent performance; reads AMBER force fields
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5457140Z       - [NAMD](../../tools/namd/index.md) – Complementary biomolecular MD; CHARMM FF focus
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5458157Z       - [OpenMM](../../tools/openmm/index.md) – Python API; can use AMBER force fields
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5459377Z       - [VMD](../../tools/vmd/index.md) – Trajectory visualization; reads AMBER .nc trajectories
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5460474Z       - [MDAnalysis](https://www.mdanalysis.org) – Python analysis of AMBER trajectories
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5461181Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5461484Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5462207Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/amber/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5463158Z       # AMBER — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5463567Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5463878Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5464265Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5464664Z       AMBER uses the classical AMBER force field functional form:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5465192Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5465465Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5465972Z       U = Σ_bonds K_b(r - r_0)² + Σ_angles K_θ(θ - θ_0)²
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5466581Z         + Σ_dihedrals V_n/2 [1 + cos(nφ - γ)]
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5467118Z         + Σ_LJ [A_ij/r¹² - B_ij/r⁶]
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5467634Z         + Σ_Coulomb q_i q_j / ε r_ij
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5468041Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5468318Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5468645Z       Key algorithmic features:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5469381Z       - **SHAKE/RATTLE**: Constrain X-H bonds to allow 2 fs timestep
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5470129Z       - **PME**: Smooth particle-mesh Ewald for electrostatics (O(N log N))
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5470916Z       - **BINTLIST**: Neighbor list construction for non-bonded interactions
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5471515Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5471843Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5472302Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5472955Z       - **pmemd**: High-performance MD engine with highly optimized PME; supports single and multi-GPU
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5474039Z       - **pmemd.cuda**: NVIDIA CUDA implementation of core MD; single-GPU or multi-GPU via NCCL
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5475094Z       - **GaMD (Gaussian accelerated MD)**: Adds harmonic boost potential to flatten the energy surface
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5476166Z       - **REST2**: Replica exchange with solute tempering 2.0 for enhanced sampling of biomolecules
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5477245Z       - **GBn/GBn2 implicit solvent**: Generalized Born models for rapid implicit solvent simulations
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5478326Z       - **PBSA**: Numerical Poisson-Boltzmann and analytical SA for MM-PBSA binding free energy
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5479237Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5479562Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5479960Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5480397Z       - **Language**: Fortran (sander, pmemd) + CUDA C++ (pmemd.cuda)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5481484Z       - **MPI**: Multi-node parallelism in sander and pmemd
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5482440Z       - **CUDA**: GPU acceleration in pmemd.cuda; highly optimized for NVIDIA V100/A100
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5483481Z       - **Single-GPU focus**: pmemd.cuda is most efficient on single GPU; multi-GPU scaling is less linear
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5484260Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5484584Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5484996Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5485298Z       pmemd.cuda benchmarks:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5485929Z       - ~1 µs/day for 25,000-atom DHFR on a single NVIDIA A100
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5486525Z       - ~150 ns/day for 250,000-atom systems on A100
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5487240Z       - Among the fastest single-GPU biomolecular MD engines (competitive with NAMD)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5487912Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5488227Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5488617Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5489352Z       - **AmberTools**: Free companion suite (tleap, cpptraj, Antechamber, MMPBSA.py, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5490341Z       - **GAFF/GAFF2**: General AMBER force field via Antechamber for drug-like molecules
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5491162Z       - **Antechamber + ACPYPE**: Small molecule parameterization
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5491875Z       - **MMPBSA.py**: MM-PBSA/MM-GBSA binding free energy in AmberTools
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5492553Z       - **MDAnalysis**: Reads AMBER .prmtop and .nc files
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5493206Z       - **parmed**: ParmEd for AMBER <-> GROMACS topology conversion
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5493778Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5494088Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5494788Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/amber/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5495744Z       # AMBER — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5496153Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5496465Z       ## Supported Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5496850Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5497166Z       ### Protein Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5497559Z       | Force Field | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5497935Z       |------------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5498500Z       | ff14SB | Recommended; improved backbone torsions from ff99SB-ILDN |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5499712Z       | ff19SB | Newer; improved χ1/χ2 rotamer populations; use with OPC water |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5500410Z       | ff03 | Older; polarizable charge model |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5500874Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5501193Z       ### Nucleic Acid Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5501617Z       | Force Field | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5502004Z       |------------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5502387Z       | OL3 | RNA; recommended |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5502807Z       | OL15 | DNA; recommended |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5503250Z       | BSC1 | DNA; alternative to OL15 |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5503671Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5503995Z       ### Small Molecule Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5504447Z       | Force Field | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5504857Z       |------------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5505314Z       | GAFF | General AMBER force field; widely used |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5505928Z       | GAFF2 | Improved GAFF; better for drug-like molecules |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5506615Z       | RESP charges | Restrained electrostatic potential charges |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5507170Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5507468Z       ### Lipid Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5508017Z       - lipid17: Phospholipids (POPC, POPE, DPPC, DPPE, cholesterol)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5508647Z       - lipid21: Updated lipid force field
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5509299Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5509584Z       ### Water Models
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5509983Z       - TIP3P: Default; fast, good structure
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5510533Z       - TIP4P-Ew: Better dynamics, diffusion coefficient
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5511268Z       - OPC: Best overall performance for proteins/RNA; recommended with ff19SB
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5511931Z       - SPC/E: Common alternative
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5512585Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5512884Z       ## Input File Types
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5513227Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5513704Z       | File | Description |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5514098Z       |------|-------------|
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5514650Z       | `.prmtop` / `.top7` | Topology (bonds, angles, force field parameters) |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5515475Z       | `.inpcrd` / `.rst7` | Coordinates (and optionally velocities, box vectors) |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5516132Z       | `.nc` | NetCDF trajectory |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5516675Z       | `mdin` | Simulation input parameters (namelist format) |
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5517206Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5517507Z       ## Typical MD Input
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5517865Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5518145Z       ```fortran
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5518489Z       Protein NPT production MD
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5518878Z        &cntrl
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5519390Z         imin=0,             ! MD simulation
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5519901Z         ntx=5, irest=1,     ! Restart from previous run
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5520439Z         nstlim=500000,      ! 500,000 steps = 1 ns
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5520927Z         dt=0.002,           ! 2 fs timestep
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5521373Z         ntf=2, ntc=2,       ! SHAKE on H
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5521874Z         cut=10.0,           ! Non-bonded cutoff (Angstrom)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5522391Z         ntpr=1000,          ! Print frequency
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5522895Z         ntwx=1000,          ! Trajectory write frequency
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5523440Z         ntwr=10000,         ! Restart write frequency
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5523955Z         ntt=3, gamma_ln=2.0,! Langevin thermostat
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5524467Z         temp0=300.0,        ! Target temperature
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5524990Z         ntp=1, barostat=2,  ! Monte Carlo barostat
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5525457Z         pres0=1.0,
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5525833Z         igb=0,              ! Explicit solvent (PME)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5526300Z         iwrap=1,
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5526611Z        &end
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5526907Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5527175Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5527469Z       ## cpptraj Analysis
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5527809Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5528071Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5528420Z       # cpptraj trajectory analysis
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5528909Z       cpptraj protein.prmtop << 'EOF'
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5529528Z       trajin md.nc
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5529869Z       rmsd :1-150@CA first out rmsd.dat
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5530311Z       rmsf :1-150@CA byres out rmsf.dat
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5530748Z       radgyr :1-150 out gyration.dat
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5531197Z       hbond :1-150 avgout hbonds.dat series
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5531632Z       run
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5531917Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5532215Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5532494Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5532792Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5533222Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5533553Z       ### MM-PBSA/MM-GBSA Binding Free Energy
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5533996Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5534282Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5534620Z       # Using MMPBSA.py from AmberTools
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5535128Z       MMPBSA.py -O -i mmgbsa.in -o results.dat \
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5535658Z                 -sp solvated_complex.prmtop \
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5536135Z                 -cp dry_complex.prmtop \
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5536602Z                 -rp receptor.prmtop \
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5537182Z                 -lp ligand.prmtop \
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5599789Z                 -y md.nc
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5600179Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5600475Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5600787Z       ### Constant pH MD
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5601156Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5601995Z       Set up systems where histidine, aspartate, glutamate protonation states are dynamically sampled according to a given pH.
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5602957Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:26:02.5603998Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/amber/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5605274Z       # AMBER — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5605634Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5606141Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5606538Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5607916Z       AMBER is the leading code for structure-based drug design, protein dynamics, and nucleic acid simulations. Its force fields and GPU-optimized pmemd.cuda make it the standard choice in pharmaceutical industry MD.
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5609629Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5609933Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5610288Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5610645Z       ### Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity (MM-GBSA)
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5611123Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5611460Z       Industry-standard screening method:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5612131Z       - Run 50–100 ns MD for each protein-ligand complex
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5612683Z       - Extract 1000 snapshots from trajectory
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5613253Z       - Compute MM-GBSA or MM-PBSA binding free energy
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5613870Z       - Screen compound series for lead optimization
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5614531Z       - Thousands of compounds screened in large pharma campaigns
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5615089Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5615395Z       ### RNA and DNA Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5615776Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5616117Z       Nucleic acid MD is a strength of AMBER:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5616665Z       - tRNA, ribozyme, aptamer folding and dynamics
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5617208Z       - DNA polymerase fidelity and proofreading
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5617743Z       - CRISPR-Cas9 guide RNA / DNA duplex dynamics
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5618295Z       - Quadruplex DNA conformation and drug binding
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5618765Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5619322Z       ### Intrinsically Disordered Proteins
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5619757Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5620098Z       ff19SB + OPC water recommended for IDPs:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5620836Z       - α-Synuclein aggregation relevant to Parkinson's disease
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5621485Z       - Tau protein microtubule-binding domain
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5622000Z       - Coupled folding and binding of IDPs
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5622441Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5622754Z       ### HIV Protease Drug Resistance
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5623174Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5623490Z       Landmark application of AMBER:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5623985Z       - MD of HIV protease with various inhibitors
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5624617Z       - Binding free energy calculations for drug-resistant mutants
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5625314Z       - Saquinavir, ritonavir, indinavir resistance mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5625840Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5626135Z       ### Enzyme Catalysis QM/MM
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5626511Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5626907Z       QM/MM with sqm (semi-empirical) or external QC codes:
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5627478Z       - Serine protease catalytic mechanism
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5627959Z       - Kinase phosphoryl transfer
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5628400Z       - P450 oxidation reactions
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5628896Z       - Compare barriers with experimental kcat values
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5629602Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5629933Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5630372Z       
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5630858Z       - **Pharmaceutical industry**: Drug binding, selectivity, resistance
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5631624Z       - **Structural biology**: Protein dynamics, NMR refinement
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5632293Z       - **RNA biology**: Ribozyme mechanisms, RNA therapeutics
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5632917Z       - **Vaccine design**: Antibody-antigen interactions
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5633617Z       - **Bioinformatics**: Force field benchmarking and validation
2026-02-26T02:26:02.5634159Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:27:10.0857143Z       # NAMD
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0857467Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0857922Z       **Category:** Molecular Dynamics Simulation (Biomolecular)  
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0859529Z       **Developer/Organization:** Theoretical and Computational Biophysics Group, University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (Klaus Schulten and successors)  
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0860743Z       **License:** Free for non-commercial use  
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0861400Z       **Website:** https://www.ks.uiuc.edu/Research/namd  
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0862282Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/ambermd/namd (mirrors exist; official distribution from UIUC)  
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0863078Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0863388Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0864012Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0866956Z       NAMD (Not Another Molecular Dynamics) is a high-performance parallel molecular dynamics program designed for large biomolecular simulations. Developed at the Beckman Institute at UIUC by Klaus Schulten's group (now led by Emad Tajkhorshid), NAMD uses the Charm++ parallel programming model, which enables efficient dynamic load balancing and scalable performance on both multi-core workstations and petascale supercomputers.
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0869963Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0872583Z       NAMD is tightly integrated with VMD (Visual Molecular Dynamics) — the two tools form a complementary ecosystem for simulation and visualization. NAMD uses CHARMM force field parameters (but also supports AMBER force fields) and shares topology file formats with CHARMM, making it natural to use CHARMM-GUI for system preparation.
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0874737Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0876152Z       A notable feature of NAMD is its implementation of steered molecular dynamics (SMD) and adaptive biasing force (ABF) method, which are widely used to study protein mechanical properties and compute potential of mean force.
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0877705Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0878003Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0878355Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0878920Z       - **Charm++ parallelism**: Dynamic load balancing for heterogeneous simulation systems
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0880153Z       - **CHARMM force fields**: CGenFF, CHARMM36, CHARMM36m; also AMBER ff14SB
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0881242Z       - **PME electrostatics**: Smooth particle-mesh Ewald; highly optimized
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0882455Z       - **GPU acceleration**: CUDA-accelerated non-bonded forces
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0883405Z       - **Multiple time stepping (MTS)**: RESPA-like MTS for efficient long-range force computation
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0884541Z       - **Steered MD (SMD)**: Apply constant force or constant velocity; compute force-extension curves
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0885597Z       - **Adaptive Biasing Force (ABF)**: Free energy of mean force along a coordinate
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0886406Z       - **Metadynamics**: Via PLUMED integration
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0887358Z       - **Collective variables (colvars)**: Module for defining reaction coordinates and applying biasing forces
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0888201Z       - **REMD**: Temperature and Hamiltonian replica exchange
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0888771Z       - **VMD integration**: Real-time visualization and analysis during simulation
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0889592Z       - **IMD (Interactive MD)**: Real-time user interaction with running simulation via VMD
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0890249Z       - **Alchemical FEP**: Free energy perturbation with dual-topology scheme
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0890765Z       - **QM/MM**: Interface with ORCA and TeraChem for hybrid calculations
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0891291Z       - **Constant pH**: Constant pH MD for protonation state sampling
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0891740Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0891927Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0892137Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0892302Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0892703Z       # Download NAMD from https://www.ks.uiuc.edu/Research/namd
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0893079Z       # System preparation with CHARMM-GUI or psfgen:
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0893480Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0893721Z       # Create PSF topology file with psfgen (VMD/NAMD tool)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0894071Z       package require psfgen
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0894406Z       topology top_all36_prot.rtf
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0894667Z       segment PROT { pdb protein.pdb }
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0895044Z       coordpdb protein.pdb PROT
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0895299Z       guesscoord
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0895501Z       writepdb protein_withH.pdb
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0895827Z       writepsf protein.psf
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0896091Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0896257Z       # Run NAMD
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0896454Z       namd2 +p8 md.conf > md.log
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0896814Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0896983Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0897150Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0897720Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0898445Z       - [GROMACS](../../tools/gromacs/index.md) – Faster for pure biomolecular MD; AMBER/OPLS FF
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0899468Z       - [AMBER](../../tools/amber/index.md) – GPU-focused biomolecular MD; competing ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0900138Z       - [VMD](../../tools/vmd/index.md) – Visualization; tightly coupled to NAMD
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0900749Z       - [OpenMM](../../tools/openmm/index.md) – Python API MD; can run CHARMM FF
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0901249Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0901433Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0901978Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/namd/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0902577Z       # NAMD — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0902911Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0903097Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0903322Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0905110Z       NAMD implements classical MD with CHARMM and AMBER force fields. Its distinguishing feature is the Charm++ parallel runtime system, which provides dynamic load balancing essential for heterogeneous biomolecular systems (where some atoms are more computationally expensive than others).
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0906750Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0907293Z       **Multiple Time Stepping (RESPA)**: NAMD uses a reversible reference system propagator algorithm:
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0907959Z       - Inner loop (1 fs): Bonded forces (bonds, angles, dihedrals)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0908465Z       - Middle loop (2 fs): Short-range non-bonded (< 8 Å)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0909131Z       - Outer loop (4 fs): Long-range PME electrostatics
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0909514Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0910018Z       This allows efficient use of the PME reciprocal space calculation which is relatively expensive per call.
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0910519Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0910769Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0911096Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0911480Z       - **Charm++ runtime**: Object-based message-driven parallel execution with adaptive load balancing
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0912149Z       - **RESPA (MTS)**: Multiple time stepping as described above
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0912719Z       - **PME**: Particle-mesh Ewald; highly optimized with CUDA offloading
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0913243Z       - **Colvars module**: Collective variables for enhanced sampling and analysis
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0913800Z       - **ABF (Adaptive Biasing Force)**: Non-equilibrium free energy method
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0914397Z       - **TMD (Targeted MD)**: Drive system toward target conformation
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0914937Z       - **SMD (Steered MD)**: Apply external force to probe mechanics or compute work
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0915425Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0915609Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0915954Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0916144Z       - **Language**: C++ built on Charm++
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0916475Z       - **Charm++**: Adaptive load balancing parallel runtime (UIUC)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0917115Z       - **MPI/OpenMP**: Standard for standard HPC; Charm++ provides additional load balancing
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0917713Z       - **CUDA**: GPU-accelerated pair interactions and PME
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0918200Z       - **Multiple executables**: `namd2` (standard), `namd3` (new GPU-optimized version)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0918682Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0918869Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0919394Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0919623Z       NAMD is designed for petascale:
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0919956Z       - Special hardware: Ran on Blue Waters (256k cores), Summit
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0920568Z       - NAMD 3.0 improved GPU performance significantly
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0921239Z       - ~50–500 ns/day for 100k-atom systems on modern GPU nodes
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0921556Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0921788Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0922111Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0922338Z       - **VMD**: Tightly coupled visualization and analysis
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0923164Z       - **CHARMM-GUI**: Web-based system builder outputting NAMD-ready files
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0923747Z       - **psfgen**: PSF topology builder (part of VMD/NAMD)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0924141Z       - **PLUMED**: Enhanced sampling (metadynamics, path-metadynamics)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0924717Z       - **Colvars**: Collective variables module (built into NAMD)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0925028Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0925208Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0925599Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/namd/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0926144Z       # NAMD — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0926381Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0926615Z       ## Supported Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0927010Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0927324Z       - **CHARMM**: CGenFF (small molecules), CHARMM36m (proteins), CHARMM36 (lipids, DNA)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0927793Z       - **AMBER**: ff14SB, GAFF/GAFF2 (via CHARMM-format conversion)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0928163Z       - **OPLS-AA**: Via CHARMM-format conversion
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0928438Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0928616Z       ## NAMD Configuration File
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0928840Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0929258Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0929535Z       # NAMD configuration file (md.conf)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0929798Z       # Input files
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0930004Z       structure       protein.psf
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0930250Z       coordinates     protein.pdb
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0930590Z       set temperature 310
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0930944Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0931118Z       # Output
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0931313Z       outputName      output
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0931545Z       outputEnergies  1000
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0931770Z       outputPressure  1000
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0931984Z       dcdfreq         1000
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0932192Z       xstFreq         1000
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0932387Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0932556Z       # Force field
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0932764Z       paraTypeCharmm  on
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0933004Z       parameters      par_all36_prot.prm
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0933282Z       parameters      par_all36_lipid.prm
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0933563Z       parameters      toppar_water_ions.str
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0933812Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0933979Z       # PME
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0934157Z       PME             yes
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0934371Z       PMEGridSpacing  1.0
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0934577Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0934740Z       # Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0934936Z       timestep        2.0    ;# 2 fs
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0935215Z       rigidBonds      all    ;# SHAKE on all H bonds
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0935494Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0935659Z       # Thermostat
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0935855Z       langevin        on
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0936081Z       langevinDamping 1.0
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0936315Z       langevinTemp    $temperature
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0936547Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0936709Z       # Barostat
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0936907Z       LangevinPiston  on
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0937131Z       LangevinPistonTarget 1.0
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0937383Z       LangevinPistonPeriod 200
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0937778Z       LangevinPistonDecay  100
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0938009Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0938171Z       # Run
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0938349Z       minimize        5000
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0938570Z       reinitvels      $temperature
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0938811Z       run             500000
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0939249Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0939418Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0939594Z       ## Steered MD and Colvars
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0939812Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0939973Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0940167Z       # Colvars configuration for SMD
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0940405Z       colvar {
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0940585Z         name z_com
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0940773Z         distanceZ {
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0940989Z           main { atomNumbersRange 1-300 }
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0941440Z           ref  { atomNumbersRange 301-600 }
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0941975Z         }
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0942288Z       }
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0942861Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0943060Z       harmonic {
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0943261Z         colvars z_com
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0943466Z         centers 0.0
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0943668Z         forceConstant 5.0
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0944086Z         targetCenters 30.0
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0944586Z         targetNumSteps 250000
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0944934Z       }
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0945198Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0945470Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0945756Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0946094Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0946428Z       ### Interactive Molecular Dynamics (IMD)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0946797Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0947009Z       NAMD supports real-time interaction via VMD:
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0947302Z       1. Launch NAMD with IMD port
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0947606Z       2. Connect VMD; apply forces with mouse in real time
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0947986Z       3. Study mechanical response, guide folding, pull ligands
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0948293Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0948480Z       ### Alchemical Free Energy
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0948707Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0948872Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0949292Z       alch            on
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0949512Z       alchType        FEP
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0949737Z       alchFile        ligand_fep.pdb
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0949983Z       alchCol         B
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0950198Z       alchOutFreq     100
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0950427Z       alchEquilSteps  50000
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0950651Z       alchLambda      0.0
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0950864Z       alchLambda2     0.05
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0951178Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0951445Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0951626Z
