2026-02-26T02:27:10.0952576Z       # NAMD — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0952785Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0952963Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0953180Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0953737Z       NAMD is used for large-scale biomolecular simulations where Charm++ scalability, CHARMM force field support, and SMD/ABF enhanced sampling are needed.
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0954354Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0954526Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0954729Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0954949Z       ### Protein Mechanical Properties (AFM Simulation)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0955234Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0955501Z       Steered MD simulates atomic force microscopy pulling experiments:
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0955895Z       - Unfold titin Ig domains under constant velocity
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0956251Z       - Compare force-extension curves with AFM experiments
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0956585Z       - Identify mechanical unfolding pathways
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0956921Z       - Study catch bonds in integrin-fibronectin complexes
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0957217Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0957415Z       ### Ion Channel Permeation and Selectivity
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0957675Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0957882Z       NAMD is standard for ion channel simulations:
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0958255Z       - Gramicidin, KcsA, aquaporin permeation free energies via ABF
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0958617Z       - K+ vs Na+ selectivity filter mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0958905Z       - Voltage-gated sodium channel gating
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0959388Z       - Mechanosensitive channel (MscL) opening under membrane tension
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0959721Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0959915Z       ### Virus Assembly and Capsid Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0960161Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0960400Z       NAMD was used for groundbreaking large-scale simulations:
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0960780Z       - HIV capsid: 64-million-atom simulation (Schulten group)
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0961101Z       - Tobacco mosaic virus assembly
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0961374Z       - Influenza hemagglutinin membrane fusion
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0961824Z       - Bacteriophage DNA packaging motor
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0962072Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0962355Z       ### Membrane Transport Proteins
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0962594Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0962834Z       ABC transporters, GPCRs, secondary active transporters:
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0963171Z       - P-glycoprotein drug efflux mechanism
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0963446Z       - GPCR activation via REMD
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0963716Z       - Conformational changes in LeuT transporter
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0963986Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0964174Z       ### Large-Scale Chromatin Simulations
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0964417Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0964624Z       Nucleosome positioning and chromatin fiber:
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0964918Z       - Histone tail interactions with DNA
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0965203Z       - Chromatin fiber higher-order structure
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0965457Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0965641Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0965880Z       
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0966138Z       - **Biophysics**: Protein mechanics, motor proteins, cytoskeleton
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0966571Z       - **Structural biology**: Protein dynamics from cryo-EM structures
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0966979Z       - **Ion channels**: Drug targeting, channelopathy mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0967354Z       - **Virology**: Viral particle assembly, membrane fusion
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0967718Z       - **Drug discovery**: GPCR and ion channel drug targeting
2026-02-26T02:27:10.0968017Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:28:26.1417138Z       # OpenMM
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1417466Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1417843Z       **Category:** Molecular Dynamics Simulation  
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1418931Z       **Developer/Organization:** Originally Pande group (Stanford); now maintained by OpenMM community (Chodera lab, SLAC, and others)  
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1420159Z       **License:** MIT License  
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1420618Z       **Website:** https://openmm.org  
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1421158Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/openmm/openmm  
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1421663Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1421950Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1422264Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1423944Z       OpenMM is a GPU-accelerated molecular dynamics library with a Python-first design philosophy. Unlike GROMACS or AMBER (which are primarily command-line tools), OpenMM is designed to be used as a Python library, making it highly extensible and easy to integrate into custom workflows, machine learning pipelines, and automated computational chemistry platforms.
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1425564Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1427869Z       OpenMM implements AMBER, CHARMM, OPLS-AA, and custom force fields via its flexible XML force field format. The library supports CUDA, OpenCL, and Metal (Apple Silicon) for GPU acceleration, and a reference CPU platform for testing. A key advantage of OpenMM is its ability to define custom forces in Python, enabling rapid prototyping of novel force field terms, enhanced sampling methods, and ML-augmented potentials.
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1430607Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1432474Z       OpenMM is widely used in drug discovery workflows via the OpenFF (Open Force Field) Initiative, in enhanced sampling packages (OpenPathSampling, OpenFE), and in ML-MD hybrid simulations. It is the backend for several commercial and academic drug discovery platforms.
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1434432Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1434821Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1435055Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1435558Z       - **Python API**: Full MD functionality from Python; no input files required
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1436335Z       - **GPU acceleration**: CUDA (NVIDIA), OpenCL (AMD/Intel), Metal (Apple M1/M2/M3)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1436990Z       - **Force fields**: AMBER ff14SB, CHARMM36, OPLS-AA, OpenFF/Sage/Parsley
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1437610Z       - **Custom forces**: Define arbitrary energy functions in Python or via C++ plugin
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1438345Z       - **Integrators**: Leapfrog, Verlet, Langevin, Nosé-Hoover, BAOAB, DPD
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1439095Z       - **Barostats**: Monte Carlo barostat, anisotropic Monte Carlo barostat
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1439864Z       - **Constraints**: SETTLE, CCMA (constraint method similar to LINCS)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1440584Z       - **PME**: Smooth PME for long-range electrostatics; GPU-optimized
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1441333Z       - **Enhanced sampling**: Via mdtraj, OpenPathSampling, PLUMED, OpenFE
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1442069Z       - **Alchemical FEP**: Via Perses (alchemical free energy package)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1442742Z       - **ML/MM**: NNPOps for neural network potential integration
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1443424Z       - **OpenFF Integration**: Open Force Field toolkit for modern force fields
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1444217Z       - **Drude polarizable**: Drude oscillator model for polarizable force fields
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1445050Z       - **Custom integrators**: Define custom integrators via leapfrog operator algebra
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1445925Z       - **Serialization**: Save/load simulation state as XML for reproducibility
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1446718Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1446893Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1447087Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1447393Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1447586Z       # Install via conda
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1447826Z       conda install -c conda-forge openmm
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1448077Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1448263Z       # Simple Python simulation
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1448507Z       import openmm as mm
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1448732Z       import openmm.app as app
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1449252Z       import openmm.unit as unit
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1449668Z       from sys import stdout
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1449951Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1450120Z       # Load PDB
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1450329Z       pdb = app.PDBFile('protein.pdb')
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1450912Z       forcefield = app.ForceField('amber14-all.xml', 'amber14/tip3pfb.xml')
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1451399Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1451574Z       # Create system
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1451828Z       system = forcefield.createSystem(pdb.topology,
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1452171Z                                         nonbondedMethod=app.PME,
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1452524Z                                         nonbondedCutoff=1.0*unit.nanometer,
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1452862Z                                         constraints=app.HBonds)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1453132Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1453319Z       # Set up integrator and simulation
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1453959Z       integrator = mm.LangevinMiddleIntegrator(300*unit.kelvin, 1/unit.picosecond, 2*unit.femtoseconds)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1454597Z       platform = mm.Platform.getPlatformByName('CUDA')
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1455098Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1455641Z       simulation = app.Simulation(pdb.topology, system, integrator, platform)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1456406Z       simulation.context.setPositions(pdb.positions)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1457013Z       simulation.minimizeEnergy()
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1457464Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1457786Z       # Add reporters
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1458316Z       simulation.reporters.append(app.DCDReporter('trajectory.dcd', 1000))
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1459209Z       simulation.reporters.append(app.StateDataReporter(stdout, 1000, step=True,
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1459780Z           potentialEnergy=True, temperature=True, progress=True, totalSteps=500000))
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1460153Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1460337Z       simulation.step(500000)  # 1 ns
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1460576Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1460740Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1460911Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1461108Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1461598Z       - [GROMACS](../../tools/gromacs/index.md) – Faster for pure classical MD; command-line focused
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1462234Z       - [AMBER](../../tools/amber/index.md) – Comparable GPU performance; AMBER FF focus
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1462846Z       - [RDKit](../../tools/rdkit/index.md) – Cheminformatics preprocessing for OpenMM simulations
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1463478Z       - [DeepChem](../../tools/deepchem/index.md) – ML potentials that can be used with OpenMM
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1463884Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1464067Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1464476Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/openmm/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1465046Z       # OpenMM — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1465342Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1465518Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1465740Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1466885Z       OpenMM implements classical molecular dynamics using the same force field functional forms as AMBER and CHARMM. Its key design innovation is a layered architecture: the high-level Python API calls into Platform-specific implementations (CUDA, OpenCL, CPU Reference) that execute the same physical model on different hardware.
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1468102Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1469726Z       **Custom Forces**: OpenMM's `CustomBondForce`, `CustomAngleForce`, `CustomTorsionForce`, `CustomNonbondedForce`, and `CustomExternalForce` allow users to define arbitrary energy functions as mathematical expressions parsed at runtime (using OpenMM's internal JIT expression compiler). This enables:
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1471472Z       - Novel force field terms without modifying source code
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1471879Z       - Machine learning potential integration
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1472462Z       - Alchemical transformations with custom lambda functions
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1472987Z       - Enhanced sampling biasing potentials
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1473246Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1473443Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1473689Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1474126Z       - **LangevinMiddleIntegrator**: BAOAB-style Langevin integrator with middle operator splitting; excellent for NVT
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1474857Z       - **MonteCarloBarostat**: Stochastic volume moves for NPT; more correct than velocity-scaling barostats
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1475409Z       - **VariableLangevinIntegrator**: Adaptive timestep Langevin
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1475852Z       - **RPMDIntegrator**: Ring polymer MD for quantum nuclear effects
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1476269Z       - **SETTLE**: Exact analytical constraints for water molecules
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1476787Z       - **CCMA (Constrained Coordinate Moving Algorithm)**: General constraint solver analogous to LINCS
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1477231Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1477411Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1477723Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1478164Z       - **Language**: C++ core library + Python API (via SWIG bindings)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1479196Z       - **Platform abstraction**: Reference (CPU), OpenCL (any GPU), CUDA (NVIDIA), Metal (Apple)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1479910Z       - **JIT compilation**: Custom force expressions compiled at runtime for each platform
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1480424Z       - **XML force fields**: Extensible XML format for force field parameters
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1480914Z       - **Python-first**: Full simulation control from Python; no config files required
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1481300Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1481493Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1481725Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1481907Z       OpenMM performance on GPU:
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1482214Z       - Competitive with pmemd.cuda for single-GPU performance
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1482575Z       - ~500 ns/day for DHFR (24k atoms) on NVIDIA A100
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1482981Z       - CUDA platform ~2–5× faster than OpenCL
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1483373Z       - Best for single-GPU; multi-GPU scaling less optimized than GROMACS/AMBER
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1483733Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1483913Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1484141Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1484407Z       - **OpenFF toolkit**: Modern force field assignment via SMIRKS patterns
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1484823Z       - **Perses**: Alchemical relative free energy calculations
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1485212Z       - **OpenPathSampling**: Path sampling methods (TIS, TPS)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1485552Z       - **OpenFE**: Open Free Energy consortium
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1485846Z       - **MDTraj**: Trajectory analysis library
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1486143Z       - **ParmEd**: Topology reading and conversion
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1486523Z       - **NNPOps**: GPU-optimized neural network potential operators
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1486949Z       - **Espaloma**: Graph neural network for force field parameterization
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1487290Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1487466Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1487859Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/openmm/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1488391Z       # OpenMM — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1488633Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1488808Z       ## Supported Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1489238Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1489440Z       ### XML Force Fields (native OpenMM format)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1489749Z       - `amber14-all.xml`: AMBER ff14SB proteins
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1490222Z       - `amber14/tip3pfb.xml`: TIP3P-FB water
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1490689Z       - `charmm36.xml`: CHARMM36 proteins
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1490986Z       - `charmm36/water.xml`: TIP3P water for CHARMM
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1491356Z       - `amber14/tip3p.xml`, `amber14/tip4pew.xml`: Various water models
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1491741Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1492093Z       ### OpenFF (Open Force Field)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1492407Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1492640Z       from openff.toolkit import ForceField, Molecule
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1492915Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1493097Z       # Load OpenFF 2.1.0 (Sage)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1493350Z       ff = ForceField('openff-2.1.0.offxml')
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1493653Z       molecule = Molecule.from_smiles('CCO')  # ethanol
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1493952Z       topology = molecule.to_topology()
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1494236Z       system = ff.create_openmm_system(topology)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1494495Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1494666Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1494839Z       ### Custom Force Field
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1495047Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1495215Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1495504Z       # Custom Lennard-Jones with custom σ and ε
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1495882Z       custom_nb = mm.CustomNonbondedForce("4*eps*((sigma/r)^12-(sigma/r)^6);"
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1496271Z                                           "eps=sqrt(eps1*eps2);"
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1496591Z                                           "sigma=0.5*(sigma1+sigma2)")
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1496911Z       custom_nb.addPerParticleParameter("sigma")
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1497224Z       custom_nb.addPerParticleParameter("eps")
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1497487Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1497649Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1497813Z       ## Integrators
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1498001Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1498160Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1498385Z       # Langevin middle (BAOAB) - recommended for NVT
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1498710Z       integrator = mm.LangevinMiddleIntegrator(
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1499234Z           300*unit.kelvin,        # temperature
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1499551Z           1/unit.picosecond,      # friction coefficient
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1499860Z           2*unit.femtoseconds)    # timestep
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1500103Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1500324Z       # Nosé-Hoover chain
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1511111Z       integrator = mm.NoseHooverIntegrator(
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1511693Z           300*unit.kelvin, 1/unit.picosecond, 2*unit.femtoseconds)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1512037Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1512256Z       # Variable Langevin (adaptive timestep)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1512580Z       integrator = mm.VariableLangevinIntegrator(
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1512923Z           300*unit.kelvin, 1/unit.picosecond, 1e-4)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1513199Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1513368Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1513560Z       ## Alchemical FEP with Perses
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1513803Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1513970Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1514311Z       from perses.app.relative_point_mutation_setup import PointMutationExecutor
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1514687Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1514904Z       # Relative binding FEP for protein mutation
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1515206Z       setup = PointMutationExecutor(
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1515533Z           'protein.pdb', 'A', 50, 'ALA',  # mutate residue 50 to ALA
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1515861Z           solvent_padding=9.0,
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1516110Z           ionic_strength=0.15*unit.molar)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1516377Z       htf = setup.get_apo_htf()
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1516602Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1516772Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1516973Z       ## Serialization and Reproducibility
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1517224Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1517392Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1517589Z       # Save system state as XML
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1517855Z       simulation.saveState('checkpoint.xml')
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1518141Z       # Save system, integrator, state
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1518592Z       with open('system.xml', 'w') as f:
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1518898Z           f.write(mm.XmlSerializer.serialize(system))
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1519589Z       # Restore exactly
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1519839Z       simulation.loadState('checkpoint.xml')
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1520094Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1520267Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1520443Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1520651Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1520866Z       ### NNP (Neural Network Potential) Integration
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1521135Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1521300Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1521503Z       from openmmml import MLPotential
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1521746Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1521924Z       # Run ANI-2x ML potential
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1522173Z       potential = MLPotential('ani2x')
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1522470Z       system = potential.createSystem(pdb.topology)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1522831Z       # Or as mixed ML/MM (QM region with ML, MM for the rest)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1523185Z       mixed_system = potential.createMixedSystem(
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1523539Z           pdb.topology, system, nnpAtoms, interpolate=False)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1523837Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1524011Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:28:26.1525215Z       # OpenMM — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1525469Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1525686Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1525954Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1527026Z       OpenMM is particularly valued for automated workflows, ML-augmented MD, custom enhanced sampling, and integration with modern force fields (OpenFF). It is the engine of choice for large-scale drug discovery pipelines that require programmatic control.
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1527979Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1528153Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1528359Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1528655Z       ### Automated Drug Discovery Pipelines
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1529343Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1529762Z       OpenMM's Python API enables fully automated pipelines:
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1530299Z       - Load thousands of ligand poses from docking
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1530629Z       - Prepare each with GAFF2/OpenFF parameters
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1530933Z       - Run short MD for each complex
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1531241Z       - Compute RMSD stability, binding free energy proxy
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1531554Z       - Rank compounds for follow-up
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1531798Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1532002Z       ### Relative Binding Free Energy with Perses
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1532270Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1532484Z       OpenFE/Perses pipeline for lead optimization:
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1532827Z       - Define perturbation network across compound series
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1533147Z       - Run alchemical FEP for each edge
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1533424Z       - Construct thermodynamic cycle
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1533798Z       - Predict binding free energy differences (ΔΔG)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1534103Z       - Guide medicinal chemistry SAR
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1534334Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1534551Z       ### Protein Folding with Custom Enhanced Sampling
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1534828Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1535071Z       OpenMM's extensibility enables custom enhanced sampling:
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1535452Z       - Implement funnel metadynamics via CustomExternalForce
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1535788Z       - Run temperature-enhanced sampling
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1536149Z       - Integrate with OpenPathSampling for transition path sampling
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1536567Z       - Benchmark new integration algorithms via CustomIntegrator
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1536887Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1537078Z       ### ML Potential Hybrid Simulations
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1537322Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1537567Z       Combine ML potential for QM region with MM for environment:
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1537969Z       - ANI-2x potential for drug-like molecules in explicit solvent
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1538475Z       - mace-off23 for proteins in water
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1538912Z       - QM/MM-like accuracy at much lower cost than DFT-based QM/MM
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1539616Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1539948Z       ### Membrane Permeability Prediction
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1540230Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1540471Z       Compute potential of mean force for drug permeation:
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1541032Z       - Umbrella sampling along membrane normal
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1541499Z       - Weighted histogram analysis (WHAM)
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1541787Z       - Predict passive permeability Peff
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1542046Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1542231Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1542466Z       
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1542688Z       - **Drug discovery**: FEP, binding affinity, ADMET
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1543082Z       - **Method development**: Novel integrators, enhanced sampling, ML/MD
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1543513Z       - **Biophysics**: Protein dynamics, membrane simulation
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1543890Z       - **ML research**: Neural network potential benchmarking
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1544283Z       - **Open science**: Open-source, MIT license; industry and academia
2026-02-26T02:28:26.1544618Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:29:48.1579677Z       # RDKit
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1580020Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1580374Z       **Category:** Cheminformatics  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1581358Z       **Developer/Organization:** Greg Landrum and contributors (originally Rational Discovery LLC, now community-driven)  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1582245Z       **License:** BSD 3-Clause  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1582732Z       **Website:** https://www.rdkit.org  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1583331Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/rdkit/rdkit  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1583824Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1584132Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1584465Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1587127Z       RDKit is the most widely used open-source cheminformatics toolkit in the world. Written in C++ with comprehensive Python bindings, it provides everything needed for molecular informatics: parsing and generating SMILES, InChI, and SDF; computing molecular fingerprints; performing substructure searches; generating 2D depictions and 3D conformers; computing physicochemical descriptors; and handling chemical reactions.
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1590149Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1592043Z       RDKit's BSD license makes it equally suitable for academic research and commercial drug discovery, and it is deeply embedded in the pharmaceutical informatics ecosystem. It serves as the molecular handling layer for many commercial platforms and is the default toolkit for Python-based cheminformatics.
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1594058Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1596324Z       Key strengths include: a comprehensive SMARTS implementation for substructure queries; multiple fingerprint algorithms (Morgan/ECFP, MACCS keys, RDKit path-based, atom pairs); the ETKDG (ETKDGv3) conformer generator; the open MMFF94 and UFF force field implementations for geometry optimization; and Chem.rdMolDescriptors for computing 200+ physicochemical descriptors.
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1598726Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1599300Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1599632Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1600256Z       - **SMILES parsing and canonicalization**: Round-trip SMILES; canonical SMILES generation
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1601158Z       - **InChI/InChIKey**: Via IUPAC's official InChI library
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1601911Z       - **SDF/MOL/MOL2 file I/O**: Read and write standard chemical file formats
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1602854Z       - **Fingerprints**: Morgan (ECFP/FCFP), RDKit path, MACCS Keys, atom pairs, topological torsions
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1603743Z       - **Similarity search**: Tanimoto, Dice, cosine similarity with fingerprints
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1604522Z       - **Substructure search**: SMARTS-based with stereochemistry awareness
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1605429Z       - **2D depiction**: `Draw.MolToImage`, SVG, PNG output; atom coloring, highlighting
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1606330Z       - **3D conformer generation**: ETKDGv3 (default), DG, random, systematic
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1607172Z       - **Force fields**: MMFF94, MMFF94s, UFF for geometry optimization
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1607943Z       - **Descriptors**: 200+ physicochemical descriptors (MW, logP, TPSA, HBD, HBA, rings, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1608686Z       - **Scaffolds**: Bemis-Murcko scaffold decomposition, ring systems
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1609367Z       - **R-group decomposition**: Identify common scaffold and variable R-groups
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1609825Z       - **Reactions**: SMARTS-based reaction handling; retrosynthesis
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1610270Z       - **Standardization**: Molecule cleaning, charge neutralization, tautomers
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1611284Z       - **Chemical registration**: Duplicate detection, InChIKey hashing
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1611724Z       - **Machine learning**: Fingerprint-based descriptors for ML models
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1612176Z       - **RDKit contrib**: Community-contributed extensions (KNIME, ACS, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1612617Z       - **PostgreSQL cartridge**: Store and search chemical databases
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1613114Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1613397Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1613695Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1613866Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1614065Z       # Install via conda (recommended)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1614347Z       conda install -c conda-forge rdkit
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1614711Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1614985Z       # Or pip
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1615244Z       pip install rdkit
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1615456Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1615620Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1615785Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1615997Z       from rdkit import Chem
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1616333Z       from rdkit.Chem import AllChem, Draw, Descriptors, DataStructs
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1616666Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1616837Z       # Parse SMILES
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1617105Z       aspirin = Chem.MolFromSmiles('CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O')
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1617402Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1617593Z       # Compute Morgan fingerprint (ECFP4)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1617988Z       fp = AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(aspirin, radius=2, nBits=2048)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1618368Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1618543Z       # Compute descriptors
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1618812Z       mw = Descriptors.ExactMolWt(aspirin)         # 180.04
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1619400Z       logp = Descriptors.MolLogP(aspirin)           # 1.31
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1619754Z       tpsa = Descriptors.TPSA(aspirin)             # 63.60
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1620090Z       hbd = Descriptors.NumHDonors(aspirin)        # 0
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1620431Z       hba = Descriptors.NumHAcceptors(aspirin)     # 3
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1620709Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1620891Z       # Generate 3D conformer
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1621132Z       mol = Chem.AddHs(aspirin)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1621417Z       AllChem.EmbedMolecule(mol, AllChem.ETKDGv3())
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1621731Z       AllChem.MMFFOptimizeMolecule(mol)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1621986Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1622158Z       # Draw 2D structure
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1622412Z       img = Draw.MolToImage(aspirin, size=(300, 300))
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1622704Z       img.save('aspirin.png')
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1622924Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1623089Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1623256Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1623449Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1624083Z       - [Open Babel](../../tools/open-babel/index.md) – Chemical file format conversion; complementary to RDKit
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1624839Z       - [DeepChem](../../tools/deepchem/index.md) – ML for chemistry; uses RDKit for featurization
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1625491Z       - [Psi4](../../tools/psi4/index.md) – QC calculations; RDKit generates input geometries
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1626098Z       - [OpenMM](../../tools/openmm/index.md) – MD simulations; RDKit for ligand preparation
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1626503Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1626681Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1627082Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/rdkit/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1627613Z       # RDKit — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1627857Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1628033Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1628256Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1629595Z       RDKit implements graph-based molecular representations. Molecules are represented as labeled graphs where atoms are nodes (with properties: atomic number, charge, isotope, chirality) and bonds are edges (with properties: bond order, stereochemistry). All cheminformatics algorithms operate on this graph representation.
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1631176Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1631938Z       **Morgan Algorithm (ECFP)**: RDKit's Morgan fingerprint implements the extended connectivity algorithm:
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1632578Z       1. Initialize each atom with its atomic invariant (atomic number, degree, charge, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1633272Z       2. For each radius r (0 to max_radius), update each atom's identifier by hashing its current identifier and those of its neighbors
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1633984Z       3. Collect all unique identifiers across all radii; each represents a circular substructure
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1634410Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1634767Z       **ETKDG (Experimental-Torsion Knowledge Distance Geometry)**: 3D conformer generation using:
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1635278Z       1. Distance bounds from atomic radii and bond lengths (DG)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1635619Z       2. MMFF charges for electrostatic terms
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1635990Z       3. Empirical torsion angle potentials from CSD crystal data (ET term)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1636460Z       4. ETKDGv3 adds improved stereohandling and intramolecular H-bond terms
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1636816Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1637007Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1637253Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1637504Z       - **SSSR**: Smallest set of smallest rings (for ring systems)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1637967Z       - **Aromaticity**: Hückel and RDKit aromaticity models
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1638399Z       - **Chirality**: R/S and E/Z assignment; enhanced stereochemistry (wedge bonds)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1638899Z       - **Sanitization**: Kekulization, valence checking, aromaticity perception
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1639594Z       - **MaxMin diversity**: MaxMinPicker for diverse subset selection
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1640073Z       - **ROCS/Shape**: Gaussian shape overlap for 3D similarity (via experimental/contrib)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1640555Z       - **FMCS**: Maximum Common Substructure (via FindMCS algorithm)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1640865Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1641053Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1641276Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1641529Z       - **Language**: C++ core with Python bindings (Boost.Python)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1642034Z       - **Python API**: Comprehensive; `rdkit.Chem`, `rdkit.Chem.AllChem`, `rdkit.Chem.Descriptors`
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1642483Z       - **Java API**: Partial; via SWIG
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1642890Z       - **JavaScript (rdkit.js)**: WebAssembly compilation for browser-based cheminformatics
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1643414Z       - **PostgreSQL cartridge**: `rdkit` schema extension for SQL chemical queries
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1643780Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1643960Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1644188Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1644493Z       - Very fast for single-molecule operations (µs per molecule)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1644909Z       - Fingerprint generation: ~10,000 molecules/second on single core
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1645387Z       - Substructure search on large databases: use KNIME or PostgreSQL RDKit cartridge
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1645875Z       - Parallel processing: Use Python multiprocessing or joblib
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1646189Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1646367Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1646590Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1646788Z       - **Boost**: Required for Python bindings
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1647122Z       - **InChI library (IUPAC)**: For InChI/InChIKey computation
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1647459Z       - **FFTW / LAPACK**: For some 3D operations
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1647769Z       - **NumPy**: Array operations in Python layer
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1648115Z       - **pandas**: Often used with RDKit for chemical data frames
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1648488Z       - **scikit-learn**: ML pipelines with RDKit descriptors
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1648782Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1649134Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1649616Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/rdkit/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1650300Z       # RDKit — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1650540Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1650826Z       ## Fingerprint Methods
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1651105Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1651298Z       ### Morgan Fingerprints (ECFP/FCFP)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1651645Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1652054Z       from rdkit.Chem import AllChem
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1652442Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1652725Z       # ECFP4 (radius=2) as bit vector
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1653338Z       fp = AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(mol, radius=2, nBits=2048)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1653777Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1653976Z       # ECFP4 as count vector (more information)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1654311Z       fp_count = AllChem.GetMorganFingerprint(mol, radius=2)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1654606Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1654820Z       # FCFP4 (feature-based, pharmacophore circular)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1655205Z       fp_fcfp = AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(mol, radius=2, 
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1655569Z           nBits=2048, useFeatures=True)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1655808Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1655971Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1656140Z       ### MACCS Keys
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1656345Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1656547Z       from rdkit.Chem import MACCSkeys
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1656876Z       maccs = MACCSkeys.GenMACCSKeys(mol)  # 166-bit structural keys
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1657197Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1657358Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1657532Z       ### Other Fingerprints
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1657745Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1657958Z       from rdkit.Chem import rdMolDescriptors
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1658213Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1658392Z       # RDKit path-based fingerprint
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1658682Z       rdkit_fp = Chem.RDKFingerprint(mol, fpSize=2048)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1659189Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1659405Z       # Atom pair fingerprint
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1659710Z       ap_fp = rdMolDescriptors.GetAtomPairFingerprint(mol)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1660015Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1660195Z       # Topological torsion
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1660527Z       tt_fp = rdMolDescriptors.GetTopologicalTorsionFingerprint(mol)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1660864Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1661025Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1661204Z       ## Similarity Calculations
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1661426Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1661589Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1661782Z       from rdkit import DataStructs
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1662017Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1662197Z       # Tanimoto similarity (Jaccard)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1662493Z       sim = DataStructs.TanimotoSimilarity(fp1, fp2)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1662774Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1662959Z       # Bulk Tanimoto: fp1 against a list
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1663308Z       bulk_sims = DataStructs.BulkTanimotoSimilarity(fp1, fp_list)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1663631Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1663807Z       # Maximum common substructure
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1664055Z       from rdkit.Chem import rdFMCS
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1664446Z       mcs = rdFMCS.FindMCS([mol1, mol2], atomCompare=rdFMCS.AtomCompare.CompareElements)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1664870Z       print(mcs.smartsString)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1665090Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1665252Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1665429Z       ## Substructure Search
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1665640Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1665800Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1665995Z       # SMARTS substructure match
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1666303Z       pattern = Chem.MolFromSmarts('[#6;R][OH]')  # aromatic C-OH
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1666662Z       matches = mol.GetSubstructMatches(pattern)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1666932Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1667110Z       # Lipinski Rule of Five filter
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1667367Z       from rdkit.Chem import Lipinski
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1667620Z       def ro5_filter(mol):
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1667873Z           return (Descriptors.MolWt(mol) <= 500 and
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1668181Z                   Descriptors.MolLogP(mol) <= 5 and
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1668634Z                   Lipinski.NumHDonors(mol) <= 5 and
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1669168Z                   Lipinski.NumHAcceptors(mol) <= 10)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1669450Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1669785Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1669977Z       ## 3D Conformer Generation
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1670200Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1670365Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1670564Z       from rdkit.Chem import AllChem
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1670841Z       from rdkit.Chem.rdDistGeom import ETKDGv3
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1671106Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1671282Z       # Add implicit hydrogens
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1671512Z       mol_3d = Chem.AddHs(mol)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1671723Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1671900Z       # Embed using ETKDGv3
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1672125Z       params = ETKDGv3()
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1672352Z       params.randomSeed = 42
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1672602Z       AllChem.EmbedMolecule(mol_3d, params)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1672856Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1673030Z       # Optimize with MMFF94
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1673291Z       AllChem.MMFFOptimizeMolecule(mol_3d)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1673546Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1673723Z       # Multiple conformers
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1674039Z       AllChem.EmbedMultipleConfs(mol_3d, numConfs=50, params=params)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1674421Z       AllChem.MMFFOptimizeMoleculeConfs(mol_3d)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1674691Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1674854Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1675024Z       ## Pandas Integration
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1675229Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1675390Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1675591Z       from rdkit.Chem import PandasTools
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1675857Z       import pandas as pd
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1676066Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1676246Z       # Load SDF into pandas DataFrame
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1676654Z       df = PandasTools.LoadSDF('compounds.sdf', smilesName='SMILES', molColName='Molecule')
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1677065Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1677261Z       # Compute descriptors for all molecules
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1677581Z       df['MW'] = df['Molecule'].apply(Descriptors.MolWt)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1677925Z       df['LogP'] = df['Molecule'].apply(Descriptors.MolLogP)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1678270Z       df['TPSA'] = df['Molecule'].apply(Descriptors.TPSA)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1678547Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1678735Z       # Render molecules inline in Jupyter
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1679176Z       PandasTools.RenderImagesInAllDataFrames()
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1679453Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1679620Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:29:48.1680199Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/rdkit/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1680741Z       # RDKit — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1680941Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1681118Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1681341Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1682237Z       RDKit is used in virtually every computational drug discovery pipeline for molecular handling, featurization, property calculation, and database management. It is the de facto standard cheminformatics toolkit in academic and industrial settings.
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1683186Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1683357Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1683562Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1683742Z       ### Virtual Screening Pipeline
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1683976Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1684141Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1684331Z       from rdkit import Chem
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1684626Z       from rdkit.Chem import AllChem, Descriptors, DataStructs
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1684946Z       import pandas as pd
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1685150Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1685325Z       # Load screening library
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1685586Z       suppl = Chem.SDMolSupplier('library.sdf')
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1685888Z       mols = [m for m in suppl if m is not None]
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1686142Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1686334Z       # Apply drug-likeness filters
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1686586Z       filtered = [m for m in mols if
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1686996Z                   Descriptors.MolWt(m) <= 500 and
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1687485Z                   Descriptors.MolLogP(m) <= 5 and
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1687892Z                   Descriptors.NumHDonors(m) <= 5]
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1688351Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1688696Z       # Compute ECFP4 fingerprints
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1689426Z       query_fp = AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(query_mol, 2, 2048)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1689926Z       fps = [AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(m, 2, 2048) for m in filtered]
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1690299Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1690480Z       # Similarity-based selection
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1690795Z       sims = DataStructs.BulkTanimotoSimilarity(query_fp, fps)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1691180Z       hits = [filtered[i] for i, s in enumerate(sims) if s > 0.4]
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1691480Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1691642Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1691817Z       ### QSAR Model Building
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1692036Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1692204Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1692449Z       from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1692767Z       from rdkit.Chem import AllChem
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1693016Z       import numpy as np
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1693219Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1693406Z       # Featurize molecules with ECFP4
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1693660Z       def get_ecfp4(smiles):
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1693902Z           mol = Chem.MolFromSmiles(smiles)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1694245Z           return AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(mol, 2, 2048)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1694571Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1694785Z       X = np.array([get_ecfp4(s) for s in smiles_list])
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1695085Z       y = np.array(activity_labels)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1695316Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1695481Z       # Train model
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1695764Z       clf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=100, random_state=42)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1696113Z       clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1696337Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1696498Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1696696Z       ### Chemical Database Analysis (ChEMBL)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1696946Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1697108Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1697350Z       # Analyze drug-likeness of ChEMBL approved drugs
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1697653Z       import chembl_downloader
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1697896Z       df = chembl_downloader.latest()
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1698165Z       # Compute RO5 compliance for all drugs
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1698471Z       df['ro5_compliant'] = df['SMILES'].apply(check_ro5)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1698751Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1698913Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1699346Z       ### Scaffold Analysis for Lead Series
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1699598Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1699762Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1699997Z       from rdkit.Chem.Scaffolds import MurckoScaffold
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1700277Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1700465Z       # Get Bemis-Murcko scaffold
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1700760Z       scaffold = MurckoScaffold.GetScaffoldForMol(mol)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1701098Z       scaffold_smiles = Chem.MolToSmiles(scaffold)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1701553Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1701878Z       # Group compound library by scaffold
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1702204Z       from collections import defaultdict
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1702476Z       scaffold_groups = defaultdict(list)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1702732Z       for mol in library:
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1703004Z           s = MurckoScaffold.MurckoScaffoldSmiles(mol=mol)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1703321Z           scaffold_groups[s].append(mol)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1703564Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1703729Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1703920Z       ### Retrosynthesis and Reaction Handling
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1704172Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1704332Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1704529Z       from rdkit.Chem import AllChem
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1704759Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1704934Z       # Define a reaction SMARTS
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1705469Z       rxn = AllChem.ReactionFromSmarts('[C:1](=[O:2])[OH].[NH2:3]>>[C:1](=[O:2])[NH:3]')
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1705953Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1706129Z       # Apply to reactants
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1706356Z       reactants = (acid, amine)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1706615Z       products = rxn.RunReactants(reactants)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1706870Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1707032Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1707210Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1707440Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1707734Z       - **Drug discovery**: Virtual screening, QSAR, ADMET prediction, registration
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1708214Z       - **Chemical informatics**: Database management, similarity/diversity
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1708638Z       - **Materials science**: Polymer informatics, MOF screening
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1709287Z       - **Food and cosmetics**: Safety assessment, ingredient databases
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1709692Z       - **Agriculture**: Agrochemical screening and registration
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1710111Z       - **Education**: Teaching computational chemistry and cheminformatics

2026-02-26T02:29:48.1711593Z       # RDKit — References
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1711797Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1711968Z       ## Primary Citations
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1712175Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1712352Z       1. **RDKit (main reference)**:  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1712970Z          Landrum, G. RDKit: Open-source cheminformatics. https://www.rdkit.org  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1713493Z          (No single paper; cite the software with version number)
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1713791Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1713984Z       2. **ETKDGv3 conformer generation**:  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1714638Z          Riniker, S. & Landrum, G. A. (2015). Better informed distance geometry: Using what we know to improve conformation generation. *J. Chem. Inf. Model.* 55, 2562.  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1715324Z          https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jcim.5b00654
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1715590Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1715768Z       3. **Morgan fingerprints**:  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1716374Z          Morgan, H. L. (1965). The generation of a unique machine description for chemical structures. *J. Chem. Doc.* 5, 107. https://doi.org/10.1021/c160017a018
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1716979Z       
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1717159Z       4. **MMFF force fields**:  
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1718220Z          Halgren, T. A. (1996). Merck molecular force field. *J. Comput. Chem.* 17, 490–519. https://doi.org/10.1002/(SICI)1096-987X(199604)17:5/6<490::AID-JCC1>3.0.CO;2-P
2026-02-26T02:29:48.1718843Z

2026-02-26T02:31:01.9162133Z       # Open Babel
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9162464Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9163006Z       **Category:** Cheminformatics, Chemical File Format Conversion  
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9164233Z       **Developer/Organization:** Open Babel community (originally OpenEye Scientific Software contribution; now independent)  
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9165286Z       **License:** GNU General Public License v2  
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9165846Z       **Website:** https://openbabel.org  
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9166428Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/openbabel/openbabel  
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9166956Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9167261Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9167581Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9169746Z       Open Babel is a chemical toolbox designed to speak the many languages of chemical data. It supports interconversion between more than 110 chemical file formats, including SMILES, InChI, SDF, MOL2, PDB, CIF, XYZ, Gaussian input/output, VASP POSCAR, Quantum ESPRESSO input, AMBER, GROMACS, LAMMPS, and many more.
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9171456Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9173222Z       Originally based on the Babel program by Andrew Dalke, Open Babel was rewritten in C++ and released under the GPL. It provides both a command-line tool (`obabel`) and a C++/Python library (`pybel`) for programmatic access. Open Babel is an essential utility in computational chemistry workflows, serving as the "Swiss Army knife" of format conversion and chemical data manipulation.
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9175599Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9177437Z       Beyond format conversion, Open Babel provides: 3D coordinate generation (via its built-in force field optimizer), fingerprint computation, SMARTS substructure searching, charge model calculations (Gasteiger, AM1BCC), and force field energy evaluation (MMFF94, UFF, GAFF, MM2).
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9180187Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9180518Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9180880Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9181650Z       - **110+ formats**: Comprehensive format support including quantum chemistry, MD, databases, spectroscopy
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9182935Z       - **Command-line tool**: `obabel` for simple conversions; powerful filter and modification options
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9184069Z       - **Python API**: `pybel` for scripting; also accessible via `openbabel` Python module
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9185064Z       - **3D generation**: `--gen3D` option generates 3D coordinates from 2D or SMILES
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9185896Z       - **Fingerprints**: FP2 (path-based), FP3 (SMARTS), FP4 (SMARTS groups), MACCS (166), Tanimoto similarity
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9186718Z       - **Force fields**: MMFF94, UFF, GAFF, MM2 for energy calculation and geometry optimization
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9187234Z       - **Charge models**: Gasteiger, AM1BCC, EEM, MMFF partial charges
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9187716Z       - **Geometry operations**: Hydrogens add/remove, 3D generation, energy minimization
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9188217Z       - **Chemical properties**: MW, logP (XlogP3), TPSA, polar/nonpolar SA
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9188894Z       - **Reaction SMILES**: Read and write reaction files
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9189758Z       - **Protonation**: pKa-based protonation state assignment
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9190180Z       - **Spectroscopy**: IR, Raman (via SMILES/structure libraries)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9190501Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9190674Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9190866Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9191029Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9191204Z       # Install
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9198467Z       conda install -c conda-forge openbabel
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9198843Z       # or: brew install open-babel (macOS)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9199394Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9199600Z       # Basic conversion: SDF to SMILES
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9199911Z       obabel compounds.sdf -osmi > compounds.smi
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9200191Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9200397Z       # SMILES to 3D SDF with hydrogen addition
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9200758Z       obabel -:"CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O" --gen3D -h -osdf > aspirin_3d.sdf
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9201108Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9201297Z       # Batch convert all PDB to MOL2
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9201628Z       obabel *.pdb -omol2 -m  # -m = multiple output files
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9201924Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9202122Z       # Convert SMILES to Gaussian input
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9202405Z       obabel aspirin.smi -ogjf -O aspirin.gjf
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9202675Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9202860Z       # Filter by molecular weight
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9203178Z       obabel library.sdf -osdf --filter "MW<500" > filtered.sdf
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9203496Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9203689Z       # Compute fingerprint similarity
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9203979Z       obabel -:"CC(=O)O" -:"CCC(=O)O" --tanimoto
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9204244Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9204414Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9204584Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9204782Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9205352Z       - [RDKit](../../tools/rdkit/index.md) – More comprehensive cheminformatics; Python-first
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9206086Z       - [Avogadro](../../tools/avogadro/index.md) – GUI for structure building; uses Open Babel internally
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9206799Z       - [MOPAC](../../tools/mopac/index.md) – Semi-empirical QM; Open Babel converts to MOPAC format
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9207489Z       - [Quantum ESPRESSO](../../tools/quantum-espresso/index.md) – Open Babel can convert to QE input
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9207927Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9208111Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9208546Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/open-babel/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9209345Z       # Open Babel — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9209600Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9209785Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9210019Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9210844Z       Open Babel's core is a molecule object model (OBMol) that stores atoms, bonds, and their properties. Converters (OBFormat plugins) implement readers and writers for each supported format. The conversion engine handles:
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9211749Z       - Atom typing (hybridization, aromaticity)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9212087Z       - Perception of bonds, rings, and stereochemistry
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9212415Z       - Coordinate generation and transformation
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9212681Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9212867Z       **Fingerprint algorithms**:
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9213273Z       - FP2: Path-based fingerprint using linear atom paths up to length 7 in SMILES notation
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9213904Z       - FP3/FP4: SMARTS-based fingerprints using predefined SMARTS query lists
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9214614Z       - MACCS: 166 structural key patterns as SMARTS
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9215038Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9215236Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9215483Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9215792Z       - **Rotor detection**: Identifies freely rotatable bonds for conformer generation
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9216537Z       - **Weighted rotor search**: Systematic conformer generator using weighted random walk
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9217216Z       - **Steepest descent / conjugate gradient**: Force field minimization for 3D generation
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9217782Z       - **Gasteiger charges**: Iterative electronegativity equalization for partial charges
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9218341Z       - **AM1BCC**: AM1 + bond charge correction (important for AMBER/GAFF parameterization)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9218868Z       - **Aromaticity models**: Default, OpenEye, Daylight, and MMFF aromaticity models
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9219587Z       - **InChI**: Uses IUPAC InChI library for InChI generation and parsing
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9220068Z       - **SMARTS matching**: Recursive SMARTS support for complex substructure queries
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9220446Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9220628Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9220847Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9221026Z       - **Language**: C++
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9221401Z       - **Python bindings**: `openbabel` Python module (via SWIG); also `pybel` (higher-level)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9222042Z       - **Plugin architecture**: Format converters, fingerprint algorithms, and force fields are all plugins
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9222664Z       - **wxWidgets GUI**: `avogadro` (Avogadro uses Open Babel) and `openbabel-gui` (separate)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9223206Z       - **Thread safety**: Limited; not designed for concurrent molecular operations
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9223582Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9223765Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9223999Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9224241Z       - Format conversion: Very fast (microseconds per molecule)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9224666Z       - 3D generation: ~1 second per molecule with force field optimization
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9225185Z       - Not designed for large-scale fingerprint searches (use RDKit or PostgreSQL cartridge)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9225597Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9225776Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9226011Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9226209Z       - **InChI library**: IUPAC InChI algorithm
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9226520Z       - **libxml2**: XML format parsing (CML, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9226832Z       - **Eigen**: Linear algebra for 3D operations
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9227150Z       - **zlib**: Compressed file handling (.gz formats)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9227496Z       - **Avogadro**: Uses Open Babel as its chemistry backend
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9227794Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9227976Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9228395Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/open-babel/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9229188Z       # Open Babel — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9229452Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9229637Z       ## Supported Formats (Selected)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9229876Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9230053Z       ### Molecular/Structural
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9230289Z       | Format | Extension | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9230543Z       |--------|-----------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9230835Z       | SMILES | .smi, .can | Canonical SMILES generation |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9231182Z       | InChI | .inchi | IUPAC InChI via official library |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9231537Z       | SDF/MOL | .sdf, .mol | Standard 2D/3D structure files |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9231887Z       | MOL2 (Tripos) | .mol2 | Atom types for AMBER/DOCK |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9232205Z       | PDB | .pdb | Protein Data Bank format |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9232508Z       | XYZ | .xyz | Simple Cartesian coordinates |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9232806Z       | CML | .cml | Chemical Markup Language |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9233123Z       | CIF | .cif | Crystallographic Information File |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9233409Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9233590Z       ### Quantum Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9233811Z       | Format | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9234018Z       |--------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9234297Z       | Gaussian input (.gjf, .com) | Route card and geometry |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9234846Z       | Gaussian output (.log, .out) | Parse energy, geometry, frequencies |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9235632Z       | MOPAC input/output (.mop, .out) | PM6/PM7 calculations |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9235970Z       | NWChem input | NWChem format |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9236237Z       | ORCA input (.inp) | ORCA format |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9236502Z       | Psi4 input | Psi4 format |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9236768Z       | Quantum ESPRESSO (.pwi) | pw.x input |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9237025Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9237203Z       ### Molecular Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9237431Z       | Format | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9237646Z       |--------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9237918Z       | AMBER (.rst7, .mol2) | AMBER coordinates and topology |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9238243Z       | GROMACS (.gro) | GROMACS structure |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9238536Z       | LAMMPS data | LAMMPS atom/molecule data |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9238862Z       | CHARMM (.psf) | Limited support |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9239423Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9239607Z       ### Materials Science
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9239827Z       | Format | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9240036Z       |--------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9240275Z       | VASP POSCAR/CONTCAR | Crystal structure |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9240562Z       | CIF | Crystal structure |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9240811Z       | WIEN2k | LAPW input |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9241046Z       | ABINIT | ab initio input |
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9241274Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9241458Z       ## Command-Line Usage
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9241668Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9241832Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9242020Z       # Format conversion
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9242266Z       obabel input.pdb -omol2 -O output.mol2
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9242525Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9242716Z       # 3D coordinate generation from SMILES
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9243030Z       obabel -:"c1ccccc1" --gen3D -h -osdf -O benzene.sdf
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9243320Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9243499Z       # Add/remove hydrogens
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9243795Z       obabel protein.pdb -h -opdb -O protein_H.pdb   # Add H
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9244188Z       obabel protein.pdb -d -opdb -O protein_noH.pdb  # Remove H
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9244511Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9244694Z       # Molecular properties
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9244966Z       obabel aspirin.sdf --append MW logP TPSA -osmi
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9245247Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9245421Z       # Substructure filter
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9245726Z       obabel library.sdf -osdf --filter "c1ccccc1"  # Aromatic ring
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9246049Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9246229Z       # Tanimoto similarity
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9246506Z       obabel query.sdf library.sdf --tanimoto --fastsearch
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9246799Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9246988Z       # Create fingerprint index database
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9247349Z       obabel library.sdf --addfp -O library_fp.fs  # Index for fast search
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9247802Z       obabel query.sdf library_fp.fs --tanimoto 0.7  # Search with Tc > 0.7
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9248148Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9248332Z       # Generate 3D from CSV of SMILES
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9248654Z       obabel smiles.csv -i "SMILES" --gen3D -osdf -O output.sdf
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9249139Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9249342Z       # Protonation at pH 7.4
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9249613Z       obabel drug.sdf -p 7.4 -osdf -O drug_ph7.sdf
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9249885Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9250050Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9250231Z       ## Python API (pybel)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9250439Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9250604Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9250795Z       import pybel
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9250977Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9251160Z       # Convert SMILES to 3D SDF
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9251453Z       mol = pybel.readstring("smi", "CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O")
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9251752Z       mol.make3D()
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9251965Z       mol.write("sdf", "aspirin.sdf")
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9252204Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9252625Z       # Compute properties
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9252856Z       print(f"MW: {mol.molwt:.2f}")
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9253214Z       print(f"Formula: {mol.formula}")
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9253457Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9253633Z       # Iterate over SDF file
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9253907Z       for mol in pybel.readfile("sdf", "library.sdf"):
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9254626Z           print(f"{mol.title}: MW={mol.molwt:.2f}, LogP={mol.calcdesc()['logP']:.2f}")
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9255235Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9255521Z       # Fingerprint similarity
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9255910Z       fp1 = mol1.calcfp("FP2")
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9256145Z       fp2 = mol2.calcfp("FP2")
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9256405Z       tanimoto = fp1 | fp2  # Tanimoto coefficient
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9256678Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9256847Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9257020Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9257223Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9257403Z       ### Fast Fingerprint Search
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9257640Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9257801Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9257987Z       # Build index
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9258218Z       obabel library.sdf --addfp -O library.fs
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9258479Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9258659Z       # Search (fast, sub-linear time)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9259149Z       obabel query.sdf library.fs --tanimoto 0.7 -oreport
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9259454Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9259620Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9259799Z       ### Crystal Structure Conversion
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9260034Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9260195Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9260462Z       # CIF → POSCAR (for VASP)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9260722Z       obabel structure.cif -ovasp -O POSCAR
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9260983Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9261179Z       # POSCAR → XYZ
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9261404Z       obabel POSCAR -oxyz -O structure.xyz
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9261655Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9261823Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9262001Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9262436Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/open-babel/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9262986Z       # Open Babel — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9263203Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9263381Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9263603Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9264188Z       Open Babel is used as a universal file format converter in computational chemistry pipelines, enabling interoperability between different codes and databases.
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9264839Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9265014Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9265219Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9265460Z       ### Format Conversion in Computational Chemistry Pipelines
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9265773Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9265933Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9266170Z       # Convert ChEMBL SDF download to SMILES for screening
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9266531Z       obabel chembl_drugs.sdf.gz -osmi > chembl_drugs.smi
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9266816Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9267072Z       # Convert docking results (MOL2) to SDF for RDKit analysis  
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9267463Z       obabel docking_results.mol2 -osdf > docking_results.sdf
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9267766Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9268002Z       # Convert protein PDB to MOL2 for AMBER parameterization
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9268347Z       obabel protein.pdb -omol2 -O protein.mol2
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9268617Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9268784Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9269166Z       ### Generating 3D Structures from SMILES
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9269444Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9269828Z       The classic use case: take SMILES strings and generate 3D coordinates for docking or QM calculations:
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9270343Z       - Input: list of SMILES from chemical databases
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9270665Z       - Open Babel 3D generation: ~1s per molecule
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9271060Z       - Output: SDF with 3D coordinates ready for docking (AutoDock, Glide, DOCK)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9271424Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9271788Z       ### Crystal Structure Interconversion
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9272160Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9272885Z       Materials scientists use Open Babel to convert between crystal structure formats:
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9273595Z       - CIF → POSCAR (for VASP DFT)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9273930Z       - CIF → pw.x input (for Quantum ESPRESSO)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9274265Z       - POSCAR → XYZ (for visualization)
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9274658Z       - This enables structure from CSD/ICSD databases to be used in any DFT code
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9275032Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9275261Z       ### Hydrogen Bond Donor/Acceptor Pre-Processing
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9275540Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9275705Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9275966Z       # Add hydrogens at physiological pH 7.4 for all compounds
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9276350Z       obabel library.sdf -p 7.4 -h -osdf -O library_pH7.sdf
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9276650Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9276821Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9277015Z       ### Reaction File Conversion
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9277247Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9277416Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9277663Z       # Reaxys RXN → SMILES reactions
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9277953Z       obabel reactions.rxn -osmi > reactions.smi
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9278222Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9278468Z       # Process reaction SMILES for retrosynthesis
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9278785Z       obabel reactions.smi -orxn -O reactions.rxn
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9279255Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9279426Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9279608Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9279848Z       
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9280164Z       - **Drug discovery**: Format conversion, 3D generation, virtual library preparation
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9280647Z       - **Materials science**: Crystal structure format conversion
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9281065Z       - **Academic research**: Interoperability between different codes
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9281469Z       - **Teaching**: Accessible command-line tool for students
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9281864Z       - **Database curation**: Standardize molecule representations
2026-02-26T02:31:01.9282193Z       ENDOFFILE
