2026-02-26T02:32:20.6696803Z       # DeepChem
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6697130Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6697596Z       **Category:** Machine Learning, Cheminformatics, Drug Discovery  
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6698523Z       **Developer/Organization:** DeepChem community (originally Bharath Ramsundar, Pande lab Stanford; now independent)  
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6699671Z       **License:** MIT License  
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6700123Z       **Website:** https://deepchem.io  
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6700650Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/deepchem/deepchem  
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6701141Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6701428Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6701757Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6703602Z       DeepChem is an open-source Python library for deep learning in chemistry, biology, materials science, and drug discovery. It provides a unified framework for applying machine learning and deep learning methods to chemical datasets, building on top of TensorFlow, PyTorch, JAX, and RDKit.
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6705464Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6707697Z       DeepChem democratizes the application of deep learning to chemistry by providing pre-built models (graph convolutional networks, message passing neural networks, transformers), featurizers (molecular fingerprints, graph representations, 3D descriptors), standardized datasets (MoleculeNet benchmark), and easy-to-use pipelines for training and evaluation.
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6710236Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6711963Z       The library is particularly strong for drug discovery tasks: molecular property prediction (solubility, toxicity, bioactivity), virtual screening, reaction prediction, protein-ligand affinity prediction, and quantum chemistry property prediction (QM8, QM9 datasets).
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6713881Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6714192Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6714527Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6715121Z       - **Graph neural networks**: GCN, MPNN, AttentiveFP, DMPNN for molecular property prediction
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6716078Z       - **Transformers**: ChemBERTa, MolBERT for molecular language modeling
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6717406Z       - **MoleculeNet**: 18+ standardized benchmark datasets (ESOL, FreeSolv, Lipophilicity, BACE, BBBP, Tox21, ToxCast, SIDER, ClinTox, HIV, PCBA, MUV, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6718834Z       - **Featurizers**: ECFP, graph convolution, Coulomb matrix, RDKit descriptors, 3D descriptors
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6720040Z       - **Datasets**: Unified dataset API for loading chemical data from files or databases
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6720957Z       - **Splitters**: Random, scaffold, stratified, Butina, time-based splits
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6721906Z       - **Metrics**: Extensive evaluation metrics: AUROC, AUPRC, RMSE, Pearson r, enrichment factors
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6722813Z       - **Hyperparameter optimization**: Hyperopt and Optuna integration
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6723557Z       - **Pre-trained models**: Atom2Vec, Mol2Vec, ChemBERTa checkpoints
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6724927Z       - **Quantum chemistry**: SchNet, DimeNet for QM property prediction from 3D structure
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6725861Z       - **Generative models**: REINVENT, Junction Tree VAE for molecule generation
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6726727Z       - **Multi-task learning**: Train single model on multiple endpoints simultaneously
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6727598Z       - **Transfer learning**: Fine-tune pre-trained molecular representations
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6728349Z       - **Active learning**: Uncertainty-guided experimental design
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6728894Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6729434Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6729757Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6730045Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6730338Z       # Install
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6730655Z       pip install deepchem
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6731021Z       # With PyTorch
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6731364Z       pip install deepchem[torch]
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6731740Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6732022Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6732291Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6732604Z       import deepchem as dc
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6733008Z       import numpy as np
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6733361Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6733661Z       # Load benchmark dataset
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6734169Z       tasks, datasets, transformers = dc.molnet.load_tox21(
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6734777Z           featurizer='ECFP', splitter='scaffold')
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6735283Z       train, valid, test = datasets
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6735696Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6736026Z       # Train a graph convolutional model
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6736702Z       model = dc.models.AttentiveFPModel(n_tasks=len(tasks), mode='classification',
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6737456Z                                           batch_size=50, learning_rate=0.001)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6738000Z       model.fit(train, nb_epoch=30)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6738402Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6738676Z       # Evaluate
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6739339Z       metric = dc.metrics.Metric(dc.metrics.roc_auc_score, np.mean)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6740075Z       train_score = model.evaluate(train, [metric], transformers)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6740782Z       test_score = model.evaluate(test, [metric], transformers)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6741484Z       print(f"Train AUROC: {train_score['mean-roc_auc_score']:.3f}")
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6742173Z       print(f"Test AUROC: {test_score['mean-roc_auc_score']:.3f}")
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6742712Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6742998Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6743280Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6743615Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6744569Z       - [RDKit](../../tools/rdkit/index.md) – Cheminformatics; DeepChem uses RDKit for molecular handling
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6745842Z       - [Open Babel](../../tools/open-babel/index.md) – Format conversion; complements DeepChem pipelines
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6747037Z       - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – QC calculations; generate training data for ML potentials
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6748168Z       - [OpenMM](../../tools/openmm/index.md) – MD simulations; ML/MM hybrid with DeepChem models
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6749337Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6749813Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6750593Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/deepchem/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6751633Z       # DeepChem — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6752076Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6752394Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6752772Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6753347Z       DeepChem applies deep learning architectures to molecular data. Key representations:
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6754076Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6756248Z       **Graph representation**: Molecules as graphs where atoms are nodes and bonds are edges. Node features encode: atomic number, hybridization, formal charge, aromaticity, ring membership. Edge features encode: bond type, ring membership, stereo. Graph neural networks (GNNs) learn representations by aggregating information from local neighborhoods.
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6758542Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6759363Z       **Message Passing Neural Networks (MPNN)**: Each atom aggregates messages from its neighbors:
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6760106Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6760580Z       m_v^(t) = Σ_{w∈N(v)} M(h_v^(t), h_w^(t), e_vw)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6761068Z       h_v^(t+1) = U(h_v^(t), m_v^(t))
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6761557Z       ŷ = R({h_v^(T)})
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6761904Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6762449Z       where M is the message function, U is the update function, R is the readout function.
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6763139Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6764547Z       **Transformer/BERT for molecules**: Pre-train on unlabeled SMILES using masked language modeling; fine-tune on downstream property prediction tasks. ChemBERTa uses RoBERTa architecture on 10M SMILES from PubChem.
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6766012Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6766335Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6766768Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6767288Z       - **Graph Convolutional Network (GCN)**: Kipf & Welling spectral graph conv
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6768034Z       - **MPNN**: Message Passing NN (Gilmer et al. 2017)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6768691Z       - **AttentiveFP**: Attention-based GNN; graph attention pooling
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6769686Z       - **DMPNN**: Directed MPNN for molecular property prediction
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6770483Z       - **SchNet**: 3D-based NN using continuous filter convolutions on atomic positions
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6771376Z       - **DimeNet / DimeNet++**: Directional message passing with distance+angle
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6772194Z       - **Junction Tree VAE**: Hierarchical VAE for valid molecule generation
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6773027Z       - **REINFORCE / REINVENT**: Reinforcement learning for molecule optimization
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6773677Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6773989Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6774376Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6774687Z       - **Language**: Python
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6775266Z       - **Backends**: TensorFlow 2.x (default), PyTorch (torch models), JAX
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6776048Z       - **RDKit integration**: For molecular featurization, SMILES parsing
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6777044Z       - **Data pipeline**: `dc.data.Dataset` → `dc.feat.Featurizer` → `dc.models.Model`
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6777932Z       - **Modular design**: Mix and match featurizers, splitters, models, metrics
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6778520Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6778817Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6779424Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6779887Z       - GPU training supported via PyTorch/TensorFlow CUDA backends
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6780619Z       - Batch processing of large datasets via `dc.data.DiskDataset`
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6781370Z       - Pre-trained models enable transfer learning with minimal data
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6782062Z       - Typical training time: minutes to hours on modern GPU
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6782581Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6783230Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6783687Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6784304Z       - **RDKit**: Molecular handling, SMILES parsing, fingerprints
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6785025Z       - **PyTorch / PyTorch Geometric (PyG)**: GNN implementations
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6785621Z       - **TensorFlow**: Alternative backend
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6786152Z       - **scikit-learn**: Baseline models and utilities
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6786733Z       - **MoleculeNet**: Benchmark dataset collection
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6787354Z       - **Transformers (HuggingFace)**: Molecular language models
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6787984Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6788290Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6789204Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/deepchem/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6790233Z       # DeepChem — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6790669Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6790973Z       ## Supported Models
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6791320Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6791642Z       ### Graph Neural Networks
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6792054Z       | Model | Notes | Use Case |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6792474Z       |-------|-------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6792992Z       | GraphConv (GCN) | Spectral GCN | Property prediction |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6793673Z       | AttentiveFP | Graph attention | SOTA on many benchmarks |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6794358Z       | MPNN | Message passing NN | Quantum property prediction |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6794932Z       | DMPNN | Directed MPNN | ChemProp model |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6795572Z       | GAT | Graph attention transformer | General property prediction |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6796178Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6796474Z       ### 3D Models
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6796819Z       | Model | Notes | Use Case |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6797232Z       |-------|-------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6797752Z       | SchNet | Continuous filter conv | QM7, QM8, QM9 properties |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6798436Z       | DimeNet++ | Directional MPNN | Improved 3D accuracy |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6799265Z       | PaiNN | Equivariant GNN | Forces and energies |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6799765Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6800094Z       ### Transformers / Language Models
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6800565Z       | Model | Notes | Use Case |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6800968Z       |-------|-------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6801535Z       | ChemBERTa | RoBERTa on SMILES | Fine-tuning for properties |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6802209Z       | MolBERT | BERT for molecules | Transfer learning |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6802709Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6803011Z       ### Classical ML (baselines)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6803559Z       - Random Forest on ECFP: Strong baseline for classification
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6804355Z       - XGBoost on RDKit descriptors: Often competitive with GNNs for small data
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6805134Z       - Multi-task DNN: Multi-layer perceptron on fingerprints
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6805673Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6805980Z       ## MoleculeNet Datasets
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6806357Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6806673Z       | Dataset | Task | Molecules | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6807143Z       |---------|------|-----------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6807650Z       | ESOL | Regression | 1128 | Aqueous solubility |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6808243Z       | FreeSolv | Regression | 642 | Hydration free energy |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6808861Z       | Lipophilicity | Regression | 4200 | logD at pH 7.4 |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6809625Z       | BACE | Classification | 1513 | BACE1 inhibitors |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6810273Z       | BBBP | Classification | 2050 | Blood-brain barrier permeability |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6810980Z       | Tox21 | Multi-task | 8014 | 12 toxicity assays |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6811557Z       | ToxCast | Multi-task | 8631 | 600+ toxicity assays |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6812209Z       | HIV | Classification | 41127 | HIV replication inhibition |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6812875Z       | SIDER | Multi-task | 1427 | 27 side effect categories |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6813916Z       | ClinTox | Classification | 1491 | Clinical trial toxicity |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6814753Z       | QM7 | Regression | 7165 | Atomization energies (DFT) |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6815397Z       | QM8 | Regression | 22k | Excited state energies (TDDFT) |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6816047Z       | QM9 | Regression | 134k | 12 quantum chemical properties |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6816693Z       | PCBA | Classification | 440k | 128 PubChem bioassays |
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6817214Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6817533Z       ## Typical Training Pipeline
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6817936Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6818218Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6818559Z       import deepchem as dc
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6819159Z       import numpy as np
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6819526Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6819838Z       # 1. Load and featurize dataset
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6820427Z       featurizer = dc.feat.MolGraphConvFeaturizer(use_edges=True)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6821242Z       tasks, datasets, transformers = dc.molnet.load_esol(featurizer=featurizer, 
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6822028Z                                                             splitter='scaffold')
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6822585Z       train, valid, test = datasets
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6822994Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6823280Z       # 2. Build model
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6823675Z       model = dc.models.AttentiveFPModel(
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6824125Z           n_tasks=1,
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6824473Z           mode='regression',
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6824851Z           num_layers=3,
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6825193Z           num_timesteps=2,
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6825562Z           graph_feat_size=200,
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6825929Z           dropout=0.2,
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6826282Z           batch_size=32,
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6826681Z           learning_rate=0.001)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6827052Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6827364Z       # 3. Train with early stopping
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6827796Z       best_rmse = float('inf')
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6828199Z       for epoch in range(100):
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6828618Z           model.fit(train, nb_epoch=1)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6829520Z           valid_score = model.evaluate(valid, [dc.metrics.Metric(dc.metrics.rms_score)],
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6830266Z                                         transformers)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6830769Z           if valid_score['rms_score'] < best_rmse:
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6831276Z               best_rmse = valid_score['rms_score']
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6831773Z               model.save_checkpoint()
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6832183Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6832480Z       # 4. Final evaluation
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6832868Z       model.restore()
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6833447Z       test_score = model.evaluate(test, [dc.metrics.Metric(dc.metrics.rms_score)],
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6834142Z                                     transformers)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6834716Z       print(f"Test RMSE: {test_score['rms_score']:.3f} log mol/L")
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6835249Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6835532Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6835840Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6836207Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6836504Z       ### Multi-Task Learning
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6836875Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6837159Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6837583Z       # Train on multiple toxicity endpoints simultaneously
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6838216Z       tasks, datasets, transformers = dc.molnet.load_tox21()
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6839090Z       model = dc.models.AttentiveFPModel(n_tasks=12, mode='classification')
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6839762Z       model.fit(datasets[0], nb_epoch=50)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6840196Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6840469Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6840818Z       ### Transfer Learning with Pre-trained Features
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6841289Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6841553Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6841902Z       # Use Mol2Vec pre-trained embeddings
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6842434Z       featurizer = dc.feat.Mol2VecFingerprint()
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6842956Z       # Fine-tune on task-specific data
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6843625Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6843909Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6844204Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6845194Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/deepchem/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6846195Z       # DeepChem — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6846568Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6846879Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6847262Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6848699Z       DeepChem is used for molecular property prediction, virtual screening, ADMET modeling, and quantum chemistry property prediction using deep learning. It is particularly valuable when large training datasets are available.
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6850462Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6850765Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6851129Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6851451Z       ### Toxicity Prediction (Tox21)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6851878Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6852376Z       Multi-task GNN for 12 NTP (National Toxicology Program) toxicity assays:
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6853294Z       - Train AttentiveFP on 8000+ molecules × 12 toxicity labels
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6854039Z       - Predict hERG, NR-AR, NR-Aromatase, SR-MMP, and other endpoints
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6854754Z       - AUROC ~0.85 on test set; state-of-the-art for this benchmark
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6855399Z       - Screen large compound libraries for toxic alerts
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6855880Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6856206Z       ### Aqueous Solubility Prediction (ESOL)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6856661Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6857045Z       Predict logS (intrinsic aqueous solubility):
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6857637Z       - Critical ADMET property for oral bioavailability
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6858346Z       - GNN models: RMSE ~0.58 log units (experimental uncertainty ~0.6 log units)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6859408Z       - Better than fingerprint + RF models for structurally diverse compounds
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6860032Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6860363Z       ### QM9 Quantum Property Prediction
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6860811Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6861351Z       SchNet / DimeNet++ on QM9 dataset (134k molecules, 12 DFT-calculated properties):
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6862212Z       - Dipole moment, polarizability, HOMO/LUMO energies, HOMO-LUMO gap
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6862935Z       - Atomization energies, zero-point vibrational energy
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6863648Z       - Replace expensive DFT calculations with instant ML predictions
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6864483Z       - Enable screening of billions of molecules for desired electronic properties
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6865164Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6865509Z       ### Drug-Target Interaction Prediction
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6865960Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6866450Z       Predict binding affinity between drug candidates and protein targets:
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6867104Z       - Input: drug SMILES + protein sequence
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6867589Z       - GraphDTA, DeepDTA, and other models
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6868301Z       - Applications: repurposing, off-target prediction, selectivity profiling
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6869188Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6869585Z       ### Reaction Prediction and Yield Optimization
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6870075Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6870403Z       Deep learning for organic reactions:
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6870960Z       - Predict reaction outcomes from reactant SMILES
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6871698Z       - Optimize reaction conditions (temperature, solvent, catalyst) for yield
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6872543Z       - Retrosynthesis planning via seq2seq or graph-to-graph models
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6873134Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6873461Z       ### Material Property Prediction
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6873895Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6874206Z       Beyond drug discovery:
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6874729Z       - Metal-organic framework (MOF) gas adsorption capacity
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6875431Z       - Perovskite solar cell efficiency from structural features
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6876096Z       - Polymer glass transition temperature (Tg)
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6876916Z       - Crystal stability from composition descriptors
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6877554Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6877856Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6878289Z       
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6878718Z       - **Drug discovery**: ADMET, virtual screening, hit-to-lead
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6879566Z       - **Materials science**: MOF screening, polymer design
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6880268Z       - **Environmental science**: Toxicity, biodegradation prediction
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6881036Z       - **Chemical synthesis**: Reaction prediction, yield optimization
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6881825Z       - **Quantum chemistry**: Replace DFT calculations with ML surrogates
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6882566Z       - **Academic research**: Benchmarking, method development
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6883114Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:32:20.6883425Z

2026-02-26T02:33:40.5725078Z       # VMD (Visual Molecular Dynamics)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5725522Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5725998Z       **Category:** Molecular Visualization, Trajectory Analysis  
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5727394Z       **Developer/Organization:** Theoretical and Computational Biophysics Group, University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (Klaus Schulten, now Emad Tajkhorshid)  
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5728622Z       **License:** Free for non-commercial use (requires registration)  
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5729617Z       **Website:** https://www.ks.uiuc.edu/Research/vmd  
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5730085Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5730361Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5730656Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5733049Z       VMD (Visual Molecular Dynamics) is the premier molecular visualization and analysis program for biomolecular simulations. Developed at UIUC alongside NAMD, VMD is designed to display, animate, and analyze large biomolecular systems using 3D graphics and built-in scripting. It handles single structures and long MD trajectories, supporting files from all major MD codes (NAMD, GROMACS, AMBER, LAMMPS, CHARMM).
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5735751Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5737818Z       VMD's strength lies in its combination of publication-quality 3D rendering, powerful Tcl and Python scripting for analysis, and support for GPU-accelerated computation. The built-in scripting languages allow users to implement complex analysis algorithms, generate publication-quality images, and automate workflows.
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5740088Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5741822Z       VMD is widely used for: trajectory visualization and movie making; RMSD, RMSF, and other structural analysis; protein-ligand interaction analysis; membrane analysis; hydrogen bond analysis; Voronoi analysis; and as the graphical frontend for NAMD simulations (including IMD, Interactive Molecular Dynamics).
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5743796Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5744165Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5744486Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5745178Z       - **Multi-format support**: PSF/DCD (NAMD), TPR/XTC/TRR (GROMACS), PRMTOP/NC (AMBER), XYZ, PDB, CUBE, and many more
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5746334Z       - **Rendering**: High-quality ray tracing via POV-Ray, Tachyon, OptiX (NVIDIA GPU ray tracing)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5747506Z       - **Representations**: Wire, CPK, licorice, ribbon, cartoon, MSMS surface, SURF, QuickSurf (GPU-accelerated)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5748518Z       - **Tcl scripting**: Full Tcl shell; custom analysis scripts; `measure` commands
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5749522Z       - **Python interface**: `VMDpython` for Python-based analysis
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5750156Z       - **RMSD/RMSF**: Built-in trajectory analysis tools
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5750694Z       - **Hydrogen bond analysis**: `hbonds` plug-in
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5751313Z       - **Membrane builder**: `membrane` plug-in; build lipid bilayers
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5752178Z       - **MDFF (Molecular Dynamics Flexible Fitting)**: Fit atomic models into cryo-EM density maps
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5753087Z       - **VolMap**: Volumetric density and occupancy maps from trajectories
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5753877Z       - **Timeline**: Timeline visualization of per-residue properties over MD
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5754918Z       - **Ramachandran**: Ramachandran plot analysis
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5755813Z       - **GPU acceleration**: Particle-based rendering, density maps on GPU
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5756674Z       - **Interactive MD (IMD)**: Connect to running NAMD simulation for real-time steering
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5757457Z       - **Multiseq**: Multiple sequence and structure alignment
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5758196Z       - **NAMD Energy Plugin**: Compute energy components from NAMD trajectories
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5758819Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5759359Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5759679Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5759946Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5760364Z       # Download from https://www.ks.uiuc.edu/Research/vmd/
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5760934Z       # (requires free registration)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5761328Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5761666Z       # Launch VMD with structure and trajectory
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5762129Z       vmd protein.psf traj.dcd
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5762504Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5762785Z       # Load GROMACS files
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5763226Z       vmd -gro system.gro -trr trajectory.trr
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5763682Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5763997Z       # Load PDB from command line
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5764413Z       vmd 1ABC.pdb
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5764740Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5765064Z       # Run a Tcl script from command line
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5765520Z       vmd -e analysis_script.tcl
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5765916Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5766202Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5766504Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5766844Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5767873Z       - [NAMD](../../tools/namd/index.md) – Tightly coupled MD simulator; VMD as analysis/visualization front-end
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5769371Z       - [PyMOL](../../tools/pymol/index.md) – Alternative visualization; better for publication figures
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5770532Z       - [GROMACS](../../tools/gromacs/index.md) – VMD reads GROMACS trajectories (.xtc, .trr)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5771616Z       - [Avogadro](../../tools/avogadro/index.md) – Molecular editor; different use case
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5772293Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5772599Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5773282Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/vmd/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5774230Z       # VMD — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5774637Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5774949Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5775341Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5777142Z       VMD's core is an efficient trajectory reading engine and rendering pipeline. The `atomselect` command implements a Boolean algebra of atomic properties (name, resname, resid, chain, x, y, z, mass, charge, beta, occupancy, etc.) combined with distance and contact queries, enabling complex selections.
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5779274Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5780488Z       **QuickSurf**: GPU-accelerated Gaussian density surface rendering for large proteins and complexes; uses CUDA for real-time density computation and Marching Cubes for surface generation.
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5781853Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5783347Z       **MDFF (Molecular Dynamics Flexible Fitting)**: Couples NAMD simulations to cryo-EM density maps by adding a potential energy term proportional to the negative of the density map at each atomic position, driving the structure into the density.
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5784947Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5785281Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5785717Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5786338Z       - `measure rmsd`: RMSD computation after alignment; Kabsch algorithm for optimal superposition
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5787292Z       - `measure contacts`: Identify atom pairs within a cutoff distance
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5788164Z       - `hbonds`: Identify hydrogen bonds by distance + angle criteria (donor-H...acceptor)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5789222Z       - `measure gofr`: Radial distribution function from trajectory
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5790277Z       - `measure volmap`: Generate volumetric occupancy density from trajectory frames
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5791341Z       - `measure sasa`: Solvent-accessible surface area via Shrake-Rupley algorithm
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5792203Z       - `measure inertia`: Moment of inertia tensor and principal axes
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5792773Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5793077Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5793457Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5793926Z       - **Language**: C++ core with OpenGL rendering; Tcl and Python scripting
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5794615Z       - **OpenGL**: 3D rendering for interactive display
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5795337Z       - **CUDA**: GPU-accelerated QuickSurf, VolMap, OptiX ray tracing (NVIDIA RTX)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5796157Z       - **Tachyon**: Built-in ray tracer for publication-quality images
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5796908Z       - **OptiX**: NVIDIA GPU ray tracing for ambient occlusion, shadows
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5797490Z       - **FLTK**: GUI toolkit
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5798073Z       - **Tcl/Tk**: Scripting language; all VMD operations accessible via Tcl
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5798808Z       - **Python**: Available via `VMDpython` module (VMD 1.9.4+)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5799546Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5799865Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5800273Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5800742Z       - Real-time animation: 30+ fps for systems up to ~100k atoms with GPU
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5801555Z       - Large trajectory loading: Memory-mapped DCD/XTC for trajectories >1 TB
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5802354Z       - GPU-accelerated surface rendering for million-atom systems
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5802922Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5803244Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5803637Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5803996Z       - **NAMD**: Primary MD backend; IMD integration
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5804498Z       - **Tachyon**: Ray tracing
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5804925Z       - **OptiX**: NVIDIA GPU ray tracing
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5805438Z       - **NetCDF library**: AMBER trajectory format
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5806026Z       - **MDFF**: Electron microscopy density fitting plugin
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5806671Z       - **RMSF, Ramachandran, Timeline**: Built-in analysis plugins
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5807208Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5807497Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5808182Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/vmd/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5809371Z       # VMD — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5809784Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5810163Z       ## Tcl Analysis Scripts
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5810562Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5810864Z       ### RMSD Over Trajectory
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5811240Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5811519Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5812020Z       # RMSD of backbone Cα atoms over full trajectory
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5812530Z       mol new protein.psf
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5812951Z       mol addfile trajectory.dcd waitfor all
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5813414Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5813733Z       set nf [molinfo top get numframes]
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5814237Z       set ref [atomselect top "backbone" frame 0]
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5814706Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5815024Z       for {set i 0} {$i < $nf} {incr i} {
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5815533Z           set sel [atomselect top "backbone" frame $i]
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5816051Z           set M [measure fit $sel $ref]
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5816475Z           $sel move $M
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5816848Z           set rmsd [measure rmsd $sel $ref]
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5817296Z           puts "$i $rmsd"
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5817659Z           $sel delete
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5817987Z       }
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5818269Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5818554Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5818866Z       ### Hydrogen Bond Analysis
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5819443Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5819732Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5820086Z       # Count hydrogen bonds over trajectory
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5820563Z       package require hbonds
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5821313Z       hbonds -mol top -writefile yes -da 3.5 -ang 30 -plot yes
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5822148Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5822460Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5822791Z       ### Radial Distribution Function
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5823223Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5823515Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5823808Z       # Water O-O RDF
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5824398Z       set gofr [measure gofr [atomselect top "name OW"] [atomselect top "name OW"] \
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5825158Z                 delta 0.1 rmax 15.0 first 0 last -1 step 1]
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5825674Z       set r [lindex $gofr 0]
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5826075Z       set gr [lindex $gofr 1]
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5826450Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5826743Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5827052Z       ### VolMap: Density Maps
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5827436Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5827775Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5828148Z       # Generate water density map around protein
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5828799Z       volmap density [atomselect top "name OW"] -res 1.0 -mol top \
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5829728Z                      -allframes -combine avg -o water_density.dx
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5830243Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5830530Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5830849Z       ## Visualization Representations
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5831279Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5831605Z       | Representation | Style | Use Case |
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5832071Z       |---------------|-------|---------|
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5832591Z       | New Ribbon | Cartoon | Protein secondary structure |
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5833174Z       | Licorice | Stick | Ligands, sidechains |
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5833699Z       | CPK | Space-fill | Packing, surface |
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5834207Z       | MSMS | Surface | Solvent-excluded surface |
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5834846Z       | QuickSurf | Gaussian surface | Large complexes, real-time |
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5835566Z       | Isosurface | Volumetric | Electron density, MO cube files |
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5836232Z       | VolumeSlice | Cross-section | Density maps |
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5836717Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5837027Z       ## Movie Making
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5837368Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5837649Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5837969Z       # Generate frames for movie
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5838390Z       proc make_movie {start end step} {
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5838887Z           for {set i $start} {$i <= $end} {incr i $step} {
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5839599Z               animate goto $i
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5840110Z               render TachyonInternal [format "frame_%05d.tga" $i]
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5840640Z           }
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5840934Z       }
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5841231Z       make_movie 0 999 5
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5841588Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5841923Z       # Convert frames to movie with ffmpeg:
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5842520Z       # ffmpeg -i frame_%05d.tga -r 30 -c:v libx264 -crf 18 movie.mp4
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5843083Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5843369Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5843681Z       ## Python Interface
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5844045Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5844331Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5844681Z       # VMD Python API (VMD 1.9.4+)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5845092Z       import vmd
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5845472Z       from vmd import molecule, atomsel, measure
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5845939Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5846277Z       molecule.load('psf', 'protein.psf')
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5846768Z       molecule.load('dcd', 'trajectory.dcd')
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5847224Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5847610Z       nframes = molecule.numframes(molecule.get_top())
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5848141Z       for i in range(nframes):
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5848595Z           sel = atomsel.atomsel('backbone', frame=i)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5849288Z           rmsd = measure.rmsd(sel, ref_sel)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5849776Z           print(f"Frame {i}: RMSD = {rmsd:.3f}")
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5850224Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5850505Z       ENDOFFILE

2026-02-26T02:33:40.5852879Z       # VMD — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5853227Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5853541Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5853925Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5855083Z       VMD is used for trajectory visualization, biomolecular analysis, MDFF electron density fitting, publication figures, and as the visualization/analysis interface for NAMD simulations.
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5856420Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5856727Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5857090Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5857419Z       ### Protein Conformational Analysis
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5857868Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5858522Z       - Visualize protein opening/closing motions (kinase activation, enzyme conformational change)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5859722Z       - Timeline plugin: plot secondary structure assignment over trajectory
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5860561Z       - Identify key contacts between drug and protein active site residues
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5861299Z       - Extract representative frames for further analysis
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5861811Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5862131Z       ### Membrane Simulation Analysis
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5862545Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5863024Z       - Visualize lipid bilayer dynamics with QuickSurf + cartoon protein
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5863863Z       - Compute membrane thickness and area per lipid using in-house Tcl scripts
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5864691Z       - Identify lipid-protein contacts for annular lipid shell analysis
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5865445Z       - Compute lipid tail order parameters (using Orient plugin)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5865991Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5866309Z       ### Cryo-EM Model Fitting (MDFF)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5866729Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5867125Z       Molecular Dynamics Flexible Fitting (MDFF) workflow:
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5867752Z       1. Load experimental cryo-EM density map (.dx or .mrc)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5868452Z       2. Load initial atomic model (homology model or X-ray structure)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5869356Z       3. Run NAMD with MDFF potential restraining atoms to density
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5870085Z       4. Iteratively improve fit quality (cross-correlation coefficient)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5870804Z       5. Used for ribosomes, virus capsids, membrane proteins
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5871338Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5871693Z       ### Publication-Quality Molecular Images
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5872158Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5872491Z       VMD with Tachyon or OptiX ray tracing:
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5873007Z       - Ambient occlusion for depth perception
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5873517Z       - Shadows and specular highlights
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5874074Z       - Custom coloring by B-factor, RMSF, or custom property
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5874790Z       - Multi-layer composite figures (protein surface + ligand sticks)
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5875381Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5875754Z       ### Analysis of Ion Permeation Through Channels
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5876225Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5876568Z       - Track ion positions over trajectory
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5877112Z       - Compute 1D density profiles along channel axis
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5877686Z       - Measure residence times at binding sites
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5878333Z       - Visualize ion trajectory with "selections by frame" technique
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5878897Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5879413Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5879834Z       
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5880353Z       - **Structural biology**: Cryo-EM fitting, protein dynamics, complex assembly
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5881198Z       - **Biophysics**: Membrane protein, ion channel, motor protein studies
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5882009Z       - **Drug discovery**: Protein-ligand visualization, binding mode analysis
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5882785Z       - **Biochemistry**: Enzyme mechanism, allosteric communication
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5883914Z       - **Education**: Teaching molecular biology concepts with 3D visualization
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5884716Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:33:40.5885054Z

2026-02-26T02:34:53.3397756Z       # PyMOL
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3398073Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3398426Z       **Category:** Molecular Visualization  
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3400150Z       **Developer/Organization:** Schrödinger, Inc. (commercial); open-source version by Schrödinger + community  
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3401543Z       **License:** Commercial (Schrödinger PyMOL); Open-source (pymol-open-source, BSD-like)  
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3402286Z       **Website:** https://pymol.org  
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3402866Z       **GitHub (open-source):** https://github.com/schrodinger/pymol-open-source  
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3403552Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3403867Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3404202Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3407606Z       PyMOL is the most widely used molecular visualization software for creating publication-quality images of proteins, nucleic acids, and small molecules. Originally created by Warren DeLano in 2000, PyMOL has become the standard tool for structural biology figures in scientific publications. Since DeLano's death in 2009, the commercial version is maintained by Schrödinger, Inc., while the open-source community version is available via GitHub.
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3411252Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3413503Z       PyMOL is distinctive for its intuitive Python scripting API that allows automation of complex visualization tasks, and its ability to produce visually stunning, photorealistic molecular images. The combination of a GUI for interactive exploration and a Python shell for scripting makes it extremely versatile.
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3415637Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3417252Z       PyMOL supports all common structural biology file formats (PDB, mmCIF, MOL/SDF, XYZ, CUBE) and trajectory formats (DCD, XTC, TRR). It has excellent support for electron density maps (CCP4, MRC, DSN6) making it the preferred tool for visualizing protein-ligand crystal structures.
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3419593Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3427692Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3428204Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3428764Z       - **Publication-quality rendering**: Ray tracing, ambient occlusion, depth-cueing
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3429775Z       - **Protein visualization**: Cartoon (ribbons), spectrum coloring, secondary structure
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3430340Z       - **Small molecule display**: Sticks, balls-and-sticks, electron density maps
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3430835Z       - **Density maps**: CCP4/MRC electron density visualization (2Fo-Fc, Fo-Fc)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3431376Z       - **Surface rendering**: Solvent-accessible surface with electrostatic potential coloring
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3431931Z       - **Python scripting**: Full Python API; `cmd` module for all operations
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3432405Z       - **Selection algebra**: Complex atom selections with `cmd.select()`
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3432850Z       - **Alignment**: Sequence-based and structure-based alignment
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3433247Z       - **Measurements**: Distances, angles, dihedral angles
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3433638Z       - **Movie making**: Smooth interpolation between views; morphing
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3434050Z       - **Ligand interaction**: H-bond display, contact analysis
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3434452Z       - **APBS electrostatics**: Surface electrostatic potential maps
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3434897Z       - **Plugins**: Extensive plugin ecosystem (APBS, CSB plugins, movie maker)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3435330Z       - **mmCIF support**: Modern PDB format for large structures
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3435736Z       - **Symmetry mates**: Display crystal packing, biological assemblies
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3436070Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3436243Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3436437Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3436603Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3436817Z       # Install open-source version via conda
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3437130Z       conda install -c conda-forge pymol-open-source
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3437417Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3437839Z       # Or install Schrödinger PyMOL (educational/academic license available)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3438213Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3438391Z       # Launch PyMOL
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3438585Z       pymol
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3438757Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3439174Z       # Fetch and visualize a structure from PDB
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3439468Z       # In PyMOL console:
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3439700Z       # fetch 1HVK       # HIV protease
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3439950Z       # hide all
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3440133Z       # show cartoon
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3440338Z       # color blue, chain A
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3440565Z       # color red, chain B
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3440786Z       # show sticks, organic
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3441000Z       # zoom
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3441194Z       # ray                # ray-trace image
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3441441Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3441613Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3441782Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3441977Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3442404Z       - [VMD](../../tools/vmd/index.md) – Better for trajectory analysis; VMD+PyMOL complementary
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3443081Z       - [VESTA](../../tools/vesta/index.md) – Crystal structure visualization; for materials science
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3444124Z       - [Avogadro](../../tools/avogadro/index.md) – Molecular editor; build structures for PyMOL visualization
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3444867Z       - [ChimeraX](https://www.rbvi.ucsf.edu/chimerax/) – Alternative for cryo-EM and large structures
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3445314Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3445497Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3446026Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/pymol/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3447100Z       # PyMOL — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3447394Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3447576Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3447955Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3449531Z       PyMOL uses a scene graph for 3D rendering where molecules, objects, and representations are nodes. The ray tracing engine (internal or external via POV-Ray) computes lighting, shadows, and reflections for high-quality images.
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3451095Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3453162Z       **Cartoon ribbon algorithm**: Secondary structure (Kabsch-Sander or DSSP algorithm) is assigned to each residue. Helices are rendered as smooth tubes or cylinders; strands as arrows; loops as ribbons. The algorithm smoothly interpolates Cα positions along the backbone.
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3454948Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3456447Z       **Surface rendering**: MSMS (Sanner's Molecular Surface) or built-in dot/solvent-excluded surface computed using a rolling probe sphere algorithm. APBS plugin computes Poisson-Boltzmann electrostatic potential mapped onto this surface.
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3458085Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3458427Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3458866Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3459663Z       - **DSSP secondary structure**: Kabsch-Sander algorithm for helix/strand/loop assignment
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3460657Z       - **Kabsch alignment**: Optimal rigid-body superposition for structure alignment
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3461426Z       - **RMSD calculation**: After Kabsch alignment
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3462009Z       - **H-bond detection**: Distance + angle criteria
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3462961Z       - **Surface computation**: Rolling probe (1.4 Å water) for SAS; MSMS for solvent-excluded
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3464022Z       - **Ray tracing**: Internal ray tracer with ambient occlusion; OpenGL for interactive rendering
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3464776Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3465090Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3465475Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3465849Z       - **Language**: C++ core + Python API layer
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3466440Z       - **Python module**: `pymol.cmd` provides the full API
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3467007Z       - **OpenGL**: Interactive 3D rendering
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3467640Z       - **Internal ray tracer**: Production rendering with ambient occlusion
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3468318Z       - **freeglut/GLUT**: Window and event management
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3469175Z       - **Plugin API**: Python-based plugin system for extensions (APBS, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3470006Z       - **cmd module**: All PyMOL commands accessible via `pymol.cmd` Python API
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3470620Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3470942Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3471343Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3471849Z       - Real-time rendering: Interactive at 30+ fps for systems up to ~500k atoms
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3472827Z       - Ray-traced images: Minutes for a single 3000×2000 image on modern CPU
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3473672Z       - OptiX GPU ray tracing available in Schrödinger version
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3474505Z       - Memory efficient: Large structures (ribosome, virus capsid) handled via mapped files
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3475220Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3475530Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3475926Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3476279Z       - **libpng, libjpeg**: Image format output
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3476889Z       - **APBS**: Poisson-Boltzmann electrostatics (via plugin)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3477750Z       - **Freetype**: Font rendering in figures
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3478473Z       - **NumPy**: Python array operations in scripts
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3479233Z       - **PyQt5**: GUI framework for dialogs and menus
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3479733Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3480039Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3480731Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/pymol/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3481720Z       # PyMOL — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3482146Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3482451Z       ## PyMOL Command Language
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3482834Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3483124Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3483543Z       # Basic PyMOL commands (Python API via pymol.cmd)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3484072Z       from pymol import cmd
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3484459Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3484758Z       # Load structure
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3485131Z       cmd.load("1hvk.pdb")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3485574Z       cmd.fetch("4PZI")   # Directly from PDB
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3486023Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3486340Z       # Representations
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3486702Z       cmd.hide("all")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3487117Z       cmd.show("cartoon", "chain A+B")   # Protein
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3487667Z       cmd.show("sticks", "organic")       # Ligand
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3488204Z       cmd.show("surface", "protein")      # Surface
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3488677Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3489126Z       # Coloring
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3489480Z       cmd.color("blue", "chain A")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3489912Z       cmd.color("yellow", "organic")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3490508Z       cmd.spectrum("b", "blue_white_red", "protein")  # Color by B-factor
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3491086Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3491371Z       # Selections
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3492124Z       cmd.select("active_site", "byres (organic around 5)")  # Residues within 5 Å of ligand
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3492860Z       cmd.show("sticks", "active_site")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3493292Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3493595Z       # Measurements
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3494103Z       cmd.distance("hbond1", "/4PZI//A/ASP/OD1", "/4PZI//B/LIG/NH2", 3.5)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3494695Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3495045Z       # Alignment
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3495495Z       cmd.align("mobile", "reference")   # Align two structures
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3496035Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3496351Z       # High-quality rendering
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3496768Z       cmd.set("ray_shadows", 0)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3497178Z       cmd.set("ambient", 0.25)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3497595Z       cmd.set("antialias", 2)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3498008Z       cmd.ray(2400, 1800)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3498396Z       cmd.png("figure.png", dpi=300)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3498817Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3499310Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3499628Z       ## File Formats Supported
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3500007Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3500286Z       ### Input
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3500641Z       | Format | Extension | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3501065Z       |--------|-----------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3501545Z       | PDB | .pdb | Standard protein structures |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3502145Z       | mmCIF | .cif | Modern PDB format; large structures |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3502713Z       | MOL/SDF | .mol, .sdf | Small molecules |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3503229Z       | MOL2 | .mol2 | Ligands with atom types |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3503727Z       | XYZ | .xyz | Coordinate files |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3504204Z       | CUBE | .cube | Electron density, MOs |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3504756Z       | CCP4/MRC | .ccp4, .mrc | Electron density maps |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3505385Z       | DSN6/BRIX | .dsn6, .brix | Older electron density formats |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3505893Z       | DCD | .dcd | Trajectories |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3506349Z       | XTC/TRR | .xtc, .trr | GROMACS trajectories |
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3506804Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3507100Z       ### Output
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3507840Z       - PNG, TIFF, JPEG: Raster images
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3508307Z       - POV-Ray: Photorealistic rendering
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3509390Z       - PSE: PyMOL session file (save complete visualization state)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3510177Z       - VRML/OBJ: 3D geometry for 3D printing or other visualization
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3510757Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3511071Z       ## Advanced Visualization
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3511458Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3511782Z       ### Electrostatic Surface Potential
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3512217Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3512501Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3512843Z       # Using APBS plugin
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3513257Z       from pymol.plugins import apbs_tools
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3513728Z       # Load protein structure
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3514127Z       cmd.load("protein.pdb")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3514608Z       # Run APBS to generate electrostatic potential
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3515121Z       # Then map onto surface:
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3515549Z       cmd.show("surface", "protein")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3516235Z       cmd.spectrum("electrostatic", "red_white_blue", "protein", minimum=-5, maximum=5)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3516937Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3517226Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3517548Z       ### Morphing Between Conformations
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3517982Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3518262Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3518613Z       cmd.load("open_state.pdb", "open")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3519323Z       cmd.load("closed_state.pdb", "closed")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3519876Z       cmd.morph("morph_obj", "open", "closed", steps=30)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3520423Z       cmd.mplay()  # Play morphing movie
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3520851Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3521137Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3521455Z       ### Custom Coloring by Property
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3521871Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3522153Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3522491Z       import numpy as np
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3522849Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3523234Z       # Color residues by RMSF computed from trajectory
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3523860Z       rmsf = np.loadtxt("rmsf.dat")  # Per-residue RMSF values
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3524424Z       for i, val in enumerate(rmsf):
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3524946Z           cmd.alter(f"resi {i+1} and name CA", f"b={val}")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3525519Z       cmd.spectrum("b", "blue_white_red", "name CA")
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3525996Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3526286Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3526593Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3527296Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/pymol/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3528259Z       # PyMOL — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3528620Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3528926Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3529480Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3530817Z       PyMOL is used for creating publication-quality structural biology figures, drug-protein complex visualization, electron density map display, and automated figure generation for papers and presentations.
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3532303Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3532611Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3532985Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3533302Z       ### Drug Discovery Visualization
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3533734Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3534259Z       Visualize protein-ligand interactions for SAR analysis and publications:
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3534989Z       - Display binding pose with H-bond distances
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3535525Z       - Color protein by electrostatic potential
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3536081Z       - Highlight key pharmacophoric interactions
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3536743Z       - Create figures showing selectivity differences between targets
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3537339Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3537654Z       ### Crystal Structure Analysis
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3538059Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3538411Z       X-ray crystallography result visualization:
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3539225Z       - Display 2Fo-Fc and Fo-Fc electron density maps around ligand
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3540119Z       - Verify ligand placement and conformation
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3541061Z       - Identify unexpected electron density (bound waters, ions, alternate conformations)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3541933Z       - Create JACS/Nature/PNAS-quality figures for publications
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3542474Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3542787Z       ### Protein Family Comparison
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3543199Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3543584Z       Structural alignment for understanding mechanism:
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3544276Z       - Load 5–10 homologous protein structures
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3544859Z       - Align to reference structure with `super` or `align`
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3545485Z       - Display structural conservation and variation
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3546120Z       - Color by sequence conservation using ConSurf integration
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3546670Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3547013Z       ### GPCR and Membrane Protein Visualization
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3547481Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3547851Z       Display transmembrane proteins in context:
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3548413Z       - Insert GPCR into membrane representation
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3549187Z       - Show orthosteric and allosteric binding sites
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3549932Z       - Visualize conformational changes (inactive → active)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3550564Z       - Create figures for signal transduction reviews
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3561396Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3561779Z       ### Protein-Protein Interactions
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3562241Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3562569Z       Display complex interfaces:
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3563037Z       - Color proteins by chain or domain
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3563538Z       - Show interface residues as sticks
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3564065Z       - Measure buried surface area (with plugins)
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3564618Z       - Visualize antibody-antigen binding
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3565057Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3565376Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3565809Z       
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3566334Z       - **Structural biology**: X-ray/cryo-EM structure analysis and publication
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3567160Z       - **Drug discovery**: SAR visualization, binding mode analysis
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3567867Z       - **Biochemistry**: Enzyme mechanism, binding site analysis
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3568646Z       - **Education**: Teaching protein structure and drug-receptor interactions
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3569685Z       - **Pharmaceutical industry**: Regulatory submissions, presentations
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3570332Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:34:53.3570649Z

2026-02-26T02:35:57.7005788Z       # Avogadro
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7006149Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7006602Z       **Category:** Molecular Visualization, Molecular Editor  
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7007618Z       **Developer/Organization:** Avogadro community (Marcus Hanwell, Geoffrey Hutchison, and contributors)  
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7008617Z       **License:** GNU Lesser General Public License v2.1 (LGPL)  
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7009412Z       **Website:** https://avogadro.cc  
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7009915Z       **GitHub:** https://github.com/avogadro/avogadro2  
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7010364Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7010639Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7010931Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7012787Z       Avogadro is an open-source, cross-platform molecular editor and visualizer designed for computational chemistry, molecular modeling, and education. Available on Linux, macOS, and Windows, it provides an intuitive graphical interface for building, editing, and visualizing molecular structures.
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7014717Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7016345Z       Avogadro 2 (the current version) was rewritten with a modern C++/Qt architecture with a plugin-based design. It uses OpenChemistry project libraries (libavogadro, libmsym) and integrates with various quantum chemistry codes through input generation wizards.
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7018104Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7020528Z       A key strength of Avogadro is its use as an **input generator** for computational chemistry codes: it can generate Gaussian, ORCA, GAMESS, NWChem, Psi4, and Quantum ESPRESSO input files directly from a 3D structure drawn in the GUI. This makes it particularly valuable for students and researchers who are not familiar with the text-based input formats of these codes.
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7022828Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7023138Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7023479Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7023997Z       - **Structure building**: Click-based atom placement; draw mode with atom/bond tools
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7025600Z       - **Force field optimization**: MMFF94, UFF, GAFF geometry optimization; energy minimization button
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7026692Z       - **3D coordinate generation**: Clean up molecular geometry automatically
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7027625Z       - **Input generation**: Wizards for Gaussian, ORCA, GAMESS, NWChem, Psi4, Q-Chem, Mopac, QE
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7028610Z       - **Output visualization**: Display MOs, electron density from Gaussian CUBE files
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7029726Z       - **Crystal structure viewer**: Unit cell visualization; symmetry operations display
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7030626Z       - **Spectral data**: IR/Raman frequency visualization; UV/Vis spectrum display
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7031554Z       - **Molecular operations**: Fragment library; symmetry operations; chirality assignment
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7032496Z       - **File formats**: SDF, MOL, MOL2, PDB, XYZ, CML, SMILES, CIF (Open Babel integration)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7033487Z       - **Molecular dynamics**: Built-in MD for geometry exploration (not production MD)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7034320Z       - **Python scripting**: Avogadro Python API for batch operations
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7035058Z       - **Unit cell builder**: Build supercells, apply space group symmetry
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7035629Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7035914Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7036231Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7036517Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7036829Z       # Install via conda
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7037231Z       conda install -c conda-forge avogadro2
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7037662Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7038059Z       # Or download binary from https://avogadro.cc/releases
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7038568Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7038849Z       # Launch Avogadro
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7039372Z       avogadro2
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7039672Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7039952Z       # Build a molecule:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7040313Z       # 1. Select "Draw" tool
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7040699Z       # 2. Click to place atoms
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7041104Z       # 3. Click and drag to form bonds
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7041656Z       # 4. Use Extensions > Molecular Mechanics > Optimize to relax
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7042278Z       # 5. Use Input Generators to create QC input files
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7042753Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7043028Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7043309Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7043635Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7044686Z       - [Open Babel](../../tools/open-babel/index.md) – Format conversion; Avogadro uses Open Babel for some formats
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7045934Z       - [VMD](../../tools/vmd/index.md) – Trajectory visualization; different focus
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7046968Z       - [PyMOL](../../tools/pymol/index.md) – Publication visualization; different use case
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7048166Z       - [VESTA](../../tools/vesta/index.md) – Crystal structure visualization; materials science focus
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7049504Z       - [Gaussian](../../tools/gaussian/index.md) – QC code; Avogadro generates Gaussian input files
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7050252Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7050560Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7051288Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/avogadro/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7052264Z       # Avogadro — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7052781Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7053082Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7053457Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7054952Z       Avogadro implements standard molecular mechanics force fields for geometry optimization and MD. The molecular representation is a graph (atoms + bonds) with 3D coordinates, and operations are performed on this graph.
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7056452Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7056837Z       **Force field implementation** (via OpenBabel/MMFF94):
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7057635Z       - MMFF94: Merck Molecular Force Field — good for organic molecules
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7058443Z       - UFF: Universal Force Field — broad periodic table coverage
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7059643Z       - GAFF: General AMBER Force Field (via Open Babel)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7060433Z       - Used for: 3D coordinate cleaning, hydrogen placement, conformer generation
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7061114Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7062582Z       **Input generator**: Avogadro 2 uses a Python scripting system (via Jupyter-like generators) to construct QC input files from the molecular structure, charge, multiplicity, and user-selected method/basis set.
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7064004Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7064332Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7064755Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7065249Z       - **Force field energy minimization**: Steepest descent, conjugate gradient
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7066081Z       - **Force field MD**: Simple Verlet integrator for geometry exploration
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7066965Z       - **Structure perception**: Bond order perception, hybridization, aromaticity from XYZ
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7067917Z       - **Hydrogen addition**: Add H atoms at standard geometries for input structures
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7068639Z       - **SMILES parsing**: Via Open Babel backend
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7069495Z       - **CIF parsing**: Crystal structure reading with unit cell and symmetry
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7070084Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7070377Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7070745Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7071064Z       - **Language**: C++ with Qt GUI framework
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7071758Z       - **Avogadro 2 core**: libavogadro2 — molecular data model
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7072550Z       - **Plugins**: All representations, tools, and extensions are plugins (C++ or Python)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7073333Z       - **VTK**: Visualization toolkit for some rendering
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7073855Z       - **OpenGL**: 3D molecular rendering
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7074317Z       - **Qt 5**: Cross-platform GUI
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7074917Z       - **Open Babel**: Backend for file format reading/writing and force fields
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7075786Z       - **Python plugin API**: Generate input files and extend functionality via Python
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7076453Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7076755Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7077141Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7077493Z       - Interactive at 30+ fps for up to ~10,000 atoms
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7078148Z       - Not designed for large-scale simulations (no trajectory analysis)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7078910Z       - Force field optimization suitable for molecules up to ~500 atoms
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7079692Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7079995Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7080378Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7080671Z       - **Qt 5**: GUI framework
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7081180Z       - **Open Babel**: Format conversion and force field evaluation
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7081791Z       - **libmsym**: Molecular symmetry analysis
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7082287Z       - **VTK**: Volume rendering (optional)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7082758Z       - **Python**: Plugin scripting
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7083163Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7083462Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7084197Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/avogadro/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7085165Z       # Avogadro — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7085579Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7085865Z       ## Force Field Methods
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7086240Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7086531Z       ### Available Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7087182Z       - **MMFF94**: Merck Molecular Force Field; recommended for drug-like organic molecules
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7088038Z       - **MMFF94s**: Static version of MMFF94; better for conjugated systems
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7088891Z       - **UFF**: Universal Force Field; all elements, including inorganic/organometallic
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7090020Z       - **GAFF**: General AMBER Force Field; organic molecules matching AMBER parameterization
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7091106Z       - **Ghemical**: Simple valence force field; historical
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7091632Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7092104Z       ### Usage in Avogadro
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7092493Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7092764Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7093295Z       Menu: Extensions → Molecular Mechanics → Setup Force Field
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7094059Z                                               → Optimize Geometry (Ctrl+Alt+G)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7094733Z                                               → Molecular Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7095228Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7095505Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7095803Z       ## Input File Generation
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7096178Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7096491Z       Avogadro 2's input generators:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7096950Z       - Build molecule in 3D
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7097351Z       - Set charge, multiplicity
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7097768Z       - Choose method and basis set
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7098211Z       - Select QC code
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7098780Z       - Click Generate → copy/save input file
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7099482Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7099897Z       ### Supported QC Code Input Generators
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7100398Z       | Code | Generator Type |
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7100839Z       |------|---------------|
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7101352Z       | Gaussian 16 | Full input with route line |
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7101967Z       | ORCA | Keyword-based input |
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7102436Z       | GAMESS | Group-based input |
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7102939Z       | NWChem | Task-based input |
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7103496Z       | Psi4 | Python input |
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7103987Z       | Q-Chem | Section-based input |
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7104464Z       | MOPAC | PM7 keyword input |
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7105019Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7105473Z       ### Example: Generate ORCA Input via GUI
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7106031Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7106381Z       1. Draw benzene (or load from file)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7107046Z       2. Extensions → Avogadro (Input Generators) → ORCA
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7107762Z       3. Select: Method = B3LYP, Basis = def2-TZVP, Task = Geometry Optimization
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7108466Z       4. Preview and save `benzene.inp`
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7108901Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7109456Z       ## Visualization of QC Results
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7109987Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7110373Z       ### Molecular Orbital Visualization
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7110831Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7111118Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7111470Z       # After running Gaussian/ORCA/QE:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7111965Z       # Load output file or Gaussian CUBE file
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7112661Z       File → Open → select .fchk or .cube file
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7113117Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7113420Z       # Display HOMO or LUMO
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7114225Z       Extensions → Surfaces → Molecular Orbitals → select orbital → render isosurface
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7114909Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7115194Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7115551Z       ### Vibrational Mode Animation
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7115969Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7116246Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7116762Z       # Load frequency calculation output (.log or .out from Gaussian/ORCA)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7117423Z       # Avogadro auto-detects frequencies
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7118188Z       Extensions → Spectra → IR → click on a frequency to animate that mode
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7118767Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7119222Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7119554Z       ## Crystal Structure Visualization
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7119968Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7120396Z       Avogadro 2 can load CIF files and display crystal structures:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7120941Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7121209Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7121614Z       File → Open → structure.cif
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7121995Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7122286Z       # Display options:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7122654Z       # - Unit cell
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7123002Z       # - Multiple unit cells (supercell)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7123709Z       # - Bond connectivity
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7124087Z       # - Atom labels
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7124589Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7124884Z       ENDOFFILE
