2026-02-26T02:35:57.7126846Z       # Avogadro — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7127205Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7127513Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7127896Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7129837Z       Avogadro is primarily used for building molecular structures, generating QC input files, and visualizing QC output (molecular orbitals, vibrational modes) in an educational or research context. It is particularly valuable for students and researchers new to computational chemistry.
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7131687Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7131979Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7132352Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7132679Z       ### Building Input for QC Calculations
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7133120Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7133443Z       The most common use case:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7133959Z       1. Draw target molecule in Avogadro (drug, reagent, catalyst)
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7134542Z       2. Add hydrogens, clean geometry with MMFF94
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7135039Z       3. Use Input Generator to create Gaussian/ORCA input
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7135649Z       4. Run calculation, then load output back into Avogadro to visualize results
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7136179Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7136450Z       ### Teaching Computational Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7136824Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7137175Z       Avogadro is extensively used in university courses:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7137795Z       - Build molecules and observe MMFF94 energy-minimized geometries
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7138528Z       - Generate G16 input files for freshman-level HF/DFT exercises
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7139339Z       - Visualize HOMO/LUMO orbitals from Gaussian output
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7139873Z       - Animate vibrational normal modes
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7140280Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7140581Z       ### Crystal Structure Exploration
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7140984Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7141390Z       Load CIF files from Cambridge Structural Database (CSD) or ICSD:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7142004Z       - Visualize unit cell contents
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7142541Z       - Display coordination environments in metal complexes
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7143123Z       - Build supercells for property analysis
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7143564Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7143891Z       ### Molecular Orbital Visualization
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7144322Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7144621Z       After DFT or HF calculation:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7145056Z       - Load Gaussian .fchk or CUBE file
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7145577Z       - Display occupied and virtual MOs as isosurfaces
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7146127Z       - Color positive/negative lobes by sign
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7146638Z       - Identify HOMO-LUMO gap visually
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7147075Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7147381Z       ### Spectral Analysis
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7147732Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7148054Z       Load frequency calculation output:
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7148521Z       - Display calculated IR spectrum
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7149295Z       - Click on peaks to animate corresponding vibrational mode
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7149945Z       - Compare calculated vs experimental frequencies
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7150473Z       - Assign observed IR bands
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7150867Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7151168Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7151570Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7152010Z       - **Academic research**: Structure building, QC input generation
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7152700Z       - **Education**: Teaching molecular structure and properties
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7153435Z       - **Crystal engineering**: Visualization of coordination compounds, MOFs
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7154560Z       - **Drug discovery**: Build initial ligand geometries before computational studies
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7155576Z       - **Chemical outreach**: Public science demonstrations with 3D molecules
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7156218Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7156424Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7157038Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/avogadro/references.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7158080Z       # Avogadro — References
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7158463Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7158767Z       ## Primary Citations
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7159347Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7159642Z       1. **Avogadro 1**:  
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7160595Z          Hanwell, M. D. et al. (2012). Avogadro: An advanced semantic chemical editor, visualization, and analysis platform. *J. Cheminform.* 4, 17.  
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7161686Z          https://doi.org/10.1186/1758-2946-4-17
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7162123Z       
2026-02-26T02:35:57.7162434Z       2. **Avogadro 2** (cite as):

2026-02-26T02:37:09.9568272Z       # VESTA
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9568547Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9569493Z       **Category:** Crystal Structure Visualization, Electron Density  
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9570475Z       **Developer/Organization:** Koichi Momma (National Museum of Nature and Science, Japan) and Fujio Izumi  
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9571409Z       **License:** Free for non-commercial use  
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9571980Z       **Website:** https://jp-minerals.org/vesta/en/  
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9572512Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9572821Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9573161Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9576245Z       VESTA (Visualization for Electronic and STructural Analysis) is the premier software for visualizing crystal structures and three-dimensional electron/nuclear densities from DFT calculations. It is widely used by materials scientists, crystallographers, and computational chemists to display the results of DFT calculations performed with VASP, Quantum ESPRESSO, WIEN2k, and other periodic codes.
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9579440Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9584908Z       VESTA excels at visualizing volumetric data: charge density, electron localization function (ELF), local density of states (LDOS), magnetization density, and electrostatic potential. These can be displayed as isosurfaces or two-dimensional slice maps overlaid on crystal structure models.
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9586974Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9588813Z       Available for Windows, macOS, and Linux, VESTA provides an intuitive GUI with point-and-click crystal structure editing, symmetry operations, space group information, and diffraction pattern simulation. It has become a community standard for materials science figures in publications.
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9594655Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9594987Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9595366Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9595994Z       - **Crystal structure display**: Unit cell, atomic positions, bond connectivity, polyhedra
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9597142Z       - **Volumetric data visualization**: Isosurfaces of CHGCAR (VASP), charge.dat (QE), .cube (Gaussian/CP2K)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9598062Z       - **ELF**: Electron Localization Function visualization
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9598730Z       - **Slice views**: 2D cross-sections through volumetric data
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9599865Z       - **Electron density difference maps**: ρ(crystal) - Σρ(atom) for bonding analysis
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9600625Z       - **Magnetic moments**: Display spin density; collinear and noncollinear magnetization
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9601360Z       - **XRD simulation**: Simulate powder diffraction patterns from crystal structure
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9602005Z       - **SAED simulation**: Selected area electron diffraction simulation
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9602454Z       - **Bond length/angle**: Automatic bond detection and measurement
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9603082Z       - **Polyhedra**: Tetrahedral, octahedral, and custom coordination polyhedra display
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9603748Z       - **Supercell construction**: Build supercells by repeating unit cell
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9604403Z       - **Import**: VASP POSCAR/CONTCAR/CHGCAR, QE output, CIF, VESTA format, WIEN2k, ABINIT, CUBE
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9605075Z       - **Export**: CIF, VESTA, DXF, VRML, POV-Ray, OpenInventor; publication-quality images (PNG, TIFF)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9605702Z       - **Space group analysis**: Identify and apply crystallographic symmetry
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9606181Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9606363Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9606564Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9606824Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9607142Z       # Download from https://jp-minerals.org/vesta/en/download.html
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9607628Z       # (Windows .exe installer, macOS .dmg, Linux binary)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9607957Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9608143Z       # Launch VESTA (GUI application)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9608621Z       # File → Open → select POSCAR, CIF, or CHGCAR
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9608897Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9609352Z       # For charge density from VASP:
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9609662Z       # File → Open → CHGCAR
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9610141Z       # Objects → New Object → select "Isosurface" → set level
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9610453Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9610621Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9610895Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9611117Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9611696Z       - [Quantum ESPRESSO](../../tools/quantum-espresso/index.md) – DFT code; VESTA visualizes QE charge density
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9612614Z       - [VASP](../../tools/vasp/index.md) – DFT code; VESTA is primary visualization tool for CHGCAR/LOCPOT
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9613431Z       - [PyMOL](../../tools/pymol/index.md) – Molecular visualization; different focus (biomolecules)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9614212Z       - [VMD](../../tools/vmd/index.md) – Trajectory visualization; can also display CUBE files
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9614877Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9615199Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9615802Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/vesta/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9616469Z       # VESTA — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9616845Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9617033Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9617256Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9617495Z       VESTA handles two main data types:
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9617826Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9618717Z       1. **Crystal structures**: Atomic coordinates in fractional or Cartesian coordinates with unit cell parameters (a, b, c, α, β, γ). Space group symmetry generates equivalent positions.
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9619652Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9620523Z       2. **Volumetric data (charge density, potential)**: 3D scalar fields defined on a uniform grid in the unit cell. The grid data is read from CHGCAR (VASP), charge-density.dat (QE), CUBE (Gaussian), and similar formats.
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9621485Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9622352Z       **Isosurface rendering**: Marching Cubes algorithm extracts the isosurface at a user-specified density value from the 3D grid. The resulting polygon mesh is rendered with OpenGL using Phong shading.
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9623317Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9623656Z       **Powder diffraction simulation**: VESTA computes the theoretical XRD pattern using:
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9624372Z       - Structure factor: F(hkl) = Σ_j f_j(θ) e^{2πi(hx_j + ky_j + lz_j)}
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9624737Z       - Lorentz-polarization factor
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9625192Z       - Multiplicity and thermal effects (Debye-Waller)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9625504Z       - Pseudo-Voigt peak profiles
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9625863Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9626058Z       ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9626304Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9626682Z       - **Marching Cubes**: Isosurface extraction from volumetric grid
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9627116Z       - **Bond detection**: Distance-based with element-specific cutoffs
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9627691Z       - **Space group symmetry**: Full 230 space group operations
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9628248Z       - **Polyhedra**: Central atom + coordinating atom identification; polyhedral rendering
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9628808Z       - **Profile fitting**: Pseudo-Voigt (Gaussian + Lorentzian) for XRD peaks
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9629404Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9629591Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9629808Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9630119Z       - **Language**: C++ with Qt GUI
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9630404Z       - **OpenGL**: 3D rendering with Phong shading
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9630818Z       - **Cross-platform**: Windows, macOS, Linux binaries
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9631359Z       - **No external scientific libraries**: Self-contained with all algorithms implemented internally
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9631931Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9632112Z       ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9632381Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9632733Z       - Interactive rendering: 60 fps for typical crystal structures
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9633417Z       - Large charge density grids (512³): Load time ~10 seconds; interactive thereafter
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9633911Z       - RAM usage: ~100 MB for typical CHGCAR; scales with grid size
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9634349Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9634530Z       ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9634755Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9634930Z       - **Qt**: GUI framework
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9635305Z       - **GLEW / GLUT**: OpenGL extensions
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9635596Z       - **VASP**: Primary source of CHGCAR files
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9635925Z       - **Quantum ESPRESSO**: QE charge density and potential
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9636330Z       - **PHONOPY**: Phonon post-processing (create VESTA-ready structures)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9636991Z       - **VESTA format (.vesta)**: Custom format that saves visualization settings alongside structure
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9637418Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9637755Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9638251Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/vesta/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9638929Z       # VESTA — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9639427Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9639613Z       ## Supported File Formats
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9639838Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9640017Z       ### Structure Input
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9640240Z       | Format | Source Code |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9640471Z       |--------|-------------|
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9640696Z       | POSCAR/CONTCAR | VASP |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9640977Z       | CIF | Crystallographic databases, all DFT codes |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9641357Z       | VESTA (.vesta) | VESTA native; saves visualization settings |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9641715Z       | .xsf | XCrySDen format; PWSCF (QE) |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9642026Z       | Wien2k (.struct) | WIEN2k all-electron code |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9642318Z       | Abinit (.in) | Abinit DFT |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9642723Z       | SHELX (.ins, .res) | Crystallographic refinement |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9643097Z       | XYZ | Generic Cartesian coordinates |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9643422Z       | PDB | Protein Data Bank (limited crystal support) |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9643715Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9643894Z       ### Volumetric Data Input
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9644123Z       | Format | Data |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9644327Z       |--------|------|
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9644567Z       | CHGCAR (VASP) | Charge density + spin density |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9644892Z       | LOCPOT (VASP) | Local electrostatic potential |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9645246Z       | charge-density.dat (QE) | Electron density from pp.x |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9645676Z       | .cube (Gaussian/CP2K) | Electron density, molecular orbitals, ELF |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9646060Z       | .xsf (PWSCF) | Electron density with geometry |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9646363Z       | .dx (VMD format) | Volumetric data |
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9646615Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9646798Z       ## Typical Workflows
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9647005Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9647196Z       ### Visualizing VASP Charge Density
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9647441Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9647661Z       1. Run VASP calculation (LCHARG = .TRUE. in INCAR)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9648038Z       2. Open VESTA → File → Open → select CHGCAR
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9648399Z       3. Objects menu → New Object → Type: Isosurface
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9648784Z       4. Set isovalue (try ~0.02 e/Å³ for valence density)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9649300Z       5. Adjust color, opacity, style
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9649671Z       6. File → Export → PNG for publication
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9650009Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9650209Z       ### Electron Density Difference (Bonding)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9650464Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9650623Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9650857Z       # Step 1: Compute total density (CHGCAR from VASP run)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9651207Z       # Step 2: Compute superposition of atomic densities
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9651576Z       # (VESTA can compute this from isolated atom densities)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9651897Z       # Step 3: Load both in VESTA
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9652253Z       # Objects → Properties → 2D Data Display (select slice)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9652609Z       # Positive difference: charge accumulation (bonding)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9652967Z       # Negative difference: charge depletion (antibonding)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9653474Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9653733Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9654022Z       ### Powder XRD Simulation
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9654380Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9654711Z       1. Load crystal structure (CIF or POSCAR)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9655430Z       2. Utilities → Powder Diffraction → set λ (Cu Kα = 1.5406 Å)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9656063Z       3. Set 2θ range and peak width
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9656579Z       4. Export pattern as CSV for comparison with experiment
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9657057Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9657363Z       ### Magnetic Structure Visualization
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9658024Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9658862Z       - Load structure with magnetic moments (VASP OUTCAR or mcif format)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9659917Z       - Properties → Magnetic moments → enable display
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9660437Z       - Arrows show local magnetic moment direction and magnitude
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9660874Z       - Visualize ferromagnetic, antiferromagnetic, ferrimagnetic ordering
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9661225Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9661562Z       ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9661911Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9662192Z       ### Slice Views (2D Maps)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9662420Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9662580Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9662855Z       # View 2D electron density map:
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9663425Z       Objects → New Object → 2D data display
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9663767Z       # Select plane (e.g., (110) plane)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9664067Z       # Adjust color mapping (rainbow, blue-white-red)
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9664385Z       # Overlay on crystal structure
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9664618Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9664780Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9664964Z       ### Building Supercells
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9665179Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9665341Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9665614Z       Edit → Edit Data → Transform → Build Supercell
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9665940Z       # Set nx × ny × nz repetitions
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9666240Z       # Useful for NEB or phonon force constant calculations
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9666530Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9666696Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9666871Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9667273Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/vesta/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9667789Z       # VESTA — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9667994Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9668171Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9668387Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9669330Z       VESTA is the standard tool for visualizing DFT calculation outputs in materials science: charge density, ELF, potential, and crystal structures from VASP, Quantum ESPRESSO, WIEN2k, and other codes.
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9670107Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9670278Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9670484Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9670695Z       ### Charge Density Analysis of Chemical Bonds
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9670969Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9671190Z       Visualizing chemical bonding in inorganic solids:
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9671577Z       - Display charge accumulation between Fe-O bonds in iron oxides
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9672018Z       - Electron density difference maps for covalent vs ionic character
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9672682Z       - ELF visualization to identify covalent bonds, lone pairs, metallic regions
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9673192Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9673391Z       ### Band Structure Visualization Support
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9673649Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9674041Z       While VESTA doesn't compute band structures, it visualizes the crystal structure and first Brillouin zone:
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9674622Z       - Display crystal structure alongside computed band structure
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9675148Z       - Identify high-symmetry k-points (Γ, X, M, etc.) from unit cell geometry
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9675596Z       - Visualize surface Brillouin zones for surface calculations
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9675911Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9676098Z       ### Crystal Polymorph Comparison
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9676340Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9676548Z       Pharmaceutical and materials polymorphism:
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9676911Z       - Load different crystal forms (polymorph I, II, III) of a drug
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9677298Z       - Compare unit cell parameters, packing arrangements
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9677642Z       - Display H-bond networks in molecular crystals
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9677951Z       - Support crystal engineering research
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9678203Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9678393Z       ### Electrostatic Potential Surfaces
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9678830Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9679233Z       Map LOCPOT (VASP) onto molecular surface:
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9679706Z       - Identify nucleophilic/electrophilic regions in solids
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9680070Z       - Compare work functions at different surfaces
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9680386Z       - Study charge redistribution at interfaces
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9680653Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9680855Z       ### Simulated X-ray and Electron Diffraction
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9681115Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9681402Z       Validate crystal structure models:
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9681949Z       - Simulate powder XRD pattern from DFT-optimized structure
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9682368Z       - Compare with experimental data to verify phase identification
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9682794Z       - Compute ring patterns for TEM electron diffraction comparison
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9683113Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9683293Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9683522Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9683816Z       - **Materials science**: Electronic structure, bonding, phase identification
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9684271Z       - **Crystallography**: Crystal structure refinement support
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9684661Z       - **Battery research**: Li/Na ion sites, conductivity channels
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9685090Z       - **Superconductors**: Charge density in BCS and high-Tc superconductors
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9685557Z       - **Magnetic materials**: Spin density, magnetic ordering visualization
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9693423Z       - **Minerals and geoscience**: High-pressure phase visualization
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9693938Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9694141Z       
2026-02-26T02:37:09.9694577Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/vesta/references.md << 'ENDOFFILE'

2026-02-26T02:38:32.8166889Z       # SwissADME
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8167262Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8167629Z       **Category:** Toxicity & ADMET Prediction  
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8168876Z       **Developer/Organization:** Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics (SIB), Molecular Modeling Group (Vincent Zoete, Antoine Daina, Olivier Michielin)  
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8170350Z       **License:** Free for academic and commercial use (web service)  
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8171000Z       **Website:** http://www.swissadme.ch  
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8171465Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8171756Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8172076Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8174255Z       SwissADME is a free web-based tool for computing ADME (Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion) properties and assessing drug-likeness of small organic molecules. Developed at the Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics (SIB) and published in 2017, it has become one of the most widely used tools for early-stage drug discovery and medicinal chemistry.
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8176288Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8178399Z       SwissADME accepts SMILES strings or drawn structures and computes a comprehensive panel of physicochemical, pharmacokinetic, drug-likeness, and medicinal chemistry properties in seconds. Its signature visualization — the "Boiled Egg" model for brain penetration and passive absorption — has become an iconic representation in drug discovery.
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8180970Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8182427Z       The tool is completely free with no registration required, making it accessible to academic researchers, medicinal chemists, and students worldwide. It can process up to 100 molecules simultaneously via its web interface.
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8184054Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8184365Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8184725Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8185485Z       - **Physicochemical properties**: MW, logP, logD, TPSA, HBD, HBA, rotatable bonds, aromatic rings, stereocenters
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8186674Z       - **Lipophilicity models**: iLogP, XLOGP3, WLOGP, MLOGP, SILICOS-IT, consensus LogP
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8187687Z       - **Water solubility**: ESOL, Ali, SILICOS-IT solubility models; predicted LogS
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8188316Z       - **Pharmacokinetics**:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8188584Z         - GI absorption (high/low)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8188873Z         - Blood-brain barrier (BBB) permeation
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8189540Z         - P-gp substrate prediction
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8189878Z         - CYP450 inhibition: CYP1A2, CYP2C9, CYP2C19, CYP2D6, CYP3A4
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8190218Z         - Skin permeation (log Kp)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8190587Z       - **Drug-likeness rules**: Lipinski's Ro5, Ghose, Veber, Egan, Muegge filters
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8191199Z       - **Bioavailability score**: Predicted oral bioavailability (0–1 scale)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8191636Z       - **PAINS alerts**: Pan-assay interference compound alerts
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8192021Z       - **Brenk alerts**: Structural alerts for problematic groups
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8192425Z       - **Medicinal chemistry**: Lead-likeness, drug-like score
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8193287Z       - **Boiled Egg Model**: Visual HIA (human intestinal absorption) vs BBB prediction plot
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8193790Z       - **Radar chart**: Drug-likeness radar visualization (6 properties)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8194121Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8194295Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8194485Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8194646Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8194854Z       # Web interface (no installation required):
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8195157Z       # 1. Go to http://www.swissadme.ch
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8195432Z       # 2. Enter SMILES or draw structure
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8195686Z       # 3. Click "Run"
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8195950Z       # 4. Inspect results table, Boiled Egg, and radar chart
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8196246Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8196474Z       # Example SMILES for aspirin: CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8196839Z       # Example SMILES for ibuprofen: CC(C)Cc1ccc(cc1)C(C)C(=O)O
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8197215Z       # Example SMILES for paracetamol: CC(=O)Nc1ccc(O)cc1
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8197498Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8197680Z       # Batch processing:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8197982Z       # Copy-paste up to 100 SMILES (one per line) in the text box
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8198295Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8198466Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8198637Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8198834Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8199652Z       - [pkCSM](../../tools/pkcsm/index.md) – Complementary ADMET prediction; more endpoints including toxicity
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8200397Z       - [RDKit](../../tools/rdkit/index.md) – Compute physicochemical descriptors programmatically
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8201088Z       - [DeepChem](../../tools/deepchem/index.md) – ML-based ADMET models for large-scale screening
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8201772Z       - [ADMETlab 2.0](https://admetmesh.scbdd.com/) – Alternative comprehensive ADMET web tool
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8202192Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8202374Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8202819Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/swissadme/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8203403Z       # SwissADME — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8203663Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8203847Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8204067Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8204592Z       SwissADME uses a combination of physicochemical descriptor calculation and machine learning models trained on experimental ADMET datasets.
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8205178Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8205385Z       ### Physicochemical Descriptors (Calculated)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8205655Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8205974Z       All physicochemical properties are computed directly from 2D molecular structure:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8206425Z       - **MW**: Molecular weight from atomic masses
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8206855Z       - **logP**: Consensus of 5 prediction models (iLogP, XLOGP3, WLOGP, MLOGP, SILICOS-IT)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8207357Z       - **TPSA**: Topological polar surface area using Ertl's fragment method
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8207849Z       - **HBD/HBA**: Counting H-bond donors (OH + NH groups) and acceptors (O + N atoms)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8208447Z       - **Rotatable bonds**: Non-terminal single bonds (excludes amide C-N due to partial double-bond character)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8208909Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8209297Z       ### Machine Learning Models
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8209526Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8209715Z       | Property | Method | Training Data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8210011Z       |----------|--------|--------------|
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8210424Z       | GI absorption | WLOGP ≤ 0.17 (regression) | Experimental HIA data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8210875Z       | BBB permeation | Boiled Egg (TPSA + WLOGP) | Brain/plasma ratio data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8211337Z       | P-gp substrate | SVM classification | Substrate/non-substrate data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8211769Z       | CYP1A2 inhibitor | Random forest | ChEMBL bioactivity data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8212333Z       | CYP2C9 inhibitor | Random forest | ChEMBL bioactivity data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8212853Z       | CYP2C19 inhibitor | Random forest | ChEMBL bioactivity data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8213255Z       | CYP2D6 inhibitor | Random forest | ChEMBL bioactivity data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8213651Z       | CYP3A4 inhibitor | Random forest | ChEMBL bioactivity data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8214071Z       | Skin permeation | Regression (MW, logP) | Experimental log Kp data |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8214447Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8214626Z       ### The Boiled Egg Model
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8214847Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8215193Z       The Boiled Egg is a 2D plot of WLOGP (x-axis) vs TPSA (y-axis) with empirically derived regions:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8215846Z       - **White (egg white)**: HIA (High GI absorption) region — TPSA < 140 Å², -1 < WLOGP < 5.88
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8216458Z       - **Yellow (yolk)**: BBB permeation region — smaller, lipophilic region within the egg
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8216960Z       - Molecules in the white are predicted to have high oral absorption
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8217437Z       - Molecules in the yolk are predicted to also penetrate the blood-brain barrier
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8217809Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8217993Z       ## Lipophilicity Models
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8218209Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8218436Z       SwissADME computes 5 logP models and their consensus:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8218731Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8218906Z       | Model | Method | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8219443Z       |-------|--------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8219750Z       | iLogP | Graph-based | Based on molecular fingerprints |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8220142Z       | XLOGP3 | Atom-based | Widely used; default for many tools |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8220503Z       | WLOGP | Fragment-based | Used in Boiled Egg |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8220812Z       | MLOGP | Topological | Moriguchi logP |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8221135Z       | SILICOS-IT | Fragment-based | Open-source method |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8221515Z       | Consensus LogP | Mean of 5 models | Most robust prediction |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8221830Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8222012Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8222235Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8222429Z       - **Web server**: Python Flask backend
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8222781Z       - **Chemistry**: RDKit for molecule handling, descriptor computation
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8223245Z       - **Models**: Scikit-learn trained models (Random Forest, SVM, regression)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8223717Z       - **Visualization**: D3.js for interactive Boiled Egg and radar charts
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8224227Z       - **No installation required**: Fully web-based; results computed server-side in seconds
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8224635Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8224813Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8225224Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/swissadme/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8225781Z       # SwissADME — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8226032Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8226213Z       ## Computed Properties
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8226439Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8226622Z       ### Physicochemical Properties
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8226881Z       | Property | Method | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8227122Z       |----------|--------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8227356Z       | MW | Atomic masses | Da |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8227658Z       | Heavy atom count | Atom counting | Non-hydrogen atoms |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8228046Z       | Aromatic heavy atoms | SSSR analysis | % of heavy atoms |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8228400Z       | Fraction Csp3 | Counting | Sp3 C / total C |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8228728Z       | Rotatable bonds | SMARTS filter | RDKit SMARTS |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8229323Z       | H-bond donors | N-H + O-H | Lipinski definition |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8229682Z       | H-bond acceptors | N + O atoms | Lipinski definition |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8230014Z       | TPSA | Fragment method | Ertl et al. 2000 |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8230501Z       | logP (5 methods) | Various | See Technology page |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8230941Z       | logS | ESOL, Ali, SILICOS-IT | Aqueous solubility |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8231271Z       | logD | logP + pKa correction | At pH 7.4 |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8231539Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8231719Z       ### Pharmacokinetics
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8231945Z       | Property | Predicted |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8232173Z       |----------|-----------|
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8232408Z       | GI absorption | High / Low |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8232661Z       | BBB permeant | Yes / No |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8232903Z       | P-gp substrate | Yes / No |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8233154Z       | CYP1A2 inhibitor | Yes / No |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8233406Z       | CYP2C9 inhibitor | Yes / No |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8233660Z       | CYP2C19 inhibitor | Yes / No |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8233912Z       | CYP2D6 inhibitor | Yes / No |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8234163Z       | CYP3A4 inhibitor | Yes / No |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8234424Z       | log Kp (skin permeation) | cm/s |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8234670Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8234846Z       ### Drug-Likeness Filters
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8235099Z       | Filter | Parameters | Violations |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8235363Z       |--------|-----------|-----------|
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8235797Z       | Lipinski Ro5 | MW≤500, logP≤5, HBD≤5, HBA≤10 | Shows #violations |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8236290Z       | Ghose | -0.4≤logP≤5.6, 160≤MW≤480, 40≤MR≤130 | Pass/Fail |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8236695Z       | Veber | RotBonds≤10, TPSA≤140 | Pass/Fail |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8237041Z       | Egan | TPSA≤131.6, WLOGP≤5.88 | Pass/Fail |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8237436Z       | Muegge | 200≤MW≤600, -2≤logP≤5, TPSA≤150 | Pass/Fail |
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8237732Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8237914Z       ### Medicinal Chemistry Alerts
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8238342Z       - **PAINS (Pan-Assay Interference Compounds)**: 480 SMARTS alerts for promiscuous compounds
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8238910Z       - **Brenk alerts**: 105 structural alerts (reactive groups, genotoxic groups, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8239763Z       - Both listed with specific matched substructures
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8240048Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8240230Z       ## Bioavailability Score
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8240446Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8240760Z       SwissADME computes the Muegge bioavailability score (0–1):
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8241148Z       - Based on number of satisfied drug-likeness criteria
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8241529Z       - Score 0.55: "Drug-like" threshold in the original publication
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8241844Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8242021Z       ## Using SwissADME via Web API
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8242254Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8242610Z       While primarily a web GUI, SwissADME results can be accessed programmatically via HTTP POST:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8243034Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8243196Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8243389Z       import requests
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8243587Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8243800Z       url = "http://www.swissadme.ch/index.php"
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8244088Z       data = {
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8244366Z           "smi": "CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O\nCCOC(=O)c1ccc(N)cc1",  # Two molecules
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8244714Z           "smiles_input": ""
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8244930Z       }
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8245129Z       response = requests.post(url, data=data)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8245423Z       # Parse HTML response or download CSV
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8245673Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8245837Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8246506Z       Note: For large-scale automated use, consider using SwissADME's underlying descriptor models via their open-source components (RDKit, scikit-learn) or alternative tools like ADMETlab.
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8247504Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8247685Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8248103Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/swissadme/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8248662Z       # SwissADME — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8249268Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8249458Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8249676Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8250422Z       SwissADME is used for early-stage drug discovery ADMET profiling, medicinal chemistry decision-making, teaching pharmacokinetics, and rapid compound prioritization.
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8251110Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8251282Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8251496Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8251677Z       ### Early-Stage ADMET Profiling
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8251918Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8252147Z       Screen compound libraries for oral bioavailability:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8252533Z       1. Input SMILES for 50–100 lead compounds
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8252883Z       2. Filter by Lipinski Ro5 compliance (no more than 1 violation)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8253233Z       3. Check GI absorption (want "High")
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8253502Z       4. Flag PAINS and Brenk alerts
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8253768Z       5. Eliminate CYP inhibitors (hERG risk)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8254157Z       6. Shortlist ~10–20 compounds for synthesis and testing
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8254458Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8254638Z       ### CNS Drug Design
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8254839Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8255106Z       The Boiled Egg model is particularly useful for CNS drug discovery:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8255545Z       - Target "yolk" region: TPSA < 90 Å², WLOGP 1–5
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8255887Z       - Avoid P-gp substrates (reduces CNS penetration)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8256295Z       - Check CYP2D6 inhibition (relevant for CNS drugs metabolized by CYP2D6)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8256738Z       - Examples: CNS drugs in yolk include alprazolam, diazepam
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8257047Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8257265Z       ### Biopharmaceutics Classification System (BCS)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8257549Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8257727Z       Classify compounds by BCS class:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8258070Z       - BCS I (High solubility, High permeability): logS > -4, logP < 3
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8258487Z       - BCS II (Low solubility, High permeability): logS < -4, logP > 3
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8258909Z       - BCS III (High solubility, Low permeability): logS > -4, high TPSA
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8259685Z       - BCS IV (Low sol, Low perm): logS < -4, high TPSA
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8260177Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8260393Z       ### Natural Product Drug-Likeness Assessment
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8260661Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8260965Z       Natural products often violate Lipinski Ro5 but are still orally bioavailable:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8261407Z       - Check "beyond rule of five" (bRo5) properties
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8261796Z       - High Csp3 fraction indicates 3D character (less likely to be PAINS)
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8262324Z       - TPSA < 140 Å² predicts reasonable GI absorption despite MW > 500
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8262658Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8262839Z       ### Teaching Medicinal Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8263110Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8263365Z       SwissADME is used in pharmacy and medicinal chemistry courses:
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8263800Z       - Compare aspirin, ibuprofen, naproxen, paracetamol properties
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8264169Z       - Discuss how structure changes affect ADMET
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8264539Z       - Visual Boiled Egg helps students understand HIA and BBB concepts
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8264871Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8265052Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8265282Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8265521Z       - **Drug discovery**: Early ADMET filtering, lead optimization
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8265933Z       - **Natural product chemistry**: Drug-likeness of plant metabolites
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8266381Z       - **Academic research**: Rapid in silico ADMET for published compounds
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8266804Z       - **Education**: Teaching pharmacokinetics and drug design
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8267203Z       - **Regulatory science**: ADMET documentation for IND applications
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8267553Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8267892Z       
2026-02-26T02:38:32.8268412Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/swissadme/references.md << 'ENDOFFILE'

2026-02-26T02:40:00.6881839Z       # pkCSM
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6882138Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6882495Z       **Category:** Toxicity & ADMET Prediction  
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6883179Z       **Developer/Organization:** David Ascher lab, University of Queensland (formerly Cambridge); David Ascher, Douglas Pires, Tom Blundell  
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6883922Z       **License:** Free for academic and commercial use (web service)  
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6884354Z       **Website:** https://biosig.lab.uq.edu.au/pkcsm/  
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6884662Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6884841Z       ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6885034Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6886388Z       pkCSM is a web-based tool for predicting pharmacokinetic and toxicological properties of drug candidates. Developed by David Ascher and Douglas Pires at the University of Cambridge (now at University of Queensland), pkCSM uses graph-based signatures to encode molecular structure and machine learning models trained on curated experimental datasets.
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6887797Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6889383Z       The name pkCSM reflects its approach: pk (pharmacokinetics) + CSM (graph-based Cutoff Scanning Matrix signatures). Graph-based signatures encode the molecular graph as topological descriptors based on the number of atoms at various topological distances, capturing the molecular environment around each atom more richly than simple fingerprints.
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6891000Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6892876Z       pkCSM provides predictions for 30+ ADMET endpoints covering all five major pharmacokinetic domains plus toxicity. It accepts SMILES and PDB structures (for proteins) and returns predictions with confidence scores, making it suitable for prioritizing drug candidates in early discovery.
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6894945Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6895260Z       ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6895594Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6895883Z       ### Absorption
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6896451Z       - **Caco-2 permeability** (log Papp): Intestinal permeability measurement
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6897448Z       - **HIA (Human Intestinal Absorption)**: % absorbed after oral administration
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6898306Z       - **P-gp substrate/inhibitor**: Efflux transporter recognition
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6898676Z       - **Water solubility** (logS at pH 7.4)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6899248Z       - **Skin permeability** (log Kp)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6899611Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6899793Z       ### Distribution
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6900050Z       - **Plasma protein binding (PPB)**: % bound
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6900692Z       - **Volume of distribution (VDss)**: Apparent Vd (log L/kg)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6901366Z       - **BBB penetration**: Blood-brain barrier (CNS+/CNS-)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6902024Z       - **CNS penetration**: Probability of CNS activity
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6902570Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6902890Z       ### Metabolism
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6903309Z       - **CYP2D6 substrate/inhibitor**
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6903782Z       - **CYP3A4 substrate/inhibitor**
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6904047Z       - **CYP1A2 inhibitor**
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6904429Z       - **CYP2C9 substrate/inhibitor**
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6904679Z       - **CYP2C19 inhibitor**
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6904894Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6905066Z       ### Excretion
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6905352Z       - **Total clearance**: Predicted plasma clearance (log ml/min/kg)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6905758Z       - **Renal OCT2 substrate**: Renal transporter recognition
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6906058Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6906227Z       ### Toxicity
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6906485Z       - **AMES test** (mutagenicity): Salmonella reverse mutation
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6907191Z       - **Max. tolerated dose (MTD)**: mg/kg/day in humans
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6907648Z       - **hERG inhibition**: Cardiotoxicity risk
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6907972Z       - **Oral rat acute toxicity (LD50)**: pLD50 mol/kg
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6908319Z       - **Oral rat chronic toxicity (LOAEL)**: log mg/kg_bw/day
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6908699Z       - **Hepatotoxicity**: Drug-induced liver injury prediction
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6909310Z       - **Skin sensitization**: LLNA test result
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6909760Z       - **T. pyriformis toxicity**: Aquatic toxicity
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6910064Z       - **Minnow toxicity**: Fathead minnow LC50
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6910321Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6910492Z       ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6910678Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6910846Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6911057Z       # Web interface (no installation required):
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6911386Z       # 1. Go to https://biosig.lab.uq.edu.au/pkcsm/
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6911692Z       # 2. Draw structure or enter SMILES
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6911954Z       # 3. Click "Predict"
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6912211Z       # 4. Receive predictions for all 30+ endpoints
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6912477Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6912652Z       # Example SMILES:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6912875Z       # Aspirin: CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6913156Z       # Ibuprofen: CC(C)Cc1ccc(cc1)C(C)C(=O)O
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6913448Z       # Propranolol: CC(C)NCC(O)c1cccc2ccccc12
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6913781Z       # Haloperidol: O=C(CCCN1CCC(CC1)(c1ccc(Cl)cc1)O)c1ccc(F)cc1
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6914091Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6914283Z       # For CNS drugs, particularly check:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6914560Z       # - BBB penetration (want positive)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6914832Z       # - hERG inhibition (want negative)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6915131Z       # - CYP2D6 substrate (often positive for CNS drugs)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6915415Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6915597Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6915774Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6915971Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6916652Z       - [SwissADME](../../tools/swissadme/index.md) – Complementary ADMET tool; stronger drug-likeness visualization
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6917393Z       - [RDKit](../../tools/rdkit/index.md) – Compute molecular descriptors programmatically
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6918028Z       - [DeepChem](../../tools/deepchem/index.md) – ML-based ADMET; scalable to large libraries
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6918675Z       - [ADMETlab 2.0](https://admetmesh.scbdd.com/) – Alternative with 88 ADMET endpoints
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6919339Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6919526Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6919963Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/pkcsm/technology.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6920512Z       # pkCSM — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6920764Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6920946Z       ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6921173Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6922293Z       pkCSM uses **graph-based signatures** (Cutoff Scanning Matrix, CSM) as molecular descriptors. Unlike fingerprints that capture the presence/absence of substructures, CSM descriptors encode the molecular graph topology by counting the number of atoms of each type at each topological distance from a reference atom.
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6923451Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6923663Z       ### Pharmacophoric Distance Graph Signatures
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6923936Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6924150Z       For each atom in the molecule, pkCSM computes:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6924706Z       1. Identify the pharmacophoric type of each atom (hydrophobic, H-bond donor, H-bond acceptor, aromatic, charged, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6925448Z       2. Count atoms of each pharmacophoric type at topological distances 1, 2, 3, ..., n from the reference atom
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6926067Z       3. Concatenate these counts into a pharmacophoric distance graph signature vector
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6926458Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6927298Z       This captures both the local chemical environment (short distances) and global molecular topology (long distances) without losing spatial information.
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6927919Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6928099Z       ### Machine Learning Models
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6928329Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6928542Z       pkCSM trains separate models for each endpoint:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6928822Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6929305Z       | Endpoint Type | Model | Performance |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6929616Z       |-------------|-------|------------|
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6930182Z       | Regression (logS, logP, Caco-2) | Gaussian Process, Support Vector Regression | RMSE ~0.5–0.8 log units |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6930901Z       | Binary classification (BBB, hERG) | Support Vector Machine, Random Forest | AUROC 0.80–0.95 |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6931440Z       | Multi-class | One-vs-rest SVM | Accuracy ~75–85% |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6931727Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6931982Z       Training datasets are curated from literature and databases:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6932398Z       - Caco-2: Pham-The et al. 2013; various literature sources
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6932752Z       - BBB: MDDR, ChEMBL, literature compilation
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6933077Z       - hERG: ChEMBL, binding/patch-clamp assay data
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6933423Z       - Hepatotoxicity: DILIrank database; FDA DILI reports
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6933716Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6933907Z       ## Graph-Based Signature Algorithm
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6934153Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6934315Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6934526Z       # Pseudocode for CSM descriptor generation:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6934812Z       for each atom i in molecule:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6935086Z           for each distance d from 1 to max_distance:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6935418Z               count_dict[pharmacophore_type, d] = 0
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6935709Z           for each atom j in molecule:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6936004Z               if topological_distance(i, j) <= max_distance:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6936320Z                   pt = pharmacophore_type(j)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6936606Z                   d = topological_distance(i, j)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6936882Z                   count_dict[pt, d] += 1
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6937147Z           signature[i] = flatten(count_dict)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6937394Z           
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6937666Z       molecule_signature = aggregate(signatures)  # e.g., sum, max, mean
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6938007Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6938171Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6938352Z       ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6938576Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6938784Z       - **Web server**: Python Flask / Django backend
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6939375Z       - **Chemistry**: RDKit for molecular input handling, SMILES parsing
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6939885Z       - **Models**: scikit-learn (SVM, Random Forest, Gaussian Process) or custom implementations
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6940379Z       - **Signature computation**: Custom Python implementation
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6940741Z       - **API**: REST API for programmatic access
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6941016Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6941199Z       ## Model Validation
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6941407Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6941589Z       pkCSM models are validated by:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6941933Z       - Leave-one-out cross-validation (LOO-CV) for small datasets
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6942284Z       - 5-fold or 10-fold cross-validation
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6942589Z       - External test set evaluation on held-out data
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6942981Z       - Comparison with published experimental data not in training set
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6943319Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6943502Z       ## Applicability Domain
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6943726Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6944456Z       All pkCSM predictions include an applicability domain estimate based on chemical similarity to the training set. Predictions outside the domain are flagged with a warning, indicating reduced confidence.
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6945508Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6945696Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6946196Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/pkcsm/methods.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6946749Z       # pkCSM — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6946994Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6947171Z       ## Prediction Endpoints
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6947391Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6947559Z       ### Absorption
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6947776Z       | Endpoint | Output | Threshold |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6948032Z       |----------|--------|-----------|
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6948316Z       | Water Solubility | log mol/L | > -4: soluble |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6948664Z       | Caco-2 Permeability | log Papp cm/s | > -5.15: high |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6949344Z       | Intestinal Absorption (HIA) | % absorbed | > 30%: absorbed |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6949807Z       | Skin Permeability | log Kp cm/s | — |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6950122Z       | P-gp Substrate | Yes/No | — |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6950429Z       | P-gp I Inhibitor | Yes/No | — |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6950736Z       | P-gp II Inhibitor | Yes/No | — |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6950987Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6951162Z       ### Distribution
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6951381Z       | Endpoint | Output | Threshold |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6951630Z       |----------|--------|-----------|
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6951935Z       | VDss | log L/kg | 0.1–20 L/kg: normal |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6952249Z       | BBB Permeant | Yes/No | — |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6952532Z       | CNS Permeant | Yes/No | — |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6952840Z       | Plasma Protein Binding | % bound | > 90%: highly bound |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6953145Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6953313Z       ### Metabolism
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6953536Z       | Endpoint | Output | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6953771Z       |----------|--------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6954064Z       | CYP2D6 Substrate | Yes/No | Important for CNS drugs |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6954437Z       | CYP3A4 Substrate | Yes/No | Most common drug metabolism |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6954791Z       | CYP1A2 Inhibitor | Yes/No | Liver enzyme |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6955126Z       | CYP2C9 Inhibitor | Yes/No | Warfarin metabolism |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6955475Z       | CYP2C19 Inhibitor | Yes/No | Omeprazole metabolism |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6955820Z       | CYP2D6 Inhibitor | Yes/No | Drug interactions |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6956166Z       | CYP3A4 Inhibitor | Yes/No | Most clinically relevant |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6956460Z

2026-02-26T02:40:00.6956836Z       | Endpoint | Output | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6957074Z       |----------|--------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6957376Z       | Total Clearance | log ml/min/kg | Predicted hepatic+renal |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6957809Z       | Renal OCT2 Substrate | Yes/No | Renal secretion |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6958290Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6958566Z       ### Toxicity
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6958896Z       | Endpoint | Output | Notes |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6959502Z       |----------|--------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6959943Z       | AMES Mutagenicity | Yes/No | Bacterial genotoxicity |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6960431Z       | Max. Tolerated Dose (human) | log mg/kg/day | Clinical dose estimation |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6960833Z       | hERG I Inhibitor | Yes/No | Cardiac safety |
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6961171Z       | hERG II Inhibitor | Yes/No | Different binding mode |

2026-02-26T02:40:00.6963760Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6964133Z       ## Batch Predictions via Web Interface
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6964386Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6964548Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6964851Z       # pkCSM web interface:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6965141Z       # 1. Go to https://biosig.lab.uq.edu.au/pkcsm/
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6965506Z       # 2. Paste multiple SMILES (newline-separated) into text box
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6965857Z       # 3. Or upload CSV/SDF file with SMILES column
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6966140Z       # 4. Download results as CSV
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6966366Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6966548Z       # Example batch input:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6966777Z       CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6967006Z       CC(C)Cc1ccc(cc1)C(C)C(=O)O
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6967244Z       CC(C)NCC(O)c1cccc2ccccc12
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6967463Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6967632Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6967817Z       ## Interpreting Results
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6968032Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6968222Z       ### Decision Rules for Drug Candidates
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6968474Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6968655Z       **Good candidate profile:**
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6968908Z       - HIA > 30% (orally absorbed)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6969449Z       - PPB < 95% (not completely protein-bound)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6969833Z       - BBB: Depends on target (CNS: positive; non-CNS: negative preferred)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6970237Z       - hERG I+II inhibitor: Both negative (cardiac safety)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6970548Z       - AMES mutagenicity: Negative
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6970804Z       - Hepatotoxicity: Negative
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6971161Z       - CYP inhibition: Negative for CYP2C9, CYP3A4 (drug-drug interaction risk)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6971521Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6971696Z       ### Flag for Concern
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6971995Z       - hERG inhibitor + > 50 nM IC50: Clinical QT prolongation risk
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6972408Z       - AMES positive: Genotoxicity; requires in vitro confirmation
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6980696Z       - Hepatotoxicity positive: DILI concern; monitor liver function
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6981280Z       - P-gp substrate + CNS target: Efflux may prevent CNS penetration
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6981645Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6981960Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6982650Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/pkcsm/use-cases.md << 'ENDOFFILE'
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6983232Z       # pkCSM — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6983452Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6983638Z       ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6983859Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6984594Z       pkCSM is used for comprehensive ADMET profiling of drug candidates, particularly for toxicity endpoints (hERG, hepatotoxicity, mutagenicity) that are not included in simpler ADME tools like SwissADME.
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6985403Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6985582Z       ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6985799Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6986025Z       ### Comprehensive ADMET Profiling for IND Package
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6986324Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6986585Z       Before filing an Investigational New Drug (IND) application:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6986992Z       - Predict all 30+ ADMET endpoints for lead candidates
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6987401Z       - Document computational ADMET data to support safety assessment
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6988112Z       - Identify areas requiring experimental follow-up (e.g., hERG inhibition prediction → in vitro hERG patch-clamp assay)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6988726Z       - Compare predicted vs actual experimental data as validation
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6989254Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6989439Z       ### hERG Safety Screening
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6989664Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6989944Z       Cardiac safety is a leading cause of drug withdrawal and attrition:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6990401Z       - Screen all new compounds for predicted hERG I and hERG II inhibition
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6990815Z       - pkCSM hERG model: AUROC ~0.87 on external validation
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6991220Z       - Flag hERG positive compounds for urgent in vitro patch-clamp testing
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6992452Z       - Structural modification guidance: avoid basic amines + aromatic rings
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6992825Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6993023Z       ### Hepatotoxicity Prediction
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6993267Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6993480Z       Drug-induced liver injury (DILI) prediction:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6993842Z       - pkCSM hepatotoxicity model trained on DILIrank database
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6994216Z       - Identify potential DILI compounds before synthesis
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6994582Z       - Compare SAR of hepatotoxic vs non-hepatotoxic analogues
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6995021Z       - Used as complement to SwissADME (which doesn't have hepatotoxicity endpoint)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6995399Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6995592Z       ### BBB and CNS Penetration Assessment
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6995844Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6996029Z       For CNS drug programs:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6996283Z       - CNS drugs require BBB penetrant = Yes
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6996676Z       - Also check P-gp substrate = No (P-gp efflux reduces brain concentration)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6997164Z       - Check CYP2D6 substrate = likely Yes (most CNS drugs metabolized by CYP2D6)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6997609Z       - Target BBB Yes + hERG I = No for preferred CNS candidates
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6997914Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6998109Z       ### Aquatic Toxicity for Green Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6998362Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6998545Z       Environmental safety assessment:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6998879Z       - Predict T. pyriformis and minnow toxicity for new compounds
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6999486Z       - Relevant for industrial chemicals and agrochemicals
2026-02-26T02:40:00.6999951Z       - Compare predicted aquatic toxicity series to guide synthesis of less toxic analogues
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7000356Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7000541Z       ### Combination with SwissADME
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7000776Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7000979Z       pkCSM and SwissADME are complementary:
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7001415Z       - SwissADME: Physicochemical descriptors, drug-likeness rules, logP models, Boiled Egg
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7001987Z       - pkCSM: More ADMET endpoints, including toxicity panel (hERG, hepatotoxicity, AMES)
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7002826Z       - Use both in workflow: SwissADME for drug-likeness, pkCSM for ADMET + toxicity
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7003359Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7003541Z       ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7003774Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7004090Z       - **Pharmaceutical industry**: IND package documentation, lead optimization, DMPK
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7004616Z       - **Drug discovery academia**: Computational ADMET for published drug candidates
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7005117Z       - **Environmental toxicology**: Aquatic and mammalian toxicity prediction
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7005549Z       - **Agrochemical industry**: Pesticide safety screening
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7005969Z       - **Natural product research**: ADMET profiling of plant-derived compounds
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7006338Z       ENDOFFILE
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7006525Z       
2026-02-26T02:40:00.7006931Z       cat > /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/pkcsm/references.md << 'ENDOFFILE'

2026-02-26T02:41:20.0770752Z       ## How to Use This Repository
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0771171Z       
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0771858Z       This repository is organized into **categories** and individual **tool pages**. Each tool page contains:
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0772670Z       
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0773291Z       - **index.md** — Overview, key features, quick start, and related tools
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0774392Z       - **technology.md** — Underlying theory, algorithms, software architecture, and HPC performance
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0775649Z       - **methods.md** — Supported computational methods, input/output formats, and typical workflows
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0776844Z       - **use-cases.md** — Primary application domains, example workflows, and industry usage
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0778041Z       - **references.md** — Primary citations, documentation links, tutorials, and community resources
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0778808Z       
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0779288Z       ### Navigation Tips
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0779652Z       
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0780223Z       - Browse by **category** if you're looking for a class of tools (e.g., all MD simulators).
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0781135Z       - Browse by **tool name** if you already know the tool you want to learn about.
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0782117Z       - Use the search function (if using MkDocs) to find specific methods or features across all tools.
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0782872Z       
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0783190Z       ## Contributing
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0783533Z       
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0784509Z       Contributions are welcome! See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on how to add new tools, improve existing entries, or report errors.
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0785579Z       
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0785853Z       ## License
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0786160Z       
2026-02-26T02:41:20.0787575Z       Documentation in this repository is provided under the [Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Tool-specific licenses are noted on each tool's page.

2026-02-26T02:41:39.1417408Z function:
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1417775Z   name: bash
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1418096Z   args:
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1419447Z     command: cat /home/runner/work/Tools-for-Chemistry/Tools-for-Chemistry/tools/quantum-espresso/index.md
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1420348Z     description: Check QE index content quality
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1423404Z   result: |
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1423727Z     # Quantum ESPRESSO
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1424087Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1424608Z     **Category:** Density Functional Theory (DFT), Ab Initio Molecular Dynamics  
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1425436Z     **Developer/Organization:** SISSA, CINECA, and the QE Foundation  
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1426156Z     **License:** GNU General Public License (GPL v2+)  
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1426757Z     **Website:** https://www.quantum-espresso.org  
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1427319Z     **GitHub:** https://github.com/QEF/q-e  
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1427762Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1428015Z     ## Overview
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1428214Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1429961Z     Quantum ESPRESSO (QE) is an open-source, integrated suite of codes for electronic-structure calculations and materials modeling at the nanoscale based on density functional theory, plane waves, and pseudopotentials. The acronym stands for **opEn-Source Package for Research in Electronic Structure, Simulation, and Optimization**.
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1431412Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1432766Z     Originally developed at SISSA (Scuola Internazionale Superiore di Studi Avanzati, Trieste) and CINECA (Italy), QE has grown into a community-driven project maintained by the QE Foundation and a large international developer base. It is one of the most widely used DFT packages in the world, particularly for condensed matter physics, surface science, and materials science.
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1434101Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1435839Z     Quantum ESPRESSO's core strength is its treatment of periodic systems using plane-wave basis sets combined with pseudopotentials (norm-conserving, ultrasoft, or PAW). The PWscf (Plane-Wave Self-Consistent Field) module handles ground-state DFT, geometry optimization, and ab initio molecular dynamics. Additional modules handle phonon calculations via density functional perturbation theory (DFPT), electron-phonon coupling, response properties, maximally-localized Wannier functions, and more.
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1437630Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1437795Z     ## Key Features
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1437982Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1438330Z     - **Plane-wave DFT** with norm-conserving (NC), ultrasoft (USPP), and PAW pseudopotentials
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1439187Z     - **PWscf module** for self-consistent field (SCF) calculations, band structures, and geometry optimization
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1439853Z     - **PHonon module** for phonon dispersion, IR/Raman intensities, dielectric constants via DFPT
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1440383Z     - **CP module** for Car-Parrinello ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD)
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1441013Z     - **PostProc utilities**: `pp.x` for charge density, ELF, STM images; `bands.x` for band structures; `dos.x` for density of states
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1441805Z     - **Wannier90 interface**: Maximally-localized Wannier functions for transport and topological properties
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1442698Z     - **EPW code**: Electron-phonon coupling and superconductivity via Wannier interpolation
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1443502Z     - **TDDFPT**: Linear-response time-dependent DFT for optical spectra
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1444214Z     - **HP module**: Hubbard U parameters from DFPT (DFT+U+J)
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1444939Z     - **turboTDDFPT**: Turbo Lanczos algorithm for large-scale optical spectra
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1446330Z     - **ENVIRON**: Implicit solvent and electrostatic embedding
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1447101Z     - **GPU acceleration** via CUDA/HIP (experimental and production)
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1447946Z     - **Hybrid exchange-correlation functionals**: PBE0, HSE06 via exact exchange
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1448809Z     - **Extensive XC library**: libxc integration with hundreds of functionals
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1449861Z     - **MPI + OpenMP parallelization** over k-points, plane waves, and bands
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1450761Z     - **AiiDA and ASE integration** for automated high-throughput calculations
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1451509Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1451798Z     ## Quick Start
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1452132Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1452420Z     ```bash
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1452724Z     # Install via conda
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1452962Z     conda install -c conda-forge qe
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1453206Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1453470Z     # Or build from source
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1453991Z     git clone https://github.com/QEF/q-e.git
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1454538Z     cd q-e && ./configure && make all
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1454987Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1455366Z     # Run a self-consistent field calculation
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1455707Z     pw.x < silicon.scf.in > silicon.scf.out
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1463901Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1464229Z     # Basic silicon SCF input (silicon.scf.in)
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1464600Z     # &CONTROL
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1464807Z     #   calculation = 'scf'
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1465147Z     #   outdir = './tmp/'
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1465414Z     #   prefix = 'silicon'
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1465644Z     # /
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1465857Z     # &SYSTEM
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1466227Z     #   ibrav = 2, celldm(1) = 10.21, nat = 2, ntyp = 1
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1466518Z     #   ecutwfc = 30.0
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1466821Z     # /
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1467033Z     # ATOMIC_SPECIES
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1467276Z     #  Si  28.086  Si.pbe-n-rrkjus_psl.1.0.0.UPF
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1467705Z     # ATOMIC_POSITIONS {alat}
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1467952Z     #  Si  0.00  0.00  0.00
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1468170Z     #  Si  0.25  0.25  0.25
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1468506Z     # K_POINTS {automatic}
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1468744Z     #  8 8 8 0 0 0
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1468929Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1469445Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1469621Z     ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1469814Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1470539Z     - [VASP](../../tools/vasp/index.md) – Commercial plane-wave DFT; similar capabilities, different license
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1471374Z     - [CP2K](../../tools/cp2k/index.md) – Mixed Gaussian/plane-wave DFT, strong for AIMD
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1472135Z     - [ORCA](../../tools/orca/index.md) – Molecular DFT/CC; complementary for molecular systems
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1472978Z     - [VESTA](../../tools/vesta/index.md) – Visualization of charge density and crystal structures from QE output
2026-02-26T02:41:39.1473769Z     - [VMD](../../tools/vmd/index.md) – Trajectory visualization for CP module AIMD runs

2026-02-26T02:41:48.5529664Z     # VMD — References
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5530064Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5530364Z     ## Primary Citations
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5530729Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5531070Z     1. **VMD main paper**:  
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5532028Z        Humphrey, W., Dalke, A., Schulten, K. (1996). VMD: Visual molecular dynamics. *J. Mol. Graphics* 14, 33–38.

2026-02-26T02:41:48.5542110Z     # Octopus — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5542365Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5542549Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5542786Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5543108Z     Octopus solves the time-dependent Kohn-Sham (TDKS) equations in real space:
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5543508Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5543675Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5543918Z     i∂ψ_j(r,t)/∂t = H_KS[n(t)] ψ_j(r,t)
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5544176Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5544334Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5545457Z     where H_KS = -½∇² + v_ext(r,t) + v_Hartree[n](r,t) + v_xc[n](r,t). The electron density n(r,t) = Σ_j |ψ_j(r,t)|² drives the self-consistent Hamiltonian at each time step.
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5546466Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5548039Z     Wavefunctions and densities are represented on a real-space grid. The Laplacian is approximated by finite differences (typically 7-point stencil) or by fast Fourier transform on a uniform grid. Pseudopotentials handle the core electrons.
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5549982Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5550308Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5550752Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5551531Z     - **Time propagation**: Magnus exponential midpoint, Lanczos propagator, exponential midpoint, Runge-Kutta (RK4)
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5552612Z     - **Diagonalization**: Conjugate gradient (CG), Davidson, RMMDIIS
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5553374Z     - **Poisson solver**: FFT-based; also supports multi-grid and ISF
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5554353Z     - **PAO (Parallel Adaptive Octree)**: Adaptive mesh refinement for atoms with steep features
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5555453Z     - **Absorbing boundaries**: Complex absorbing potential (CAP), PML (perfectly matched layer) for ionization
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5556347Z     - **Berry phase**: Geometric phases for polarization in periodic systems
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5556705Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5556890Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5557108Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5557293Z     - **Language**: Fortran 90 + C
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5557653Z     - **MPI + OpenMP**: Hybrid parallel; grid points distributed across MPI tasks
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5558105Z     - **CUDA/OpenCL**: GPU acceleration for FFT and grid operations
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5558530Z     - **PFFT**: Parallel FFT for distributed-memory grid computations
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5558913Z     - **libxc**: External XC functional library
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5559497Z     - **libvdwxc**: Non-local vdW correlation for real-space codes
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5559826Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5560009Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5560243Z     
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5560492Z     - Scales to thousands of MPI tasks for large simulation boxes
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5560852Z     - GPU acceleration for FFT-intensive steps
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5561277Z     - Real-space grids: efficient for finite systems, requires large boxes for molecules
2026-02-26T02:41:48.5561693Z     <exited with exit code 0>
