2026-02-26T02:42:35.2902735Z Key accurate facts to use:
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2903816Z - GROMACS: uses LINCS constraints, PME electrostatics, Verlet list, AMBER/CHARMM/OPLS/GROMOS force fields, GPU acceleration via CUDA, written in C++/SIMD
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2905665Z - LAMMPS: Large-scale Atomic/Massively Parallel Simulator, Sandia National Labs, Fortran originally now C++, supports ReaxFF, EAM, Tersoff, AIREBO potentials
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2907378Z - AMBER: Assisted Model Building with Energy Refinement, ff14SB, ff19SB protein force fields, GAFF for small molecules, pmemd.cuda for GPU
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2909181Z - NAMD: uses charm++ parallel framework, CHARMM36 force field, collective variables (colvars), pressure coupling with Langevin piston
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2910545Z - OpenMM: uses OpenCL/CUDA/CPU platforms, CustomForce API, Drude oscillators, constant-pH simulations
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2911634Z - ORCA: RIJCOSX approximation, RI-J, DLPNO-CCSD(T), CASSCF/CASPT2, TD-DFT, EOM-CCSD
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2912755Z - Gaussian: Gaussian basis sets (6-31G*, cc-pVTZ, aug-cc-pVDZ), B3LYP, M06-2X, CCSD(T), NBO analysis, IRC, GFN2-xTB
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2913876Z - NWChem: TCE (Tensor Contraction Engine), AIMD, Car-Parrinello, MP2, CCSD(T), DFT, plane-wave DFT
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2914862Z - CP2K: QUICKSTEP module, GTH pseudopotentials, cp2k DZVP-MOLOPT basis, metadynamics via PLUMED
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2915745Z - Psi4: PSI4NUMPY, density fitting, F12 methods, SAPT, EFP, active space methods
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2916479Z - MOPAC: PM6, PM7, PM3, AM1, RM1 methods; Stewart 2007 paper; COSMO solvation
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2917260Z - Octopus: TDDFT for optical absorption, real-time electron dynamics, Ehrenfest dynamics
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2918055Z - GAMESS: US version from Gordon group, GVB, MCSCF, CCSD(T), FMO method
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2919178Z - RDKit: GetMorganFingerprint, AllChem.EmbedMolecule, Chem.MolFromSmiles, rdMolDescriptors, MMFF94 force field
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2920339Z - Open Babel: obabel command, SDF/MOL2/PDB/SMILES/InChI conversion, 3D structure generation
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2921422Z - DeepChem: GraphConv, AttentiveFP, MPNN models, MoleculeNet benchmark, deepchem.feat, deepchem.models
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2922563Z - VMD: Tcl/Python scripting, STRIDE secondary structure, MDFF, volmap density, trajectory analysis, NAMD integration
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2923541Z - PyMOL: PyMOL scripting, cmd.load, show representation, color schemes, ray tracing
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2924437Z - Avogadro2: Qt5/C++, VTK rendering, Open Chemistry project, avogadro2 Python bindings
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2925294Z - VESTA: CIF file reading, VESTA format, isosurface rendering, Fourier map
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2926178Z - SwissADME: Lipinski RO5, TPSA, LogP (iLogP, XLOGP3), bioavailability radar, BOILED-Egg model
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2927275Z - pkCSM: graph-based signatures, ADMET properties including BBB permeability, CYP inhibition, hERG toxicity
2026-02-26T02:42:35.2927971Z

2026-02-26T02:42:49.5258123Z     === tools/octopus/use-cases.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5258844Z     # Octopus — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5259401Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5259704Z     ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5260079Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5261171Z     Octopus is used for time-domain electron dynamics, attosecond science, and non-equilibrium phenomena that cannot be captured by ground-state or frequency-domain methods.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5262386Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5262680Z     ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5263038Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5263388Z     ### Optical Absorption Spectra via RT-TDDFT
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5263847Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5265113Z     Apply a broadband delta-kick perturbation and propagate TDKS equations for ~20 fs. Fourier transform the time-dependent dipole moment to obtain the full optical absorption spectrum in one calculation.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5266506Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5266834Z     ### High-Harmonic Generation (HHG)
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5267278Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5268702Z     Simulate an atom or molecule in an intense IR laser field (10¹⁴–10¹⁵ W/cm²). Track electron ionization and recombination to compute HHG spectrum — a key process in attosecond science.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5270170Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5270487Z     ### Above-Threshold Ionization
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5270892Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5272021Z     Compute photoelectron spectra of atoms in strong laser fields. Extract ionization rates and above-threshold ionization peaks for comparison with experimental COLTRIMS data.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5273290Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5273618Z     ### Laser-Induced Demagnetization
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5274041Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5274883Z     Study ultrafast demagnetization in transition metals (Fe, Ni, Co) after femtosecond laser excitation using non-collinear spin TDDFT.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5275877Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5276224Z     ### Non-Equilibrium Dynamics in 2D Materials
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5276706Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5277647Z     Study electron-phonon coupling and carrier thermalization in graphene, MoS2 after optical excitation using coupled electron-nuclear RT-TDDFT.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5278716Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5279175Z     ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5279589Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5280164Z     - **Attosecond science**: HHG, above-threshold ionization, electron wavepacket dynamics
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5281130Z     - **Ultrafast spectroscopy**: Transient absorption, pump-probe simulations
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5281914Z     - **Photovoltaics**: Exciton dynamics, carrier generation
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5282639Z     - **Nanophotonics**: Optical properties of quantum dots, nanoclusters
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5283421Z     - **Spintronics**: Laser-induced demagnetization, spin transport
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5283986Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5284304Z     === tools/pymol/references.md ===

2026-02-26T02:42:49.5307001Z     === tools/octopus/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5307559Z     # Octopus — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5307979Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5308279Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5308661Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5309427Z     Octopus solves the time-dependent Kohn-Sham (TDKS) equations in real space:
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5309900Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5310070Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5310330Z     i∂ψ_j(r,t)/∂t = H_KS[n(t)] ψ_j(r,t)
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5310595Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5310757Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5311425Z     where H_KS = -½∇² + v_ext(r,t) + v_Hartree[n](r,t) + v_xc[n](r,t). The electron density n(r,t) = Σ_j |ψ_j(r,t)|² drives the self-consistent Hamiltonian at each time step.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5312015Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5312861Z     Wavefunctions and densities are represented on a real-space grid. The Laplacian is approximated by finite differences (typically 7-point stencil) or by fast Fourier transform on a uniform grid. Pseudopotentials handle the core electrons.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5313767Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5313960Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5314204Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5314631Z     - **Time propagation**: Magnus exponential midpoint, Lanczos propagator, exponential midpoint, Runge-Kutta (RK4)
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5315232Z     - **Diagonalization**: Conjugate gradient (CG), Davidson, RMMDIIS
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5315660Z     - **Poisson solver**: FFT-based; also supports multi-grid and ISF
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5316179Z     - **PAO (Parallel Adaptive Octree)**: Adaptive mesh refinement for atoms with steep features
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5316833Z     - **Absorbing boundaries**: Complex absorbing potential (CAP), PML (perfectly matched layer) for ionization
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5317436Z     - **Berry phase**: Geometric phases for polarization in periodic systems
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5317787Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5317968Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5318196Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5318379Z     - **Language**: Fortran 90 + C
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5318739Z     - **MPI + OpenMP**: Hybrid parallel; grid points distributed across MPI tasks
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5319500Z     - **CUDA/OpenCL**: GPU acceleration for FFT and grid operations
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5319930Z     - **PFFT**: Parallel FFT for distributed-memory grid computations
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5320297Z     - **libxc**: External XC functional library
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5320650Z     - **libvdwxc**: Non-local vdW correlation for real-space codes
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5321144Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5321431Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5321662Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5321917Z     - Scales to thousands of MPI tasks for large simulation boxes
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5322279Z     - GPU acceleration for FFT-intensive steps
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5322702Z     - Real-space grids: efficient for finite systems, requires large boxes for molecules
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5323093Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5323273Z     === tools/psi4/references.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5323578Z     # Psi4 — References
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5323778Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5323953Z     ## Primary Citations

2026-02-26T02:42:49.5348407Z     === tools/gamess/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5348726Z     # GAMESS — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5349093Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5349272Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5349489Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5350120Z     GAMESS uses Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis sets for all molecular calculations. Its distinctive features are the EFP and FMO methods, both developed by the Gordon group.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5350806Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5351885Z     **EFP (Effective Fragment Potential)**: Replace solvent molecules with polarizable, non-covalent fragments that interact with the QM region through electrostatic, polarization, dispersion, and exchange-repulsion terms derived from ab initio calculations on isolated fragments. EFP2 adds charge transfer.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5353033Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5354338Z     **FMO (Fragment Molecular Orbital)**: Divide large molecule into overlapping fragments, compute each fragment's wavefunction in the electrostatic field of all other fragments (using Mulliken charges initially), iterate to self-consistency, then compute pair, triple fragment interactions for total energy. FMO2 includes pair corrections; FMO3 includes trimer corrections.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5355703Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5355892Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5356140Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5356465Z     - **GUGA-CI (Graphical Unitary Group Approach)**: Efficient CI via unitary group algebra
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5356957Z     - **ALDET/ORMAS**: Determinant-based CI for general references
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5357345Z     - **MCSCF with FORS**: Full Optimized Reaction Space MCSCF
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5357804Z     - **FMO parallelization**: Each fragment and fragment pair runs on separate MPI group
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5358268Z     - **GAMESS DFT**: Internal DFT implementation + libxc
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5358552Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5358730Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5359153Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5359435Z     - **Language**: Fortran 77/90
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5359871Z     - **MPI**: For multi-node parallel execution; DDI (Distributed Data Interface) communication layer
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5360789Z     - **DDI (Distributed Data Interface)**: GAMESS's own parallel communication library, similar to GA
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5361254Z     - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Dense linear algebra
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5361579Z     - **Limited OpenMP**: Some OpenMP in integral evaluation
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5361875Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5362052Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5362276Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5362525Z     - FMO can scale to thousands of cores for protein-sized systems
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5362963Z     - EFP solvent model allows large simulations without MM approximations
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5363472Z     - MP2 and CC scale to ~50–100 atoms efficiently on single node
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5363795Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5363976Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5364210Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5364393Z     - **libxc**: XC functional library
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5364722Z     - **FMO world**: Community of FMO users with dedicated tutorials
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5365171Z     - **Firefly (PC GAMESS)**: Separately maintained fork by Alex Granovsky
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5365584Z     - **MacroModel**: Some interfaces for hybrid calculations
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5365881Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5366069Z     === tools/nwchem/references.md ===

2026-02-26T02:42:49.5379705Z     === tools/nwchem/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5380021Z     # NWChem — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5380257Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5380435Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5380653Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5381837Z     NWChem implements quantum chemistry methods using both Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis functions for molecular calculations and plane-wave basis sets for periodic systems. The molecular code supports the full range from HF to CCSD(T) and multi-reference methods. The plane-wave code (NWPW) implements DFT and Car-Parrinello MD for solids.
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5383082Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5383269Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5383513Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5383994Z     - **Global Arrays (GA)**: NWChem's unique parallel programming model; one-sided communication for distributed arrays across nodes
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5384956Z     - **TCE (Tensor Contraction Engine)**: Automated source code generation for high-order many-body methods; generates efficient, parallel coupled cluster codes
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5385764Z     - **CR-EOMCCSD(T)**: Completely renormalized EOM-CC for quasidegenerate excited states
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5386346Z     - **Analytical gradients**: Available for HF, DFT, MP2, CCSD, TDDFT for geometry optimization
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5386927Z     - **RI/DF approximation**: Resolution of identity for efficient MP2 and DFT calculations
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5387325Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5387499Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5387714Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5387901Z     - **Language**: Fortran 77/90 and C
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5388256Z     - **Parallelism**: MPI + Global Arrays (one-sided communication), ARMCI
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5388701Z     - **Memory model**: Distributed-memory; Global Arrays abstraction
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5389186Z     - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Intel MKL or OpenBLAS
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5389586Z     - **Plane-wave code (NWPW)**: Car-Parrinello MD with CPMD equations of motion
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5389950Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5390138Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5390368Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5390563Z     NWChem was designed for HPC:
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5390830Z     - Production runs on thousands of MPI tasks
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5391145Z     - Used on Cray and IBM BG/Q supercomputers
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5391535Z     - Global Arrays enable efficient communication patterns for large molecules
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5391899Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5392079Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5392302Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5392569Z     - **Global Arrays (GA) toolkit**: Core parallel communication library
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5392945Z     - **ARMCI**: One-sided communication via GA
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5393236Z     - **libxc**: External XC functional library
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5393532Z     - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Intel MKL, OpenBLAS, AOCL
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5393861Z     - **ASE**: Python interface for NWChem calculations
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5394205Z     - **AiiDA**: Workflow management with NWChem plugin
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5394548Z     - **cclib**: Python parser for NWChem output files
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5394826Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5395010Z     === tools/rdkit/references.md ===

2026-02-26T02:42:49.5410390Z     # CP2K — References
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5410592Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5410767Z     ## Primary Citations
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5410971Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5411149Z     1. **CP2K main paper**:  
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5412056Z        Kühne, T. D. et al. (2020). CP2K: An electronic structure and molecular dynamics software package — Quickstep: Efficient and accurate electronic structure calculations. *J. Chem. Phys.* 152, 194103.  
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5412853Z        https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0007045
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5413098Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5413268Z     2. **GPW method**:  
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5413961Z        VandeVondele, J. et al. (2005). Quickstep: Fast and accurate density functional calculations using a mixed Gaussian and plane waves approach. *Comput. Phys. Commun.* 167, 103.  
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5414699Z        https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cpc.2004.12.014
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5414964Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5415131Z     3. **OT solver**:  
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5415699Z        VandeVondele, J. & Hutter, J. (2003). An efficient orbital transformation method for electronic structure calculations. *J. Chem. Phys.* 118, 4365.  
2026-02-26T02:42:49.5416328Z        https://doi.org/10.1063/1.1543154

2026-02-26T02:42:58.1316533Z     === tools/pymol/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1317060Z     # PyMOL — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1317453Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1317743Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1318109Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1319649Z     PyMOL uses a scene graph for 3D rendering where molecules, objects, and representations are nodes. The ray tracing engine (internal or external via POV-Ray) computes lighting, shadows, and reflections for high-quality images.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1321169Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1323112Z     **Cartoon ribbon algorithm**: Secondary structure (Kabsch-Sander or DSSP algorithm) is assigned to each residue. Helices are rendered as smooth tubes or cylinders; strands as arrows; loops as ribbons. The algorithm smoothly interpolates Cα positions along the backbone.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1324854Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1326348Z     **Surface rendering**: MSMS (Sanner's Molecular Surface) or built-in dot/solvent-excluded surface computed using a rolling probe sphere algorithm. APBS plugin computes Poisson-Boltzmann electrostatic potential mapped onto this surface.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1327960Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1328263Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1328673Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1329412Z     - **DSSP secondary structure**: Kabsch-Sander algorithm for helix/strand/loop assignment
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1330369Z     - **Kabsch alignment**: Optimal rigid-body superposition for structure alignment
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1331093Z     - **RMSD calculation**: After Kabsch alignment
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1331645Z     - **H-bond detection**: Distance + angle criteria
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1332529Z     - **Surface computation**: Rolling probe (1.4 Å water) for SAS; MSMS for solvent-excluded
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1333463Z     - **Ray tracing**: Internal ray tracer with ambient occlusion; OpenGL for interactive rendering
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1334177Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1334466Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1334825Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1335152Z     - **Language**: C++ core + Python API layer
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1335712Z     - **Python module**: `pymol.cmd` provides the full API
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1336256Z     - **OpenGL**: Interactive 3D rendering
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1336835Z     - **Internal ray tracer**: Production rendering with ambient occlusion
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1337468Z     - **freeglut/GLUT**: Window and event management
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1338108Z     - **Plugin API**: Python-based plugin system for extensions (APBS, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1338930Z     - **cmd module**: All PyMOL commands accessible via `pymol.cmd` Python API
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1339814Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1340125Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1340514Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1341031Z     - Real-time rendering: Interactive at 30+ fps for systems up to ~500k atoms
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1342649Z     - Ray-traced images: Minutes for a single 3000×2000 image on modern CPU
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1343719Z     - OptiX GPU ray tracing available in Schrödinger version
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1349717Z     - Memory efficient: Large structures (ribosome, virus capsid) handled via mapped files
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1350437Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1350751Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1351135Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1351474Z     - **libpng, libjpeg**: Image format output
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1352089Z     - **APBS**: Poisson-Boltzmann electrostatics (via plugin)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1352678Z     - **Freetype**: Font rendering in figures
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1353183Z     - **NumPy**: Python array operations in scripts
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1353717Z     - **PyQt5**: GUI framework for dialogs and menus
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1354150Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1354528Z     === tools/swissadme/references.md ===

2026-02-26T02:42:58.1384178Z     === tools/gamess/use-cases.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1384758Z     # GAMESS — Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1385095Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1385383Z     ## Primary Applications
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1385734Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1386759Z     GAMESS is used for multi-reference calculations, large-system FMO calculations, EFP solvent modeling, and cases where its unique methods (EFP2, FMO) are required.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1387910Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1388190Z     ## Example Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1388527Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1388825Z     ### FMO Drug-Protein Interaction
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1389423Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1389884Z     Fragment Molecular Orbital calculations on drug-bound proteins:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1390795Z     - Divide protein+ligand into amino acid fragments (~200–500 fragments)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1391566Z     - FMO2/MP2 or FMO2/DFT to compute fragment pair interaction energies
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1392342Z     - Identify key binding residues and their contribution to binding energy
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1393170Z     - Used in drug optimization for EGFR, HIV protease, influenza neuraminidase
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1393790Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1394080Z     ### EFP Solvation Studies
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1394444Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1394749Z     Explicit quantum solvent via EFP:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1395412Z     - Reaction in aqueous solution with 100–500 EFP water molecules
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1396060Z     - Better than implicit solvent; cheaper than full QM
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1396719Z     - Proton transfer reactions, nucleophilic substitutions in water
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1397273Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1397587Z     ### Multi-Reference Excited States
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1397990Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1398297Z     MCSCF + MRPT2 for photochemistry:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1398833Z     - Conical intersection optimization for photodegradation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1399634Z     - S₁/S₂ ordering in aromatic systems
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1400194Z     - Photoisomerization of retinal (Schiff base models)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1400690Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1401001Z     ### ONIOM-like Zeolite Catalysis
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1401406Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1401717Z     Cluster model of zeolite with DFT:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1402262Z     - Active site (T-site) treated with high-level DFT or MP2
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1402860Z     - Extended framework treated with lower method
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1403484Z     - Study Brønsted acid catalysis mechanisms
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1403928Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1404233Z     ## Industries & Research Areas
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1404629Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1405124Z     - **Drug discovery**: FMO for protein-ligand interaction energy decomposition
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1405868Z     - **Materials science**: Periodic DFT via GAMPERI
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1406545Z     - **Physical chemistry**: EFP solvation, reaction mechanisms in solution
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1407323Z     - **Atmospheric chemistry**: Cluster nucleation, aerosol formation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1408027Z     - **Bioinorganic chemistry**: Multi-reference active sites
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1408547Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1408848Z     === tools/namd/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1409527Z     # NAMD — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1409926Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1410217Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1410582Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1412359Z     NAMD implements classical MD with CHARMM and AMBER force fields. Its distinguishing feature is the Charm++ parallel runtime system, which provides dynamic load balancing essential for heterogeneous biomolecular systems (where some atoms are more computationally expensive than others).
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1414471Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1415258Z     **Multiple Time Stepping (RESPA)**: NAMD uses a reversible reference system propagator algorithm:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1416164Z     - Inner loop (1 fs): Bonded forces (bonds, angles, dihedrals)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1416898Z     - Middle loop (2 fs): Short-range non-bonded (< 8 Å)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1417481Z     - Outer loop (4 fs): Long-range PME electrostatics
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1417954Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1418628Z     This allows efficient use of the PME reciprocal space calculation which is relatively expensive per call.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1419585Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1419893Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1420304Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1420927Z     - **Charm++ runtime**: Object-based message-driven parallel execution with adaptive load balancing
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1421815Z     - **RESPA (MTS)**: Multiple time stepping as described above
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1422507Z     - **PME**: Particle-mesh Ewald; highly optimized with CUDA offloading
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1423325Z     - **Colvars module**: Collective variables for enhanced sampling and analysis
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1424144Z     - **ABF (Adaptive Biasing Force)**: Non-equilibrium free energy method
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1424879Z     - **TMD (Targeted MD)**: Drive system toward target conformation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1425631Z     - **SMD (Steered MD)**: Apply external force to probe mechanics or compute work
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1426248Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1426539Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1426905Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1427205Z     - **Language**: C++ built on Charm++
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1427757Z     - **Charm++**: Adaptive load balancing parallel runtime (UIUC)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1428569Z     - **MPI/OpenMP**: Standard for standard HPC; Charm++ provides additional load balancing
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1429559Z     - **CUDA**: GPU-accelerated pair interactions and PME
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1430306Z     - **Multiple executables**: `namd2` (standard), `namd3` (new GPU-optimized version)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1430960Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1431266Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1431644Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1431943Z     NAMD is designed for petascale:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1432480Z     - Special hardware: Ran on Blue Waters (256k cores), Summit
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1433106Z     - NAMD 3.0 improved GPU performance significantly
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1433816Z     - ~50–500 ns/day for 100k-atom systems on modern GPU nodes
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1434323Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1434627Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1434991Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1435351Z     - **VMD**: Tightly coupled visualization and analysis
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1436013Z     - **CHARMM-GUI**: Web-based system builder outputting NAMD-ready files
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1436691Z     - **psfgen**: PSF topology builder (part of VMD/NAMD)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1437332Z     - **PLUMED**: Enhanced sampling (metadynamics, path-metadynamics)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1438041Z     - **Colvars**: Collective variables module (built into NAMD)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1438573Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1438871Z     === tools/vmd/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1439566Z     # VMD — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1439958Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1440262Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1440639Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1442367Z     VMD's core is an efficient trajectory reading engine and rendering pipeline. The `atomselect` command implements a Boolean algebra of atomic properties (name, resname, resid, chain, x, y, z, mass, charge, beta, occupancy, etc.) combined with distance and contact queries, enabling complex selections.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1444229Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1445377Z     **QuickSurf**: GPU-accelerated Gaussian density surface rendering for large proteins and complexes; uses CUDA for real-time density computation and Marching Cubes for surface generation.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1446912Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1448560Z     **MDFF (Molecular Dynamics Flexible Fitting)**: Couples NAMD simulations to cryo-EM density maps by adding a potential energy term proportional to the negative of the density map at each atomic position, driving the structure into the density.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1450380Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1450704Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1451112Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1451723Z     - `measure rmsd`: RMSD computation after alignment; Kabsch algorithm for optimal superposition
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1452670Z     - `measure contacts`: Identify atom pairs within a cutoff distance
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1453517Z     - `hbonds`: Identify hydrogen bonds by distance + angle criteria (donor-H...acceptor)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1454358Z     - `measure gofr`: Radial distribution function from trajectory
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1455167Z     - `measure volmap`: Generate volumetric occupancy density from trajectory frames
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1456047Z     - `measure sasa`: Solvent-accessible surface area via Shrake-Rupley algorithm
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1456913Z     - `measure inertia`: Moment of inertia tensor and principal axes
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1457495Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1457801Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1458169Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1458623Z     - **Language**: C++ core with OpenGL rendering; Tcl and Python scripting
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1459529Z     - **OpenGL**: 3D rendering for interactive display
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1460240Z     - **CUDA**: GPU-accelerated QuickSurf, VolMap, OptiX ray tracing (NVIDIA RTX)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1461047Z     - **Tachyon**: Built-in ray tracer for publication-quality images
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1461756Z     - **OptiX**: NVIDIA GPU ray tracing for ambient occlusion, shadows
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1462332Z     - **FLTK**: GUI toolkit
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1462893Z     - **Tcl/Tk**: Scripting language; all VMD operations accessible via Tcl
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1463627Z     - **Python**: Available via `VMDpython` module (VMD 1.9.4+)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1464165Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1464479Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1464868Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1465333Z     - Real-time animation: 30+ fps for systems up to ~100k atoms with GPU
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1466164Z     - Large trajectory loading: Memory-mapped DCD/XTC for trajectories >1 TB
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1466948Z     - GPU-accelerated surface rendering for million-atom systems
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1467518Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1467825Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1468194Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1468545Z     - **NAMD**: Primary MD backend; IMD integration
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1469196Z     - **Tachyon**: Ray tracing
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1469625Z     - **OptiX**: NVIDIA GPU ray tracing
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1470130Z     - **NetCDF library**: AMBER trajectory format
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1470706Z     - **MDFF**: Electron microscopy density fitting plugin
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1471355Z     - **RMSF, Ramachandran, Timeline**: Built-in analysis plugins
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1471895Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1472222Z     === tools/open-babel/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1472841Z     # Open Babel — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1473268Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1473563Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1473945Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1475274Z     Open Babel's core is a molecule object model (OBMol) that stores atoms, bonds, and their properties. Converters (OBFormat plugins) implement readers and writers for each supported format. The conversion engine handles:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1476813Z     - Atom typing (hybridization, aromaticity)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1477382Z     - Perception of bonds, rings, and stereochemistry
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1477948Z     - Coordinate generation and transformation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1478390Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1478695Z     **Fingerprint algorithms**:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1479576Z     - FP2: Path-based fingerprint using linear atom paths up to length 7 in SMILES notation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1480715Z     - FP3/FP4: SMARTS-based fingerprints using predefined SMARTS query lists
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1481589Z     - MACCS: 166 structural key patterns as SMARTS
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1482073Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1482393Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1482811Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1483323Z     - **Rotor detection**: Identifies freely rotatable bonds for conformer generation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1484273Z     - **Weighted rotor search**: Systematic conformer generator using weighted random walk
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1485347Z     - **Steepest descent / conjugate gradient**: Force field minimization for 3D generation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1486355Z     - **Gasteiger charges**: Iterative electronegativity equalization for partial charges
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1487311Z     - **AM1BCC**: AM1 + bond charge correction (important for AMBER/GAFF parameterization)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1488238Z     - **Aromaticity models**: Default, OpenEye, Daylight, and MMFF aromaticity models
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1489263Z     - **InChI**: Uses IUPAC InChI library for InChI generation and parsing
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1490112Z     - **SMARTS matching**: Recursive SMARTS support for complex substructure queries
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1490768Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1491074Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1491453Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1491756Z     - **Language**: C++
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1492406Z     - **Python bindings**: `openbabel` Python module (via SWIG); also `pybel` (higher-level)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1493506Z     - **Plugin architecture**: Format converters, fingerprint algorithms, and force fields are all plugins
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1494590Z     - **wxWidgets GUI**: `avogadro` (Avogadro uses Open Babel) and `openbabel-gui` (separate)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1495533Z     - **Thread safety**: Limited; not designed for concurrent molecular operations
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1496171Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1496473Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1496896Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1497319Z     - Format conversion: Very fast (microseconds per molecule)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1498067Z     - 3D generation: ~1 second per molecule with force field optimization
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1499177Z     - Not designed for large-scale fingerprint searches (use RDKit or PostgreSQL cartridge)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1499911Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1500219Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1500613Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1500954Z     - **InChI library**: IUPAC InChI algorithm
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1501489Z     - **libxml2**: XML format parsing (CML, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1502013Z     - **Eigen**: Linear algebra for 3D operations
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1502568Z     - **zlib**: Compressed file handling (.gz formats)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1503176Z     - **Avogadro**: Uses Open Babel as its chemistry backend
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1503690Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1504004Z     === tools/orca/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1504544Z     # ORCA — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1504965Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1505263Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1505643Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1507368Z     ORCA is built on post-Hartree-Fock quantum chemistry theory using Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis sets. All methods start from the Hartree-Fock reference and systematically add electron correlation via perturbation theory, coupled cluster, or multi-reference methods.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1509379Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1511027Z     The one-electron integral evaluation, SCF procedure, and post-HF methods share common infrastructure. ORCA uses an efficient Resolution of Identity (RI) approximation throughout to reduce the formal computational scaling of Coulomb and exchange integrals.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1512796Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1513121Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1513553Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1514508Z     - **RI-J (density fitting)**: Approximate Coulomb integrals using auxiliary basis sets (def2/J, def2/JK); reduces cost of DFT Coulomb from O(N^4) to O(N^3)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1517102Z     - **RI-K / COSX**: Approximate exchange integrals — RI-K uses auxiliary basis, COSX (Chain-of-Spheres Exchange) uses numerical integration on a grid; enables efficient hybrid DFT
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1518667Z     - **RIJCOSX**: Combined RI-J + COSX for hybrid functionals (default in most ORCA hybrid DFT runs)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1520722Z     - **DLPNO (Domain-based Local Pair Natural Orbital)**: The flagship feature of ORCA; expresses electron correlation in localized orbital basis, enabling near-linear scaling CCSD(T) for large molecules (up to ~1000 atoms)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1522581Z     - **PNO (Pair Natural Orbitals)**: Compact representation of pair correlation; basis for DLPNO
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1523537Z     - **ICE-CI**: Iterative Configuration Expansion CI for large active spaces
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1524537Z     - **CASSCF**: State-averaged CASSCF with analytical gradient; active spaces up to CAS(20,20) practical
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1525640Z     - **NEVPT2**: Strongly-contracted and partially-contracted variants on top of CASSCF
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1526586Z     - **MRCI**: Size-consistent Davidson-corrected MRCI (MRCI+Q, MRACPF, MRAQCC)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1527226Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1527531Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1527906Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1528308Z     - **Language**: C++ (main codebase) with Fortran libraries
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1529306Z     - **Parallelization**: MPI-based; embarrassingly parallel for DFT and linear scaling CC
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1530410Z     - **Integral engine**: ORCA implements its own efficient integral routines using Obara-Saika scheme
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1531719Z     - **SHARK integral package** (ORCA 5+): New, faster integral engine replacing older code; uses SIMD and other modern optimizations
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1533170Z     - **Grid**: Numerical integration on Lebedev angular grids + radial grids for DFT XC; Grid1 through Grid7 (accuracy levels)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1534402Z     - **Geometry**: Uses both internal coordinates (GDIIS/L-BFGS) and Cartesian for optimization
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1535134Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1535437Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1535821Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1536193Z     ORCA scales well for molecular DFT and DLPNO-CC:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1537105Z     - **DLPNO-CCSD(T)**: Near-linear scaling with molecule size; practical for proteins (~500 aa) in a large basis
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1538173Z     - **TDDFT**: Efficient for excited state calculations with Davidson diagonalizer
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1539630Z     - MPI scaling: Good to ~16–32 cores for most calculations; limited MPI scaling for some advanced methods
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1540768Z     - ORCA 5.x introduced OpenMP threading in addition to MPI, improving single-node performance
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1541514Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1541825Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1542220Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1542710Z     - **libxc**: External XC functional library (200+ functionals available)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1543407Z     - **SHARK**: Internal integral library (ORCA 5+)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1543992Z     - **CPCM/SMD**: Solvation via internal implementation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1544576Z     - **ORCA_2AIM**: Interface for Bader AIM analysis
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1545272Z     - **Multiwfn**: Post-processing of ORCA wavefunction files (.gbw format)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1546091Z     - **VMD/Avogadro**: Visualization of MOs and structures from ORCA output
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1546899Z     - **orca_plot**: Internal tool for generating cube files of MOs and density
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1547509Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1547835Z     === tools/avogadro/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1548403Z     # Avogadro — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1548821Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1549295Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1549679Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1551269Z     Avogadro implements standard molecular mechanics force fields for geometry optimization and MD. The molecular representation is a graph (atoms + bonds) with 3D coordinates, and operations are performed on this graph.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1552931Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1553324Z     **Force field implementation** (via OpenBabel/MMFF94):
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1554146Z     - MMFF94: Merck Molecular Force Field — good for organic molecules
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1554976Z     - UFF: Universal Force Field — broad periodic table coverage
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1555633Z     - GAFF: General AMBER Force Field (via Open Babel)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1556356Z     - Used for: 3D coordinate cleaning, hydrogen placement, conformer generation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1556994Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1558292Z     **Input generator**: Avogadro 2 uses a Python scripting system (via Jupyter-like generators) to construct QC input files from the molecular structure, charge, multiplicity, and user-selected method/basis set.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1560113Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1560407Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1560738Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1561129Z     - **Force field energy minimization**: Steepest descent, conjugate gradient
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1561731Z     - **Force field MD**: Simple Verlet integrator for geometry exploration
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1562366Z     - **Structure perception**: Bond order perception, hybridization, aromaticity from XYZ
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1563039Z     - **Hydrogen addition**: Add H atoms at standard geometries for input structures
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1563566Z     - **SMILES parsing**: Via Open Babel backend
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1564033Z     - **CIF parsing**: Crystal structure reading with unit cell and symmetry
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1564471Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1564732Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1564979Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1565189Z     - **Language**: C++ with Qt GUI framework
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1565674Z     - **Avogadro 2 core**: libavogadro2 — molecular data model
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1566203Z     - **Plugins**: All representations, tools, and extensions are plugins (C++ or Python)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1566716Z     - **VTK**: Visualization toolkit for some rendering
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1567057Z     - **OpenGL**: 3D molecular rendering
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1567358Z     - **Qt 5**: Cross-platform GUI
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1567747Z     - **Open Babel**: Backend for file format reading/writing and force fields
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1568306Z     - **Python plugin API**: Generate input files and extend functionality via Python
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1568737Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1569148Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1569494Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1569730Z     - Interactive at 30+ fps for up to ~10,000 atoms
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1570164Z     - Not designed for large-scale simulations (no trajectory analysis)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1570660Z     - Force field optimization suitable for molecules up to ~500 atoms
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1571032Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1571230Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1571484Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1571675Z     - **Qt 5**: GUI framework
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1572021Z     - **Open Babel**: Format conversion and force field evaluation

2026-02-26T02:42:58.1577871Z     === tools/openmm/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1578196Z     # OpenMM — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1578431Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1578605Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1578818Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1580219Z     OpenMM implements classical molecular dynamics using the same force field functional forms as AMBER and CHARMM. Its key design innovation is a layered architecture: the high-level Python API calls into Platform-specific implementations (CUDA, OpenCL, CPU Reference) that execute the same physical model on different hardware.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1581422Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1582475Z     **Custom Forces**: OpenMM's `CustomBondForce`, `CustomAngleForce`, `CustomTorsionForce`, `CustomNonbondedForce`, and `CustomExternalForce` allow users to define arbitrary energy functions as mathematical expressions parsed at runtime (using OpenMM's internal JIT expression compiler). This enables:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1583694Z     - Novel force field terms without modifying source code
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1584031Z     - Machine learning potential integration
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1584371Z     - Alchemical transformations with custom lambda functions
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1584709Z     - Enhanced sampling biasing potentials
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1584969Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1585157Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1585400Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1585827Z     - **LangevinMiddleIntegrator**: BAOAB-style Langevin integrator with middle operator splitting; excellent for NVT
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1586556Z     - **MonteCarloBarostat**: Stochastic volume moves for NPT; more correct than velocity-scaling barostats
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1587103Z     - **VariableLangevinIntegrator**: Adaptive timestep Langevin
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1587517Z     - **RPMDIntegrator**: Ring polymer MD for quantum nuclear effects
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1587927Z     - **SETTLE**: Exact analytical constraints for water molecules
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1588439Z     - **CCMA (Constrained Coordinate Moving Algorithm)**: General constraint solver analogous to LINCS
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1588877Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1589179Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1589397Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1589647Z     - **Language**: C++ core library + Python API (via SWIG bindings)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1590143Z     - **Platform abstraction**: Reference (CPU), OpenCL (any GPU), CUDA (NVIDIA), Metal (Apple)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1590703Z     - **JIT compilation**: Custom force expressions compiled at runtime for each platform
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1591199Z     - **XML force fields**: Extensible XML format for force field parameters
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1591686Z     - **Python-first**: Full simulation control from Python; no config files required
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1592059Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1592236Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1592471Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1592648Z     OpenMM performance on GPU:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1592955Z     - Competitive with pmemd.cuda for single-GPU performance
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1593315Z     - ~500 ns/day for DHFR (24k atoms) on NVIDIA A100
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1593676Z     - CUDA platform ~2–5× faster than OpenCL
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1594056Z     - Best for single-GPU; multi-GPU scaling less optimized than GROMACS/AMBER
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1594408Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1594585Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1594812Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1595081Z     - **OpenFF toolkit**: Modern force field assignment via SMIRKS patterns
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1595495Z     - **Perses**: Alchemical relative free energy calculations
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1595868Z     - **OpenPathSampling**: Path sampling methods (TIS, TPS)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1596200Z     - **OpenFE**: Open Free Energy consortium
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1596478Z     - **MDTraj**: Trajectory analysis library
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1596916Z     - **ParmEd**: Topology reading and conversion
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1597373Z     - **NNPOps**: GPU-optimized neural network potential operators
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1597810Z     - **Espaloma**: Graph neural network for force field parameterization
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1598142Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1598322Z     === tools/vesta/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1598626Z     # VESTA — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1598863Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1599146Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1599362Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1599540Z     VESTA handles two main data types:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1599778Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1600531Z     1. **Crystal structures**: Atomic coordinates in fractional or Cartesian coordinates with unit cell parameters (a, b, c, α, β, γ). Space group symmetry generates equivalent positions.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1601238Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1601972Z     2. **Volumetric data (charge density, potential)**: 3D scalar fields defined on a uniform grid in the unit cell. The grid data is read from CHGCAR (VASP), charge-density.dat (QE), CUBE (Gaussian), and similar formats.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1602771Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1603468Z     **Isosurface rendering**: Marching Cubes algorithm extracts the isosurface at a user-specified density value from the 3D grid. The resulting polygon mesh is rendered with OpenGL using Phong shading.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1604226Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1604540Z     **Powder diffraction simulation**: VESTA computes the theoretical XRD pattern using:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1605105Z     - Structure factor: F(hkl) = Σ_j f_j(θ) e^{2πi(hx_j + ky_j + lz_j)}
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1605465Z     - Lorentz-polarization factor
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1605762Z     - Multiplicity and thermal effects (Debye-Waller)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1606067Z     - Pseudo-Voigt peak profiles
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1606295Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1606480Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1606723Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1606982Z     - **Marching Cubes**: Isosurface extraction from volumetric grid
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1607406Z     - **Bond detection**: Distance-based with element-specific cutoffs
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1607812Z     - **Space group symmetry**: Full 230 space group operations
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1608273Z     - **Polyhedra**: Central atom + coordinating atom identification; polyhedral rendering
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1608785Z     - **Profile fitting**: Pseudo-Voigt (Gaussian + Lorentzian) for XRD peaks
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1609393Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1609576Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1609791Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1609965Z     - **Language**: C++ with Qt GUI
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1610245Z     - **OpenGL**: 3D rendering with Phong shading
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1610572Z     - **Cross-platform**: Windows, macOS, Linux binaries
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1611053Z     - **No external scientific libraries**: Self-contained with all algorithms implemented internally
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1611493Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1611672Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1611895Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1612147Z     - Interactive rendering: 60 fps for typical crystal structures
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1612688Z     - Large charge density grids (512³): Load time ~10 seconds; interactive thereafter
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1613171Z     - RAM usage: ~100 MB for typical CHGCAR; scales with grid size
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1613477Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1613658Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1613877Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1614046Z     - **Qt**: GUI framework
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1614281Z     - **GLEW / GLUT**: OpenGL extensions
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1614562Z     - **VASP**: Primary source of CHGCAR files
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1614887Z     - **Quantum ESPRESSO**: QE charge density and potential
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1615295Z     - **PHONOPY**: Phonon post-processing (create VESTA-ready structures)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1615816Z     - **VESTA format (.vesta)**: Custom format that saves visualization settings alongside structure
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1616383Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1616663Z     === tools/octopus/methods.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1616979Z     # Octopus — Computational Methods
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1617214Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1617384Z     ## Supported Methods
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1617587Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1617875Z     - Ground-state DFT: LDA, GGA (PBE), meta-GGA, hybrid (via OEP or perturbative)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1618298Z     - Adiabatic LDA/GGA time-dependent XC
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1618592Z     - Real-time TDDFT (full non-linear regime)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1618936Z     - Linear-response TDDFT (Casida equations or Sternheimer)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1619676Z     - Spin-DFT: collinear and non-collinear spin
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1619982Z     - DFT+U for correlated systems (periodic)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1620369Z     - Exact exchange (OEP — optimized effective potential)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1620659Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1620828Z     ## Input/Output
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1621028Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1621308Z     Octopus uses a free-format input file named `inp` with key=value pairs:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1621666Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1621823Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1622096Z     CalculationMode = td           # Time-dependent (td) or ground state (gs)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1622463Z     ExperimentalFeatures = yes
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1622715Z     XYZCoordinates = "benzene.xyz"
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1622963Z     Spacing = 0.18*angstrom
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1623188Z     Radius = 6.0*angstrom
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1623412Z     XCFunctional = lda_x + lda_c_pz
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1623733Z     TDPropagator = aetrs            # 4th-order Taylor expansion
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1624057Z     TDTimeStep = 0.003
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1624267Z     TDMaxSteps = 2000
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1624536Z     TDExternalFields = laser_field  # External laser field
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1624830Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1624992Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1625161Z     ## Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1625365Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1625542Z     ### Optical Absorption Spectrum
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1625776Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1625935Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1626111Z     # Step 1: Ground state
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1626330Z     CalculationMode = gs
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1626569Z     # Step 2: Apply delta kick and propagate
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1626836Z     CalculationMode = td
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1627069Z     TDDeltaKickStrength = 0.01*atomic_units
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1627394Z     # Step 3: Fourier transform dipole moment to get spectrum
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1627699Z     octopus_spectrum
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1627891Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1628048Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1628216Z     ## Advanced Features
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1628416Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1628591Z     ### High-Harmonic Generation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1628811Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1629570Z     Apply a long laser pulse and compute the emitted HHG spectrum by Fourier transforming the time-dependent dipole acceleration. Octopus is a standard tool for attosecond science.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1630275Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1630459Z     === tools/amber/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1630755Z     # AMBER — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1630985Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1631156Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1631367Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1631607Z     AMBER uses the classical AMBER force field functional form:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1631920Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1632079Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1632357Z     U = Σ_bonds K_b(r - r_0)² + Σ_angles K_θ(θ - θ_0)²
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1632698Z       + Σ_dihedrals V_n/2 [1 + cos(nφ - γ)]
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1633001Z       + Σ_LJ [A_ij/r¹² - B_ij/r⁶]
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1633273Z       + Σ_Coulomb q_i q_j / ε r_ij
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1633501Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1633658Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1633833Z     Key algorithmic features:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1634135Z     - **SHAKE/RATTLE**: Constrain X-H bonds to allow 2 fs timestep
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1634711Z     - **PME**: Smooth particle-mesh Ewald for electrostatics (O(N log N))
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1635264Z     - **BINTLIST**: Neighbor list construction for non-bonded interactions
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1635608Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1635790Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1636035Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1636389Z     - **pmemd**: High-performance MD engine with highly optimized PME; supports single and multi-GPU
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1636990Z     - **pmemd.cuda**: NVIDIA CUDA implementation of core MD; single-GPU or multi-GPU via NCCL
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1637578Z     - **GaMD (Gaussian accelerated MD)**: Adds harmonic boost potential to flatten the energy surface
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1638179Z     - **REST2**: Replica exchange with solute tempering 2.0 for enhanced sampling of biomolecules
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1638785Z     - **GBn/GBn2 implicit solvent**: Generalized Born models for rapid implicit solvent simulations
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1639506Z     - **PBSA**: Numerical Poisson-Boltzmann and analytical SA for MM-PBSA binding free energy
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1639922Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1640100Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1640316Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1640560Z     - **Language**: Fortran (sander, pmemd) + CUDA C++ (pmemd.cuda)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1640932Z     - **MPI**: Multi-node parallelism in sander and pmemd
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1641354Z     - **CUDA**: GPU acceleration in pmemd.cuda; highly optimized for NVIDIA V100/A100
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1641928Z     - **Single-GPU focus**: pmemd.cuda is most efficient on single GPU; multi-GPU scaling is less linear
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1642369Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1642545Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1642767Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1642940Z     pmemd.cuda benchmarks:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1643272Z     - ~1 µs/day for 25,000-atom DHFR on a single NVIDIA A100
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1643603Z     - ~150 ns/day for 250,000-atom systems on A100
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1644008Z     - Among the fastest single-GPU biomolecular MD engines (competitive with NAMD)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1644381Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1644562Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1644780Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1645089Z     - **AmberTools**: Free companion suite (tleap, cpptraj, Antechamber, MMPBSA.py, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1645632Z     - **GAFF/GAFF2**: General AMBER force field via Antechamber for drug-like molecules
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1646081Z     - **Antechamber + ACPYPE**: Small molecule parameterization
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1646484Z     - **MMPBSA.py**: MM-PBSA/MM-GBSA binding free energy in AmberTools
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1646862Z     - **MDAnalysis**: Reads AMBER .prmtop and .nc files
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1647224Z     - **parmed**: ParmEd for AMBER <-> GROMACS topology conversion
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1647531Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1647715Z     === tools/lammps/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1648012Z     # LAMMPS — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1648245Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1648433Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1648644Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1649577Z     LAMMPS integrates Newton's equations of motion using velocity Verlet (or other) integrators. Its distinguishing feature is its interatomic potential library, which spans from simple analytical forms to many-body reactive potentials.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1650476Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1651469Z     **ReaxFF**: Reactive force field developed by van Duin and Goddard. Uses charge equilibration (QEq) at each step to assign partial charges. Bond order is dynamically computed from interatomic distances, enabling bond breaking/forming. Parameters available for C/H/O/N/S/Si/metals/oxides.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1652526Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1653758Z     **EAM (Embedded Atom Method)**: Total energy = sum of pair repulsions + embedding energy. The embedding function F depends on the local electron density ρ contributed by neighboring atoms. EAM potentials accurately reproduce elastic constants, stacking fault energies, and surface energies of FCC metals.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1655116Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1655929Z     **Machine Learning Potentials**: LAMMPS interfaces with external ML models (DeePMD, NequIP, ACE) that use neural networks or kernel methods trained on DFT data to provide DFT-accuracy at classical MD cost.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1656723Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1656910Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1657152Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1657386Z     - **Velocity Verlet integrator**: Default time integrator
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1657743Z     - **Runge-Kutta (4th order)**: For stiff systems
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1670449Z     - **PPPM (Particle-Particle Particle-Mesh)**: O(N log N) long-range electrostatics
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1671238Z     - **Ewald summation**: Exact but O(N^3/2)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1671863Z     - **QEq charge equilibration**: For ReaxFF and charge-equilibration models
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1672577Z     - **SHAKE/RATTLE**: Bond/angle constraints for rigid groups
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1673079Z     - **Domain decomposition**: 3D spatial decomposition for MPI; dynamic load balancing
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1673882Z     - **Neighbor list**: Verlet neighbor list with skin distance; rebuilt only when atoms move Δ > skin/2
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1674567Z     - **KOKKOS**: Performance-portable abstraction layer; same kernel runs on CPU and GPU
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1675020Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1675205Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1675432Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1675649Z     - **Language**: C++ (main) with some C and Fortran
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1675992Z     - **MPI**: 3D domain decomposition; excellent scaling
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1676357Z     - **OpenMP**: Thread-level parallelism within MPI domains
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1676757Z     - **CUDA**: GPU package for pair potentials; KOKKOS for portable GPU
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1677217Z     - **Python interface**: `lammps` Python module for scripting and automation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1677779Z     - **Modular packages**: LAMMPS compiled with optional packages (MOLECULE, KSPACE, GPU, KOKKOS, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1678228Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1678424Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1678656Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1678862Z     LAMMPS is among the most scalable MD codes:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1679675Z     - Demonstrated scaling to 1M+ MPI tasks on petascale machines
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1680089Z     - GPU version: ~1 billion LJ atoms/second on 1024 GPUs
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1680494Z     - ReaxFF: ~1M atoms practical on 100 nodes; slower than non-reactive
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1680825Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1681006Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1681225Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1681450Z     - **DeePMD-kit**: ML potential training and inference
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1681818Z     - **NequIP / MACE**: Equivariant neural network potentials
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1682249Z     - **PLUMED**: Enhanced sampling (metadynamics, committor, path-metadynamics)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1682675Z     - **i-PI**: Path integral MD and QM/MM via socket
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1683037Z     - **OVITO**: Visualization and analysis of LAMMPS trajectories
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1683392Z     - **MDAnalysis**: Python trajectory analysis
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1683688Z     - **ASE**: Python interface for LAMMPS
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1683935Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1684122Z     === tools/gaussian/references.md ===
