2026-02-26T02:42:58.1702700Z     === tools/psi4/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1703024Z     # Psi4 — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1703261Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1703436Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1703655Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1704553Z     Psi4 uses Gaussian-type orbital (GTO) basis functions and implements the full hierarchy of wavefunction-based methods. A central design philosophy is clean, modular code that separates integral computation, wavefunction drivers, and property evaluation.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1705536Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1705819Z     The density fitting (DF/RI) approximation is pervasive throughout Psi4:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1706298Z     - DF-HF: Approximate Coulomb and exchange integrals with auxiliary basis
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1706890Z     - DF-MP2: O(N^4) instead of O(N^5) with minimal accuracy loss
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1707438Z     - DF-CCSD: Faster coupled cluster with density-fitted two-electron integrals
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1707806Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1707989Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1708232Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1708592Z     - **Optking**: Geometry optimization in redundant internal coordinates; handles transition states
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1709431Z     - **geomeTRIC**: Modern optimizer (separate package) used as default in Psi4 1.5+
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1710228Z     - **SAPT**: Energy decomposition into electrostatics, exchange, induction, dispersion at multiple levels (SAPT0, SAPT2, SAPT2+, SAPT2+(3), SAPT2+3, SAPT2+(CCD))
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1710936Z     - **DFMP2**: Density-fitted MP2 with analytic gradients
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1711284Z     - **CCENERGY**: Canonical CCSD, CCSD(T), BCCD, BCCD(T)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1711613Z     - **EOM-CC**: EOM-CCSD for EE, IP, EA, DEA, DIP
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1711944Z     - **DETCI**: Determinantal CI; FCI, RAS-CI, CAS-CI
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1712287Z     - **ADCman**: ADC(2), ADC(2)-x, ADC(3) for excited states
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1712571Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1712748Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1712963Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1713175Z     - **Language**: C++ (core) + Python (driver and API)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1713669Z     - **Python module**: Import as `import psi4` — full Psi4 functionality in Python
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1714157Z     - **MPI**: Not standard (Psi4 uses shared-memory parallelism primarily)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1714585Z     - **OpenMP**: Multi-threaded integrals and tensor contractions
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1714957Z     - **Libint2**: Gaussian electron repulsion integrals
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1715297Z     - **libJK**: Coulomb/exchange matrix construction
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1715602Z     - **BLAS/LAPACK**: Dense linear algebra
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1715980Z     - **PSI4NumPy**: Python prototyping layer using NumPy for matrix operations
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1716346Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1716536Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1716765Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1717015Z     Psi4 is optimized for single-node shared-memory performance:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1717471Z     - Excellent multi-core performance with OpenMP (16–64 cores)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1717879Z     - DF-MP2 and DF-CCSD scale well to large systems (> 50 heavy atoms)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1718332Z     - No MPI: not designed for multi-node; use NWChem or ORCA for HPC cluster jobs
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1718692Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1718871Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1719387Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1719586Z     - **Libint2**: Two-electron integrals
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1719877Z     - **libxc**: External XC functional library
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1720175Z     - **geomeTRIC**: Modern geometry optimizer
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1720519Z     - **CFOUR**: High-accuracy CC; Psi4 can call CFOUR as backend
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1720888Z     - **MRCC**: Arbitrary-order CC via MRCC interface
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1721202Z     - **CheMPS2**: DMRG for large active spaces
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1721502Z     - **v2rdm-casscf**: Variational 2-RDM CASSCF
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1721836Z     - **dftd3 / dftd4**: Grimme dispersion correction programs
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1722133Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1722317Z     === tools/rdkit/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1722622Z     # RDKit — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1722856Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1723032Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1723249Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1724383Z     RDKit implements graph-based molecular representations. Molecules are represented as labeled graphs where atoms are nodes (with properties: atomic number, charge, isotope, chirality) and bonds are edges (with properties: bond order, stereochemistry). All cheminformatics algorithms operate on this graph representation.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1725595Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1726128Z     **Morgan Algorithm (ECFP)**: RDKit's Morgan fingerprint implements the extended connectivity algorithm:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1726852Z     1. Initialize each atom with its atomic invariant (atomic number, degree, charge, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1727547Z     2. For each radius r (0 to max_radius), update each atom's identifier by hashing its current identifier and those of its neighbors
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1728253Z     3. Collect all unique identifiers across all radii; each represents a circular substructure
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1728676Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1729282Z     **ETKDG (Experimental-Torsion Knowledge Distance Geometry)**: 3D conformer generation using:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1729800Z     1. Distance bounds from atomic radii and bond lengths (DG)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1730136Z     2. MMFF charges for electrostatic terms
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1730502Z     3. Empirical torsion angle potentials from CSD crystal data (ET term)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1730961Z     4. ETKDGv3 adds improved stereohandling and intramolecular H-bond terms
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1731319Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1731509Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1731756Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1731997Z     - **SSSR**: Smallest set of smallest rings (for ring systems)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1732453Z     - **Aromaticity**: Hückel and RDKit aromaticity models
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1732885Z     - **Chirality**: R/S and E/Z assignment; enhanced stereochemistry (wedge bonds)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1733381Z     - **Sanitization**: Kekulization, valence checking, aromaticity perception
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1733836Z     - **MaxMin diversity**: MaxMinPicker for diverse subset selection
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1734316Z     - **ROCS/Shape**: Gaussian shape overlap for 3D similarity (via experimental/contrib)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1734793Z     - **FMCS**: Maximum Common Substructure (via FindMCS algorithm)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1735103Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1735280Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1735504Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1735744Z     - **Language**: C++ core with Python bindings (Boost.Python)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1736243Z     - **Python API**: Comprehensive; `rdkit.Chem`, `rdkit.Chem.AllChem`, `rdkit.Chem.Descriptors`
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1736689Z     - **Java API**: Partial; via SWIG
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1737087Z     - **JavaScript (rdkit.js)**: WebAssembly compilation for browser-based cheminformatics
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1737610Z     - **PostgreSQL cartridge**: `rdkit` schema extension for SQL chemical queries
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1737969Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1738144Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1738366Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1738663Z     - Very fast for single-molecule operations (µs per molecule)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1739205Z     - Fingerprint generation: ~10,000 molecules/second on single core
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1739682Z     - Substructure search on large databases: use KNIME or PostgreSQL RDKit cartridge
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1740157Z     - Parallel processing: Use Python multiprocessing or joblib
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1740479Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1740656Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1740880Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1741074Z     - **Boost**: Required for Python bindings
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1741409Z     - **InChI library (IUPAC)**: For InChI/InChIKey computation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1741746Z     - **FFTW / LAPACK**: For some 3D operations
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1742056Z     - **NumPy**: Array operations in Python layer
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1742401Z     - **pandas**: Often used with RDKit for chemical data frames
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1742768Z     - **scikit-learn**: ML pipelines with RDKit descriptors
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1743061Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1743245Z     === tools/mopac/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1743545Z     # MOPAC — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1743780Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1743960Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1744179Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1745563Z     MOPAC implements neglect of diatomic differential overlap (NDDO) semi-empirical Hamiltonians. In NDDO, three- and four-center two-electron integrals are neglected, and remaining two-center integrals are approximated using multipole expansions. The remaining parameters are fit to experimental heats of formation, geometries, and ionization potentials.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1746991Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1747165Z     The total energy is:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1747372Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1747535Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1747742Z     E_total = E_electronic + E_nuclear_repulsion
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1748010Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1748172Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1748919Z     where E_nuclear_repulsion includes the core-core repulsion that compensates for the neglected integrals. The electronic energy uses the self-consistent Roothaan equations with the approximate NDDO integrals.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1749854Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1750043Z     ### PM7 Improvements Over PM6
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1750284Z     PM7 includes:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1750538Z     - Improved dispersion (D/H correction from PM6-DH2)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1750864Z     - Better treatment of hydrogen bonds
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1751227Z     - Re-parameterized for 70 elements including many transition metals
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1751650Z     - Improved solid-state and crystal property predictions
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1751948Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1752136Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1752375Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1752646Z     - **BFGS minimizer**: Quasi-Newton optimization for molecular geometry
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1753157Z     - **EF (Eigenvector Following)**: TS optimization following a single negative eigenvalue
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1753714Z     - **MOZYME**: Linear-scaling algorithm for large proteins; O(N) cost via localized MOs
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1754207Z     - **COSMO**: Conductor-like screening model for implicit solvation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1754733Z     - **CI (Configuration Interaction)**: CIS and CISD for excited states and ground state of open-shell
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1755266Z     - **Periodic boundary conditions**: For crystalline materials
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1755584Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1755762Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1755979Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1756154Z     - **Language**: Fortran
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1756545Z     - **Single-core**: Primarily single-threaded (MOPAC is fast enough for most applications)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1757029Z     - **OpenMP**: Limited parallel support in recent versions
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1757398Z     - **Simple binary**: Download and run; minimal dependencies
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1757697Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1757913Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1758141Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1758348Z     MOPAC is extremely fast compared to ab initio:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1758742Z     - Typical molecule (< 50 atoms): milliseconds to seconds for optimization
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1759406Z     - Drug-sized molecule (100 atoms): seconds to minutes
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1759773Z     - Protein (1000+ atoms): MOZYME enables minutes to hours
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1760132Z     - Crystal: Periodic PM7-SS in minutes for unit cells
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1760415Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1760592Z     ## Dependencies & Ecosystem
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1760815Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1761054Z     - **openbabel**: File format conversion to/from MOPAC format
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1761457Z     - **RDKit**: Generate initial 3D coordinates for MOPAC optimization
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1761839Z     - **ASE**: Python interface for MOPAC calculations
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1762268Z     - **AMS (Amsterdam Modeling Suite)**: Commercial suite includes MOPAC methods via DFTB
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1762657Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1762846Z     === tools/deepchem/technology.md ===
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1763182Z     # DeepChem — Underlying Technology
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1763424Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1763598Z     ## Theoretical Foundation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1763814Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1764292Z     DeepChem applies deep learning architectures to molecular data. Key representations:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1764699Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1766045Z     **Graph representation**: Molecules as graphs where atoms are nodes and bonds are edges. Node features encode: atomic number, hybridization, formal charge, aromaticity, ring membership. Edge features encode: bond type, ring membership, stereo. Graph neural networks (GNNs) learn representations by aggregating information from local neighborhoods.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1767319Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1767662Z     **Message Passing Neural Networks (MPNN)**: Each atom aggregates messages from its neighbors:
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1768085Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1768347Z     m_v^(t) = Σ_{w∈N(v)} M(h_v^(t), h_w^(t), e_vw)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1768627Z     h_v^(t+1) = U(h_v^(t), m_v^(t))
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1768891Z     ŷ = R({h_v^(T)})
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1769328Z     ```
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1769661Z     where M is the message function, U is the update function, R is the readout function.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1770063Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1770837Z     **Transformer/BERT for molecules**: Pre-train on unlabeled SMILES using masked language modeling; fine-tune on downstream property prediction tasks. ChemBERTa uses RoBERTa architecture on 10M SMILES from PubChem.
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1771664Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1771851Z     ## Algorithms & Methods Implemented
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1772092Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1772380Z     - **Graph Convolutional Network (GCN)**: Kipf & Welling spectral graph conv
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1772801Z     - **MPNN**: Message Passing NN (Gilmer et al. 2017)
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1773174Z     - **AttentiveFP**: Attention-based GNN; graph attention pooling
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1773580Z     - **DMPNN**: Directed MPNN for molecular property prediction
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1774027Z     - **SchNet**: 3D-based NN using continuous filter convolutions on atomic positions
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1774526Z     - **DimeNet / DimeNet++**: Directional message passing with distance+angle
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1774988Z     - **Junction Tree VAE**: Hierarchical VAE for valid molecule generation
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1775466Z     - **REINFORCE / REINVENT**: Reinforcement learning for molecule optimization
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1775824Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1776001Z     ## Software Architecture
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1776215Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1776390Z     - **Language**: Python
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1776703Z     - **Backends**: TensorFlow 2.x (default), PyTorch (torch models), JAX
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1777139Z     - **RDKit integration**: For molecular featurization, SMILES parsing
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1777683Z     - **Data pipeline**: `dc.data.Dataset` → `dc.feat.Featurizer` → `dc.models.Model`
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1778174Z     - **Modular design**: Mix and match featurizers, splitters, models, metrics
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1778528Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1778709Z     ## Performance & Scalability
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1778937Z     
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1779345Z     - GPU training supported via PyTorch/TensorFlow CUDA backends
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1779778Z     - Batch processing of large datasets via `dc.data.DiskDataset`
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1780196Z     - Pre-trained models enable transfer learning with minimal data
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1780598Z     - Typical training time: minutes to hours on modern GPU
2026-02-26T02:42:58.1780894Z

2026-02-26T02:44:35.8388344Z       ## Key Physical/Mathematical Foundations
2026-02-26T02:44:35.8388843Z       
2026-02-26T02:44:35.8389853Z       - **Verlet algorithm**: Verlet, L. (1967). *Phys. Rev.* 159, 98. Basis for trajectory integration used alongside VMD.
2026-02-26T02:44:35.8391025Z       - **Langevin dynamics**: Used in NAMD/VMD workflows for temperature control.
2026-02-26T02:44:35.8392335Z       - **PME electrostatics**: Essmann, U. et al. (1995). *J. Chem. Phys.* 103, 8577. Standard for long-range electrostatics in NAMD+VMD workflows.
2026-02-26T02:44:35.8393659Z       - **CHARMM force field**: MacKerell, A. D. et al. (1998). All-atom empirical potential for molecular modeling. *J. Phys. Chem. B* 102, 3586. Primary force field for NAMD/VMD biomolecular simulations.

2026-02-26T02:45:03.9743502Z       ## Extended Documentation
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9744119Z       
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9745282Z       - **Format overview**: https://openbabel.org/docs/dev/FileFormats/Overview.html — 100+ supported formats listed
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9746712Z       - **API documentation (C++)**: https://openbabel.org/api/2.3.0/
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9747824Z       - **Pybel documentation**: https://openbabel.org/docs/dev/UseTheLibrary/Python_Pybel.html
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9749279Z       - **Supported fingerprints**: FP2, FP3, FP4, MACCS described in manual
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9750679Z       - **Force fields in Open Babel**: MMFF94, UFF, GAFF, Ghemical, XTB (via interface)
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9751986Z       - **stereo handling**: Tetrahedral (R/S) and cistrans (E/Z) documented in wiki
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9752820Z       
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9753147Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9753714Z       
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9754495Z       - **obabel command-line primer**: https://openbabel.org/docs/dev/Command-line_tools/babel.html
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9755275Z         ```bash
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9755641Z         obabel aspirin.sdf -O aspirin.mol2
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9756194Z         obabel -:c1ccccc1C(=O)O --gen3d -O aspirin_3d.sdf
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9756794Z         obabel input.sdf -O output.csv --append MW logP
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9757264Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9757585Z       - **Python pybel example**:
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9757992Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9758316Z         import pybel
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9758769Z         mol = pybel.readstring("smi", "CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O")
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9759474Z         mol.make3D()
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9759835Z         mol.write("sdf", "aspirin.sdf")
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9760235Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9760745Z       - **Batch format conversion**: `obabel *.mol2 -O *.pdb` style batch processing
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9762162Z       - **3D coordinate generation**: `--gen3d` flag uses force field to generate initial 3D structure
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9762909Z       
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9763224Z       ## Community Resources
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9763595Z       
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9764115Z       - **GitHub Discussions**: https://github.com/openbabel/openbabel/discussions

2026-02-26T02:45:03.9767146Z       - **KNIME Chemistry Extensions**: Open Babel integrated into KNIME workflows
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9767780Z       
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9768093Z       ## Related & Complementary Tools
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9768514Z       
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9769167Z       - **RDKit**: More Pythonic API; preferred for ML/cheminformatics workflows
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9770006Z       - **CDK (Chemistry Development Kit)**: Java-based; strong 2D rendering
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9770699Z       - **Indigo**: Cheminformatics toolkit from EPAM
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9771368Z       - **ChemDraw**: Commercial; open Babel can convert many of its formats
2026-02-26T02:45:03.9772150Z       - **Avogadro**: Uses Open Babel as its backend for file I/O

2026-02-26T02:45:56.8128727Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8129140Z       
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8129557Z       - **GAMESS first steps**: Input file structure — $CONTRL, $BASIS, $DATA blocks
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8129918Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8130153Z         $CONTRL SCFTYP=RHF RUNTYP=ENERGY DFTTYP=B3LYP $END
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8130480Z         $BASIS GBASIS=N31 NGAUSS=6 NDFUNC=1 $END
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8130740Z         $DATA
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8130924Z         Water
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8131092Z         C1
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8131273Z         O 8.0  0.0  0.0  0.117
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8131495Z         H 1.0  0.0  0.757 -0.468
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8131984Z         H 1.0  0.0 -0.757 -0.468
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8132198Z         $END
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8132465Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8132927Z       - **FMO tutorial**: Divide protein into residue-sized fragments; use FMO2 with MP2
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8133468Z       - **EFP tutorial**: Replace explicit water with EFP fragments for a solvated reaction
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8134016Z       - **TDDFT with GAMESS**: `TDDFT=EXCITE` in $TDDFT group for vertical excitation energies
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8134412Z       
2026-02-26T02:45:56.8134592Z       ## Community Resources

2026-02-26T02:46:18.2233346Z       
2026-02-26T02:46:18.2233898Z       When citing GAMESS, use Schmidt 1993. For recent features (2019+), also cite Barca 2020.
2026-02-26T02:46:18.2234686Z       For FMO calculations, cite Kitaura 1999.
2026-02-26T02:46:18.2235262Z       For EFP calculations, cite Gordon 2001.
2026-02-26T02:46:18.2236101Z       Always note the version of GAMESS used (obtainable from the header of the output file).
2026-02-26T02:46:18.2236916Z       EOF

2026-02-26T02:46:20.5348897Z       
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5349654Z       - **LAMMPS manual (comprehensive)**: https://docs.lammps.org/Manual.html
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5350472Z       - **LAMMPS pair style guide**: https://docs.lammps.org/pair_style.html
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5350960Z       - **Potential database (NIST)**: https://www.ctcms.nist.gov/potentials/
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5351447Z       - **KOKKOS documentation**: https://docs.lammps.org/Speed_kokkos.html
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5351901Z       - **Fix and compute reference**: https://docs.lammps.org/fix.html
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5352229Z       
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5352411Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5352625Z       
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5352817Z       - **LAMMPS tutorial day 1 (basics)**:
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5353078Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5353252Z         units metal
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5353451Z         atom_style atomic
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5353675Z         lattice fcc 3.52
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5353899Z         region box block 0 5 0 5 0 5
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5354150Z         create_box 1 box
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5354366Z         create_atoms 1 box
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5354583Z         pair_style eam
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5354799Z         pair_coeff * * Ni.eam
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5355032Z         minimize 1e-4 1e-6 100 1000
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5355261Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5355529Z       - **Shock wave simulation**: `fix 1 all nvt/sllod` with deformation
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5355980Z       - **ReaxFF molecular simulation**: `pair_style reaxff` + `fix qeq/reaxff`
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5356513Z       - **Metadynamics with PLUMED**: `fix 1 all plumed plumedfile plumed.dat outfile plumed.out`
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5357037Z       - **GPU acceleration**: Run with `lmp_gpu` executable; `package gpu N`
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5357587Z       
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5357770Z       ## Community Resources
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5357986Z       
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5358418Z       - **LAMMPS mailing list**: Very active; solutions to most problems available in archive
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5359219Z       - **NIST Interatomic Potentials Repository**: Curated EAM, Tersoff, MEAM, ReaxFF, etc.
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5359724Z       - **MatSci.org LAMMPS board**: https://matsci.org/c/lammps
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5360314Z       - **LAMMPS GitHub**: https://github.com/lammps/lammps — PRs welcome for new features
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5360736Z       - **Stack Overflow**: Tag `lammps`
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5361088Z       - **Annual LAMMPS workshop**: Sandia National Labs; materials online
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5361432Z       
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5361603Z       ## Related Tools
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5361798Z       
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5362112Z       - **OVITO**: Primary visualization for LAMMPS trajectories; https://www.ovito.org
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5362586Z       - **PLUMED**: Enhanced sampling; https://www.plumed.org
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5362960Z       - **i-PI**: Path integral and QM/MM via socket interface
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5363378Z       - **VESTA**: Crystal structure visualization for periodic LAMMPS simulations
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5363804Z       - **ASE**: Python interface to write/read LAMMPS files
2026-02-26T02:46:20.5364092Z       EOF

2026-02-26T02:47:00.0177814Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0178169Z       - **Basic MOPAC input for water**:
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0178640Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0179141Z         PM7
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0179467Z         Water molecule optimization
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0179874Z         
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0180167Z         O 0.000  1  0.000  1  0.000  1
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0180576Z         H 0.960  1  0.000  0  0.000  0
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0180997Z         H -0.240 1  0.926  1  0.000  0
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0181440Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0181957Z       - **COSMO solvation**: Add `EPS=78.4 RSOLV=1.3` keywords for water solvation
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0182522Z       - **MOZYME for proteins**: Use `MOZYME` keyword with PDB file converted to MOPAC format
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0183321Z       - **PM7 thermochemical properties**: Use `THERMO` keyword for enthalpy of formation
2026-02-26T02:47:00.0183898Z

2026-02-26T02:47:00.4182954Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4183341Z       - **VESTA tutorials (PDF)**: Included with download at https://jp-minerals.org/vesta/en/download.html
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4183985Z       - **VESTA file formats supported**: CIF, VESTA, VASP CHGCAR/LOCPOT, Gaussian CUBE, XSF, DX, ...

2026-02-26T02:47:00.4185603Z       - **Crystal shape morphology**: VESTA can predict crystal morphology from surface energies
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4186031Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4186212Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4186427Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4186659Z       - **Visualizing VASP band structure charge density**:
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4186991Z         1. Run VASP with `LCHARG = .TRUE.`
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4187262Z         2. Open CHGCAR in VESTA
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4187536Z         3. Set isosurface level for electron density
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4187860Z         4. Adjust colors and lighting for publication
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4188133Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4188327Z       - **Importing CIF from ICSD or COD**:
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4188866Z         File → Import → CIF file from crystallographic database
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4189370Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4189561Z       - **XRD pattern simulation**:
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4190029Z         Utilities → Powder Diffraction Pattern → Set wavelength (CuKα = 1.5406 Å)
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4190408Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4190602Z       - **Creating structure videos**:
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4190938Z         Export images for each rotation angle; combine with FFmpeg
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4191461Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4191648Z       ## Community Resources
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4191864Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4192306Z       - **IUCr crystallography forums**: International Union of Crystallography community
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4192766Z       - **Matsci.org VESTA tag**: https://matsci.org
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4193136Z       - **ResearchGate**: Search "VESTA visualization" for user tips
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4193583Z       - **YouTube**: "VESTA tutorial" returns many user-created instructional videos
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4194087Z       - **Materials Project community**: VESTA commonly used alongside MP database
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4194461Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4194649Z       ## Related Crystallography Tools
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4194892Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4195234Z       - **ICSD (Inorganic Crystal Structure Database)**: Commercial database; export CIF for VESTA
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4195741Z       - **Mercury (CCDC)**: Organic crystal structure visualization
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4196156Z       - **CrystalMaker**: Commercial alternative with similar features
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4196617Z       - **FINDSYM**: Symmetry analysis tool (compatible with VESTA structures)
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4197127Z       - **PHONOPY**: Phonon calculation using structures from VESTA-compatible DFT codes
2026-02-26T02:47:00.4197522Z       EOF

2026-02-26T02:47:23.0660680Z       cat >> tools/octopus/use-cases.md << 'EOF'
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0661036Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0661250Z       ## Extended Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0661505Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0661784Z       ### Coupled Electron-Ion Dynamics (Ehrenfest Dynamics)
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0662145Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0662702Z       In Ehrenfest MD, nuclei move on the mean-field potential energy surface while electrons evolve quantum mechanically:
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0663471Z       - Apply a sudden perturbation (ionization, electronic excitation)
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0663993Z       - Let nuclei respond to the real-time electronic forces
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0664484Z       - Study proton transfer after optical excitation in DNA bases
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0664981Z       - Simulate laser-induced bond breaking in small molecules
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0665357Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0665590Z       ### Plasmon Dynamics in Metal Clusters
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0666146Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0666721Z       Study collective electron oscillations in metallic nanoparticles:
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0667466Z       - Au₂₀, Ag₁₄₇ clusters in external oscillating fields
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0667995Z       - Compute optical absorption to identify plasmon resonance frequencies
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0668523Z       - Map near-field enhancement around nanoparticle arrays
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0669396Z       - Applications in SERS (surface-enhanced Raman scattering) substrate design
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0670023Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0670315Z       ### Charge Transfer Dynamics
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0670726Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0671280Z       Compute charge transfer between donor-acceptor molecular pairs:
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0671994Z       - Apply an initial charge displacement
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0672644Z       - Track electron density evolution between donor and acceptor
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0673477Z       - Compute charge transfer rate from time-dependent dipole moment
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0674409Z       - Benchmark against EOM-CCSD or Marcus theory predictions
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0675160Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0675553Z       ### Magneto-optical Kerr Effect (MOKE)
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0675999Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0676391Z       Study laser-induced ultrafast demagnetization:
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0676949Z       - Use non-collinear spin-TDDFT
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0677535Z       - Apply femtosecond laser pulse to magnetic material model
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0678180Z       - Track spin magnetization decay timescales
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0678816Z       - Applications in spintronics and data storage research
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0679570Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0679949Z       ### Transport Through Molecular Junctions
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0680396Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0680687Z       Compute electron transport using non-equilibrium Green's functions:
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0681218Z       - System: electrode–molecule–electrode geometry
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0681539Z       - Apply voltage bias
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0681833Z       - Compute transmission function T(E) and current I(V)
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0682240Z       - Applications: molecular electronics, single-molecule conductance
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0682581Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0682799Z       ### GW Approximation and Quasiparticle Energies
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0683075Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0683282Z       Compute quasiparticle band gaps beyond DFT:
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0683573Z       - Run DFT ground state first
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0683963Z       - Apply G₀W₀ perturbative correction to Kohn-Sham eigenvalues
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0684400Z       - Obtain accurate ionization potentials and electron affinities
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0684853Z       - Applications: band gap prediction for semiconductors and molecules
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0685198Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0685391Z       ## Comparison with Alternative Codes
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0685639Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0685844Z       | Method | Octopus | Alternative codes |
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0686124Z       |--------|---------|------------------|
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0686500Z       | RT-TDDFT optical spectra | ✓ native | SALMON, Siesta |
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0686901Z       | HHG simulation | ✓ best-in-class | QPROP, tRecX |
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0687300Z       | Linear-response TDDFT | ✓ | Gaussian, ORCA, Q-Chem |
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0687721Z       | GW approximation | ✓ experimental | ABINIT, BerkeleyGW |
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0688107Z       | Ehrenfest dynamics | ✓ | CPMD, CP2K |
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0688504Z       | Periodic DFT | ✓ | VASP, QE (better for periodic) |
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0688791Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0689257Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0689503Z       
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0689764Z       - **Input**: Free-format `inp` file with keyword = value syntax
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0690172Z       - **Structure input**: XYZ, PDB, or inline `%Coordinates` block
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0690640Z       - **Output**: ASCII text for energies/spectra; NetCDF for densities and wavefunctions
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0691559Z       - **Visualization**: Output density to CUBE format for Avogadro/VESTA/VMD visualization
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0691982Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0692253Z       echo "Octopus use-cases: $(wc -l < tools/octopus/use-cases.md)"

2026-02-26T02:47:23.0692863Z   result: |
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0693056Z     Octopus use-cases: 103
2026-02-26T02:47:23.0693288Z     <exited with exit code 0>

2026-02-26T02:48:02.3499849Z       cat >> tools/octopus/technology.md << 'EOF'
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3500373Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3500764Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3501174Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3501509Z       ### Real-Space Grid Representation
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3501924Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3503772Z       Wavefunctions ψ_j(r) are stored on a real-space grid — typically a sphere of radius R centered at the molecule (for finite systems). Grid spacing h is the key convergence parameter, similar to kinetic energy cutoff in plane-wave codes:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3505385Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3505660Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3506073Z       E_cut ≈ π²/(2h²) in atomic units
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3506664Z       h = 0.15–0.25 Å is typical for organic molecules
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3507390Z       h = 0.10–0.15 Å for high accuracy or heavy elements
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3507886Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3508162Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3508469Z       ### Pseudopotentials
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3508845Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3509487Z       Octopus uses norm-conserving pseudopotentials (NCPP) from:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3510390Z       - **PseudoDojo**: Relativistic NC pseudopotentials; https://www.pseudo-dojo.org
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3511354Z       - **Hartwigsen-Goedecker-Hutter (HGH)**: Simple analytical form
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3512088Z       - **Troullier-Martins**: Smooth, well-established NCPPs
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3512613Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3512956Z       ### Time Propagation Algorithms
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3513400Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3513966Z       Octopus implements several propagators for i∂ψ/∂t = H ψ:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3514873Z       - **ETRS (Enforced Time-Reversal Symmetry)**: 4th-order accurate; default for converged results
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3515875Z       - **Magnus 4th order**: Higher accuracy via commutator corrections
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3516671Z       - **Lanczos propagator**: Efficient for stiff problems
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3517346Z       - **CN (Crank-Nicholson)**: Simple 2nd-order; useful for debugging
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3518020Z       - **Taylor expansion**: Explicit, fast but requires small timestep
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3518698Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3519181Z       ### libxc Integration
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3519585Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3519922Z       All XC functionals through libxc 5.0+:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3520426Z       - LDA: LDA_X + LDA_C_PW (standard), LDA_C_VWN
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3521007Z       - GGA: GGA_X_PBE + GGA_C_PBE (PBE), BLYP, PBEsol
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3521570Z       - Meta-GGA: MGGA_X_SCAN + MGGA_C_SCAN
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3522197Z       - Hybrid: HSE06, B3LYP (via OEP or perturbative treatment)
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3522733Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3523032Z       ### Performance Optimization
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3523421Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3523784Z       - **SIMD (AVX, AVX-512)**: Vectorized grid operations
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3524417Z       - **PFFT library**: Massively parallel FFT for Poisson solver
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3525096Z       - **CUDA**: GPU acceleration for grid operations (ORCA 2020+)
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3526263Z       - **Mixed-precision**: Some grid operations use single precision for speed
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3526899Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3527415Z       ### Typical Workflow
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3527792Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3528064Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3528415Z       # 1. Ground state calculation
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3528889Z       CalculationMode = gs
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3529980Z       XYZCoordinates = "molecule.xyz"
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3530266Z       Spacing = 0.18*angstrom
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3530501Z       Radius = 8.0*angstrom
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3530910Z       XCFunctional = gga_x_pbe + gga_c_pbe
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3531172Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3531356Z       # 2. TDDFT with delta kick
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3531748Z       CalculationMode = td
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3531989Z       TDPropagator = etrs
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3532342Z       TDTimeStep = 0.002  # in atomic units (~0.05 attoseconds)
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3532761Z       TDMaxSteps = 10000
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3533008Z       TDDeltaKickStrength = 1e-2*eV*angstrom
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3533428Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3533622Z       # 3. Post-process spectrum
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3533878Z       oct-propagation_spectrum
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3534246Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3534416Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3534596Z       ## Strengths and Limitations
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3534834Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3535180Z       | Feature | Octopus | Plane-wave codes |
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3535462Z       |---------|---------|-----------------|
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3535833Z       | RT-TDDFT | Excellent | Limited support |
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3536250Z       | Ground-state DFT | Good for molecules | Better for solids |
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3536901Z       | Memory efficiency | Grid ~O(N³) | Plane waves adaptive |
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3537302Z       | Strong-field physics | Native support | Not standard |
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3537801Z       | Periodic systems | Supported | Optimal |
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3538068Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3538505Z       echo "Octopus technology: $(wc -l < tools/octopus/technology.md)"
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3538901Z     description: Expand Octopus technology
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3539484Z   result: |
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3539676Z     Octopus technology: 111
2026-02-26T02:48:02.3539904Z     <exited with exit code 0>

2026-02-26T02:48:02.7527149Z       ## Additional Key Publications
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7527604Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7527955Z       4. **APBS electrostatics (PyMOL plugin)**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7528927Z          Baker, N. A. et al. (2001). Electrostatics of nanosystems: Application to microtubules and the ribosome. *Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci.* 98, 10037.
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7530272Z          https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.181342398
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7530725Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7531085Z       5. **DSSP secondary structure (used by PyMOL)**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7531908Z          Kabsch, W. & Sander, C. (1983). Dictionary of protein secondary structure. *Biopolymers* 22, 2577.
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7532675Z          https://doi.org/10.1002/bip.360221211
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7533128Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7533444Z       6. **Kabsch alignment**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7534250Z          Kabsch, W. (1976). A solution for the best rotation to relate two sets of vectors. *Acta Crystallogr. A* 32, 922.
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7535139Z          https://doi.org/10.1107/S0567739476001873
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7535605Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7535968Z       7. **PyMOL in drug discovery visualization**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7536987Z          Sanner, M. F. et al. (2009). PyMOL and other molecular visualization applications in drug discovery. *Drug Discov. Today* 2009.
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7538256Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7538913Z       8. **PyMOL for cryo-EM structures**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7539947Z          Used extensively for visualization of cryo-EM maps and fitted atomic models from PDB depositions.
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7540710Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7541028Z       9. **Open-source PyMOL (Anaconda)**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7541629Z          Available via `conda install -c conda-forge pymol-open-source`
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7542193Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7542494Z       ## Extended Documentation
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7542855Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7543350Z       - **PyMOL command reference**: https://pymol.org/pymol-command-ref.html
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7544209Z       - **PyMOL API (pymol.cmd module)**: https://pymol.org/dokuwiki/doku.php?id=api
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7546196Z       - **PyMOL wiki (community)**: https://pymolwiki.org — most comprehensive user resource
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7547283Z       - **Selection algebra**: https://pymolwiki.org/index.php/Selection_Algebra
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7548119Z       - **Color guide**: https://pymolwiki.org/index.php/Color_Values
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7548688Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7549178Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7549539Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7549853Z       - **Basic PyMOL session**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7550260Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7550590Z         from pymol import cmd
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7550991Z         cmd.load("protein.pdb")
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7551405Z         cmd.show("cartoon", "all")
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7551843Z         cmd.color("cyan", "chain A")
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7552278Z         cmd.show("sticks", "resn LIG")
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7552701Z         cmd.ray(2400, 1800)
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7553101Z         cmd.save("figure.png")
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7553463Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7553739Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7554065Z       - **Publication-quality figure**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7554544Z         1. Load structure; `bg_color white`
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7554989Z         2. `show cartoon; hide lines`
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7555456Z         3. `set ray_shadows, 1; set ambient, 0.3`
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7556122Z         4. `ray 3000, 2000` → `save figure.png`
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7556572Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7556903Z       - **APBS electrostatics surface**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7557783Z         Plugin → APBS → run Poisson-Boltzmann → map on surface with `spectrum b, blue_white_red`
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7558507Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7558816Z       - **Structural alignment**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7559801Z         `align chain_A, chain_B` — aligns two structures and reports RMSD
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7560362Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7560660Z       - **Creating movies**:
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7561148Z         Use `mplay` and `mpng` commands for trajectory movies
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7561649Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7561959Z       ## Community Resources
2026-02-26T02:48:02.7562321Z

2026-02-26T02:48:43.1983225Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1983586Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1983911Z       - **AMBER protein simulation tutorial**:
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1984396Z         1. Prepare topology with `tleap`
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1985084Z         2. Minimize: `sander -O -i min.in -o min.out -p protein.prmtop -c protein.inpcrd`
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1986194Z         3. Heat to 300 K with `NTT=3` (Langevin thermostat)
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1987019Z         4. Production MD with `pmemd.cuda`
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1987553Z         5. Analysis with `cpptraj`
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1987974Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1988306Z       - **cpptraj analysis examples**:
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1988721Z         ```bash
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1989378Z         cpptraj protein.prmtop
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1989803Z         trajin production.nc
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1990197Z         rmsd :1-100@CA out rmsd.dat
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1990653Z         hbond out hbonds.dat
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1991034Z         run
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1991351Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1991550Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1991755Z       - **MM-GBSA binding free energy**:
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1992597Z         `MMPBSA.py -O -i mmpbsa.in -o FINAL_RESULTS_MMPBSA.dat -sp solvated.prmtop -cp complex.prmtop -rp receptor.prmtop -lp ligand.prmtop -y trajectory.nc`
2026-02-26T02:48:43.1993408Z

2026-02-26T02:48:43.3977410Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3977627Z       
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3977814Z       - **Basic Psi4 Python input**:
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3978162Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3978430Z         import psi4
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3978783Z         psi4.geometry("""
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3979233Z           O 0.0 0.0 0.117
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3979449Z           H 0.0 0.757 -0.468
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3979670Z           H 0.0 -0.757 -0.468
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3980006Z         """)
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3980292Z         psi4.set_options({'basis': 'cc-pVTZ', 'scf_type': 'df'})
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3980621Z         energy = psi4.energy('B3LYP')
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3981015Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3981210Z       - **SAPT interaction energy**:
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3981448Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3981910Z         psi4.energy('sapt2+', molecule=dimer)  # automatic monomer decomposition
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3982270Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3982596Z       - **PSI4NumPy for learning**: Walk through HF, MP2, CCSD implementations using NumPy
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3983233Z       - **Density fitting MP2**: `psi4.energy('df-mp2')` — faster than canonical MP2
2026-02-26T02:48:43.3983597Z

2026-02-26T02:49:25.5503135Z       - **NWChem DFT geometry optimization**:
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5503599Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5503920Z         title "Water DFT opt"
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5504336Z         geometry units angstrom
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5504743Z           O 0.000  0.000  0.117
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5506045Z           H 0.000  0.757 -0.468
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5506620Z           H 0.000 -0.757 -0.468
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5507017Z         end
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5507319Z         basis
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5507649Z           * library 6-31G*
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5508032Z         end
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5508317Z         dft
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5508632Z           xc b3lyp
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5509160Z         end
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5509503Z         task dft optimize
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5509919Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:49:25.5510135Z

2026-02-26T02:49:25.5511186Z         task ccsd(t)  # after basis and geometry blocks

2026-02-26T02:49:25.6474202Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6474507Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6474895Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6475193Z       ### GTO Basis Set Library
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6475560Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6475992Z       GAMESS includes all standard Gaussian-type orbital basis sets:
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6476724Z       - **Pople basis sets**: STO-3G, 3-21G, 6-31G*, 6-31G**, 6-311G(d,p)
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6477619Z       - **Dunning correlation consistent**: cc-pVDZ through cc-pVQZ, aug-cc-pVTZ
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6478310Z       - **Ahlrichs**: def2-SVP, def2-TZVP
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6478858Z       - **Petersson**: G3MP2Large, CBSB7 for compound methods
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6480109Z       - **Effective core potentials (ECP)**: SBKJC, HW, SBK for heavy elements
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6480913Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6481250Z       ### ONIOM Implementation (LMOMP2)
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6481676Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6483016Z       GAMESS implements a QM/QM scheme where the outer layer uses HF or DFT and the inner layer uses MP2 or CCSD(T). This is distinct from ONIOM-QM/MM but provides an efficient method for improving accuracy on a molecular subregion.
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6484530Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6484872Z       ### FMO Technical Implementation
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6485284Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6485595Z       Fragment Molecular Orbital:
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6486412Z       1. **Monomer SCF**: Solve HF/DFT for each fragment in the ESP of all other fragments (using Mulliken charges)
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6487403Z       2. **Pair SCF**: Solve coupled fragment pairs (FMO2) or triplets (FMO3)
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6488344Z       3. **Pair interaction energy**: ΔE_IJ = E_IJ - E_I - E_J
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6489524Z       4. Total energy: E = Σ_I E_I + Σ_{I>J} ΔE_IJ + Σ_{I>J>K} ΔE_IJK (FMO3)
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6490129Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6490811Z       Memory requirement scales as O(N) with FMO (N = number of fragments), enabling protein-scale calculations.
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6491663Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6491990Z       ### Distributed Data Interface (DDI)
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6492456Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6492873Z       GAMESS's parallel communication layer (DDI) implements:
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6493547Z       - One-sided "put" and "get" operations on distributed arrays
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6494357Z       - Better suited than MPI for irregular communication patterns in quantum chemistry
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6495241Z       - Available for MPI, SHM (shared memory), and socket-based backends
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6495849Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6496186Z       ### Input File Structure
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6496570Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6496866Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6497321Z        $CONTRL SCFTYP=RHF RUNTYP=OPTIMIZE DFTTYP=B3LYP COORD=ZMT $END
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6497953Z        $SYSTEM TIMLIM=600 MEMORY=256000000 $END
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6498562Z        $BASIS GBASIS=N311 NGAUSS=6 NDFUNC=1 NPFUNC=1 DIFFSP=.T. $END
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6499565Z        $STATPT OPTTOL=0.0001 NSTEP=100 $END
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6500011Z        $DATA
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6500379Z        Ethanol B3LYP/6-311+G(d,p) optimization
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6500832Z        C1
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6501130Z        C 6.0  0.0  0.0  0.0
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6501482Z        C 6.0  1.53 0.0  0.0
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6501873Z        O 8.0  2.0  1.3  0.0
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6502225Z        H 1.0  ...
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6502536Z        $END
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6502833Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6503122Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6503451Z       ### Post-HF Methods Implementation
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6503882Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6504427Z       - **CISD**: Configuration interaction up to double excitations from RHF reference
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6505408Z       - **GUGA-MCSCF**: Active space restricted to user-defined orbitals; uses GUGA algebra
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6506325Z       - **MP2**: Conventional O(N^5); also density-fitted (RI-MP2) available
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6507229Z       - **Coupled cluster**: CCSD and CCSD(T) for single-reference; ROHF-CCSD for open-shell
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6507925Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6508238Z       ## Performance & Optimization
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6508652Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6509340Z       - **Parallel DFT**: Grid-based XC evaluation parallelized over MPI groups
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6510296Z       - **FMO scaling**: Sub-linear with fragment count; demonstrated for >1000 fragment systems
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6511327Z       - **Memory usage**: GAMESS stores integrals in memory or disk (DIRSCF=.T. for direct)
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6512342Z       - **HPC examples**: Runs efficiently on national supercomputers (XSEDE/ACCESS, KAUST Shaheen)
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6513442Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6513756Z       ## Comparison with Other QC Codes
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6514350Z       
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6514715Z       | Feature | GAMESS | Gaussian | Psi4 | ORCA |
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6515239Z       |---------|--------|----------|------|------|
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6515851Z       | FMO | ✓ best | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6516372Z       | EFP | ✓ best | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6516898Z       | DLPNO-CC | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ best |
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6517393Z       | SAPT | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ best | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6517795Z       | Cost | Free | Commercial | Free | Free (academic) |
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6518328Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:49:25.6518884Z       echo "GAMESS technology: $(wc -l < tools/gamess/technology.md)"

2026-02-26T02:49:25.6520433Z     GAMESS technology: 111

2026-02-26T02:50:03.6241233Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6241535Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6241974Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6242307Z       ### Global Arrays Programming Model
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6242760Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6243311Z       NWChem's parallel programming uses the Global Arrays (GA) toolkit, providing:
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6244319Z       - **One-sided communication**: `ga_put`, `ga_get`, `ga_acc` without explicit message passing
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6245373Z       - **Distributed arrays**: Large arrays partitioned across nodes with local/remote access
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6246128Z       - **Atomic operations**: Fetch-and-add for race-condition-free parallel accumulation
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6246726Z       - **Mirrored arrays**: Replicated small arrays for efficient broadcast
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6247103Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6247721Z       This differs from traditional MPI all-to-all patterns and is better suited for irregular quantum chemistry communication patterns (e.g., CCSD amplitude updates).
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6248396Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6248585Z       ### TCE (Tensor Contraction Engine)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6248834Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6249415Z       TCE automatically generates efficient CC code:
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6249754Z       1. User specifies CC equations symbolically
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6250112Z       2. TCE applies factorization and graph-based optimization
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6250502Z       3. Generates FORTRAN or C code with optimal loop ordering
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6250868Z       4. Parallel distribution built into generated code
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6251398Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6251942Z       Supports: CCSD, CCSD(T), CCSDT, CCSDTQ, EOM-CCSD, EOM-CCSD(T), CR-EOMCCSD(T), IP/EA-EOM-CC
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6252641Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6252957Z       ### Plane-Wave DFT (NWPW Module)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6253263Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6253444Z       NWPW implements:
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6254501Z       - **CPMD**: Car-Parrinello MD equations; fictitious electron mass μ, dual variables
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6255440Z       - **PSPW**: Pseudopotential plane-wave DFT; PBE, LDA, hybrid functionals
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6256244Z       - **Band**: Periodic Kohn-Sham for insulators and semiconductors
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6257078Z       - **AIMD**: Born-Oppenheimer MD with wavefunction-on-the-fly optimization
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6257713Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6258226Z       Pseudopotentials: Troullier-Martins, Hamann, Goedecker-Teter-Hutter (GTH) types.
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6259141Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6259811Z       ### QM/MM in NWChem
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6260188Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6260555Z       NWChem supports ONIOM-style QM/MM:
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6261309Z       - MM region: AMBER or DREIDING force field
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6261962Z       - QM region: Any DFT or wavefunction method
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6262590Z       - Electrostatic embedding: MM charges in QM Hamiltonian
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6263230Z       - Mechanical embedding: Simpler, less accurate
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6263870Z       - Applications: Enzyme active sites, chromophore proteins
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6264426Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6264747Z       ### Relativistic Capabilities
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6265154Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6265577Z       NWChem includes several relativistic treatments:
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6266160Z       - **ZORA**: Zeroth-order regular approximation (scalar and spin-orbit)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6266711Z       - **DKH**: Douglas-Kroll-Hess 2nd order (DKH2)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6267378Z       - **X2C**: Exact two-component relativistic transformation
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6268206Z       - Essential for heavy elements (lanthanides, actinides, transition metals beyond 4th row)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6269167Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6269485Z       ### Input/Output Format
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6269863Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6270440Z       - Input: Block-structured keyword format (geometry, basis, dft, mp2, etc. blocks)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6271061Z       - Output: Text with final energy, gradient, properties
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6271424Z       - Wavefunction files: Binary `.mo` files for restart
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6271797Z       - Integration with ASE, cclib, AiiDA for automated workflows
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6272110Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6272302Z       ## Performance Guidelines
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6272521Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6272868Z       - Best on large HPC clusters (100–10,000 MPI tasks)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6273233Z       - TCE CC methods: Good parallel scaling to thousands of cores
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6273697Z       - NWPW: Excellent parallel scaling (plane-wave algorithms natural for MPI)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6274202Z       - Single workstation: GAMESS, Psi4, ORCA are usually more efficient
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6274547Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6274729Z       ## Comparison with Peer Codes
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6274960Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6275191Z       | Feature | NWChem | Gaussian | ORCA | Psi4 |
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6275494Z       |---------|--------|----------|------|------|
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6275865Z       | Plane-wave DFT | ✓ (NWPW) | Limited | ✗ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6276271Z       | TCE high-order CC | ✓ best | Limited | ✗ | partial |
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6276624Z       | AIMD (CP/BO) | ✓ | Limited | ✗ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6276932Z       | DLPNO-CC | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ best | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6277299Z       | HPC scaling | ✓ excellent | Limited | Limited | Limited |
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6277706Z       | Free/Open | ✓ | Commercial | Free acad. | ✓ |
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6277976Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6278269Z       echo "NWChem technology: $(wc -l < tools/nwchem/technology.md)"

2026-02-26T02:50:03.6292068Z       - **rdkit.Chem module reference**: https://www.rdkit.org/docs/source/rdkit.Chem.html

2026-02-26T02:50:03.6294049Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6294222Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6294430Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6294619Z       - **Common RDKit operations**:
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6294862Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6295067Z         from rdkit import Chem
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6295349Z         from rdkit.Chem import AllChem, Descriptors, Draw
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6295633Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6295845Z         # Parse SMILES
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6296100Z         mol = Chem.MolFromSmiles("CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O")
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6296385Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6296565Z         # Generate 3D conformation
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6296807Z         mol = Chem.AddHs(mol)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6297078Z         AllChem.EmbedMolecule(mol, AllChem.ETKDGv3())
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6297390Z         AllChem.MMFFOptimizeMolecule(mol)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6297644Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6297833Z         # Compute Morgan fingerprint
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6298196Z         fp = AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(mol, radius=2, nBits=2048)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6298557Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6298737Z         # Calculate descriptors
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6299211Z         mw = Descriptors.MolWt(mol)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6299545Z         logp = Descriptors.MolLogP(mol)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6299812Z         tpsa = Descriptors.TPSA(mol)
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6300045Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6300211Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6300390Z       - **Substructure search**:
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6300616Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6300879Z         pattern = Chem.MolFromSmarts("c1ccccc1")  # benzene ring
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6301273Z         mols = [m for m in library if m.HasSubstructMatch(pattern)]
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6301584Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6301749Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6301935Z       - **Maximum Common Substructure**:
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6302181Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6302378Z         from rdkit.Chem import MCS
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6302642Z         result = MCS.FindMCS([mol1, mol2, mol3])
2026-02-26T02:50:03.6303082Z         ```

2026-02-26T02:50:26.6354683Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6355091Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6355468Z       - **Simple DFT calculation**:
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6355956Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6356274Z         &GLOBAL
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6356591Z           PROJECT water
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6357001Z           RUN_TYPE ENERGY
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6357409Z         &END GLOBAL
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6357777Z         &FORCE_EVAL
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6358124Z           METHOD Quickstep
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6358523Z           &DFT
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6359146Z             BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME BASIS_MOLOPT
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6359648Z             POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME GTH_POTENTIALS
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6360083Z             &QS
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6360399Z               EPS_DEFAULT 1.0E-12
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6360797Z             &END QS
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6361117Z             &XC
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6361426Z               &XC_FUNCTIONAL PBE
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6361812Z               &END XC_FUNCTIONAL
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6362193Z             &END XC
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6362511Z           &END DFT
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6362826Z           &SUBSYS
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6363141Z             &CELL
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6363486Z               ABC [angstrom] 12 12 12
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6363907Z             &END CELL
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6364231Z             &COORD
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6364540Z               O 0.0 0.0 0.117
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6364912Z               H 0.0 0.757 -0.468
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6365292Z               H 0.0 -0.757 -0.468
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6365689Z             &END COORD
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6366017Z             &KIND O
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6366374Z               BASIS_SET DZVP-MOLOPT-SR-GTH
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6366841Z               POTENTIAL GTH-PBE-q6
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6367237Z             &END KIND
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6367576Z           &END SUBSYS
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6367904Z         &END FORCE_EVAL
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6368245Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6368536Z       
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6369203Z       - **Metadynamics tutorial**: Use `&METADYN` section with PLUMED interface
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6370005Z       - **AIMD workflow**: Set `RUN_TYPE MD` with `NVT` or `NPT` ensemble
2026-02-26T02:50:26.6370504Z

2026-02-26T02:51:09.3180665Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3181059Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3181780Z       - **Basic property prediction**:
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3182697Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3183274Z         import deepchem as dc
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3183531Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3183725Z         # Load Tox21 from MoleculeNet
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3184208Z         tasks, datasets, transformers = dc.molnet.load_tox21()
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3184542Z         train, valid, test = datasets
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3184939Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3185193Z         # Build AttentiveFP model
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3185875Z         model = dc.models.AttentiveFPModel(n_tasks=len(tasks), mode='classification')
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3186614Z         model.fit(train)
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3186991Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3187304Z         # Evaluate
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3187672Z         metric = dc.metrics.Metric(dc.metrics.roc_auc_score)
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3188124Z         print(model.evaluate(test, [metric], transformers))
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3188524Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3188705Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3189099Z       - **SMILES featurization**:
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3189535Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3190067Z         featurizer = dc.feat.MolGraphConvFeaturizer(use_edges=True)
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3190785Z         X = featurizer.featurize(["CCO", "c1ccccc1", "CC(=O)O"])
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3191303Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3191584Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3191906Z       - **Molecular generation with GAN**:
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3192490Z         DeepChem includes MolGAN and VAE-based generation models.
2026-02-26T02:51:09.3193059Z

2026-02-26T02:51:09.6174537Z       11. **Volume of distribution (VD) prediction**:
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6175434Z           Lombardo, F. et al. (2002). Prediction of volume of distribution values in humans. *J. Med. Chem.* 45, 2867.
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6176302Z           https://doi.org/10.1021/jm0200004
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6176727Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6177036Z       ## Extended Documentation
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6177433Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6178010Z       - **pkCSM prediction endpoint descriptions**: https://biosig.lab.uq.edu.au/pkcsm/help
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6179328Z       - **pkCSM API (programmatic access)**: Available via HTTP POST to https://biosig.lab.uq.edu.au/pkcsm/api
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6180307Z       - **Input format**: SMILES strings submitted via web form or API
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6181012Z       - **Output**: JSON or CSV with predictions and confidence scores

2026-02-26T02:51:09.6194668Z       ## Tutorials & Learning Resources
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6195097Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6195646Z       - **Step-by-step pkCSM tutorial**: https://biosig.lab.uq.edu.au/pkcsm/prediction
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6196470Z         1. Enter SMILES (e.g., `CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O` for aspirin)
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6197070Z         2. Submit and retrieve JSON results
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6197720Z         3. Interpret traffic-light indicators (green=favorable, red=unfavorable)
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6198345Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6198676Z       - **Batch prediction workflow**:
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6199297Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6199639Z         import requests
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6200194Z         smiles_list = ["CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O", "c1ccc(cc1)CN2CCN(CC2)c3ccc(cc3)Cl"]
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6200852Z         for smi in smiles_list:
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6201381Z             r = requests.post("https://biosig.lab.uq.edu.au/pkcsm/api",
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6202013Z                               data={"smiles": smi})
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6202507Z             print(r.json())
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6202871Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:51:09.6203161Z

2026-02-26T02:51:34.8931597Z       ## Extended Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8931810Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8932294Z       - **Justin Lemkul GROMACS tutorials**: http://www.mdtutorials.com/gmx/ — Gold standard tutorials covering:
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8932990Z         - Protein in water (lysozyme)
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8933368Z         - Membrane protein
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8933592Z         - Virtual sites
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8933811Z         - Free energy of solvation
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8934045Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8934222Z       - **Basic GROMACS workflow**:
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8934457Z         ```bash
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8934714Z         gmx pdb2gmx -f protein.pdb -o protein.gro -water spce
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8935095Z         gmx editconf -f protein.gro -o box.gro -c -d 1.0 -bt cubic
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8935516Z         gmx solvate -cp box.gro -cs spc216.gro -o solvated.gro -p topol.top
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8935959Z         gmx grompp -f minim.mdp -c solvated.gro -p topol.top -o em.tpr
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8936302Z         gmx mdrun -v -deffnm em
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8936524Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8936691Z       
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8936875Z       - **Analysis examples**:
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8937106Z         ```bash
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8937325Z         gmx rms -s md.tpr -f md.xtc -o rmsd.xvg
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8937637Z         gmx rmsf -s md.tpr -f md.xtc -o rmsf.xvg -res
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8937960Z         gmx gyrate -s md.tpr -f md.xtc -o gyrate.xvg
2026-02-26T02:51:34.8938231Z         ```

2026-02-26T02:52:04.3095054Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3095376Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3095755Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3095961Z       ### Gaussian Basis Set Hierarchy
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3096229Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3096493Z       Gaussian 16 supports the full hierarchy of basis sets:
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3096828Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3097034Z       **Pople-style basis sets**:
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3097352Z       - STO-3G: Minimal; 3 Gaussians per STO
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3097715Z       - 3-21G: Split-valence; affordable for large systems
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3098407Z       - 6-31G(d,p): Polarization functions; standard for organic molecules
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3099260Z       - 6-311+G(d,p): Triple-zeta + diffuse; better energetics
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3099701Z       - 6-311++G(2df,2p): High accuracy for thermochemistry
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3100031Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3100239Z       **Dunning correlation-consistent**:
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3100617Z       - cc-pVDZ, cc-pVTZ, cc-pVQZ: Systematically improve to CBS limit
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3101051Z       - aug-cc-pVDZ: + diffuse functions for anion, excited states
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3101409Z       - cc-pCVTZ: Core-valence correlation
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3101681Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3101879Z       **Ahlrichs/Weigend def2 family**:
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3102167Z       - def2-SVP: Economy; for geometry only
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3102460Z       - def2-TZVP: Accurate properties
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3102741Z       - def2-QZVP: Near-complete basis
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3102996Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3103247Z       ### DFT Implementation
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3103623Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3104045Z       Gaussian implements exchange-correlation functionals via:
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3104735Z       - **Pure DFT**: LDA (SVWN), GGA (BLYP, PBE, BP86), meta-GGA (M06-L, TPSS)
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3105438Z       - **Hybrid DFT**: B3LYP, PBE0, M06-2X, M06-HF, wB97X-D
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3106207Z       - **Range-separated**: CAM-B3LYP, wB97X-D3 for charge transfer excited states
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3107071Z       - **Double-hybrid**: B2PLYP, mPW2PLYP (include MP2 correlation)
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3107669Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3108293Z       Grid for numerical XC: `ultrafine` (99,590 points) standard; `superfine` for benchmarks.
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3109341Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3109708Z       ### Coupled Cluster Implementation
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3110164Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3110464Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3110786Z       %nprocshared=16
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3111186Z       %mem=32GB
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3111539Z       #p CCSD(T)/cc-pVTZ
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3111864Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3112177Z       CCSD(T) single point
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3112555Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3112852Z       0 1
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3113174Z       [coordinates]
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3113448Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3113750Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3114204Z       CCSD(T) scales as O(N^7); practical for up to ~20 heavy atoms.
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3114842Z       For larger systems: MP2 O(N^5), MP3 O(N^6), CCSD O(N^6).
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3115315Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3115604Z       ### ONIOM Multi-Layer QM/MM
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3115983Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3116296Z       Gaussian implements ONIOM with:
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3116881Z       - 2-layer: High-level QM for active site + low-level (HF, PM7, MM)
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3117483Z       - 3-layer: High QM / Medium QM / MM
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3117932Z       - Mechanical or electronic embedding
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3118602Z       - Applications: Enzyme mechanisms, protein NMR chemical shifts, zeolite catalysis
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3119546Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3119830Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3120128Z       #p ONIOM(B3LYP/6-31G*:PM6)
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3120490Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3120757Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3121039Z       ### NBO Analysis
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3121348Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3121744Z       Natural Bond Orbital analysis via NBO 7.0 integration:
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3122455Z       - **Natural charges**: More physical than Mulliken; polarization-corrected
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3123052Z       - **Natural Population Analysis (NPA)**
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3123398Z       - **Second-order perturbation theory (donor-acceptor)**:
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3123948Z         E(2) = -n_σ * <σ|F|σ*>² / (ε_σ* - ε_σ)
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3124229Z       - **Natural Resonance Theory (NRT)**
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3124497Z       - **NBO Wiberg bond orders**
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3124725Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3124924Z       ### IRC (Intrinsic Reaction Coordinate)
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3125386Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3125816Z       Follow the steepest descent path from transition state to reactants and products:
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3126221Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3126467Z       #p B3LYP/6-31G* IRC=(forward,stepsize=10,maxpoints=50)
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3126762Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3127026Z       Verifies that a TS connects the correct reactant and product.
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3127341Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3127520Z       ### Solvation Models
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3127724Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3127978Z       - **PCM (Polarizable Continuum Model)**: Default; `SCRF=PCM`
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3128390Z       - **SMD**: Best for thermochemical solvation; `SCRF=(SMD,Solvent=Water)`
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3128765Z       - **CPCM**: Conductor-PCM; `SCRF=CPCM`
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3129660Z       - Solvent dielectric constant ε and cavity radius determine solvation free energy
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3130067Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3130264Z       ## Performance Guidelines
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3130490Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3130870Z       - **GPU acceleration**: Only limited support (unofficial via NVIDIA); ORCA/GROMACS better for GPU
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3131628Z       - **Disk I/O**: Gaussian uses temporary files on scratch disk; fast SSD essential
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3132256Z       - **Memory**: `%mem=32GB` for large MP2/CCSD; 4-8 GB for typical DFT
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3132822Z       - **Scaling**: Excellent single-node shared-memory via OpenMP; limited multi-node (no MPI for main routines)
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3133380Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3133658Z       ## Comparison
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3133962Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3134246Z       | Feature | Gaussian | ORCA | Psi4 | NWChem |
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3134552Z       |---------|----------|------|------|--------|
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3134898Z       | ONIOM | ✓ best | limited | ✗ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3135206Z       | DLPNO-CC | ✗ | ✓ best | ✗ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3135506Z       | Free | ✗ | free acad. | ✓ | ✓ |
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3135788Z       | SAPT | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ best | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3136138Z       | NBO | ✓ built-in | ✓ interface | ✓ interface | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3136420Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:52:04.3136702Z       echo "Gaussian technology: $(wc -l < tools/gaussian/technology.md)"

2026-02-26T02:52:04.3137334Z   result: |
