2026-02-26T02:52:29.9963506Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9963771Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9963998Z       ### OpenGL Rendering Pipeline
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9964258Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9964573Z       PyMOL's real-time rendering uses OpenGL with these representations:
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9965029Z       - **Lines**: Fastest; simple bond sticks
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9965394Z       - **Sticks**: Cylinders for bonds; spheres for atoms
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9965800Z       - **Spheres (VDW)**: Van der Waals radii as solid spheres
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9966458Z       - **Cartoon**: Secondary structure ribbon with Bézier curves
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9966936Z       - **Surface**: Solvent-excluded surface (SES) with MSMS algorithm
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9967335Z       - **Mesh**: Wireframe surface
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9967641Z       - **Dots**: Dot representation of surface
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9967988Z       - **Nonbonded**: For ions and unconnected atoms
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9968295Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9968760Z       ### Ray Tracing Engine
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9969237Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9969823Z       PyMOL's built-in ray tracer supports:
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9970300Z       - Ambient occlusion (soft shadows from environment lighting)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9970693Z       - Shadows with soft and hard edges
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9970988Z       - Interior lighting
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9971237Z       - Depth cueing (fog)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9971528Z       - Toon shading (cel-shading for illustrations)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9971842Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9972030Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9972248Z       # High-quality ray trace
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9972507Z       cmd.set("ray_shadows", 1)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9972767Z       cmd.set("ambient", 0.4)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9973022Z       cmd.set("direct", 0.8)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9973276Z       cmd.set("specular", 0.5)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9973528Z       cmd.ray(3000, 2000)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9973792Z       cmd.save("publication_figure.png")
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9974244Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9974527Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9974826Z       ### Molecular Surface Algorithm
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9975211Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9975527Z       PyMOL uses either:
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9976292Z       1. **Built-in surface**: Rolling probe (1.4 Å water) using uniform grid; fast
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9977218Z       2. **MSMS** (Sanner's Molecular Surface): More accurate; requires MSMS binary
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9977890Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9978252Z       Surface properties can be colored by:
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9978831Z       - B-factor (crystallographic thermal motion)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9979579Z       - Partial charges (Gasteiger, AMBER)
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9980157Z       - Electrostatic potential from APBS Poisson-Boltzmann
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9980797Z       - Custom per-atom values from `alter` command
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9981298Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9981632Z       ### Python Command API
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9981998Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9982402Z       All PyMOL operations accessible via `pymol.cmd`:
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9982945Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9983335Z       from pymol import cmd
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9983739Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9984063Z       # Selection operations
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9984658Z       cmd.select("binding_site", "byres protein within 4 of resn LIG")
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9985255Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9985532Z       # Representation
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9985936Z       cmd.show("surface", "protein")
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9986238Z       cmd.hide("lines", "all")
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9986485Z       cmd.show("sticks", "binding_site")
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9986860Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9987151Z       # Color by secondary structure
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9987635Z       cmd.color("red", "ss h")     # helices red
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9988132Z       cmd.color("yellow", "ss s")  # strands yellow
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9988609Z       cmd.color("cyan", "ss ''")   # loops cyan
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9988875Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9989331Z       # Alignment and RMSD
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9989706Z       rmsd = cmd.align("mobile", "target")
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9990165Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9990448Z       # Distance measurement
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9990928Z       cmd.distance("d1", "/protein/A/LYS`123/NZ", "/protein/A/ASP`89/OD1")
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9991289Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9991472Z       # Structure output
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9991824Z       cmd.save("aligned.pdb")
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9992050Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9992225Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9992454Z       ### Plugin System
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9992722Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9992955Z       PyMOL plugins are Python modules that integrate via:
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9993509Z       - Menu entries in Plugin → Plugin Manager
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9993862Z       - Additional commands accessible from PyMOL command line
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9994398Z       - APBS plugin: Poisson-Boltzmann electrostatics
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9994724Z       - Autodock plugin: Docking visualization
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9995470Z       - CAVER: Protein tunnel/channel analysis
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9996129Z       - DynDom: Domain motion analysis
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9996531Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9996881Z       ### Molecular Alignment Algorithms
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9997134Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9997398Z       - **fit**: Align by all atoms (no sequence requirement); minimizes RMSD
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9997997Z       - **align**: Sequence alignment + structure superposition; best for homologs
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9998635Z       - **super**: Structure-based alignment ignoring sequence; good for remote homologs
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9999463Z       - **cealign**: CE (combinatorial extension) algorithm for structural alignment
2026-02-26T02:52:29.9999844Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0000152Z       ## Performance Characteristics
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0000406Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0000683Z       - **Interactive**: 30-60 fps for proteins up to 100k atoms on modern GPU
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0001270Z       - **Surfaces**: MSMS surface generation: ~1 s for 300 aa protein
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0001887Z       - **Ray tracing**: ~30-120 seconds at 3000×2000 resolution
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0002592Z       - **Memory**: ~1 GB for typical structure + surface; ~4 GB for virus capsid
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0003168Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0003512Z       ## Dependencies in Detail
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0003795Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0004000Z       - **freeglut**: Window management and input
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0004411Z       - **libpng**: PNG image output
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0004677Z       - **libjpeg**: JPEG image output  
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0005071Z       - **freetype2**: Vector font rendering for labels
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0005442Z       - **MMTF/mmCIF parsers**: Modern PDB format support
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0005903Z       - **APBS** (optional): Poisson-Boltzmann electrostatics plugin
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0006315Z       - **PyQt5**: GUI dialogs (menus, preferences)
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0006663Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:52:30.0006987Z       echo "PyMOL technology: $(wc -l < tools/pymol/technology.md)"

2026-02-26T02:52:58.6265417Z       ## Additional Key Publications
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6265676Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6265897Z       7. **Veber rules for oral bioavailability**:
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6266543Z          Veber, D. F. et al. (2002). Molecular properties that influence the oral bioavailability of drug candidates. *J. Med. Chem.* 45, 2615.
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6267202Z          https://doi.org/10.1021/jm020017n
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6267474Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6267659Z       8. **Ghose filter**:
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6268253Z          Ghose, A. K. et al. (1999). A knowledge-based approach in designing combinatorial or medicinal chemistry libraries. *J. Comb. Chem.* 1, 55.
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6268889Z          https://doi.org/10.1021/cc9800071
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6269318Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6269534Z       9. **Egan rule (BOILED-Egg predecessor)**:
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6270080Z          Egan, W. J. et al. (2000). Prediction of drug absorption using multivariate statistics. *J. Med. Chem.* 43, 3867.
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6270619Z          https://doi.org/10.1021/jm000292e
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6270922Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6271218Z       10. **XLOGP3**:
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6272058Z           Wang, R. et al. (1997). Calculation of partition coefficients using atom-additive methods. *Perspect. Drug Discov. Des.* 7, 168.
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6273288Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6274080Z       11. **WLogP**:
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6275247Z           Wildman, S. A. & Crippen, G. M. (1999). Prediction of physicochemical parameters by atomic contributions. *J. Chem. Inf. Comput. Sci.* 39, 868.
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6277351Z           https://doi.org/10.1021/ci990307l
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6277834Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6278112Z       12. **MLogP**:
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6278929Z           Moriguchi, I. et al. (1992). Simple method of calculating octanol/water partition coefficient. *Chem. Pharm. Bull.* 40, 127.
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6280131Z           https://doi.org/10.1248/cpb.40.127
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6280601Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6280934Z       ## Extended Documentation
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6281337Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6282380Z       - **SwissADME help**: http://www.swissadme.ch/help.php — detailed description of all descriptors
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6283716Z       - **BOILED-Egg theory**: http://www.swissadme.ch/index.php — background on Egan plot
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6284307Z       - **API usage**: SwissADME can be called programmatically for batch calculations
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6284955Z       - **SIB group**: https://www.sib.swiss/olivier-michielin-group — developer group page
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6285694Z       - **SwissTargetPrediction**: http://www.swisstargetprediction.ch — companion target prediction tool
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6286170Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6286364Z       ## All Computed Properties
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6286595Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6286788Z       ### Physicochemical Properties
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6287057Z       - **MW**: Molecular weight (Da)
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6287355Z       - **Heavy atoms**: Number of non-hydrogen atoms
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6287692Z       - **Aromatic heavy atoms**: Count of aromatic atoms
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6288097Z       - **Fraction Csp3**: Fraction of sp³ carbon atoms
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6288448Z       - **Rotatable bonds**: Number of rotatable bonds
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6288809Z       - **H-bond acceptors/donors**: Lipinski HBA and HBD counts
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6289677Z       - **MR**: Molecular refractivity (polarizability measure)
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6290301Z       - **TPSA**: Topological polar surface area (Ertl method, Å²)
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6290616Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6290806Z       ### Lipophilicity (5 methods)
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6291112Z       - **iLogP**: DFT-based logP (most physically rigorous)
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6291434Z       - **XLOGP3**: Atom contribution model
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6291717Z       - **WLogP**: Wildman-Crippen method
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6291987Z       - **MLogP**: Moriguchi logP
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6292262Z       - **CLogP** (if available): Hansch-Leo method
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6292599Z       - **Consensus LogP**: Average of available methods
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6292879Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6293061Z       ### Water Solubility
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6293317Z       - **ESOL** (Delaney): Estimated solubility
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6293608Z       - **Ali**: ML-based prediction
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6293890Z       - **Silicos-IT**: Filter-based prediction
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6294148Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6294333Z       ### Drug-likeness Filters
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6294678Z       - **Lipinski Ro5**: MW ≤500, LogP ≤5, HBA ≤10, HBD ≤5
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6295053Z       - **Veber**: Rotatable bonds ≤10, TPSA ≤140
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6295458Z       - **Egan**: TPSA ≤131.6, WLogP ≤5.88 (BOILED-Egg white zone)
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6295812Z       - **Muegge**: Extended Ro5 + ring count
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6296164Z       - **Ghose**: 160 ≤ MW ≤ 480, -0.4 ≤ LogP ≤ 5.6
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6296430Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6296611Z       ### Medicinal Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6296899Z       - **PAINS alerts**: Pan-assay interference compounds
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6297308Z       - **Brenk structural alerts**: Potentially problematic functional groups
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6297953Z       - **Lead-likeness**: MW ≤350, LogP ≤3
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6298796Z       - **Bioavailability score**: Oral bioavailability likelihood
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6299615Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6299901Z       ## Tutorials
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6300222Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6300519Z       - **Aspirin analysis**:
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6300942Z         1. Go to http://www.swissadme.ch
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6301427Z         2. Enter SMILES: `CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O`
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6302046Z         3. Run analysis → MW=180 Da, LogP=1.2, TPSA=63 Å², passes Ro5
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6302376Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6302570Z       - **Multi-compound comparison**:
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6302980Z         Enter multiple SMILES (one per line) for comparative analysis across a lead series
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6303376Z       
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6303556Z       - **Batch via API**:
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6303786Z         ```python
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6303981Z         import requests
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6304220Z         response = requests.post(
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6304528Z             "http://www.swissadme.ch/include/swissadme.php",
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6304926Z             data={"smiles": ["CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O", "c1ccc2ccccc2c1"]}
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6305242Z         )
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6305412Z         ```
2026-02-26T02:52:58.6305582Z

2026-02-26T02:53:24.2971370Z       ## Extended Use Cases
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2971742Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2972075Z       ### MCSCF/NEVPT2 Photochemistry
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2972510Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2972852Z       For photochemically relevant systems:
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2973543Z       - Define active space: e.g., CAS(10,10) for retinal protonated Schiff base
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2974656Z       - Include π and π* orbitals, lone pairs, and σ bonds of breaking bonds
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2975352Z       - Optimize excited state geometries
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2975753Z       - Locate conical intersections (degenerate MCSCF)
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2976220Z       - Application: Azobenzene photoisomerization mechanism, DNA photoprotection
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2976737Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2977049Z       ### Nucleation and Cluster Chemistry
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2977460Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2977775Z       GAMESS is commonly used for:
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2978322Z       - Sulfuric acid-water cluster formation (atmospheric nucleation)
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2979396Z       - HNO3-NH3-H2O clusters up to (H2SO4)₄(H2O)₁₀
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2979837Z       - MP2/aug-cc-pVDZ binding energies; BSSE correction with counterpoise
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2980614Z       - Compare with DFT+D3 for dispersion accuracy
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2981104Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2981489Z       ### Excited States of Transition Metal Complexes
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2982268Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2982600Z       Multi-reference methods for:
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2983544Z       - Spin-forbidden transitions in Ir, Ru, Pt complexes (OLED phosphors)
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2985380Z       - CASSCF + NEVPT2 for d-d transitions
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2986367Z       - EOM-CC for charge-transfer excited states in coordination compounds
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2987981Z       - Prediction of absorption spectra for photosensitizers
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2988624Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2989327Z       ### Protein-Ligand Binding with FMO-PIEDA
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2989833Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2990354Z       Pair Interaction Energy Decomposition Analysis (PIEDA) from FMO:
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2991160Z       - For each residue-ligand pair, decompose interaction into:
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2991751Z         - Electrostatic (ES)
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2992163Z         - Exchange-repulsion (EX)
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2992582Z         - Charge transfer (CT)
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2992985Z         - Dispersion (DI)
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2993493Z       - Identify pharmacophore contributions quantitatively
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2994235Z       - Used in structure-based drug design for kinase inhibitors, HIV protease
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2994830Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2995276Z       ### Zeolite Brønsted Acid Catalysis
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2995679Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2995961Z       Cluster model DFT:
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2996574Z       - Extract T-site cluster (T12 model: 1 Al + 2–4 Si + bridging OH)
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2997204Z       - HF/3-21G for geometry; B3LYP/6-31G* for energetics
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2997824Z       - Study methanol-to-olefin (MTO) mechanism, alkane activation
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2998512Z       - Compare with periodic DFT (CP2K, VASP) for consistency
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2999233Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2999506Z       ### EFP Solvation for SN2 Reactions
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2999755Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.2999974Z       Benchmark ab initio EFP vs. implicit solvent:
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3000388Z       - Cl⁻ + CH₃Cl → ClCH₃ + Cl⁻ in water
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3000695Z       - QM region: reactants with MP2/aug-cc-pVDZ
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3001137Z       - 50–200 EFP water molecules from pre-equilibrated simulation
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3001506Z       - Compute free energy profile along IRC
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3001766Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3001946Z       ### Infrared and Raman Spectra
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3002179Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3002406Z       GAMESS computes harmonic vibrational frequencies:
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3002692Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3002879Z        $CONTRL RUNTYP=HESSIAN $END
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3003107Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3003339Z       - Frequency calculation after geometry optimization
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3003663Z       - IR intensities from dipole derivative
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3004056Z       - Raman activities from polarizability derivative (with additional keyword)
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3004488Z       - Compare with experiment for structural assignment
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3004777Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3004957Z       ## Input/Output Formats
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3005179Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3005444Z       - **Input**: Plain text .inp files with $-delimited namelist blocks
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3005883Z       - **Output**: .log or .out text file with energies, gradients, frequencies
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3006374Z       - **Punch file**: .dat or .pun — MO coefficients, Hessian, etc.
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3006848Z       - **Visualization**: Use MacMolPlt, Molden, Avogadro, or VMD for MO/structure viewing
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3007243Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3007416Z       ## Common Pitfalls
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3007619Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3007955Z       - **Basis set superposition error (BSSE)**: Use `COUNTERPOISE=2` in $MOREAD for CP correction
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3008611Z       - **FMO fragment definition**: Each fragment must be a complete residue; avoid cutting through aromatic rings
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3009572Z       - **Memory**: Increase `MEMORY=` in $SYSTEM for large molecules; GAMESS uses stack-like memory management
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3010643Z       - **ECP + diffuse functions**: Check compatibility; some combinations cause convergence issues
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3011068Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3011332Z       echo "GAMESS use-cases: $(wc -l < tools/gamess/use-cases.md)"
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3011685Z     description: Expand GAMESS use-cases
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3011936Z   result: |
2026-02-26T02:53:24.3012121Z     GAMESS use-cases: 123

2026-02-26T02:53:50.4000269Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4000497Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4000766Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4000983Z       ### Charm++ Parallel Framework
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4001257Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4001508Z       NAMD's use of Charm++ provides unique advantages:
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4002081Z       - **Overdecomposition**: More virtual processors (chares) than physical cores; scheduler fills idle cores
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4002819Z       - **Adaptive load balancing**: Detects load imbalance every N steps; redistributes work without restart
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4003515Z       - **Asynchronous messaging**: No global synchronization; overlaps computation and communication
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4004170Z       - **Migratable objects**: Work units can move between physical processors during simulation
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4004620Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4004842Z       This makes NAMD particularly efficient for:
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4005268Z       - Heterogeneous biomolecular systems (membrane + water + protein)
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4006266Z       - Systems with many solute–solvent interactions
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4006942Z       - Systems where some regions are computationally heavier than others
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4007523Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4007881Z       ### PME Implementation
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4008163Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4008365Z       Particle-Mesh Ewald in NAMD:
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4008924Z       - **Real-space sum**: Short-range correction; cutoff ~12 Å
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4009852Z       - **Reciprocal-space sum**: FFT on grid; grid spacing ~1 Å
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4010792Z       - **GPU offloading**: Reciprocal PME on separate GPU stream; overlap with bonded force evaluation
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4011714Z       - **Multi-GPU**: PME decomposed across GPUs in NAMD 3.0
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4012264Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4012634Z       ### Collective Variables (Colvars) Framework
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4013116Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4013545Z       Colvars allows defining arbitrary collective coordinates:
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4014133Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4014433Z       colvar {
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4014763Z         name dist
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4015108Z         distance {
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4015487Z           group1 { atomnumbers 1 2 3 }
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4015969Z           group2 { atomnumbers 100 101 102 }
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4016432Z         }
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4016733Z       }
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4017029Z       harmonic {
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4017379Z         colvars dist
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4017745Z         centers 5.0
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4018118Z         forceConstant 10.0
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4018524Z       }
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4018831Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4019318Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4019515Z       Available collective variables:
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4019787Z       - `distance`, `angle`, `dihedral`
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4020054Z       - `rmsd` (from reference structure)
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4020309Z       - `gyration`
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4020773Z       - `eigenvector` (along PCA mode)
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4021055Z       - `distanceVec`, `distanceZ` (components)
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4021473Z       - `coordNum` (coordination number)
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4021741Z       - `alpha` (helical content)
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4021971Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4022148Z       Available bias methods:
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4022421Z       - **ABF**: Adaptive Biasing Force (CZAR estimator)
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4022791Z       - **Metadynamics**: Free energy surface via Gaussian hills
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4023138Z       - **Harmonic restraints**: Umbrella sampling
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4023433Z       - **TMD**: Targeted MD along a path
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4023733Z       - **Steered MD (SMD)**: Constant velocity pulling
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4024006Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4024189Z       ### NAMD Input File Structure
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4024423Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4024582Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4024781Z       # NAMD configuration file template
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4025052Z       structure          protein.psf
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4025311Z       coordinates        protein.pdb
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4025569Z       outputName         output_prefix
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4025806Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4025972Z       # Force field
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4026181Z       paraTypeCharmm     on
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4026426Z       parameters         par_all36m_prot.prm
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4026699Z       parameters         par_water.prm
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4026932Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4027109Z       # Simulation parameters
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4027345Z       temperature        300
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4027573Z       timestep           2.0   ;# 2 fs
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4027817Z       nonbondedFreq      1
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4028090Z       fullElectFrequency 2      ;# PME every 4 fs (RESPA)
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4028386Z       PME                yes
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4028607Z       PMEGridSpacing     1.0
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4028814Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4029208Z       # Thermodynamics
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4029463Z       langevin           on
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4029707Z       langevinDamping    1      ;# ps^-1
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4036365Z       langevinTemp       300
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4036700Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4036960Z       # Barostat (NPT)
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4037198Z       useGroupPressure   yes
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4037438Z       useFlexibleCell    no
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4037805Z       langevinPiston     on
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4038066Z       langevinPistonTarget 1.01325   ;# bar
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4038442Z       langevinPistonPeriod 200
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4038724Z       langevinPistonDecay  100
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4039138Z       langevinPistonTemp   300
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4039431Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4039607Z       # Constraints
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4039855Z       rigidBonds         all   ;# SHAKE for all X-H bonds
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4040280Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4040462Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4040645Z       ### NAMD3 GPU Optimizations
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4041012Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4041190Z       NAMD 3.0 introduced:
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4041482Z       - **Resident GPU mode**: Minimize CPU-GPU data transfers
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4041981Z       - **GPU-resident neighbor list**: Updated on GPU
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4042326Z       - **GPU SETTLE**: Water constraint solving on GPU
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4042894Z       - **Merged force kernels**: Combine bonded + short-range nonbonded in single kernel
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4043491Z       - Benchmark: ~300 ns/day for DHFR (24k atoms) on NVIDIA A100
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4043812Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4043996Z       ## Ecosystem Integration
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4044349Z       
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4044676Z       - **VMD**: Direct IMD (Interactive MD) connection; live visualization during simulation
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4045357Z       - **CHARMM-GUI**: Complete system builder outputting NAMD .psf, .pdb, .conf files
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4045989Z       - **PLUMED**: Enhanced sampling; accessible via `plumedscript` keyword
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4046725Z       - **QM/MM**: NAMD-ORCA, NAMD-Gaussian interfaces for QM/MM
2026-02-26T02:53:50.4047362Z       - **AlphaFold structures**: Direct use of AlphaFold PDB models as NAMD input (via psfgen)

2026-02-26T02:54:14.2559263Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2559564Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2559932Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2560232Z       ### AtomSelect Language
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2560581Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2561064Z       The VMD atom selection language is a Boolean expression parser supporting:
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2561684Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2562300Z       # Protein backbone within 5 Å of ligand
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2562912Z       set sel [atomselect top "backbone and within 5 of resname LIG"]
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2563440Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2563739Z       # Charged residues
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2564199Z       set sel [atomselect top "resname ARG LYS ASP GLU"]
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2564708Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2565167Z       # RMSD of Cα atoms, residues 10-100
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2565743Z       set ref [atomselect top "name CA and resid 10 to 100" frame 0]
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2566412Z       set cur [atomselect top "name CA and resid 10 to 100"]
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2566988Z       set rmsd [measure rmsd $cur $ref]
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2567400Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2567693Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2568865Z       Keywords: `name`, `type`, `resname`, `resid`, `chain`, `segname`, `x`, `y`, `z`, `mass`, `charge`, `beta`, `occupancy`, `within`, `exwithin`, `same residue as`, `backbone`, `protein`, `nucleic`, `water`, `ions`
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2570298Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2570616Z       ### Trajectory Analysis in Tcl
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2571032Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2571354Z       Common analysis patterns:
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2574586Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2574944Z       # Radius of gyration over trajectory
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2575411Z       set n [molinfo top get numframes]
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2575863Z       set outfile [open "rg.dat" w]
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2576285Z       for {set i 0} {$i < $n} {incr i} {
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2576766Z           set sel [atomselect top "protein" frame $i]
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2577234Z           set rg [measure rgyr $sel]
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2577666Z           puts $outfile "$i $rg"
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2578047Z           $sel delete
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2578389Z       }
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2578675Z       close $outfile
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2579172Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2579459Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2579761Z       ### VolMap Density Analysis
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2580149Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2580418Z       ```tcl
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2580776Z       # Generate 3D occupancy map from trajectory
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2581395Z       volmap occupancy [atomselect top "resname WAT and name OW"] \
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2582038Z           -res 1.0 -minmax {{-30 -30 -30} {30 30 30}} \
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2582550Z           -o water_density.dx
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2582924Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2583193Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2583709Z       The resulting `.dx` file can be loaded back into VMD for isosurface visualization.
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2584364Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2584650Z       ### MDFF Workflow
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2585288Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2585671Z       1. Load cryo-EM density into VMD as volumetric data
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2586399Z       2. Load initial atomic model
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2586925Z       3. Create MDFF input for NAMD: `mdff gridpdb`, `mdff setup`
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2587496Z       4. Run NAMD with MDFF potential
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2587963Z       5. Evaluate fit quality with `mdff check`
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2588397Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2589565Z       MDFFfit score = cross-correlation coefficient between simulated and experimental density.
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2590365Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2590688Z       ### QuickSurf GPU Algorithm
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2591075Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2591435Z       QuickSurf renders Gaussian density surfaces:
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2592080Z       1. Compute per-atom Gaussian density on 3D grid (GPU CUDA kernel)
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2592775Z       2. Marching Cubes extract isosurface at threshold level
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2593358Z       3. Smooth mesh normals for appearance
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2593860Z       4. Render with OpenGL using Phong shading
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2594316Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2594873Z       This enables real-time rendering of million-atom surfaces at 30 fps on modern GPU.
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2595552Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2595884Z       ### VMD Python Interface (VMD-Python)
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2596318Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2596594Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2596912Z       import vmd
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2597294Z       from vmd import molecule, atomsel, graphics
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2597718Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2597960Z       # Load structure
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2598308Z       molid = molecule.load("pdb", "protein.pdb")
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2598827Z       molecule.addfile(molid, "dcd", "trajectory.dcd")
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2599547Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2599836Z       # Atom selection
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2600329Z       sel = atomsel.atomsel("name CA", molid=molid, frame=0)
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2600851Z       masses = sel.get("mass")
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2601211Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2601501Z       # RMSD calculation
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2601931Z       coords0 = sel.get("x"), sel.get("y"), sel.get("z")
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2602370Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2602696Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:14.2602985Z       ## Rendering Options

2026-02-26T02:54:40.7885044Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7885388Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7885789Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7886074Z       ### OBMol Data Model
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7886395Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7886677Z       The central OBMol class stores:
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7887253Z       - `OBAtom`: Atomic number, coordinates, formal charge, isotope, chirality
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7888069Z       - `OBBond`: Bond order (single/double/triple/aromatic), stereo, ring membership
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7888834Z       - `OBResidue`: For protein/nucleic acid residues (PDB input)
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7889721Z       - `OBAtomData`, `OBPairData`: Extension data for properties
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7890263Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7890585Z       ### Format Converter Architecture
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7890977Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7891261Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7891567Z                        OBConversion
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7891978Z                        /     |     \
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7892429Z                  OBFormat  OBFormat  OBFormat
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7892884Z                   (SDF)   (MOL2)    (SMILES)
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7893292Z                        \     |     /
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7893689Z                          OBMol
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7894051Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7894339Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7894945Z       Each format is an `OBFormat` plugin loaded dynamically. Adding a new format requires:
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7895681Z       1. Derive from `OBFormat`
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7896199Z       2. Implement `ReadMolecule()` and `WriteMolecule()`
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7896882Z       3. Register with `OBConversion::RegisterFormat()`
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7897363Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7897693Z       ### 3D Coordinate Generation Pipeline
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7898130Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7898442Z       When generating 3D coordinates:
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7899505Z       1. **SMILES → Graph**: Parse SMILES, perceive connectivity
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7900149Z       2. **Ring SSSR**: Find smallest set of smallest rings
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7900783Z       3. **Initial coordinates**: Distance geometry (random embed in 3D)
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7901628Z       4. **Force field minimization**: MMFF94 or UFF for 100–500 steps
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7902360Z       5. **Conformer search** (optional): Systematic or Monte Carlo rotor search
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7902960Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7903237Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7903623Z       obabel -:c1ccccc1C(=O)O --gen3d -O aspirin_3d.sdf
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7904104Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7904388Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7904680Z       ### Charge Models Available
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7905069Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7905395Z       | Model | Method | Application |
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7905828Z       |-------|--------|-------------|
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7906366Z       | Gasteiger | Iterative electronegativity | Fast, general |
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7907061Z       | AM1BCC | QM + bond correction | AMBER/GAFF parameterization |
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7907707Z       | MMFF94 | MMFF partial charges | MMFF94 force field |
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7908342Z       | Mulliken | QM-derived (requires external QC) | Accurate |
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7909212Z       | EEM | Electronegativity equalization | Fast, reasonable |
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7909765Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7910051Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7910501Z       obabel input.sdf -O output.mol2 --partialcharge gasteiger
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7911032Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7911310Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7911602Z       ### InChI Generation
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7911946Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7912288Z       Open Babel uses the IUPAC InChI 1.06 library:
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7913163Z       - Standard InChI: No options; fully reproducible, canonical
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7914036Z       - Standard InChIKey: 27-character hash of InChI; used in databases
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7914898Z       - InChI options: `/T` for tautomers, `/I` for isotopes, `/S` for stereochemistry
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7915559Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7915851Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7916182Z       obabel aspirin.sdf -o inchi
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7916734Z       # InChI=1S/C9H8O4/c1-6(10)13-8-5-3-2-4-7(8)9(11)12/h2-5H,1H3,(H,11,12)
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7917331Z       obabel aspirin.sdf -o inchikey
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7917785Z       # BSYNRYMUTXBXSQ-UHFFFAOYSA-N
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7918198Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7918473Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7918806Z       ### Fingerprint Types and Similarity
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7919401Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7919679Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7920035Z       # Tanimoto similarity between two molecules
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7920562Z       obabel mol1.sdf mol2.sdf -o fps --fps FP2
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7921051Z       # Returns Tanimoto coefficient
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7921449Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7921788Z       # FP2: path-based linear fingerprint
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7922255Z       # FP3: SMARTS patterns fingerprint  
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7922727Z       # FP4: extended SMARTS fingerprint
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7923157Z       # MACCS: 166 MACCS keys
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7923526Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7923812Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7924141Z       ### SMARTS Substructure Matching
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7924562Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7924840Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7925196Z       import openbabel.pybel as pybel
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7925714Z       mol = pybel.readstring("smi", "c1ccccc1C(=O)O")
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7926347Z       pattern = pybel.Smarts("[#6](=[OD1])[OD1H]")  # carboxylic acid
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7926932Z       matches = pattern.findall(mol)
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7927337Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7927617Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7927936Z       ## Performance Benchmarks
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7928321Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7928928Z       - **SDF → SMILES conversion**: >100,000 molecules/min on single core
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7930029Z       - **3D coordinate generation**: 1–60 seconds per molecule depending on size
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7930794Z       - **Fingerprint computation (FP2)**: >50,000 molecules/min
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7931595Z       - **Tanimoto similarity screening**: Limited; use RDKit or OpenEye for large-scale
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7932279Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7932576Z       ## Integration Examples
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7932950Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7933254Z       ### Avogadro Integration
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7933896Z       Open Babel handles all file I/O in Avogadro 1.x and provides force field evaluation.
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7934569Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7934882Z       ### Pipeline Integration
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7935258Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7935600Z       # Convert PubChem SDF to SMILES
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7936141Z       obabel pubchem_compounds.sdf -O smiles.txt -osmi
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7936630Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7936956Z       # Generate 3D structures for docking
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7937424Z       obabel hits.smi --gen3d -O 3d_hits.sdf
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7937858Z       
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7938152Z       # Filter by molecular weight
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7938696Z       obabel library.sdf -O drug_like.sdf --filter "MW>200 MW<500"
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7939683Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:54:40.7939984Z       EOF

2026-02-26T02:55:11.2070687Z       cat >> tools/orca/technology.md << 'EOF'
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2071170Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2071497Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2071917Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2072265Z       ### SHARK Integral Package (ORCA 5+)
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2072703Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2073109Z       ORCA 5.0 introduced SHARK as its new integral engine:
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2073882Z       - **SIMD vectorization**: AVX2/AVX-512 for batch integral evaluation
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2074705Z       - **Reduced memory footprint**: Better batching of integral shells
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2075599Z       - **Faster basis transformations**: Optimized Cartesian-to-spherical transforms
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2076819Z       - Result: ~2–3× speedup for DFT and HF calculations vs. ORCA 4
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2077437Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2077750Z       ### DFT Grid Levels
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2078111Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2078490Z       ORCA uses numerical grids for XC evaluation:
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2079264Z       | Grid | Angular/Radial points | Use case |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2079789Z       |------|----------------------|----------|
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2080313Z       | Grid1 | 50/5 | Tight geometry convergence only |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2080832Z       | Grid2 | 90/7 | Default geometry |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2081310Z       | Grid3 | 194/9 | Default single point |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2081749Z       | Grid4 | 302/11 | Accurate energies |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2083818Z       | Grid5 | 434/13 | High accuracy |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2084274Z       | Grid6 | 590/16 | Benchmark quality |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2085008Z       | Grid7 | 770/20 | Extremely tight |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2085413Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2085846Z       `DefGrid2` is the ORCA 5 default; `DefGrid3` for benchmarks.
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2086390Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2086672Z       ### DLPNO-CCSD(T) Details
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2087049Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2087418Z       Domain-based Local Pair Natural Orbital CCSD(T):
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2088128Z       1. **Localize orbitals**: Boys/Pipek-Mezey localization of occupied orbitals
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2089164Z       2. **Pair screening**: Classify orbital pairs as strong, weak, distant
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2090062Z       3. **PNO generation**: For each significant pair, diagonalize pair correlation matrix
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2090956Z       4. **Truncation**: Keep PNOs above threshold (TCutPNO = 1e-7 by default)
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2091719Z       5. **CC in PNO basis**: Solve CC equations in compressed pair subspaces
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2092704Z       6. **Perturbative triples**: (T) correction in PNO basis (TightPNO recommended for energy differences)
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2093527Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2093845Z       Settings:
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2094815Z       - `NormalPNO`: Fast, suitable for energy differences
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2095522Z       - `TightPNO`: Accurate, use for thermochemistry benchmarks
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2095928Z       - `LoosePNO`: Exploratory; may have larger errors
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2096227Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2096396Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2096624Z       ! DLPNO-CCSD(T) def2-TZVP def2-TZVP/C TightPNO
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2096902Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2097065Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2097247Z       ### TD-DFT Implementation
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2097471Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2097646Z       ORCA implements:
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2097988Z       - **Full TDDFT**: Casida matrix diagonalization; large number of states
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2098538Z       - **TDA (Tamm-Dancoff approximation)**: Removes B matrix; faster, sometimes better for triplets
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2099438Z       - **RI-TDDFT**: Density-fitting acceleration; very fast
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2099903Z       - **SOC**: Spin-orbit coupling between states for phosphorescence
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2100699Z       - **EOM-CCSD**: For accurate excited states beyond DFT
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2101225Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2101447Z       ### ORCA Input File Format
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2101672Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2101834Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2102173Z       # ORCA input example: DFT optimization
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2102487Z       ! B3LYP def2-SVP OptimizeH def2/J RIJCOSX D3BJ
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2102879Z       %maxcore 4000  # MB per core
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2103301Z       %pal nprocs 8 end  # parallel cores
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2103748Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2103929Z       * xyz 0 1
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2104118Z       C  0.000  0.000  0.000
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2104330Z       H  0.000  0.000  1.089
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2104680Z       H  1.027  0.000 -0.363
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2104891Z       H -0.513  0.890 -0.363
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2105097Z       H -0.513 -0.890 -0.363
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2105399Z       *
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2105589Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2105762Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2105939Z       ### Geometry Optimization
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2106284Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2106460Z       ORCA uses:
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2106787Z       - **Redundant internal coordinates (RIC)**: Default; efficient for most systems
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2107382Z       - **GDIIS**: Geometry direct inversion of iterative subspace
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2107852Z       - **L-BFGS**: Limited-memory BFGS; fast for large systems
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2108297Z       - **NEB (Nudged Elastic Band)**: Minimum energy path; reaction pathway
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2109119Z       - **CI optimization**: State-averaged CASSCF with penalty function for conical intersections
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2109607Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2109794Z       ### Property Calculations
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2110153Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2110346Z       After geometry optimization:
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2110672Z       - **NMR chemical shifts**: `! NMR` keyword; GIAO method
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2111174Z       - **EPR g-tensors and HFCs**: `! EPR` for radical properties
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2111873Z       - **Mössbauer parameters**: Isomer shift and quadrupole splitting
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2112458Z       - **X-ray absorption (XAS/XES)**: Core-level excitations via TDDFT
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2112974Z       - **ECD (Electronic Circular Dichroism)**: Rotational strength from TDDFT
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2113367Z       - **VCD**: Vibrational circular dichroism
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2113838Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2114140Z       ## Scaling Summary
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2114347Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2114541Z       | Method | Scaling | Practical limit |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2114817Z       |--------|---------|----------------|
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2115089Z       | DFT (RIJCOSX) | ~O(N^2.5) | ~2000 atoms |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2115362Z       | DF-MP2 | ~O(N^4) | ~200 atoms |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2115659Z       | DLPNO-CCSD(T) NormalPNO | ~O(N^3) | ~1000 atoms |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2116024Z       | CASSCF (small active space) | ~O(N^3) | ~500 atoms, CAS(16,16) |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2116376Z       | EOM-CCSD | ~O(N^6) | ~50 atoms |
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2116615Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2116784Z       ## Output Files
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2116974Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2117252Z       - `*.gbw`: Binary wavefunction file (Gaussian binary format-compatible)
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2117630Z       - `*.engrad`: Energies and gradients
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2117895Z       - `*.hess`: Hessian in ORCA format
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2118170Z       - `*.trj`: Geometry optimization trajectory
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2118504Z       - `orca_plot`: Tool to generate MO cube files from .gbw
2026-02-26T02:55:11.2118804Z       EOF

2026-02-26T02:55:36.2460518Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2460844Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2461259Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2461626Z       ### Molecular Data Model (libavogadrolibs)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2462091Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2462416Z       Core data structures in Avogadro 2:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2462851Z       ```cpp
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2463198Z       // Core molecule representation
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2463722Z       Avogadro::Core::Molecule mol;
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2464190Z       mol.addAtom(6);  // Carbon
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2464608Z       mol.addAtom(8);  // Oxygen
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2465070Z       mol.addBond(0, 1, 2);  // Double bond C=O
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2465523Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2465830Z       // 3D coordinates
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2466269Z       mol.setAtomPosition3d(0, Vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0));
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2466761Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2467057Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2467366Z       Python bindings mirror the C++ API:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2467660Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2467862Z       import avogadro
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2468096Z       mol = avogadro.core.Molecule()
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2468372Z       # or use Avogadro2's io module
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2468874Z       mol = avogadro.io.FileFormatManager.readFile("structure.cif")
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2469608Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2469874Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2470145Z       ### Open Babel Backend
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2470506Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2470836Z       Avogadro 2 uses Open Babel for:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2471407Z       - Reading 100+ file formats (SDF, MOL2, PDB, CIF, XYZ, Gaussian output, etc.)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2472180Z       - Force field evaluation (MMFF94, UFF, GAFF)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2472734Z       - Hydrogen atom placement and valence
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2473235Z       - Aromaticity perception
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2473681Z       - 3D coordinate generation
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2474133Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2474486Z       ### Input Generator Framework
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2474954Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2475477Z       Input generators are Python scripts that produce QC input files:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2476105Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2476463Z       #!/usr/bin/env python3
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2476847Z       # Gaussian input generator
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2477278Z       def get_options():
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2477671Z           return {
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2478160Z               "theory": {"type": "combo", "values": ["B3LYP", "PBE0", "M06-2X"]},
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2478912Z               "basis": {"type": "combo", "values": ["6-31G*", "cc-pVTZ", "def2-TZVP"]}
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2479742Z           }
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2480079Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2480453Z       def render(opts, molecule):
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2481014Z           # Generate Gaussian input using molecule geometry and user options
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2481485Z           ...
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2481674Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2481837Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2482169Z       Built-in generators for: Gaussian, ORCA, NWChem, Psi4, GAMESS, Q-Chem, Molpro, MOPAC.
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2482569Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2482753Z       ### CIF Crystal Structure Handling
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2483008Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2483205Z       VESTA-format operations in Avogadro:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2483525Z       - Read CIF with full crystallographic symmetry
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2484098Z       - Display unit cell with axes labels (a, b, c in Å)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2484480Z       - Apply space group operations to generate equivalent positions
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2484937Z       - Build supercell (1×1×1 to N×M×P repetitions)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2485549Z       - Display as ball-and-stick with polyhedral option
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2485971Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2486167Z       ### VTK Visualization (Volume Data)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2486412Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2486590Z       Avogadro 2 uses VTK for:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2486905Z       - Molecular orbital isosurface rendering (from .cube files)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2487241Z       - Electron density surfaces
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2487541Z       - Electrostatic potential maps on density isosurface
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2487938Z       - Color maps: blue-white-red for ESP, rainbow for other properties
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2488281Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2488468Z       ### Normal Mode Visualization
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2488706Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2488919Z       After loading a frequency calculation output:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2489602Z       - Avogadro displays all normal modes with frequencies
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2489953Z       - Click on any mode to animate the vibration
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2490255Z       - Control animation speed and amplitude
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2490552Z       - Export animated GIF for presentations
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2490923Z       - Color coding: red = imaginary (TS), blue = low frequency, green = high
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2491272Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2491446Z       ## Build System
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2491637Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2491813Z       Avogadro 2 uses CMake:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2492032Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2492235Z       cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2492510Z                -DQt5_DIR=/usr/lib/cmake/Qt5 \
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2492828Z                -DOPENBABEL2_INCLUDE_DIR=/usr/include/openbabel3
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2493126Z       make -j8
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2493308Z       make install
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2493490Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2493651Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2493855Z       Conda installation is strongly recommended:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2494135Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2494340Z       conda install -c conda-forge avogadro2
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2494595Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2494755Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2494934Z       ## Integration with QC Workflows
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2495176Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2495418Z       Typical computational chemistry workflow with Avogadro:
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2495961Z       1. Draw molecule or open file (PDB, SMILES, CIF)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2496626Z       2. Add hydrogens (Build → Add Hydrogens)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2497233Z       3. Optimize geometry (Extensions → Optimize Geometry with MMFF94)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2497714Z       4. Generate QC input (Extensions → Input Generators → Gaussian)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2498056Z       5. Run calculation externally
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2498421Z       6. Load output (Gaussian .log, ORCA .out, NWChem .out)
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2498900Z       7. Visualize orbitals from cube files
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2500300Z       8. Animate normal modes
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2500551Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2500733Z       ## Avogadro vs. Similar Tools
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2500974Z       
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2501189Z       | Feature | Avogadro 2 | PyMOL | VMD | VESTA |
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2501494Z       |---------|------------|-------|-----|-------|
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2501879Z       | Molecule building | ✓ best | Limited | ✗ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2502227Z       | QC input generation | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2502580Z       | Trajectory analysis | ✗ | Limited | ✓ best | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2502949Z       | Crystal structures | ✓ | ✗ | Limited | ✓ best |
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2503280Z       | MO visualization | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2503582Z       | Free | ✓ | Open-source | ✓ | ✓ |
2026-02-26T02:55:36.2503826Z       EOF

2026-02-26T02:56:03.7621539Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7621879Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7622317Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7622684Z       ### Custom Force Expression Language
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7623095Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7623451Z       OpenMM parses mathematical expressions at runtime:
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7623908Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7624272Z       # Custom Lennard-Jones with modified exponents
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7624921Z       force = CustomNonbondedForce("4*epsilon*((sigma/r)^12-(sigma/r)^6); "
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7625596Z                                     "sigma=0.5*(sigma1+sigma2); "
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7626137Z                                     "epsilon=sqrt(epsilon1*epsilon2)")
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7626702Z       force.addPerParticleParameter("sigma")
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7627232Z       force.addPerParticleParameter("epsilon")
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7627683Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7627959Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7628246Z       This enables:
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7629275Z       - Alchemical transformations: `U = λ*U_new + (1-λ)*U_old`
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7630026Z       - Modified vdW combining rules (Lorentz-Berthelot vs. geometric)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7630703Z       - Polarizable potentials via Drude oscillators
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7631333Z       - Machine learning potentials (via OpenMM-ML plugin)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7631859Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7632141Z       ### Platforms
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7632453Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7632825Z       | Platform | Hardware | Acceleration |
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7633352Z       |----------|----------|-------------|
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7633874Z       | Reference | CPU | Single-threaded, always correct |
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7634446Z       | CPU | CPU | OpenMP multi-threaded, optimized |
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7635059Z       | CUDA | NVIDIA GPU | Best performance; requires CUDA toolkit |
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7635728Z       | OpenCL | Any GPU | Cross-platform; AMD/Intel/NVIDIA |
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7636201Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7636497Z       Selecting platform:
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7636869Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7637256Z       platform = Platform.getPlatformByName('CUDA')
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7637829Z       properties = {'DeviceIndex': '0', 'Precision': 'mixed'}
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7638568Z       simulation = Simulation(topology, system, integrator, platform, properties)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7639421Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7639701Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7640291Z       ### Force Field File Format (XML)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7640705Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7640971Z       ```xml
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7641458Z       <ForceField>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7641785Z         <AtomTypes>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7642211Z           <Type name="CT" class="CT" element="C" mass="12.011"/>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7642721Z         </AtomTypes>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7643072Z         <HarmonicBondForce>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7643546Z           <Bond type1="CT" type2="HC" length="0.109" k="284512.0"/>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7644077Z         </HarmonicBondForce>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7644548Z         <AmoebaMultipoleForce direct11Scale="0.0" ...>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7645091Z           <!-- AMOEBA polarizable force field -->
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7645574Z         </AmoebaMultipoleForce>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7645960Z       </ForceField>
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7646273Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7646555Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7646868Z       Supported force field XMLs:
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7647301Z       - AMBER ff14SB, ff19SB (protein)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7647766Z       - AMBER GAFF, GAFF2 (small molecules)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7648243Z       - CHARMM 36 (protein, lipid, nucleic)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7648700Z       - OPLS-AA (organic molecules)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7649347Z       - Amber14 (comprehensive package)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7649760Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7650058Z       ### BAOAB Integrator
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7650415Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7650783Z       The LangevinMiddleIntegrator implements BAOAB:
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7651269Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7651623Z       B: Half-step velocity update from forces
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7652115Z       A: Half-step position update
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7661095Z       O: Stochastic velocity resampling (Ornstein-Uhlenbeck)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7661735Z       A: Half-step position update  
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7662206Z       B: Half-step velocity update from forces
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7662646Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7662931Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7663797Z       This "middle" Langevin scheme is more accurate than naive Euler integration for configurational properties, showing less configurational temperature deviation.
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7664969Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7665297Z       ### Monte Carlo Barostat
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7665672Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7666067Z       NPT pressure control uses Monte Carlo volume moves:
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7666719Z       - Every N steps (default 25), propose a random volume change
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7667499Z       - Accept/reject based on Metropolis criterion including Boltzmann factor
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7668476Z       - Advantages over velocity-rescaling: rigorously correct ensemble, no spurious coupling
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7669471Z       - Works with OpenMM's constraint solvers
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7669928Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7670284Z       ### Drude Oscillator Polarizable Force Fields
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7670748Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7671062Z       OpenMM supports the Drude model:
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7671603Z       - Each polarizable atom carries a Drude particle
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7672324Z       - Drude particle position minimized at each step (self-consistent field)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7673138Z       - `DrudeIntegrator`: Extended Lagrangian dynamics for Drude DOF
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7674021Z       - `DrudeLangevinIntegrator`: Thermostat for Drude particles at low temperature (1 K)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7674750Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7675059Z       ### Alchemical Transformations
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7675477Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7675759Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7676093Z       # Lambda schedule for FEP
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7676532Z       for lambda_val in [0.0, 0.1, 0.2, ..., 1.0]:
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7677120Z           system.getForce(nonbonded_index).setLambda(lambda_val)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7677688Z           simulation.step(100000)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7678116Z           energies = collect_energies()
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7678549Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7679259Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7679629Z       Use with MBAR (pymbar) for free energy analysis:
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7680117Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7680614Z       import pymbar
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7680984Z       result = pymbar.MBAR(u_kln, N_k)
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7681495Z       DeltaF = result.getFreeEnergyDifferences()
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7681968Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7682247Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7682549Z       ## Benchmark Performance
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7682921Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7683285Z       | System | Atoms | A100 GPU | DHFR reference |

2026-02-26T02:56:03.7684887Z       | Cellulose | 408,609 | ~50 ns/day | Large system |

2026-02-26T02:56:03.7685906Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:03.7686680Z       Precision: Mixed precision (float32 forces, float64 accumulation) gives 95% accuracy at full double precision cost.

2026-02-26T02:56:38.2855543Z       cat >> tools/vesta/technology.md << 'EOF'
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2856044Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2856382Z       ## Extended Technical Details
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2856818Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2857192Z       ### Volumetric Data Processing
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2857615Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2857973Z       VESTA reads 3D scalar field data from:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2858871Z       - **VASP CHGCAR**: `NGXF × NGYF × NGZF` grid of electron density
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2859798Z       - **QE charge-density.dat**: Quantum ESPRESSO charge density
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2860554Z       - **Gaussian CUBE**: Volumetric data on a rectangular grid in Angstroms
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2861215Z       - **XSF format**: Extended XYZ with volumetric data
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2861560Z       - **DX format**: OpenDX format used by APBS and NAMD
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2861843Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2862029Z       Grid operations in VESTA:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2862444Z       - **Add/subtract densities**: Compare spin-up vs. spin-down, compute magnetization density
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2863089Z       - **ELF**: Load electron localization function from VASP ELFCAR
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2863981Z       - **Difference density**: ρ_total - Σ_atoms ρ_atomic to show bonding regions
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2864607Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2864926Z       ### Isosurface Algorithm Details
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2865429Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2865648Z       Marching Cubes algorithm:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2865904Z       - Divide 3D grid into cubic cells
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2866462Z       - For each cell, determine which of 8 corners are above/below threshold
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2867462Z       - 256 possible configurations → 15 unique cases with triangulation tables
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2868243Z       - Generate triangular mesh; compute normals for smooth shading
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2868893Z       - Optional: Smooth mesh with Laplacian smoothing
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2869512Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2869806Z       VESTA allows:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2870294Z       - Multiple simultaneous isosurfaces at different levels
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2871089Z       - Positive/negative surfaces with different colors (e.g., for ELF or deformation density)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2872245Z       - Transparency control for layered visualization
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2872770Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2873359Z       ### Crystal Structure Operations
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2873836Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2874181Z       Space group symmetry in VESTA:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2874735Z       - Enter fractional coordinates for asymmetric unit
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2875427Z       - VESTA applies all symmetry operations of the space group
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2876142Z       - Generates equivalent positions within unit cell
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2876929Z       - Supports all 230 space groups from International Tables for Crystallography
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2877564Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2877795Z       Bond calculation:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2878109Z       - Search for all atom pairs within user-specified distance range
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2878749Z       - Element-specific default cutoffs (e.g., C-C: 1.2–1.9 Å)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2879519Z       - Bond valence sum: Assess bond strength from distances
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2879835Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2880111Z       ### Powder Diffraction Simulation
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2880426Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2880625Z       Structure factor calculation:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2880940Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2881350Z       F_hkl = Σ_j f_j(sin θ/λ) × T_j(hkl) × exp(2πi(hx_j + ky_j + lz_j))
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2881714Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2881977Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2882142Z       Where:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2882470Z       - `f_j(sin θ/λ)`: Tabulated X-ray scattering factor for element j
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2883087Z       - `T_j`: Debye-Waller factor = exp(-B_j sin²θ/λ²); B in Å²
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2883645Z       - Intensity: `I_hkl = M_hkl × |F_hkl|² × LP(θ)`
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2883949Z         - M = multiplicity
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2884251Z         - LP = Lorentz-polarization factor
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2884600Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2885020Z       Peak profile: Pseudo-Voigt (η × Lorentz + (1-η) × Gauss) with FWHM as input parameter.
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2885585Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2885767Z       ### Morphology Prediction
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2886122Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2886348Z       VESTA can compute crystal morphology using:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2886973Z       - **BFDH (Bravais-Friedel-Donnay-Harker)** rule: Face importance ∝ 1/d_hkl
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2887479Z       - Input: Crystal structure; output: predicted crystal shape as polyhedron
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2888129Z       - Useful for predicting crystal habits for pharmaceutical crystal engineering
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2888639Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2888829Z       ### VESTA File Format (.vesta)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2889534Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2889794Z       The `.vesta` format is an XML-based format that saves:
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2890347Z       - Crystal structure (fractional coordinates, cell parameters, space group)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2890797Z       - Visualization settings (colors, radii, bond settings)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2891143Z       - Isosurface settings (level, color, opacity)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2891450Z       - Orientation and zoom settings
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2891694Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2891988Z       This allows sharing a complete visualization state with collaborators.
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2892343Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2892528Z       ## Common Workflows
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2892739Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2892949Z       ### VASP + VESTA Band Structure Charge Density
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2893224Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2893390Z       ```bash
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2893648Z       # After VASP band structure calculation with LCHARG=.TRUE.
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2893973Z       # CHGCAR file available
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2894203Z       # 1. Open VESTA
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2894567Z       # 2. File → Open → CHGCAR (or PARCHG for partial density)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2894999Z       # 3. Objects → New data → 3D data (select CHGCAR)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2895366Z       # 4. Set isosurface level (e.g., 0.03 e/Å³)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2895647Z       # 5. Adjust color and opacity
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2896098Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2896267Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2896608Z       ### DFT Charge Density Difference (Bonding Analysis)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2896896Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2897062Z       ```python
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2897297Z       # Using pymatgen to compute density difference
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2897622Z       from pymatgen.io.vasp.outputs import Chgcar
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2897954Z       chgcar_mol = Chgcar.from_file("CHGCAR_molecule")
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2898282Z       chgcar_atom1 = Chgcar.from_file("CHGCAR_atom1")
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2898609Z       chgcar_atom2 = Chgcar.from_file("CHGCAR_atom2")
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2899202Z       diff = chgcar_mol - chgcar_atom1 - chgcar_atom2
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2899735Z       diff.write_file("CHGCAR_diff")
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2900280Z       # Open CHGCAR_diff in VESTA for charge transfer visualization
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2900822Z       ```
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2901098Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2901405Z       ### ELF Visualization
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2901784Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2902081Z       1. Run VASP with `LELF = .TRUE.`
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2902359Z       2. Load ELFCAR in VESTA (range 0 to 1)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2902848Z       3. ELF > 0.75: electron localization (bonding pairs, lone pairs)
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2903299Z       4. ELF ≈ 0.5: metallic/electron gas character
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2903736Z       5. ELF < 0.25: ionic/open regions
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2903979Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2904163Z       ## VESTA vs. Alternative Tools
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2904537Z       
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2904751Z       | Feature | VESTA | Avogadro | PyMOL | OVITO |
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2905136Z       |---------|-------|----------|-------|-------|
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2905562Z       | Crystal structures | ✓ best | ✓ | ✗ | Limited |
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2906014Z       | Charge density | ✓ best | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ |
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2906382Z       | XRD simulation | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2906830Z       | Protein structures | ✗ | Limited | ✓ best | ✗ |
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2907255Z       | MD trajectories | ✗ | ✗ | Limited | ✓ best |
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2907674Z       | Free | ✓ | ✓ | Open-source | ✓ |
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2907957Z       EOF
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2908224Z       echo "VESTA technology: $(wc -l < tools/vesta/technology.md)"
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2908703Z     description: Expand VESTA technology
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2909157Z   result: |
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2909432Z     VESTA technology: 169
2026-02-26T02:56:38.2909662Z     <exited with exit code 0>
