2026-02-26T03:00:48.3426069Z     # Avogadro
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3426241Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3426544Z     **Category:** Molecular Visualization, Molecular Editor  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3427131Z     **Developer/Organization:** Avogadro community (Marcus Hanwell, Geoffrey Hutchison, and contributors)  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3427810Z     **License:** GNU Lesser General Public License v2.1 (LGPL)  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3428237Z     **Website:** https://avogadro.cc  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3428598Z     **GitHub:** https://github.com/avogadro/avogadro2  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3428923Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3429350Z     ## Overview
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3429534Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3430734Z     Avogadro is an open-source, cross-platform molecular editor and visualizer designed for computational chemistry, molecular modeling, and education. Available on Linux, macOS, and Windows, it provides an intuitive graphical interface for building, editing, and visualizing molecular structures.
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3432129Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3433171Z     Avogadro 2 (the current version) was rewritten with a modern C++/Qt architecture with a plugin-based design. It uses OpenChemistry project libraries (libavogadro, libmsym) and integrates with various quantum chemistry codes through input generation wizards.
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3434292Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3435895Z     A key strength of Avogadro is its use as an **input generator** for computational chemistry codes: it can generate Gaussian, ORCA, GAMESS, NWChem, Psi4, and Quantum ESPRESSO input files directly from a 3D structure drawn in the GUI. This makes it particularly valuable for students and researchers who are not familiar with the text-based input formats of these codes.
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3437385Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3437558Z     ## Key Features
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3437747Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3438141Z     - **Structure building**: Click-based atom placement; draw mode with atom/bond tools
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3438841Z     - **Force field optimization**: MMFF94, UFF, GAFF geometry optimization; energy minimization button
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3439642Z     - **3D coordinate generation**: Clean up molecular geometry automatically
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3440167Z     - **Input generation**: Wizards for Gaussian, ORCA, GAMESS, NWChem, Psi4, Q-Chem, Mopac, QE
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3440716Z     - **Output visualization**: Display MOs, electron density from Gaussian CUBE files
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3441260Z     - **Crystal structure viewer**: Unit cell visualization; symmetry operations display
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3441780Z     - **Spectral data**: IR/Raman frequency visualization; UV/Vis spectrum display
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3442467Z     - **Molecular operations**: Fragment library; symmetry operations; chirality assignment
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3443003Z     - **File formats**: SDF, MOL, MOL2, PDB, XYZ, CML, SMILES, CIF (Open Babel integration)
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3443523Z     - **Molecular dynamics**: Built-in MD for geometry exploration (not production MD)
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3444027Z     - **Python scripting**: Avogadro Python API for batch operations
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3444457Z     - **Unit cell builder**: Build supercells, apply space group symmetry
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3444791Z     ...
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3444984Z     === tools/namd/index.md (59 lines) ===
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3445236Z     # NAMD
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3445402Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3445653Z     **Category:** Molecular Dynamics Simulation (Biomolecular)  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3446380Z     **Developer/Organization:** Theoretical and Computational Biophysics Group, University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (Klaus Schulten and successors)  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3447046Z     **License:** Free for non-commercial use  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3447382Z     **Website:** https://www.ks.uiuc.edu/Research/namd  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3447855Z     **GitHub:** https://github.com/ambermd/namd (mirrors exist; official distribution from UIUC)  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3448276Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3448439Z     ## Overview
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3448618Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3450355Z     NAMD (Not Another Molecular Dynamics) is a high-performance parallel molecular dynamics program designed for large biomolecular simulations. Developed at the Beckman Institute at UIUC by Klaus Schulten's group (now led by Emad Tajkhorshid), NAMD uses the Charm++ parallel programming model, which enables efficient dynamic load balancing and scalable performance on both multi-core workstations and petascale supercomputers.
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3451895Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3453344Z     NAMD is tightly integrated with VMD (Visual Molecular Dynamics) — the two tools form a complementary ecosystem for simulation and visualization. NAMD uses CHARMM force field parameters (but also supports AMBER force fields) and shares topology file formats with CHARMM, making it natural to use CHARMM-GUI for system preparation.
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3454567Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3455337Z     A notable feature of NAMD is its implementation of steered molecular dynamics (SMD) and adaptive biasing force (ABF) method, which are widely used to study protein mechanical properties and compute potential of mean force.
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3456177Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3456341Z     ## Key Features
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3456525Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3456843Z     - **Charm++ parallelism**: Dynamic load balancing for heterogeneous simulation systems
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3457354Z     - **CHARMM force fields**: CGenFF, CHARMM36, CHARMM36m; also AMBER ff14SB
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3458077Z     - **PME electrostatics**: Smooth particle-mesh Ewald; highly optimized
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3458507Z     - **GPU acceleration**: CUDA-accelerated non-bonded forces
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3459112Z     - **Multiple time stepping (MTS)**: RESPA-like MTS for efficient long-range force computation
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3459849Z     - **Steered MD (SMD)**: Apply constant force or constant velocity; compute force-extension curves
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3460408Z     - **Adaptive Biasing Force (ABF)**: Free energy of mean force along a coordinate
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3460819Z     - **Metadynamics**: Via PLUMED integration
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3461308Z     - **Collective variables (colvars)**: Module for defining reaction coordinates and applying biasing forces
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3461855Z     - **REMD**: Temperature and Hamiltonian replica exchange
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3462282Z     - **VMD integration**: Real-time visualization and analysis during simulation
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3462973Z     - **IMD (Interactive MD)**: Real-time user interaction with running simulation via VMD
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3463368Z     ...
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3463558Z     === tools/vmd/index.md (59 lines) ===
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3463830Z     # VMD (Visual Molecular Dynamics)
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3464067Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3464314Z     **Category:** Molecular Visualization, Trajectory Analysis  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3465052Z     **Developer/Organization:** Theoretical and Computational Biophysics Group, University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (Klaus Schulten, now Emad Tajkhorshid)  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3465809Z     **License:** Free for non-commercial use (requires registration)  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3466209Z     **Website:** https://www.ks.uiuc.edu/Research/vmd  
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3466709Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3466993Z     ## Overview
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3467281Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3469719Z     VMD (Visual Molecular Dynamics) is the premier molecular visualization and analysis program for biomolecular simulations. Developed at UIUC alongside NAMD, VMD is designed to display, animate, and analyze large biomolecular systems using 3D graphics and built-in scripting. It handles single structures and long MD trajectories, supporting files from all major MD codes (NAMD, GROMACS, AMBER, LAMMPS, CHARMM).
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3471225Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3472375Z     VMD's strength lies in its combination of publication-quality 3D rendering, powerful Tcl and Python scripting for analysis, and support for GPU-accelerated computation. The built-in scripting languages allow users to implement complex analysis algorithms, generate publication-quality images, and automate workflows.
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3473553Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3474623Z     VMD is widely used for: trajectory visualization and movie making; RMSD, RMSF, and other structural analysis; protein-ligand interaction analysis; membrane analysis; hydrogen bond analysis; Voronoi analysis; and as the graphical frontend for NAMD simulations (including IMD, Interactive Molecular Dynamics).
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3475759Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3475925Z     ## Key Features
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3476109Z     
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3476513Z     - **Multi-format support**: PSF/DCD (NAMD), TPR/XTC/TRR (GROMACS), PRMTOP/NC (AMBER), XYZ, PDB, CUBE, and many more
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3477192Z     - **Rendering**: High-quality ray tracing via POV-Ray, Tachyon, OptiX (NVIDIA GPU ray tracing)
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3477843Z     - **Representations**: Wire, CPK, licorice, ribbon, cartoon, MSMS surface, SURF, QuickSurf (GPU-accelerated)
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3478443Z     - **Tcl scripting**: Full Tcl shell; custom analysis scripts; `measure` commands
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3478900Z     - **Python interface**: `VMDpython` for Python-based analysis
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3479573Z     - **RMSD/RMSF**: Built-in trajectory analysis tools
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3479903Z     - **Hydrogen bond analysis**: `hbonds` plug-in
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3480441Z     - **Membrane builder**: `membrane` plug-in; build lipid bilayers
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3481046Z     - **MDFF (Molecular Dynamics Flexible Fitting)**: Fit atomic models into cryo-EM density maps
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3481582Z     - **VolMap**: Volumetric density and occupancy maps from trajectories
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3482049Z     - **Timeline**: Timeline visualization of per-residue properties over MD
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3482441Z     - **Ramachandran**: Ramachandran plot analysis
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3482822Z     - **GPU acceleration**: Particle-based rendering, density maps on GPU
2026-02-26T03:00:48.3483163Z     ...
